comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: TILE-SHAPE DESIGN NOTE (2026-07-10, closes the 'widen the tile' question): batched-LANE GEMM cannot broadcast either operand (every lane is an independent matrix), so FMA:load ratio = tile_area/perimeter. Within NEON's 32 vregs: 4x4 = 16 acc + 8 operands (ratio 2.0), 4x6 = 24 acc + 10 operands = 34 regs (spills, ratio 2.4 = at most +20%). With 3 load ports vs 4 FMA pipes, ratio 2.0 already saturates the FMA pipes THEORETICALLY at ~36 GFLOP/s (4 vec-FMA/cy at ~4.5GHz); measured best-of ceiling for the fully-unrolled 4x4 is 24.6 (issue bubbles - 16 acc chains x 4cy latency = zero slack). CONSEQUENCE: even a perfect NEON-128 kernel reaches ~36/52 = 0.69 of peak-FMA; the realistic ceiling is ~0.45-0.50, and measured today under load is 0.20-0.31. The >=60% acceptance number implicitly assumes wider vectors (AVX-512: 8 lanes/reg, 32 zmm - the same 4x4 tile is 4x wider per load and the ratio economics differ). RECOMMENDATION: re-scope acceptance per-ISA - keep >=60% for the x86-64/AVX-512 row (measure on ts1/ts2 where the capsule work would also live), set the NEON-128 row at >=40% of peak-FMA (achievable with the packed kernel on a quiet host), both ledgered per fs-roofline conventions. Remaining implementation scope unchanged: quiet-host measurement, (c) autotuned interleave, (e) f32/mixed (f32 DOUBLES lanes per vreg on NEON - likely the biggest single win for this target and worth doing before more f64 shape work).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
x86-64 MEASURED (ts1, Threadripper 5975WX Zen 3, quiet load 4.66, release):
  batch-gemm attainment 0.083/0.068/0.048/0.039/0.027/0.022/0.022/0.023 at
  k=4/6/8/12/16/24/32/48 — ALL below_band, 2-8% of the roofline (target >=60%).
  batch-lu 0.47/0.23 at k=4/8 (no_target set yet).
ROOT CAUSE, confirmed in the dispatch wiring: on x86 the batched-GEMM tile kernels
run the SCALAR twin — crates/fs-simd/src/lib.rs:195-196 sets
  btile4x4_f64:  scalar::btile4x4_f64,
  btile4x4p_f64: scalar::btile4x4p_f64,
(comment: "btile stays the twin until 9ekv's consumer") while NEON dispatches
neon::btile4x4_f64 / neon::gemm::btile4x4p_f64. So Zen 3 runs the unvectorized 4x4
tile — the same scalar-fallback pattern that capped x86 fs-fft at 0.23 before the
AVX2 r4qrun capsule. The consumer now EXISTS (batched_perf_lane measures it), so the
deferral condition is met. FIX: an AVX2 btile4x4p_f64 capsule (bitwise twin of
scalar, gated by the tier-equivalence battery), wired at lib.rs:196 — same playbook
as the landed x86 r4qrun (204078a) and mk8x4 (xlvx). That should lift batch-gemm off
2-8% toward the 60% target on x86.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
AVX2 btile4x4p_f64 CAPSULE WRITTEN + ready to wire (crates/fs-simd/src/x86/gemm.rs,
registered in unsafe-capsules.json, SAFETY.md section added, x86/mod.rs re-exports
it). Design: mirrors the NEON gemm capsule — 4 A row-bases + 4 B col-bases, 16
__m256d accumulators from _mm256_setzero_pd (+0.0), _mm256_fmadd_pd over l in
ascending order = EXACTLY the scalar twin's per-lane mul_add; 4 lanes per block over
the mb dimension, scalar tail for mb%4. Compile-clean on the x86 cross-check
(cargo check --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aside from one unused import now
removed). CAVEAT: the 4x4 tile keeps 16 live accumulators > 16 YMM regs, so LLVM
will spill — a correctness-first lane vectorization, not register-optimal; expect a
PARTIAL lift toward 60% (the small-k cases are bandwidth-roofed per my measurement,
so SIMD helps the compute-bound k>=24 classes most — same lesson as the r4qrun FFT
capsule).

DELIBERATELY LEFT INERT (ops() at lib.rs:196 still dispatches scalar::btile4x4p_f64)
and NOT yet bitwise-verified — because fs-simd's x86 dispatch table is mid-refactor
by a concurrent agent (elem.rs, neon/gemmf32, new btile4x4pf32 Ops field) and is
CURRENTLY NON-COMPILING on x86. To finish: (1) wire lib.rs:196 ->
x86::btile4x4p_f64; (2) verify bitwise via tier_equivalence_battery on ts1/ts2
(cargo test -p fs-simd --release), which asserts (t.btile4x4p_f64) == scalar over the
(k,mb) grid — the capsule must be GREEN there before it goes live; (3) re-run
batched_perf_lane to measure the lift.

>>> BUILD FLAG (x86-only, invisible to the ARM fleet): the concurrent f32-tile
refactor added `btile4x4pf32` to the Ops struct + SCALAR_OPS + the NEON initializer +
the tier battery, but the x86 initializer (SimdTier::Avx2|Avx512 branch) is MISSING
the field -> `error[E0063] missing field btile4x4pf32` on x86. aarch64 compiles, so
the sweeper's gate is green and this won't be caught until someone builds x86.
Whoever completes the f32 refactor must add `btile4x4pf32: scalar::btile4x4pf32,`
(or an x86 f32 capsule) to the x86 branch.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SCOPE (e) COMPLETE (f32 path, 2026-07-10): converged with a parallel agent - they landed BatchMatF32/batch_gemm_f32/mixed-f64-accumulate in batched_f32.rs (d671cf1) while I built the fs-simd side; my duplicate type draft was removed in favor of theirs, and their plane-loop gemm is now WIRED to the new packed btile4x4pf32 capsule (four f32 lanes per NEON register - the ceiling analysis's biggest single win for this ISA; capsule + scalar twin + SAFETY.md + registry + tier battery all landed, bitwise per element, quad-tail delegation). Same packed doctrine as the f64 path (l-contiguous repack, 1/k overhead, MBLK_F32=256). All fs-la suites green including their f32 batteries (bitwise membership invariance holds through the restructure). Remaining on this bead: quiet-host/x86 measurement + the per-ISA acceptance re-scope (prior comment); scope (c) autotune.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
X86 CAPSULE WIRING LANDED (a78b976) + fresh quiet-ts1 roofline table. Found the x86 side collapsed at ~0.7 GFLOP/s flat across ALL size classes - the classic libm-fma trap: the AVX2+FMA btile4x4p_f64 capsule body existed in x86/gemm.rs (SAFETY.md registered, bounds-hardened) but the dispatch table still routed the packed batched tile to the scalar twin, whose mul_add is a per-element libm call on baseline x86. The batched perf lane was FAILING its 8% floor on ts1.

After the one-line wiring, quiet ts1 (Zen 3 5975WX, single-thread, peak_single 38.8 GFLOP/s):
k=4: 2.07 GFLOP/s (target_att 0.058) | k=6: 1.17 (0.033) | k=8: 5.42 (0.153) | k=12: 6.50 (0.184) | k=16: 8.52 (0.240) | k=24: 8.71 (0.246) | k=32: 9.72 (0.274) | k=48: 11.99 (0.338). Floor met everywhere, lane green again; batteries 19/19 + fs-simd bitwise self-checks 11/11 on BOTH ISAs (capsule is bit-identical: libm fma == vfmadd == NEON fmadd, goldens unchanged).

REMAINING GAP to the 60% compute-peak gate on x86: best 0.34 at k=48. Structure of the gap: (1) k=6 is pathological (1.2 GFLOP/s) - 4x4 tiling leaves 2-row/col ragged edges that dominate at k=6 (k=4/8/12/16 divide cleanly and jump); a 2-lane edge microkernel or 6-specialized tile would fix the worst class. (2) monotone rise with k says per-tile overhead (accumulator zeroing, pointer setup per (ti,tj), feature-check amortized already) still dominates small k. (3) even k=48 at 0.34 suggests the l-loop is load-port bound: 8 loads per 16 FMAs (a[4]+b[4] vectors) = 0.5 load/FMA vs Zen3's 3 loads/cycle : 2 FMA/cycle budget - register-blocking B (reuse bv across ti) would halve loads. Next concrete step: hoist the 4 bv loads out of the ti accumulation (restructure acc order), expected ~2x at large k, then the k=6 edge kernel. M4/NEON side previously measured 10-26 GFLOP/s (older table in this bead) - needs a quiet-window re-measure with the same lane before any both-ISA gate claim.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
Second increment landed (fef15c4): register-blocked the x86 capsule into two ti-half passes (16 acc + 8 operand vectors exceeded the 16-entry YMM file; now 14 live vectors fit). Post-split quiet-ts1 table: k=4 2.64 | k=6 1.00 | k=8 5.23 | k=12 8.18 | k=16 9.62 | k=24 10.72 | k=32 10.22 | k=48 10.51 GFLOP/s. Mid-k +15-25%, k=48 -12% (B re-read at the largest panels), k=6 unchanged-pathological. Bit-identity re-verified 11/11 both ISAs.

STATUS vs the 60% both-ISA gate: x86 best class is now ~0.28-0.30 target attainment; the profile has flattened around 10-11 GFLOP/s for k>=16, which with the strided-by-mb l-walk (lane-contiguous packed layout is REQUIRED for the lanes-across-matrices vectors, so the l stride is inherently mb) points at the L1/TLB streaming ceiling the lane doc already named, not register pressure anymore. Remaining lever ideas for the next stretch, in expected-value order: (1) k=6 ragged-edge microkernel (2-row half-tile) - worst class, cheap; (2) mb-block sizing sweep in fs-la's packing (smaller mb => shorter l strides, more L1 reuse per panel; currently fixed by MBLK doctrine); (3) software prefetch of the next l line per stream; (4) AVX-512 tier on a machine that has it (Zen 3 fleet is AVX2-only - the 60% x86 number may simply need the wider file/2x lanes). M4/NEON side needs a quiet-window re-measure before any gate claim; keeping the bead open with the floor-restored state as the checkpoint. Both wiring (a78b976) and split (fef15c4) are golden-neutral.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
Third increment landed (04584a7): ragged tails (k % 4 != 0) now route through ops().fma3 with ping-ponged accumulator rows instead of the per-lane mul_add loop (libm calls on baseline x86). k=6: 1.00 -> 2.65 GFLOP/s (2.6x), target_attainment 0.033 -> 0.086. Bit-neutral (same l-ascending fused chain per element); batteries green both ISAs, goldens unchanged.

Updated quiet-ts1 table (GFLOP/s | target_att): k=4 3.20|0.104, k=6 2.65|0.086, k=8 5.47|0.177, k=12 6.64|0.215, k=16 9.94|0.322, k=24 9.63|0.312, k=32 9.17|0.297, k=48 11.07|0.359. Note ~15-20% run-to-run wobble between lane invocations on ts1 (5-rep medians per class; visible k=12 6.6-8.2 across runs) - any future gate certification should use the dfh3 quiet-window discipline with more reps.

This lane's three increments (17x wiring fix, register split, fma3 tails) have taken x86 from floor-failing to a 0.09-0.36 attainment profile. The remaining 2-6x to the 60% gate is a genuine kernel-architecture problem on AVX2 (2 FMA ports x 4 lanes at ~4 GHz gives ~32 GFLOP/s ceiling per core; 60% of measured 38.8 peak needs 23.3 sustained - the packed streaming layout's L1/TLB behavior currently caps at ~11). Releasing my claim after this stretch; the ranked levers from the previous comment stand (mb sweep, prefetch, k-specialized microtiles, AVX-512 hardware).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: X86 ROW COMPLETE - LANE GREEN ON BOTH ISAs (CloudyFinch, quiet-enough ts2 window, commit 1d7e8bc). Story: first quiet-host x86 run EXPOSED the baseline-x86 scalar mul_add libm hole across the whole batched path (batch-gemm 0.5 GFLOP/s = 2-4% of roof, batch-lu 1.1, floor TRIPPED - the honest instrument catching a real collapse, not noise). Fixes: (a) fef15c4's f64 tile capsule (the other 9ekv lane - landed after my first measurement, credit to them) + my f32 sibling x86::btile::btile4x4pf32 (same two ti-half register blocking); (b) new fmacc op (elementwise fused accumulate) with AVX2+FMA facade; batch_lu elimination routed through it with the factor negated at fill - IEEE sign-commute keeps every golden bitwise (batteries green both ISAs). RESULTS ts2: batch-gemm k=48 7.31 GFLOP/s (14x), batch-lu k=32 6.99 (6.2x), attainment 0.28-0.33, floor_met=true environment_valid=true. REMAINING = THE TARGET DECISION ONLY: 0.60 of compute peak is unmet on BOTH reference ISAs (x86 0.28-0.33 now measured; M4 similar band) and the recorded batched-lane ceiling analysis explains why (4x4 entry-tile FMA:load ratio ~2.0 saturates neither machine's FMA pipes at this arithmetic intensity; lanes are independent matrices so no operand broadcast exists to raise it). RECOMMENDATION standing: re-scope the acceptance per-ISA to an achievable band (e.g. >=0.25 binding-roof floor-gated, which BOTH ISAs now clear with margin) or accept the tile-geometry redesign (8x8 entry tiles double the intensity but change bits = golden bump). That is a planner call - both-ISA data is now on the table. Releasing.

title: fs-la: batched GEMM alpha-zero and shape-overflow semantics
description: Fresh-eyes audit found that f64, f32, and mixed batched GEMM evaluated A*B even when alpha was +/-0, so poisoned operands produced NaN instead of beta*C, unlike the core GEMM contract. Batch stride/plane products also wrapped in release. Acceptance: checked construction arithmetic; alpha-zero no-read behavior for f64/f32/mixed and beta 0/1/nontrivial; scalar f32 oracle; exact controlled-panic regressions; explicit f64 and f32 semantic surfaces/golden coupling rows; docs must call this an intended LBM substrate until a production consumer exists.

title: fs-adjoint certification soundness: Verified color must honor the FD falsifier
close_reason: All 5 findings fixed on main: F1 (ef750cc, downgrade refuted gradients), F2 (e6cba61, scale verified intervals), F5 (1d1cf6e, dual_solve guard) by me; F3 (vacuous reconciles) + F4 (dwr accept-on-estimate) by fellow agents via b0a6665, both citing this bead. fs-adjoint all-features tests green.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
Soundness audit of fs-adjoint (gradient/adjoint + DWR certification) via a
focused review agent + independent verification. Arithmetic cores (interval
residual bound, Cauchy-Schwarz brackets, verify_gradient/fd_falsifier direction)
are SOUND. Findings in the color-assignment / explain / dwr layers:

F1 [FIXED, HIGH] commit ef750cc — certify() stamped Verified{0,bound} from the
   transpose-residual bound while IGNORING the fd_checks it stores. The residual
   bound certifies apply_t is the structural transpose of apply (tiny for ANY
   matrix) — it says NOTHING about gradient-formula correctness. So a gradient
   whose OWN FD falsifier flagged it (gc_002's sign-flipped grad_bad) still wore
   Verified{0,~1e-16}, a ledger-writable false certificate; only the out-of-band
   merge_gate caught it. Fix: certify now downgrades to Estimated when any
   fd_check is inconsistent (never launder a refuted gradient). gc_002 extended
   to assert the color. 22 fs-adjoint tests green.

REMAINING (documented, verified reproductions — candidates for follow-up):
F2 [MED] explain.rs:376-379 (adjoint) + :568 (drag): Verified colors built with a
   FIXED ±1e-12 (adjoint) / cdi/n*4 (drag) half-width, NOT a rigorous enclosure
   and inconsistent with the node's own relative `bound` field. For a channel
   with acc~1e6 the true fp summation error ~1e-8 >> 1e-12, so Verified
   [acc-1e-12, acc+1e-12] EXCLUDES the exact discrete value. Fix: use the node's
   relative bound for the interval (or downgrade). Diagnostic layer.
F3 [MED] explain.rs reconciles() is VACUOUS: finalize() sets residual =
   observed - attributed, so reconciles checks (attributed + residual -
   observed).abs() <= bounds == |0| <= bounds, ALWAYS true. The stated "kill
   criterion / permanent invariant" (tests/explain.rs:116,193) can never fire.
   Fix: reconciles should verify the node bounds COVER |residual|, not the
   tautology.
F4 [MED-LOW] dwr_accept::accept (dwr_accept.rs:81->104): a GUARANTEED bracket
   whose bound EXCEEDS tolerance is filtered out, then the code accepts on the
   DWR estimate with estimator_inconsistent=false. Rigorous "under-resolved"
   evidence silently dropped (color stays Estimated, so not a false Verified,
   but accepted:true for a solution a rigorous bound flags as failing).
F5 [LOW] edge cases: adjoint_residual_bound(op,0) -> Verified{0,0} with ZERO
   probes (no probes>=1 assertion); certify accepts Some(INFINITY) ->
   Verified{0,∞} (no fail-closed like fs-evidence); dwr_accept::dual_solve
   `let free = n - 2` underflows usize for n<2 (panic/huge-alloc CRASH on a
   degenerate ≤1-node mesh).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
F2 [FIXED] commit e6cba61 (src) — adjoint_attribution's Verified interval now
scales by the node's declared relative bound (1e-12*max(|acc|,1)) instead of a
fixed absolute ±1e-12, so it no longer excludes the exact discrete value when
|acc| is large. NOTE: the accompanying xp_001 assertion was dropped (85ee103) —
this fixture has |acc|<1 (every bound at the 1e-12 floor), so it cannot
distinguish the fix from the bug and tripped on ~1e-17 fp-cancellation noise. A
proper regression needs a large-|value| fixture (follow-up). The drag_decomposition
Verified bound (explain.rs:568, cdi/n*4) is left as-is — it carries a documented
conservative Trefftz bound; revisit for non-elliptic gamma.

Also audited fs-surrogate certify_or_escalate + conformal_band: SOUND — the
conformal quantile is the correct finite-sample ceil((1-alpha)(n+1)) with the
(n+1) correction and r[rank-1] indexes it with no off-by-one; the policy fails
closed on NaN.

STATUS: F1 (HIGH, ef750cc) + F2 (MED, e6cba61) fixed on main. Remaining
documented: F3 (vacuous reconciles), F4 (dwr accept-on-estimate), F5 (edge
cases incl. dual_solve n<2 usize underflow crash).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: F5 [FIXED, 1d1cf6e] dual_solve guards n<2 (was usize underflow crash). fs-adjoint remaining: F3 (vacuous reconciles), F4 (dwr accept-on-estimate) — documented.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent) - taking the REMAINING findings after verifying current state: F2 BOTH sites already fixed in-tree (adjoint relative bound at explain.rs:376 w/ 9sf6 comment; drag interval now consistent with the node bound at :575-585); F5 mesh-underflow guard committed (1d1cf6e). TAKING: F3 - reconciles() is vacuous (residual = observed - attributed by construction => the check is |0| <= bounds, the kill criterion can never fire); fix = the invariant becomes BOUNDS COVER |residual| (non-vacuous: bad channel math leaves an uncovered residual and FIRES) + a can-fail test. F4 - accept() silently drops a GUARANTEED bracket whose bound exceeds tolerance and accepts on the bare estimate; the Bracket carries only an UPPER bound so over-tolerance = rigorously INCONCLUSIVE, and accepting against that evidence violates fail-closed certificate doctrine; fix = refuse acceptance (accepted:false, under-resolved audit line, bracket recorded) when a guaranteed bracket cannot confirm tolerance + tests. F5-leftover - certify() accepting Some(INFINITY) residual bounds; fix = non-finite bound => no Verified (downgrade path) + probes>=1 assertion on adjoint_residual_bound if still absent.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: POST-CLOSE ADDENDUM (my in-flight work landed as the bead closed - fixes are in-tree, all suites green): F3 reconciles() de-vacuized (coverage invariant: bounds must absorb |residual|; can-fail regression added - the old identity form could never fire); F4 accept() now FAILS CLOSED when a guaranteed bracket cannot confirm tolerance (REFUSED audit line; regressions: certified inconclusiveness vetoes a passing-looking estimate, heuristic brackets do not); F5-leftovers: certify(Some(INFINITY/NaN)) can no longer mint Verified (downgrade path + regression), adjoint_residual_bound asserts probes >= 1 (zero-probe evidence panics + should_panic regression). fs-adjoint 37/37 under --all-features, clippy zero warnings, fmt. Certification-layer semantics only - numerical kernels and cross-ISA goldens untouched.

title: fs-bo integration lanes: FrankenTorch tape acquisition gradients + heteroscedastic/e-process stopping via fs-uq
description: SPLIT from frankensim-tzeh (implementation lanes all landed cross-ISA: TuRBO trust-region BO, two-fidelity ICM multi-fidelity w/ MFEI cost-aware selection, DTC/Titsias sparse GPs w/ the ELBO bound instance-checked). THIS bead = the two cross-crate integration lanes: (a) TAPE ACQUISITION GRADIENTS: differentiate q-EI through the Cholesky reparameterization f = mu(X) + L(X)z via the FrankenTorch tape (the fixed-bank surfaces are differentiable by construction - needs dL/dX through Cholesky, which ft's tape has; replaces the CMA-ES inner argmax where dimension makes gradient ascent worthwhile; acceptance = tape gradient vs FD on the fixed-bank q-EI surface + argmax quality/cost vs CMA-ES at matched budget). (b) HETEROSCEDASTIC + ANYTIME: noise variances fed from fs-uq estimates (the other agent's fs-uq::anytime landed estimate_probability_anytime + sub-Gaussian confidence sequences - the primitives exist); per-point noise in the GP (trivially: diagonal sigma_i^2 instead of scalar - the Cholesky path is unchanged) + e-process-compatible stopping for noisy-objective BO loops (stop when the incumbent's CS separates from the challenger's - coordinate semantics with fs-eproc/fs-uq owners). Acceptance: heteroscedastic GP recovers known per-point noise on a synthetic fixture; anytime-stopped BO on a noisy objective terminates validly (miss-rate simulation under adversarial stopping per the certifying-the-certifiers doctrine).
close_reason: BOTH LANES LANDED (fs-bo). LANE (b) heteroscedastic + e-racing (recorded earlier: try_fit_diag w/ hand-checked coincident-point algebra + 100x no-drag demonstration; e-racing at 49% sample savings with every stopped CS valid at its stopping time - cross-ISA verified). LANE (a) TAPE ACQUISITION GRADIENTS (feature tape-acq; fs-ad torch-bridge): EXACT q-EI gradients by ONE reverse pass through the full taped chain - Matern kernels, posterior assembly, the q x q CHOLESKY (differentiated as scalar ops - no matrix-level backward needed), and the reparameterized hinge; qei_ascent = probe-then-polish L-BFGS argmax on the surface. THE WAR STORY (documented in CONTRACT + a permanent probe regression): the first FD gate PASSED VACUOUSLY - random candidates sat in a no-improvement region where grad == fd == 0 - while the Sigma-diagonal's sqrt(0) BACKWARD (infinite at zero) poisoned every active-region gradient with NaN. Caught by refusing to accept a suspicious pass (gnorm exactly 0.0 in 1 reverse pass); fixed CORRECTLY not defensively: Matern-5/2 is C-squared so k at r=0 is a constant with exactly zero gradient. Post-fix: primal parity 1.3e-7 rel, gradient vs FD worst 2.0e-8 WITH aliveness guards (surface active + all entries finite + gnorm alive), polish 0.314 -> 0.374 in 47 reverse passes (FD-equivalent 423 evals); CMA-ES REPORTED at 0.488 - an MC needle spike at near-duplicate blocks, ledgered not gated (dishonest in both directions otherwise). DETERMINISM CLASS per the bridge contract: tolerance-verified, never bitwise, EXCLUDED from cross-ISA goldens (aarch64 evidence; the production f64 q-EI stays on det kernels with its existing cross-ISA golden). Gates: clippy zero warnings incl. the feature build, fmt, CONTRACT current. fs-bo is now COMPLETE per its bead family: exact GPs, EI/q-EI, BO loops, TuRBO, multi-fidelity, sparse, heteroscedastic, e-racing, tape gradients.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: LANE (b) STARTED - heteroscedastic GPs + anytime-stopped noisy BO (the tape-gradient lane (a) stays; FrankenTorch integration). fs-bo::gp extension: Gp::try_fit_diag - PER-POINT noise variances (K + diag(sigma_i^2); the Cholesky path is otherwise unchanged); the LML and predict paths are noise-agnostic once the matrix is built. GATES: (1) COINCIDENT-POINT closed form: two observations at the SAME x with different noises - the 2x2 posterior algebra is hand-computable exactly (the posterior mean is the precision-weighted pull toward the low-noise observation; wrong-weighting implementations fail this immediately); (2) DOWN-WEIGHTING demonstration: a corrupted-but-declared-noisy cluster does not drag predictions (vs the SAME data fit homoscedastically - the comparison measured); (3) ANYTIME-STOPPED NOISY BO: per-candidate replication driven by fs-uq estimate_probability_anytime (the other agent's o5kc primitive - cross-agent composition) with the stop valid at the stopping time; gate = the loop finds the noiseless optimum region while consuming FEWER samples on clearly-bad candidates than a fixed-replication baseline (adaptive allocation measured); (4) golden + trj.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: LANE (b) HETEROSCEDASTIC + E-RACING COMPLETE (fs-bo::gp try_fit_diag + hetero_battery composing fs-eproc/fs-uq - CROSS-AGENT integration: my GPs + their confidence sequences). LANDED: Gp::try_fit_diag - per-point noise variances K + diag(sigma_i^2), Cholesky/LML/predict paths unchanged once built. EVIDENCE (4/4 BOTH ISAs - trj /data/tmp/fshet.Hp4AX3, GOLDEN 0xe9b3_f6b5_69ee_258b frozen-assert-verified): COINCIDENT-POINT closed form - two observations at the same x with sigma_a << sigma_b, the 2x2 posterior algebra matched BY HAND to 1e-10 and the mean pulled to the low-noise observation; NO-DRAG demonstration - a corrupted-but-declared-noisy cluster leaves the heteroscedastic fit at err 0.011 while the SAME data fit homoscedastically drags to err 1.14 (measured comparison); E-RACING candidate elimination - challengers sampled until their CS separates from the incumbent's: optimum found at 49% fewer samples than uniform max replication AND every stopped CS covers its true mean AT the stopping time (the Bet-5 anytime-validity claim, instance-checked - certifying-the-certifiers). TWO FIXTURE BUGS CAUGHT AND DOCUMENTED IN-TEST: (1) half-width-target stopping is MEAN-INDEPENDENT for fixed-sigma CSs (every candidate stops at the same n - the first cross-candidate comparison was vacuous by construction; racing is the real e-process pattern); (2) the Hoeffding sigma=1/2 default was 3x conservative vs the true noise 0.15 (clamping is a contraction, so the actual sd is a valid sub-Gaussian parameter - an 11x sample-count difference). Gates: clippy zero warnings, fmt, CONTRACT updated. REMAINING: lane (a) FrankenTorch tape acquisition gradients.

title: fs-robust CVaR under-reports non-integral empirical tails
description: Fresh-eyes cross-crate trace found fs-robust::cvar still averages ceil(n*(1-alpha)) worst samples at equal weight, despite fs-uq fixing the identical anti-conservative bug under frankensim-zsvk. When n*(1-alpha) is non-integral this dilutes the boundary mass and under-reports expected shortfall, directly affecting robust_optimum decisions. Replace the epsilon/ceil heuristic with standard Acerbi-Tasche finite-sample CVaR using fractional boundary order-statistic weight; add exact non-integral-tail regression, parity-style integer-boundary coverage, contract wording, focused tests/clippy/UBS.

title: fs-feec sum-factorized kernels hit the baseline-x86 mul_add libm hole: 0.026 attainment on 5995WX vs 0.372 on M4 Pro
description: Found by the fz2.2 attainment scoreboard. fs-feec's sum-factorized apply (feec-apply lane) measures 17.2 GFLOP/s / 0.372 attainment on aarch64 (gate floor 0.30 passes) but 0.61 GFLOP/s / 0.026 attainment on the 5995WX (linux-x86_64, release) — a ~28x per-core deficit that tracks the known baseline-x86 hazard: f64::mul_add lowers to a PER-ELEMENT libm fma() CALL because x86-64's baseline target lacks compile-time FMA (same class as the fs-roofline peak-probe bug and the fs-simd fma3 dispatch hole, both fixed under xlvx/fz2.2). fs-feec's inner kernels use raw mul_add rather than the fs-simd dispatch table. Fix options, in preference order: (1) route the hot loops through fs_simd::ops() primitives (fma3/axpy/dot already have runtime-detected AVX2+FMA capsule paths, bit-identical per element); (2) a target_feature-dispatched kernel body like fs-roofline's fma_pass_x86. Mind the crate's determinism contract when restructuring loops: elementwise fused ops are bit-safe to reroute; reduction SHAPES must stay fixed per tier. Same hazard likely applies to fs-la's packed f32/mixed macro kernels (noted in fz2.2 s5) — one bead can sweep all raw-mul_add hot loops on the x86 lane; grep for mul_add in L1/L2 kernel crates and check each against its perf lane. Acceptance: feec-apply attainment on linux-x86_64 within 2x of the aarch64 attainment (or an honest documented reason), gate floor 0.30 green on both machines, bitwise batteries green.
close_reason: Restoring the capsule owner's close after reconciliation - the COMPLETE fix is theirs (super::fma body-level target_feature(avx2,fma) capsule w/ SAFETY.md: apply_mono_dispatch compiles the WHOLE contraction body with native fused ops, one dispatch per apply). MY PARALLEL WORK, reconciled: the per-row fs-simd axpy cfg-split I landed concurrently is REVERTED (my own edits only) - inside their fma-enabled body, indirect per-row calls would re-introduce the exact overhead disease; the contract kernels are back to plain mul_add loops with a comment recording all three measurements (libm hole 0.026 -> per-row axpy 0.076 -> body-level capsule = the fix; and the aarch64 4x-slower lesson that rules out per-row dispatch anywhere). Also: hoisted their use into fn scope (items_after_statements lint), removed my now-unused fs-simd dep from fs-feec. Verification: highorder battery 6/6 w/ goldens intact through ALL THREE routings (the fs-simd per-element contract + their capsule's identical-body guarantee both held bitwise), clippy zero warnings, fmt. The two-agent collision resolved to the correct final state: their capsule, my measurements documenting WHY per-row dispatch is the wrong shape at P=4-8.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PARTIAL FIX LANDED + PRECISE HANDOFF (fs-feec hex.rs contract kernels). WHAT LANDED: cfg-split routing - x86_64 routes contraction rows through fs_simd::ops().axpy (runtime AVX2+FMA capsule, bit-identical per element); aarch64 KEEPS the plain inlined mul_add loops. MEASURED BOTH WAYS (the lesson that forced the split): my first attempt routed ALL arches through the dispatch and made aarch64 4x SLOWER (17.2 -> 4.2 GFLOP/s quiet-machine) - an indirect call per 4-8-element row costs more than it saves where mul_add is already a native inlined fma; the dispatch must never cost more than it saves, now documented at the kernel. RESULTS: aarch64 RESTORED/improved: 19.35 GFLOP/s, attainment 0.375, floor 0.30 GREEN (goldens intact - bit-neutrality held through both routings, confirming the fs-simd per-element contract). x86_64 (trj, load ~30): 0.61 -> 2.66 GFLOP/s at the r=3 gate = 4.4x improvement, attainment 0.026 -> 0.076 - REAL but still below the 0.30 floor and the within-2x-of-aarch64 acceptance. REMAINING GAP, precisely: per-row indirect-call overhead at P=4-8 elements (call + slice setup per 4-8 fmas) - the same disease that killed the aarch64 routing, just smaller than the libm hole it replaced. THE COMPLETION is the bead's option (2): a BODY-LEVEL target_feature(enable=fma) clone of the contraction trio dispatched ONCE per apply (the fs-roofline fma_pass_x86 pattern) - that is an unsafe-capsule addition (<300 lines + SAFETY.md + registration) squarely in the fs-simd/capsule owner's lane (same discipline as their btile/8nfp work). Evidence: fs-feec 34/34 correctness green both ISAs (trj /data/tmp/fsax2.zi9WvK battery 6/6), clippy zero warnings, fmt; perf rows ledgered above. Releasing with the handoff.

title: Separate declared colors from admitted scientific evidence
description:
Fresh-eyes audit found a remaining type-boundary mismatch after Certified<T> became opaque: fs_evidence::Color is still both a plain declaration enum and the value many reports expose as positive scientific evidence. Structural payload validation can reject NaN, inverted intervals, malformed identities, and stale derived prefixes, but it cannot authenticate that a direct Color::Verified or Color::Validated constructor came from an admitted certificate/dataset. Roughly two dozen production modules still construct positive colors directly; ColorGraph/package/checker paths are stricter, but consumers can accidentally bypass those paths or call declared_color_unverified.

Root cause: epistemic rank representation and admission authority share one public type.

Design a staged, layer-correct split between untrusted DeclaredColor/Color candidates and an opaque AdmittedColor (or equivalent ScientificColor handle) that can be created only by a replay-verifiable ColorGraph/checker admission receipt. Lower numerical layers must remain independent of HELM: they produce typed certificate candidates and owner-specific proof payloads, while the admission boundary authenticates them. Waived, unresolved, stale-algebra, and out-of-regime nodes cannot convert to admitted scientific evidence. Preserve raw Color for rendering/diagnostics where useful, but make positive-evidence APIs require the opaque type.

Acceptance: direct construction of Color::Verified/Validated cannot satisfy package release, headline, ledger scientific-rank, or public capability APIs; compile/API tests cover every bypass; source-certificate and dataset-origin receipts replay; regime exit demotes structurally; all current direct-constructor sites are inventoried and migrated in bounded owner groups; no upward layer dependency is introduced; G0 laundering, serialization, downgrade, and tamper batteries plus end-to-end checker/package/flywheel tests pass.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Additional owner-group finding from fs-robust audit: weakest_color/headline_color currently accept raw publicly constructible Color values, do not validate payloads, and return an arbitrary same-rank payload based on input order. Thus a direct Color::Verified/Validated can satisfy a positive headline API, and two equal-rank inputs have no permutation-invariant aggregate provenance/payload. Migration must make lower-layer inputs explicit declared candidates, require an admitted view for positive headline/capability reporting, and avoid pretending rank-only selection authenticates or composes payloads. Fragility/color rendering may retain declared candidates under explicit unverified naming.

title: fs-solver saddle-point block preconditioners: Stokes-class Schur approximations over the FEEC mixed spaces
description: SPLIT from tfz.10 (slices 1-3 landed in crates/fs-solver: resumable CG/MINRES/GMRES with deterministic reductions + structured stall diagnoses, matrix-free p-MG over the fs-feec tensor hierarchy with exact-injection transfers and SA-AMG-CG coarse, f32-inner/f64-refine mixed precision). THIS bead: block preconditioners for saddle-point systems (Stokes, incompressibility, contact) - block-diagonal/triangular preconditioners with Schur-complement approximations (pressure-mass for Stokes; PCD-class documented per physics). BUILD ON: fs-solver MinresState (the natural saddle-point outer solver), LinearOp trait (block operators compose as struct wrappers), fs-feec tensor de Rham spaces (velocity in H1^3 or H(div), pressure in L2 - the discrete pair from tfz.6 slice 2; note the L2 Legendre mass is DIAGONAL, making the pressure-mass Schur approximation trivially invertible - a real payoff). FIXTURE: lid-driven-cavity-free Stokes on the unit cube via the tensor spaces (assemble B = div coupling from the Kronecker derivative G; the discrete inf-sup pair Q_r^3/Q_{r-1}^disc is the classic stable choice); MINRES on [[A B^T],[B 0]] with blockdiag(AMG-or-pMG on A, pressure mass on Schur) - iteration counts flat-ish across h (envelope gate). GATES: solutions vs dense LU on fixture scale; divergence-freeness of the velocity solution to solver tol; iteration envelope; resume bitwise (inherited); both-ISA golden. DEPENDS on nothing open (tensor spaces + solvers all landed); the simplicial-vector-families bead (frankensim-dcng) is the UNSTRUCTURED counterpart, independent.
close_reason: Stokes-class saddle-point block preconditioners shipped. New: PminresState (resumable preconditioned MINRES, Lanczos in the M-inner product, SPD preconditioner — closes the CONTRACT's own 'follow-up scope' gap; M-norm residual estimate cross-checked vs true residuals; bitwise resume at cuts 1/7/23) + stokes module (StokesSystem/StokesOp/StokesBlockDiag): Q_r^3 velocity (H1 Lobatto tensor, homogeneous Dirichlet) x P_{r-1}^disc pressure (per-cell TOTAL-DEGREE Legendre — L2 mass DIAGONAL, the promised Schur payoff), weak-divergence B assembled per cell from 1D quadrature couplings, constant-pressure null projected (the equal-h diagonal mass commutes with the projection so PMINRES stays in the complement). MEASURED rejections on record: (1) the bead's literal Q_{r-1}^disc full-tensor pressure is NOT uniformly inf-sup stable — counts grew 44->101->137 across m=2..4 at r=2 (Q1/Q0 checkerboard family); the P_{r-1}^disc subset (classic Q2/P1disc family) flattens them to 26..61, envelope-gated. (2) pinning a HIGH pressure mode in the dense LU reference drops an independent constraint (pressure off 100%, measured); pin a cell-constant P0 coefficient whose row is dependent — also caught independently by a fresh-eyes collaborator audit (credited in CONTRACT). Battery stokes-001..005: dense pinned-P0 LU agreement (velocity 1.6e-14, mean-zero pressure 1.7e-14), |Bu|_inf 2.2e-12 divergence certificate, envelope (2,2,26)(3,2,55)(4,2,61)(2,3,44)(3,3,59) max 61<=120, bitwise resume, frozen separate golden 0x5754_3908_cb41_7281 (tfz.10 golden untouched). Lanes: fs-solver 18/18 across 4 test bins, clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, ubs 0 critical, xtask clean for fs-solver. PCD-class/Navier-Stokes/pressure-robust remain follow-up per CONTRACT.

title: fs-topopt CutFEM-octree marquee: zero-remeshing topology evolution, DWR-driven refinement, literature benchmark envelopes
description: SPLIT from 7tv.11 - THE MARQUEE (powers the P2 demo). Execute the landed fs-topopt density pipeline on fs-cutfem (landed by the other agent - SCOUT ITS API FIRST: certified cut cells, ghost penalty, SDF boundaries) over octree grids (fs-octree/fs-vdb lanes): density field lives on octree cells; physics assembled via CutFEM on the SDF of the CURRENT design (Heaviside-projected density -> SDF via redistancing, or density-as-indicator with ersatz void); TOPOLOGY EVOLVES WITH ZERO REMESHING - verified across full runs (the acceptance's marquee property: assert no mesh rebuild events in the run log; refinement = octree splits only). DWR goal-oriented adaptivity (fs-dwr in flight by the other agent - coordinate) refines where the OBJECTIVE feels it: compliance-goal dual weights the density-gradient indicator. LITERATURE BENCHMARK ENVELOPES live here (the 2D-class fixtures need the flexible geometry): MBB beam, cantilever, L-bracket at matched volume fractions - converged compliance within ledgered envelopes of literature values (envelopes recorded as evidence, not point-matching). Per-iteration timing logged (the interactive-cadence claim measured). Medial-axis thickness oracle audit of min length scale on results (geometry layer auditing optimization output - beyond slice 2's erosion-retention signal). Robust three-field + continuation schedules from slices 1-2 reused as-is.
close_reason: The CutFEM-octree topology marquee shipped as fs_topopt::marquee ([F]-gated behind cutfem-marquee): the density pipeline on CutFEM over an octree background grid — TOPOLOGY EVOLVES WITH ZERO REMESHING, and the run log proves it. (1) THE ARCHITECTURE: DensityDesign nodal densities where the SOLID region {rho > 1/2} IS the CutFEM domain (bilinear CutSdf with an exact-containment enclosure — extrema at lattice nodes and box corners); the background quadtree is built ONCE, refinement is grid.split/refine_where only, rebuilds are STRUCTURALLY impossible (no rebuild path exists in the function) and asserted zero across every run (tm-001/002); (2) THE HARD-WON SOLVER-CONTRACT LESSONS, all now conformance-guarded and in the CONTRACT: fs-cutfem's ghost penalty demands the cut band AND ITS ONE-CELL HALO at a uniform level (three CutBandNotUniform strikes taught halo_cut + per-iteration band re-conforming because the interface MOVES); interface membership is a neighbor SIGN CHANGE not a |phi| threshold (phi is a density gap, not a distance — the |phi|<eps test found zero band nodes and froze the run); flux probes project THROUGH the interface so void-side nodes read real flux (raw-node probes biased the run toward shrinking cooled boundary and J ROSE 135%); and the volume projection lives ON THE BAND (a global shift silently fills voids from the inside — the +6% J creep); with all four fixed, compliance descends 0.00956 -> 0.00818 at held volume (tm-001); (3) DWR-GATED REFINEMENT: fs-dwr per-leaf compliance-goal indicators x |grad rho| gate WHEN the band refines; concentration measured at 0.667 with the honest ceiling documented (~2/3 — the halo contract, not sloppiness; a fellow agent's upgraded concentration test, threshold aligned with the physics); (4) THE ENVELOPE + CADENCE (tm-004): converged compliance inside the recorded golden band on the volume-to-point fixture (heat-conduction benchmark class — elasticity envelopes ride CutFEM elasticity, no-claim), per-iteration wall times ledgered with the debug label; (5) THE THICKNESS ORACLE (tm-005): the medial-axis-class audit certifies the optimized design's minimum feature at/above the 2-cell floor — geometry auditing optimization output. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green (~197s); clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; check-all: 125 crates, ZERO violations.

title: fs-geom: chart agreement must not certify absent or malformed evidence
description: Region::check_agreement currently reports agreed=true for zero samples, one chart, NaN/infinite tolerance, NaN chart output, and NoClaim certificates because boolean comparisons fail open and evidence kind is erased. Acceptance: validated config/input/output; tri-state Agreed/Disagreed/Unknown; zero comparisons and NoClaim are Unknown; report weakest numerical evidence; adversarial G0 tests and contract update.
close_reason: Tri-state agreement now validates configuration, supports, finite chart outputs, and certificate semantics; missing/NoClaim evidence is Unknown, numerical strength is preserved, and focused fs-geom tests pass (19 unit, 6 conformance).

title: fs-iga: degree-aware quadrature to prevent high-order rank loss
description: Assembly and l2_error use fixed 4-point Gauss quadrature for arbitrary spline degree. A one-element degree-6 space has five interior DOFs but stiffness rank at most four. Acceptance: deterministic degree-aware Gauss-Legendre order at least p+1 in assembly/error evaluation; one-element degree 2..8 non-singularity and polynomial-reproduction gates; contract names quadrature rule.
close_reason: Replaced fixed quadrature with deterministic p+1 Gauss-Legendre and added degree 2..8 one-element rank/reproduction gate; iga 8/8 pass.

title: Make fs-verify fem1d solvers and fallback economics total, bounded, and convergence-explicit
description: Fresh-eyes audit found public solve_p1, solve_nonlinear, true_energy_error, hierarchical_estimate, warm_start, zoo, and economics paths accept malformed shapes/domains, can panic or allocate unboundedly, propagate NaN/Inf, and treat capped nonconvergence as successful iteration savings. Certified verify still fails closed, but Estimated effectivity/economics and fallback reliability are wrong. Implement one Fem1dError/shared bounded admission; fallible P1/oracle APIs; NonlinearSolveReport with residual and converged status; finite pivot/intermediate/final checks; canonical homogeneous BC; mesh/poly/iteration/work caps; propagate refusal through zoo/economics; correctly rounded Gauss constants; migrate all callers and add hostile tests. No legacy dependency or wrapper.

comments.author: codex-audit
comments.text: Audit implementation now makes the FEM1D solve/estimator/zoo/economics surfaces fallible, finite, capped, convergence-explicit, and nonconvergence-honest. A further planner review replaced floating endpoint admission with a fixed-size exact binary64 superaccumulator, catching hidden residues such as [0,1e16,-1e16,1] while accepting true cancellation and extreme exponents. Focused fs-verify/fs-ir/fs-adjoint/flywheel compile+strict-clippy passed via RCH local fallback; exact endpoint and hostile public-input runtime regressions each passed 1/1 locally. Keep open pending broad executable battery and the immutable shared canonical MMS identity tracked by frankensim-oaxj.

title: Bound roofline provenance reads before materialization
description: cost_model_from_tune claims bounded provenance validation, but fs-plan::validate_provenance materializes the dependency receipt artifact without checking ArtifactInfo.len and calls Ledger::op before applying its 1 MiB JSON bounds. Ledger artifact payloads and op IR/version fields are not byte-preflighted, so a writer can force unbounded allocation/hash work before refusal. Add explicit producer-derived byte caps, guarded/streaming artifact reads, and bounded op-row read/write preflight. Exact-cap and limit+1 tests must prove refusal occurs before payload materialization. Coordinate with the shared roofline evidence verifier extraction so both fs-roofline and fs-plan use one bounded path. Discovered while independently reviewing bead frankensim-0aeh / commit e63d3ad.

title: Thread explicit Cx cancellation through bounded long-running scientific workflows
description: Fresh-eyes cross-cut audit found several synchronous APIs whose scalar work is bounded but large enough to violate FrankenSim's cancellation-correct compute contract at admitted maxima: fs-asbuilt registration/diff can process up to 1,000,000 points; fs-assimilate admits a cubic proxy approaching 67,000,000 units; fs-adjoint DWR accepts up to 100,000,000 work units; fs-oed-e2e accepts up to 1,000,000 evaluations. Their existing Result/resource caps prevent unbounded allocation but do not carry an explicit asupersync Cx or poll at bounded tile boundaries. Acceptance: define layer-appropriate Cx-aware entry points without legacy wrappers that hide cancellation; preflight the complete work shape; poll at deterministic tile boundaries; implement request -> drain -> finalize semantics with structured partial/no-result diagnostics; bind cancellation mode/budgets into evidence identity; add G4 latency, no-leak, deterministic non-cancelled equivalence, and hostile-max regressions for each workflow; update CONTRACT no-claim language only after executable proof. Preserve pure-Rust sovereignty and do not substitute foreign runtimes.

title: Portable checker plugins for solver-free certificate semantics
description:
The schema-v7 package/checker boundary now strictly parses, re-derives roots/composition, authenticates typed origins through injected policies, and states its no-claim honestly. It still cannot independently verify the mathematics of certificate families whose verifier is outside the package. This is not a reason to import solvers: several high-value families are portable and solver-free once their witness data is included.

Add a versioned certificate-plugin registry to fs-checker for independently checkable witness families, starting with exact/interval enclosure arithmetic and residual certificates whose operator data and norms fit a bounded portable witness. Each plugin owns a closed schema, canonical bytes, resource bounds, semantic recheck, failure localization, and no-claim boundary. Unknown families remain explicit capability refusals, never generic Pass. Keep fs-checker free of geometry/solver dependencies and use only native Franken primitives.

Acceptance: at least two real certificate families round-trip from producer to package and are recomputed by standalone checker code; seeded structurally valid but mathematically false witnesses fail; every scalar/array length and arithmetic operation is bounded; plugin/version substitution and witness-field tamper fail; release admission distinguishes integrity, origin authentication, and independent semantic verification; checker dependency-policy and WASM/standalone builds remain green.

title: Claim-state lint: tie README/docs capability prose to code markers (implemented/measured/certified/targeted)
description:
Public prose must not drift ahead of code. The four claim states (implemented / measured / certified / targeted) already have machine markers in the tree: roofline SECTION_14_1_TARGETS landed flags, CONTRACT.md no-claim sections, placeholder or frozen golden hashes, #[ignore]-gated lanes, feature-gated [F]/[M] modules. Nothing currently checks the README (or future site prose) against them.

Deliver a narrow xtask check (or extension of check-contracts): (1) README Limitations rows that reference concrete markers (golden sentinels, ignored lanes, landed flags) are verified against the code state — e.g. the 'Randomized NLA golden sentinel' row must not claim placeholder status once the hash is deliberately recorded, and must not disappear while a cross-ISA proof is still pending; (2) capability rows in 'What You Can Use Today' name crates that must exist and expose the named symbols; (3) seeded drift (prose upgraded without code evidence) fails the check.

This is the repo-level precursor to frankensim-epic-foundations-huq.15.1 (the website flagship generates claims FROM evidence; this lint stops hand-written claims from drifting until that exists). Related: frankensim-epic-foundations-huq.5 (contract lint infrastructure).
close_reason: DONE. xtask check-claims (new xtask/src/claims.rs module + check-all wiring): (1) every 16-hex-digit hash cited in README.md must exist verbatim (underscore/case-insensitive) in some crate source/test — stale golden prose fails; (2) backticked fs-* crate references must exist under crates/ (wildcards/paths skipped); (3) backticked *_hash sentinel names must exist as fn definitions. Self-tests seed all three drift classes (18 xtask tests green); real-tree run: 0 violations across 125 crates (README currently honest — consistent with the sentinel-prose fixes committed in 23f4a71/a2a2b10). clippy -D warnings clean; wired as the seventh check-all lane. Deeper claim-state coupling (roofline landed flags, no-claim rows, evidence-generated site prose) remains huq.15.1 scope as designed.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (CloudyFinch). Implementing as xtask/src/claims.rs (new module, minimal main.rs hook — main.rs is contended with the y4pt check_goldens work). v1 scope: (1) every 16-hex-digit hash cited in README.md must exist verbatim (underscore-insensitive) somewhere in crates/ sources/tests — catches stale golden prose; (2) backticked fs-* crate names in README must exist under crates/; (3) backticked *_hash test symbols must exist as fn definitions in some test. Self-tests with seeded drift; wired into check-all.

title: fs-feec high-order perf: >=30% peak FLOPs sum-factorized apply at p=4 both ISAs (roofline-gated)
description: SPLIT from tfz.6 (the perf acceptance criterion; correctness landed there with the O(r^4)-per-element sum-factorized tensor apply matching the assembled Kronecker reference to 1e-12). THIS bead: measure and hit >=30% of peak FLOPs at p=4 on M4 Pro AND Threadripper, ledgered per fs-roofline conventions. Scope: (a) baseline measurement of the existing safe-Rust apply (crates/fs-feec/src/highorder/hex.rs apply_stiffness - per-element gather/contract/scatter with dense (r+1)^2 1D matrices); (b) the contractions are small dense GEMMs - route through fs-la batched/GEMM kernels or fs-tilelang lowering where profitable; (c) allocation hoisting (the per-apply scratch vectors); (d) apply-throughput-vs-p sweep table (the bead's perf-lane deliverable); (e) if the target needs intrinsics, the fs-simd capsule lane per xdgf/9ekv precedent. CONSTRAINTS: golden 0xaaf1_076a_196c_6902 must not change (bit-identical restructures only, or semantic-justification bump); release-profile measurements (debug numbers are meaningless for roofline claims); tie into fz2.4 perf-regression CI when it lands.
close_reason: GATE MET, BOTH ISAs, CITABLE. Acceptance was >=30% of peak at p=4 on M4 Pro AND Threadripper, ledgered: macos-aarch64 (M4, fingerprint 80cb534fbaf60b50) p=4 attainment 0.404 / 20.8 GFLOP/s and linux-x86_64 (ts1 5975WX, fingerprint 614b09f101a1e33b) p=4 attainment 0.439 / 15.6 GFLOP/s — both baseline-admitted citable rows under the dfh3 governance (stores in perf-baselines/, promoted via the new c40j roofline promote tooling this bead was queued behind). THE FIX SEQUENCE (each step bit-identical, sf-kron golden 0xaaf1076a196c6902 never moved, full suite green both modes at every step): x86 0.026 baseline (libm-fma calls) -> 0.046 (a55x avx2+fma capsule; vfmadds still scalar) -> 0.091 (register-array accumulators, commit 2168810) -> 0.44-0.59 citable (const-bound index loops, commit 291c7db — the shape x86 SLP finally packs; the iterator-zip chains were the blocker, exactly as the a55x handoff's scalar-69-vfmadds objdump implied). aarch64 also improved: 0.44 -> 0.556-0.699 raw / 0.404 citable-under-load. Also observed live during verification: the admission guard REFUSED the first ts1 run during its own compile burst (environment_invalid, neither pass nor fail) — the dfh3 design catching real contamination on its first production outing. CONTRACT perf-evidence section rewritten with the citable rows, the journey, and the low-order gather/scatter follow-up note. Credits: a55x lane (capsule + objdump diagnosis), dfh3 lane (admission governance), the handoff comments that made the relay work.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
x86 ARMED-PENDING ROW NOW MEASURED (user granted Threadripper access) — and it
FAILS the gate, so the "both ISAs" acceptance is NOT met. Ran the perf lane on ts1
(Threadripper PRO 5975WX, Zen 3, linux-x86_64), quiet (load 4.66), release, current
main:
  feec-apply attainment_single = 0.026 at EVERY order r=1..6 (p=r+1); gflops ~1.0.
  feec-gate p=4: attainment 0.026 vs floor 0.30 -> test sum_factorized_attainment
  PANICKED / FAILED.
Contrast: M4 hits 21.3 GFLOP/s = 43-44% at p=4 (this bead's close evidence). Same
compute-peak denominator (~38-49 GFLOP/s both machines), so 30% IS achievable and
this is NOT a roofline-denominator artifact — it is a ~16-21x REAL x86 codegen gap:
the const-P monomorphized inlined contract kernels autovectorize under LLVM for
NEON but generate scalar/poorly-vectorized code for AVX2 (x86 at ~1.0 GFLOP/s is
even below M4's pre-optimization 2.27 baseline). This is exactly scope item (e):
the x86 path needs the explicit fs-simd intrinsic capsule that NEON didn't (NEON
autovec sufficed; AVX2 did not). REOPENING — the acceptance requires >=30% at p=4
on BOTH ISAs and x86 is at 2.6%. Precedent for the fix: the AVX2 mk8x4 GEMM capsule
(xlvx) and the AVX2 r4qrun FFT capsule (27d3, committed 204078a) — a bitwise-twin
AVX2 contract kernel, verified via the golden aaf1076a196c6902 staying byte-stable.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
x86 VERIFICATION of the "44% of peak" fix (4456672) — IT DOES NOT WORK ON x86.
Re-measured on ts1 (Threadripper 5975WX, Zen 3, quiet load 4.53), origin/main @
0a5f0ee (includes 4456672's fs_simd::ops().axpy rewrite of contract_{x,y,z}_p):
  feec-apply attainment_single = 0.027/0.026/0.026/0.026/0.026/0.026 at r=1..6
  feec-gate p=4: 0.024 vs floor 0.30 -> sum_factorized_attainment PANICS/FAILS.
These numbers are BIT-FOR-BIT the SAME as the pre-fix measurement (1.03/1.00/1.00/
0.99/0.99/0.99 GFLOP/s) — the axpy rewrite gives ZERO net x86 benefit. So the
"44% of peak" is an aarch64-only result; the >=30%-BOTH-ISAs acceptance is still
UNMET on x86 (cwjn correctly stays OPEN).

WHY the dispatch-axpy doesn't help x86 (hypothesis): the inner contract lengths are
tiny (P=r+1, so 5 for p=4). ops().axpy is an INDIRECT fn-pointer call into the safe
façade, which per call re-runs is_x86_feature_detected!("avx2"/"fma"), then the AVX2
body does one 4-lane vfmadd + a SCALAR TAIL that is crate::scalar::axpy = mul_add =
the per-element libm fma() CALL again. For length-5, the dispatch+tail overhead
swamps the single vfmadd, netting ~the same as the old 5-libm-call loop. The libm-
fma root cause is real (confirmed: baseline x86 mul_add lowers to a libm call), but
routing through the dispatch façade at length-5 does not fix it.

RIGHT FIX (scope e, done properly): a fs-feec-LOCAL #[target_feature(enable="fma")]
variant of the const-P apply (runtime is_x86_feature_detected gate + scalar
fallback, registered capsule) so the const-P mono loops' mul_add lowers to inline
vfmadd with NO dispatch/indirect-call/tail overhead — bit-identical (libm fma ==
hardware vfmadd, both correctly-rounded), golden aaf1076a stays byte-stable. The
per-call dispatch axpy is the wrong tool for length-5 inner loops. (Verified on x86;
aarch64 44% unaffected.)

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE + FIX DEFINITIVELY CONFIRMED (controlled x86 experiment, ts1 Zen 3,
quiet load 3.37). Rebuilt the SAME source with RUSTFLAGS="-C target-cpu=native"
(makes mul_add lower to inline vfmadd — no libm call, no dispatch):
  feec-apply attainment: 0.388/0.266/0.348/0.390/0.455/0.433 at r=1..6
  feec-gate p=4 (r=3): 0.350 vs floor 0.30 -> sum_factorized_attainment ... ok (PASS)
  GFLOP/s 1.0 -> 12-15 (~13x). Compare the default build: flat 0.026, gate FAILS.
So: (1) the x86 gap IS the baseline-x86-64 libm-fma call for mul_add — proven, not
inferred; (2) an inline-fma path CLEARS the >=30% gate on x86 (0.35 at the gated
size); (3) the dispatch-axpy in 4456672 does NOT (per-call overhead at length-5).
Determinism preserved either way: libm fma == hardware vfmadd == aarch64 fmadd, all
IEEE correctly-rounded -> golden aaf1076a byte-stable (verify after any change).

TWO FIX OPTIONS (owner's call):
(A) WORKSPACE x86 build config: .cargo/config.toml
    [target.x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu] rustflags=["-C","target-feature=+fma"]
    Minimal, bit-neutral, and fixes the WHOLE libm-fma class on x86 (fs-roofline's
    probe already hit this — xlvx; any mul_add-heavy x86 kernel is affected).
    CAVEAT: makes x86 binaries require an FMA-capable CPU (all Zen/Haswell+; fine
    for the Threadripper/EPYC target families, not truly baseline-x86-64-portable).
(B) fs-feec-LOCAL capsule: #[target_feature(enable="fma")] const-P apply variant,
    runtime is_x86_feature_detected("fma") gate + scalar fallback, registered
    capsule + SAFETY.md. Portable (keeps baseline), scoped to fs-feec. This is
    scope item (e) done correctly (inline vfmadd, not the dispatch façade).
Recommend (A) as a workspace performance-infra decision since the libm-fma trap is
general; (B) if baseline-x86 portability must be preserved. Both proven to clear the
gate by the target-cpu=native experiment above. (aarch64 44% unaffected by either.)

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Handoff from a55x (closed): the baseline-x86 libm mul_add hole in the sum-factorized apply is FIXED (fma capsule, native codegen proven by objdump: 69 vfmadds, no libm calls in the hot path; ts2 2.9x to 1.79 GFLOP/s at r=3, bit-identical). THE REMAINING x86 GAP IS VECTORIZATION: all 69 vfmadds are SCALAR — LLVM fully unrolls the P=2..8 contraction inner loops (contract_y/z rows are P-length) and does not pack them on x86, while aarch64 autovectorizes the same code to 18.6 GFLOP/s. Concrete leads for your >=30%-both-ISAs goal: (1) contract_x_p's inner rows are already P*P long (16-64 f64) — check whether IT vectorizes and why y/z do not; restructuring y/z to sweep the P*P plane per coefficient would give 2-8 ymm ops per row; (2) fs_simd::ops().fma3/axpy have AVX2 capsule paths (bit-identical per element) but indirect-call overhead needs rows >= ~32 elements to amortize — fine for the P*P restructure, wrong for P-length rows; (3) keep the per-element accumulation ORDER fixed (ascending-l) or the sf-kron golden legitimately bumps — the P*P-plane sweep preserves it, lane-parallel-over-outputs also preserves it (independent outputs). perf_event_paranoid=4 on ts2 blocks perf stat; objdump on /data/tmp/cargo-target/release/deps/perf_lane-* is the workable inspection route.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (CloudyFinch), taking the handoff's lead 1: restructure contract_y/contract_z inner loops to sweep P*P-length contiguous planes per 1D-matrix coefficient (16-64 f64 rows - x86-vectorizable) instead of the P-length rows LLVM fully unrolls scalar; per-element ascending-l accumulation order preserved exactly, so the sf-kron golden aaf1076a stays frozen (verify, not assume). aarch64 must not regress from 44%. Measurement on quiet ts1/ts2 with the landed fma capsule. Reservations: crates/fs-feec/src/highorder/hex.rs (+ perf lane file if the model needs it).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
CORRECTION to my earlier "two fix options" note — OPTION (A) IS REJECTED. A global
`.cargo/config.toml` `-C target-feature=+fma` for x86 BREAKS CROSS-ISA DETERMINISM.
Verified on ts1 before committing: with +fma, LLVM CONTRACTS ordinary `a*b + c`
expressions (not just explicit mul_add) into a single-rounding vfmadd, diverging
from the non-contracted aarch64/baseline. Measured:
  cargo test -p fs-sparse -p fs-fft -p fs-la -p fs-topo -p fs-evidence -p fs-cheb
  -p fs-rand  (built with the +fma config)
  -> fs-cheb cheb_golden_hash FAILED (0xeea04b0a01de46cd; Clenshaw uses a*b+c);
     the other 6 crates stayed byte-stable.
So the G5 determinism guarantee is INCOMPATIBLE with a global +fma. (My "bit-neutral"
claim was wrong for `a*b+c` — only EXPLICIT mul_add is bit-neutral under +fma.)
The in-repo .cargo/config.toml now documents this rejection so nobody re-adds it.

The ONLY determinism-safe fix is OPTION (B), scoped: a fs-feec-LOCAL
`#[target_feature(enable="fma")]` variant of the const-P contract kernels (which
contain ONLY explicit `ail.mul_add(s, *d)` — no `a*b+c` to contract), runtime
is_x86_feature_detected("fma") gate + scalar fallback, registered capsule +
SAFETY.md. Bit-neutral (verified the contract kernels have no contractable a*b+c);
golden aaf1076a stays byte-stable; +fma-only (2-wide vfmadd) already clears the gate
on x86 (measured 0.398 at r=3 with target-feature=+fma). I can implement + x86-verify
this if desired — note it lands in the fleet's active fs-feec area (4456672) and
replaces the ineffective ops().axpy routing there.

comments.author: jemanuel

title: fs-feec simplicial high-order vector families: Nedelec/RT tree (H(curl)/H(div)/L2 on tets, r>=2) with vector-dof orientation
description: SPLIT from tfz.6 per the established discipline (slices 1-3 landed there: full TENSOR-PRODUCT de Rham complex at r=1..6 with exact Kronecker derivatives + commuting projections, and simplicial hierarchical H1 to r=6 with the sorted-global orientation convention and G3 permutation batteries). THIS bead: the simplicial high-order VECTOR families - hierarchical Nedelec first-kind H(curl) and Raviart-Thomas H(div) on tets at r>=2 plus discontinuous L2, completing the simplicial PrLambda-k tree. WHAT EXISTS TO BUILD ON: crates/fs-feec/src/highorder/simplex.rs (H1 hierarchy, Duffy quadrature, entity dof management, boundary masks, the sorted-global orientation convention PROVEN by the r=3 signed-permutation equivariance test); whitney.rs lowest-order edge/face forms (the r=1 members of these families); derham.rs shows the commuting-projection test pattern. KEY DESIGN CONSTRAINTS from the landed work: (1) orientation for VECTOR dofs is harder than H1 - edge tangents and face normals need the sorted-global convention extended to FRAMES (edge tangent = towards larger global index; face frame = sorted-triple circulation, matching what deram1/deram2 already pin at r=1); (2) the G3 battery must cover both the signed-permutation level (where kernels have definite parity) and the physics level (where bases mix); (3) commuting diagram tests vs the H1 gradient (pick the family consistently - first-kind Nedelec pairs with the full sequence); (4) unisolvence via mass SPD; MMS via L2 PROJECTION ladders (the derham.rs precedent - full curl-curl solves need tfz.10) + dd=0 through the discrete operators. Duffy quadrature and Legendre kernels are all in place. ACCEPTANCE: exact-sequence dimension counts at r=1..4; grad/curl/div chain maps with dd=0 to machine cancellation; conformity across shared entities (tangential trace for H(curl), normal trace for H(div)); projection convergence ladders; permutation batteries; golden + trj cross-ISA.
close_reason: Simplicial vector families shipped: highorder/vecfam.rs - first-kind Nedelec H(curl) + RT H(div) on tets r=1..4 + DG P_{r-1}, completing the simplicial P_r Lambda^k tree. NO hand-derived shape functions: Koszul bases in centered/scaled monomials (RT = (P_{r-1})^3 (+) xi*Htilde_{r-1}; Nedelec with the independent cross-product subset alpha_0=0-when-i=0, dim r+r(r+1)=r(r+2) exact), square dof-Vandermonde by pivoted LU per element. Normal-equations least-norm was tried first and REJECTED on evidence: cond^2 cost sqrt(eps) = 1.6e-7 tangential trace jumps; the independent basis puts them at 2.3e-12. Sorted-GLOBAL orientation frames (edge Legendre moments signed toward larger index = deram1 direction; face frames + circulation normal from sorted triple = deram2 normal). Battery vecfam_battery.rs 7/7: vec-001 dims 6/20/45/84 (N), 4/15/36/70 (RT) + Euler alternating sum = 1 both fixtures r=1..4; vec-002 dof-Kronecker 5.8e-13 + mass SPD r=2,3; vec-003 tangential jump 2.3e-12 / normal 1.4e-11 across shared entities r=2..4; vec-004 curl.grad 1.7e-14, div.curl 1.4e-13 (grad P_r subset N_r, curl N_r subset RT_r - interpolation matrices are exact chain maps); vec-005 canonical-interpolation ladders slopes 1.90/1.88 (r=2) 2.92/2.89 (r=3); vec-006 two-tier G3 per the bead spec: edge dofs transform with DEFINITE PARITY (-1)^{k+1} under relabeling (1.1e-16), physics tier field-invariance 6.6e-13 (found+fixed: analytic-field dof quadrature at r+3 leaked 2.8e-5 frame-dependent error, raised to r+7); vec-007 r=1 = Whitney forms to 4.4e-16 vs deram1/deram2 + golden 0x2737401f72b499f2 frozen (cross-ISA row LEDGERED PENDING per simplex policy). Lanes: fs-feec tests 9 suites all green (no regressions), clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, ubs 0 critical in vecfam (5 pre-existing degree-contract panic!s in hodge/cochain from earlier beads), xtask clean. CONTRACT.md extended (types/invariants/tests/no-claims: r>=5 conditioning + curl-curl MMS = tfz.10 recorded successors).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PLAN (CloudyFinch): new module crates/fs-feec/src/highorder/vecfam.rs - hierarchical-by-entity Nedelec 1st-kind H(curl) and RT H(div) on tets, r=1..4, plus DG P_{r-1} L2, completing the simplicial P_r^-Lambda^k tree. CONSTRUCTION (no hand-derived shape functions - the fs-flux bdm.rs precedent): per element, spaces from Koszul spanning sets in centered/scaled Cartesian monomials: RT_r = (P_{r-1})^3 (+) x*Htilde_{r-1} (EXACT direct sum -> square dof-Vandermonde, fs-la LU); Ned_r = (P_{r-1})^3 + {x cross x^alpha e_i} (overspans by r(r-1)/2 -> least-norm basis c = A^T (A A^T)^{-1} e via fs-la Cholesky). DOFS with sorted-GLOBAL orientation frames: edge = line integrals against Legendre P_k(s), s signed toward larger global index (deram1-compatible, r per edge); Ned faces = tangential moments against frame (p_b-p_a, p_c-p_a) of the SORTED triple x P_{r-2}(f) monomials (r(r-1) per face); RT faces = normal moments against sorted-circulation normal (deram2-compatible) x P_{r-1}(f) (r(r+1)/2 per face); interiors = (P_{r-3})^3 / (P_{r-2})^3 moments (element-private). Dimension checks r=1..4: Ned r(r+2)(r+3)/2 = 6/20/45/84, RT r(r+1)(r+3)/2 = 4/15/36/70, entity counts as listed. CHAIN MAPS grad/curl/div as global interpolation matrices (dof_i of the exact-poly derivative of basis_j - derivatives exact in monomial coeffs); dd=0 to roundoff since grad P_r subset Ned_r, curl Ned_r subset RT_r. BATTERY vecfam_battery.rs: vec-001 dims + Euler alternating sum = 1 (contractible); vec-002 dof-Kronecker + mass SPD (unisolvence); vec-003 tangential/normal trace continuity across two_tets shared entities; vec-004 ||curl*grad||, ||div*curl|| <= 1e-11 scaled at r=2,3; vec-005 L2-projection ladders order r on kuhn_cube; vec-006 G3 relabeling invariance (sorted-global convention makes entity bases pointwise equal under vertex relabeling); vec-007 golden hash (cross-ISA row ledgered pending, per simplex precedent). r=1 cross-checks vs whitney.rs edge/face forms. Full curl-curl solves stay tfz.10 scope.

title: Roofline: trusted historical axis baselines for sustained-contention detection
description: Static plausibility floors plus pre/post probe agreement cannot detect a host already degraded before the first probe and consistently degraded through the second (e.g. 6 GB/s pre/post on a normally 100+ GB/s reference host still self-normalizes). Acceptance: separately admitted fingerprint-specific baseline axes with provenance/age/firmware policy; current pre/post axes must agree with the trusted baseline inside declared bands before citable gates; first-run measurements are candidate evidence, not self-authorizing baselines; baseline update requires explicit governed promotion; quiet/degraded/stale/firmware-drift tests. Keep absolute floors as coarse last-resort sanity.
close_reason: Fully landed across two convergent slices, verified end-to-end on the committed tree: 2d6d3f9 (CloudyFinch: baseline module - governed promotion >=3 mutually-agreeing runs + named operator/justification, fingerprint/OS/arch/firmware identity, age policy, [0.70,1.15] bands, first-run-as-candidate law, strict bounded store, citable_axis_admission composing floors + reprobe + baseline) + a4d6eb5/5c6b721 (the other roofline lane: production enforcement - AxisBaselinePolicy threaded through run_is_citable / run_admission_error / record_run and the roofline CLI, fs-roofline-axis-admission-v1 receipts with BLAKE3 baseline content hash, schema_version on BaselineAxes). The 5.6 reopen gap (helper with zero production callers) is closed: the production evidence transaction and CLI now refuse un-baselined/degraded/stale/identity-drifted axes before anything citable is minted, with stable_sustained_contention_cannot_cross_the_production_admission_boundary pinning the original 6-vs-100 GB/s counterexample at the production boundary. Verified: full fs-roofline suite green on committed HEAD (45 lib + 8 conformance + 7 regress + 5 bin); floors kept as last-resort sanity

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Landed in 2d6d3f9 (crates/fs-roofline/src/baseline.rs + lib.rs export + CONTRACT.md section). Design: BaselineAxes = fingerprint-specific trusted quiet axes + provenance (named operator, justification, promotion day, source-run count) + age policy (<=365d) + declared environment identity (topology/OS/arch/firmware verbatim). promote_baseline is the only constructor: >=3 floor-plausible same-identity runs mutually agreeing within MAX_AXIS_REPROBE_DRIFT + non-blank operator/justification; promoted value = per-axis max (too-low baseline would inflate attainment); updates = re-promotion only. citable_axis_admission composes floors (kept, last-resort) + pre/post agreement + bands [0.70, 1.15]; verdicts: Trusted (only citable), Unbaselined (first-run = candidate evidence, never self-authorizing), Degraded (the 6-vs-100 GB/s counterexample: passes floors AND self-agreement, refused at ratio 0.06), Suspect (too fast = not the baselined machine), Stale, IdentityDrift (before band math). BaselineStore strict bounded JSONL, dup fingerprints = corruption. All drills green (quiet/degraded/suspect/stale-boundary/firmware-drift/first-run/6 promotion refusals/store tamper+dup); full fs-roofline suite green alongside the in-flight z353/ss0n tree; check-all 0 violations. HANDOFF to ss0n owner: the binding-gate seam should call citable_axis_admission before minting citable receipts - the API is additive and does not touch your Attainment/binding signatures.

title: Upstream DSR must fail closed and retain evidence-complete aggregate receipts
description:
Repo-local quality/x86 scripts now retain full logs and before/after source snapshots, but the required external DSR wrapper remains an evidence gap. Live dry-run output labels every unexecuted command Passed. Code review also found missing/malformed/unreadable repository configuration or zero derived checks can return success, command output is truncated rather than durably retained, configured top-level Cargo commands are not uniformly --locked, and one aggregate does not bind all checks to a single source/constellation identity.

Track and fix this in the DSR repository without replacing DSR with GitHub Actions. Missing/invalid config and zero checks must be hard failures. Dry-run entries must be Planned/NotExecuted and can never increment passed counts. Every executed command gets a complete durable log and exact argv/cwd/env subset/exit status. The aggregate receipt binds DSR version, config hash, ordered checks, full-log hashes, before/after repo+constellation snapshots, lock hashes, and artifacts; moving state invalidates the aggregate. Cargo quality/release commands use --locked.

Acceptance: seeded missing config, malformed YAML, unreadable config, empty check list, dry run, truncated output, command failure after earlier passes, moving tree, and lock drift all produce correct non-Pass states; standalone receipt verifier recomputes the aggregate; frankensim DSR quality cites exact artifact/log paths and cannot report 9/9 for a dry run.
close_reason: Fixed IN THE DSR REPOSITORY (commit 6e840a7 there; per the bead, DSR stays the CI runner — no GitHub Actions substitution). EVERY code-review finding closed fail-closed: (1) dry-run entries are now status=planned/executed=false — they CANNOT increment passed counts, qg_run_checks exits rc 2 for a planned-only run, and cmd_quality maps rc 2 to a distinct 'planned' JSON envelope, so dsr quality can never report 9/9 for a dry run; (2) missing, unreadable, or malformed repos.yaml, an unknown tool key, and a ZERO-length derived check list are all hard configuration failures (rc 4, status=config-error) instead of silent successes — malformed YAML surfaces via yq -e rather than being swallowed into []; (3) every executed command retains a COMPLETE durable log (exact argv, resolved cwd, DSR_/CARGO_/RUST env subset, full untruncated output, exit-code trailer) under the state dir, with its sha256 bound into the aggregate; the JSON keeps only a preview plus the log path+hash; (4) the aggregate receipt binds DSR version, config path+sha256, the ordered per-check records, and BEFORE/AFTER source snapshots (git HEAD + porcelain-dirt hash + Cargo.lock hash) — a tree that moves during the run yields status=invalidated-moving-source rc 1; (5) frankensim's cargo quality commands now carry --locked (clippy/test/xtask run) in the live registry; (6) scripts/verify_quality_receipt.sh is the STANDALONE verifier — recomputes count closure, status/count consistency, the moving-source invariant, the config hash, and every executed check's log hash + truncation trailer from receipt bytes alone. ACCEPTANCE EVIDENCE (all drilled live on the real module): seeded missing config rc4, malformed YAML rc4, unreadable/unknown-tool rc4, empty check list rc4, dry run rc2+planned+passed=0, command failure after earlier passes rc1 status=failed, moving tree rc1 status=invalidated-moving-source, verifier green-on-honest / red-on-tampered-log / green-after-restore; BSD-date arithmetic crash found+fixed en route (macOS %s%3N). bash -n clean on module and main script.

title: Make VoI scheduling evidence-gated, sealed, and truly myopic
description: Post-6d9c43d adversarial review found that audit_verdict does not gate schedule_probes; public raw ranked slices allow omission/splicing/staleness; one-state myopic scores are spent as an additive top-k batch; asymmetric uncertainty intervals can expand outside their prior support; grid aliasing can authorize false spend-nothing claims; and hint rendering loses price/name truth. Repair with a sealed complete RankedMenu/context receipt, a bounded anytime-valid matched-cost audit that mints private scheduling authority and is required by the scheduler, at most one executable purchase per ranking epoch followed by reranking, endpoint-wise interval contraction preflight before callbacks, estimated grid-qualified scores/no-claim language, and structured safe hints. Add boundary/adversarial tests and update callers/contracts.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
ROOT-CAUSE fix for a recurring problem. The pre-commit/sweeper gate compiles only
aarch64 LIBS (cargo check, lib targets). That misses two whole classes of breakage
that land x86-red on origin/main repeatedly (all aarch64-green, so invisible to the
ARM-only fleet + sweeper). Caught by MANUAL x86 verification this session alone:
  - select_mk8x4_f64 not in x86::gemm (mk8x4 refactor) — origin/main x86 non-compiling
  - missing btile4x4pf32 field in the x86 Ops initializer — I fixed in 731c96a
  - invalid JSON in unsafe-capsules.json (comma) — I fixed in edc26ab
  - hex::TensorSpace missing Debug in a TEST (`cargo check` lib-only never compiles
    tests) — the fleet fixed in 5320d00, but it was red in between
  - fs-session unresolved gemm_tune imports on x86 (rename not followed) — 622a41c
  - (+ the runtime ones only real x86 execution finds: fs-substrate page-audit
    alignment [fixed 9e6d1eb], fs-roofline/fsqlite tune-ledger corruption [u8og])

RECOMMENDATION (cheap, no x86 hardware needed for the compile classes):
  cargo check --workspace --all-targets --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
in the pre-commit/sweeper gate. `--target x86_64-...` cross-compiles from the
aarch64 dev box (~1-2 min, rustup target add once); `--all-targets` compiles TEST +
bench targets so the TensorSpace-class breaks are caught too. This would have caught
5 of the 7 above BEFORE they hit main. The runtime-only x86 bugs (page-audit, u8og)
still need real x86 execution — but the huq.4 both-ISA CI + Threadripper lanes cover
those post-commit; the GAP is that the PRE-commit gate never cross-checks x86 at all.
