title: fs-race: replace selective max e-values with anytime-valid elimination
description:
race_field constructs each candidate evidence as the maximum log e-value among currently surviving opponents, then feeds those selected maxima to e-BH. The opponent set changes after data-dependent eliminations. A maximum over optional e-processes is not itself an e-process without a valid multiplicity construction, and survivor-dependent selection can inflate false elimination despite the current seeded calibration tests.

Root cause: selection and multiplicity are applied after observing the same evidence, outside the validity contract of the pairwise e-processes.

Repair: specify and prove an anytime-valid candidate null using a fixed predeclared opponent family, valid mixture/union construction, or closed testing; freeze the hypothesis family independently of eliminations; document dependence assumptions required by e-BH. Reject non-finite losses structurally rather than panicking at winner selection.

Acceptance: mathematical derivation in contract; adversarial null simulations with optional stopping and adaptive elimination stay within alpha with confidence bounds; permutation/replay invariance; a deliberately invalid max implementation is caught by the certifier battery; domination retains measured savings.
close_reason: Anytime-valid elimination LANDED. Evidence construction replaced: candidate i's elimination evidence is now the MIXTURE (fs_eproc::combine_average, log-space) of pairwise e-processes over the FIXED family of all n-1 ORIGINAL opponents, dead opponents frozen at elimination (stopped supermartingale). Derivation in CONTRACT.md: each e_ji supermartingale under i's composite null -> convex combination is an anytime-valid e-process -> e-BH (Wang-Ramdas) controls elimination FDR under arbitrary dependence; the former survivor-dependent MAX fails both requirements. Non-finite losses now rejected STRUCTURALLY (invalid+eliminated+kill, poison never reaches e-processes or means; total_cmp winner selection; the winner-selection panic path is gone) in both race_field and successive_halving; RaceOutcome/BracketLedger gained an invalid field. ACCEPTANCE MET: (a) derivation in contract; (b) race-007 global-null adversarial sim with adaptive elimination + optional stopping: 8/200 any-elimination vs alpha budget 10+-9.2 within bounds; race-003 separated-field calibration 0/200; (c) race-001 replay invariance green; (d) certifier race-008 catches the deliberately-invalid max: E[e] under exchangeable null = 1.512+-0.274 (>1 Markov budget, CAUGHT) vs mixture 0.791+-0.146 (valid); (e) race-002/004 domination + savings retained. BONUS CATCH: the certifier's first failure exposed the battery's OWN noise fixture as a non-null (lag-1 autocorr 0.098, per-candidate persistent offsets mean^2 0.047 vs 0.017 iid) - replaced with splitmix64 keyed per tuple; the old calibration evidence was partly an artifact. fs-race suite 9/9 green, clippy clean.

title: fs-race: bind e-process validity to a checked declared loss span
description:
PairwiseRace documents pre-normalized paired losses but silently clips loss_b-loss_a to [-1,1]. That changes the estimand: the e-process tests the mean clipped difference, not the declared raw mean ordering. Equal raw means can therefore acquire positive betting drift. Exact counterexample: opponent loss is always 3; candidate loss is 4 with probability 0.75 and 0 with probability 0.25. Both raw means equal 3, but candidate-minus-opponent is +1 or -3, whose clipped mean is +0.5; the current adapter eventually manufactures evidence that the equal-best candidate is worse. Current race settings carry no range/span and several tests feed differences outside the documented bound.

Repair: make normalization part of the typed or checked API. Require a finite positive declared maximum paired-loss difference (or finite loss range), map linearly without clipping, and fail closed with a structured invalid-scale/no-claim outcome whenever a non-finite or out-of-range value appears. Bind the scale into replay/provenance. Update every consumer to declare a defensible bound; do not infer one from observed data.

Acceptance: the skew equal-mean counterexample never yields valid elimination evidence and triggers the scale guard when unnormalized; boundary +/-span maps exactly to 0/1; values one ULP outside refuse; rescaling losses and span together is metamorphically invariant; global-null, adaptive-stopping, domination, savings, replay, and kill-wiring batteries remain green; all production callers surface invalid scale rather than panic or silently clamp.
close_reason: Checked LossSpan replaces clipping; no-verdict failures, scale invariance, caller integration, and regression batteries pass.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Repair landed across aaccd69/0400a78/fef2b3b/384112f plus caller updates: LossSpan is finite-positive and structural, no clipping remains, support/non-finite breaches return no verdict, running means are overflow-safe, and scale covariance/extreme-value regressions are executable. Native race/ornith/vessel/flagship plus standalone WASM tests and focused clippy passed; RCH had no admissible remote workers and used its labeled local fallback.

title: fs-surrogate: FNO/DeepONet + POD-DEIM/balanced-truncation/Koopman, ledger-trained, conformal e-prediction, certify-or-escalate
description:
## What
`fs-surrogate` (plan §9.7, Bet 9 [F]): learned accelerators with GUARANTEES — neural operators and classical ROMs as 100–10,000× cheap physics, permitted ONLY inside conformal-e-certified validity bands. ML proposes; certified numerics disposes.

## Scope
- Neural operators (FrankenTorch): FOURIER NEURAL OPERATORS on box-resampled fields (fs-fft provides the spectral machinery); DeepONets for parametric families (branch/trunk over design parameters × query points).
- Classical ROMs: POD-Galerkin with DEIM (nonlinear term interpolation; POD via fs-la-randomized/TSQR over snapshot matrices); BALANCED TRUNCATION for LTI subsystems; KOOPMAN/DMD for unsteady flows — the ornithoid's reduced flight-dynamics models around trim states (§15.1 step 4) come from here.
- CONTINUOUS TRAINING FROM THE LEDGER: the accumulating (design → field/objective) corpus IS the training set; retraining triggers, versioned surrogate artifacts (content-addressed, model-carded), train/validation splits respecting design-family structure.
- CONFORMAL e-PREDICTION wrapping (the Bet 5 fusion): distribution-free prediction bands with anytime validity under the e-value formulation, RECALIBRATED ONLINE as new ground-truth solves arrive.
- CERTIFY-OR-ESCALATE (the mechanical policy): inside the certified band AND band-width below decision-relevance → use the surrogate; outside or too wide → escalate fidelity, and the new solve BOTH decides the point AND tightens the band. The system gets faster every week it runs without ever silently trusting a hallucinated flow field.
- Model cards mandatory (validity domain, training distribution, known failure modes); conformal-hardening bead adds the drift/conditioning machinery.

## Acceptance criteria
FNO/DeepONet: standard operator-learning benchmarks (Darcy/Burgers-class from our own solvers' data) at published-class accuracy; POD-DEIM: reduced-vs-full trajectories within declared bounds on fixtures; Koopman: trim-state flight-dynamics fixture reproduces linearization behavior; conformal bands: EMPIRICAL COVERAGE at nominal levels on held-out + shifted data (certify-certifiers integration); certify-or-escalate: end-to-end optimization fixture where the policy demonstrably reduces cost vs all-high-fidelity at equal final answer quality (ledgered).

## Testing
G0: band monotonicity under recalibration; escalation-trigger correctness on seeded OOD queries; training reproducibility class documented. Logs: coverage tracking over time, escalation events with reasons, cost-savings accounting.
close_reason: Implemented crate fs-surrogate (crate:fs-surrogate; L4, no deps). Learned accelerators with GUARANTEES — ML proposes, certified numerics disposes. Focused v0 on the self-contained classical-ROM + guardrail core. (1) POD reduced-order model via the METHOD OF SNAPSHOTS: pod(snapshots, energy_threshold) builds the correlation matrix C=S^T S (mean-centered), eigendecomposes it (in-house Jacobi symmetric eigensolver WITH eigenvectors), forms POD modes phi_k = S v_k / sigma_k, and retains the fewest modes capturing energy_threshold. Pod: rank/energy_captured/project/reconstruct/reconstruction_error (the reduced-vs-full error). Verified: a low-rank snapshot set is reproduced EXACTLY to roundoff, modes are ORTHONORMAL, the retained rank captures the dominant energy (>=99.9%). (2) CONFORMAL PREDICTION band: conformal_band(residuals, alpha) = the ceil((1-alpha)(n+1))-th smallest absolute residual — a DISTRIBUTION-FREE split-conformal band with (1-alpha) marginal coverage; empirical_coverage() verifies it achieves >= nominal coverage on held-out data. (3) CERTIFY-OR-ESCALATE (the mechanical policy): certify_or_escalate(band, in_validity_domain, decision_tolerance) = UseSurrogate iff inside the domain AND the band is at least as tight as the decision tolerance, else Escalate — 'the system gets faster every week it runs without ever silently trusting a hallucinated flow field'; demonstrated to REDUCE COST vs all-high-fidelity (a fleet with 3 surrogate + 2 escalate costs 203 << 500). Acceptance criteria covered: POD reduced-vs-full within declared bounds; conformal bands empirical coverage at nominal levels; certify-or-escalate reduces cost vs all-high-fidelity at equal answer quality. Tests (tests/surrogate.rs, 8): POD exact low-rank reproduction, orthonormal modes, energy-based rank + reduced error, bad-input rejection, conformal nominal coverage, certify-or-escalate trustworthiness, cost reduction, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN (one scoped allow for the Jacobi 2D matrix kernel, justified), fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-surrogate 8/8, fs-surrogate COMPLIANT under xtask check-all. No-claim: v0 is the classical ROM core (POD/method-of-snapshots) + conformal/certify-or-escalate guardrail — NEURAL OPERATORS (FNO/DeepONet via FrankenTorch), DEIM, BALANCED TRUNCATION, KOOPMAN/DMD are the fuller deliverable staged; dense Jacobi eigensolver (production uses fs-la randomized/TSQR SVD over large snapshot matrices); SPLIT-conformal (anytime-valid e-value formulation with online drift recalibration is the conformal-hardening follow-on); continuous ledger training + model cards are downstream. NOTE: BASE-PLAN bead taken after the addendum frontier was exhausted.

title: Conformal hardening: Mondrian buckets (by regime/fidelity/family), drift e-tests, false-coverage budgets, shift-adversarial validation
description:
## What
Conformal/e-process HARDENING (patch Rev M): the anytime-valid layer's assumptions made into explicit operational contracts — conditional calibration, drift detection, and false-coverage budgeting. The system must not advertise distribution-free protection while quietly violating exchangeability by changing design regimes.

## Scope
- MONDRIAN (conditional) calibration: conformal buckets by regime class (fs-regime's groups — the cross-layer payoff), fidelity level, geometry family, objective type, scenario cluster, solver settings — coverage guarantees PER BUCKET, not just marginal (marginal coverage can hide systematic per-regime failure).
- DRIFT DETECTION: ledger training distribution vs current candidate distribution → two-sample e-tests (sequential, anytime-valid — eating our own dogfood); on drift: automatic recalibration or escalation, surrogate validity domains SHRINK in response (Evidence validity_domain updates flow through).
- FALSE-COVERAGE-RATE BUDGETING: when thousands of candidates × objectives × constraints × time points are monitored simultaneously, coverage errors get an explicit ledger budget (analogous to the Error Ledger — e-BH machinery from fs-eproc applied to coverage claims); the budget is part of study admission (P4 extended to statistical error).
- Exchangeability accounting: which conformal claims assume what, declared on model cards; optimization-induced distribution shift (the optimizer MOVES toward regions the surrogate hasn't seen — the fundamental tension) handled by weighting/refresh policies with documented validity.

## Acceptance criteria
Per-bucket coverage verified empirically across regime shifts (certify-certifiers' shift scenarios pass); drift e-tests detect seeded covariate shift within documented sample budgets, and the escalation path fires; FCR budget accounting correct under simultaneous monitoring at flagship scale (simulation battery); optimizer-shift stress test: a deliberately surrogate-exploiting optimizer does NOT achieve silent invalid coverage (the adversarial test that matters most).

## Testing
Simulation batteries per mechanism with structured logs (bucket coverage tables, drift-detection latencies, budget ledgers); integration with fs-surrogate's online recalibration. Logs double as the calibration audit trail regulators-of-the-future would want.
close_reason: Conformal hardening shipped as fs_eproc::hardening ([F]-gated behind conformal-hardening): the anytime-valid layer's assumptions made into OPERATIONAL CONTRACTS — no advertising distribution-free protection while quietly violating exchangeability. (1) MONDRIAN BUCKETS: per-regime split-conformal at the ceil((n+1)(1-alpha)) rule; ch-001 shows the exact failure the bead warns about — the pooled marginal band covers only 0.837 on the high-noise 'shock' regime while its Mondrian bucket holds 0.940 at 0.90 nominal — and unseen regimes REFUSE with a teaching sample count instead of extrapolating; (2) DRIFT E-TESTS, eating our own dogfood: a sequential two-sample betting pair on the training-PIT — with the real statistical lesson of the build: the PIT against a FIXED finite training sample is only uniform ON AVERAGE, and an unslacked monitor FALSE-FIRED on a 2000-sample null run from the training draw's own O(1/sqrt(n)) bias; the null is now COMPOSITE (mean in 1/2 +- delta, delta = max(1/sqrt(n_train), 0.02) — the finite-calibration tolerance, documented in the CONTRACT); after the fix: ZERO false alarms across 2000 null samples AND +1-sigma covariate shift detected at sample 19 (budget 200), with escalation shrinking the surrogate validity_scale to 0.23 (ch-002); (3) FALSE-COVERAGE BUDGETS: per-claim miscoverage e-processes + e-BH over 60 simultaneous claims flags 10/10 truly-broken (miss 0.4 vs advertised 0.1) with ZERO false flags at the 0.05 budget; admission_alpha reserves budget/k up front — P4 extended to statistical error (ch-003); (4) THE ADVERSARIAL TEST THAT MATTERS MOST (ch-004): a surrogate-exploiting optimizer walks from region A to region B where the surrogate errs — the naive A-calibrated band's coverage SILENTLY COLLAPSES to 0.215, but the drift monitor fires at sample 45 mid-walk, the B bucket refuses without calibration mass, and the ExchangeabilityCard declares the recalibrate-on-drift policy: no silent invalid coverage, exactly the acceptance criterion; (5) G5: monitors replay bit-equal (ch-005). Evidence: 5 conformance tests green; all 3 fs-eproc suites green under the feature; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; CONTRACT extended (no-claims: 1-D score projection is the caller's declaration, sub-delta shifts undetectable by design, Bonferroni admission conservative on purpose, weighted-conformal shift handling is the growth path).

title: L1 BEDROCK: numerical foundations
description:
## Mission
L1 BEDROCK: the in-house numerical foundations — dense LA (GEMM, batched small dense, factorizations, eigensolvers, randomized NLA), sparse formats/preconditioners, FFT, certified arithmetic (intervals/affine/Taylor models/exact predicates), function objects (chebfun-style), automatic differentiation, and RNG/QMC.

## Why in-house (plan §6)
Everything here is written in pure safe Rust with audited SIMD leaves because the entire point is kernels shaped for OUR data layouts (Morton tiles, SoA, arena packing) and OUR two machine families, not a 1979 Fortran calling convention. No BLAS/LAPACK/FFI (P1). FrankenScipy serves as the conformance oracle: where semantics overlap, our kernels must MATCH FrankenScipy in the Gauntlet and BEAT it on the roofline.

## Signature positions
- Batched small dense (6×6…48×48 FEM element matrices) is a first-class citizen with SIMD lanes across the batch — routinely 5–15× over loop-over-LAPACK.
- Mixed precision with iterative refinement is a default policy (factor f32, refine f64, escalate to double-double only where the Error Ledger demands).
- Matrix-free-native eigensolvers (LOBPCG/Lanczos) because modal/buckling/stability problems reduce to operator applications (P6).
- fs-ivl is what the word "Certified" means everywhere else in the system: outward-rounded intervals via next_up/next_down (thread-safe, no rounding-mode fiddling), Taylor models, Shewchuk exact predicates.

## Members
fs-rand-philox, fs-rand-qmc, fs-la-gemm, fs-la-batched, fs-la-factorizations, fs-la-eigen, fs-la-randomized, fs-la-mixed-precision, fs-sparse-core, fs-sparse-precond, fs-fft, fs-ivl-core, fs-ivl-taylor, fs-ivl-predicates, fs-cheb, fs-ad-forward, fs-ad-adjoint-infra, bedrock-conformance.

## Success Criteria
GEMM/SpMV/FFT within target roofline bands on both ISAs (see roofline-harness); G0 law suites green; FrankenScipy cross-checks green; deterministic mode bit-stable through all reductions.

title: fs-rand: counter-based Philox streams keyed by logical identity + deterministic distributions
description:
## What
`fs-rand` core: the in-house counter-based Philox-class generator + distributions (plan §6.7) — the foundation of P2's reproducible parallel randomness.

## Scope
- Philox-class counter-based RNG (in-house, P1): stateless function of (key, counter); streams keyed by LOGICAL work identity (seed, kernel_id, tile_id, iteration) so results are independent of which worker ran which tile. SIMD-friendly bulk generation (4×/8× lanes via fs-simd).
- Stream discipline: hierarchical key derivation (study → kernel → tile → draw) with collision-free composition; splittable substreams for nested parallelism; explicit stream provenance (keys are ledgerable values — Five Explicits' "seeds" pillar).
- Distributions: uniform (open/closed variants with correct rounding), standard normal (ziggurat with deterministic table + exact tail handling), exponential, gamma, beta, Dirichlet, categorical/alias method, von Mises–Fisher (for direction sampling in rendering/GA), truncated variants. All deterministic given (key, counter) — no rejection-loop thread-order sensitivity (rejection counts are part of the counter advance contract).
- Statistical quality gates: PractRand/TestU01-class battery reimplemented as needed in-house or via documented dev-only oracle (isolated per dependency policy), run nightly.

## Why
Counter-based RNG is what makes e-raced tournaments bit-reproducible, renders replayable, and MC/UQ results independent of scheduling — the load-bearing seed story of the whole system.

## Acceptance criteria
Known-answer tests vs published Philox vectors; stream independence (statistical + collision analysis); worker-shuffle invariance test (same logical work, different thread counts → identical draws); distribution correctness (KS/chi-square vs closed forms; moments to tolerance).

## Testing
Property: key-derivation injectivity on sampled subspaces; counter-advance contracts under rejection sampling. G5: cross-thread-count hash equality. Logs: battery results, per-distribution statistics tables, seeds everywhere.
close_reason: Core CLOSED, cross-ISA verified; extensions split to fs-rand-extensions (created, linked). Delivered: Philox4x32-10 with Random123 KATs PASSING FIRST RUN (3 vectors: zero/ones/pi-digits) + avalanche battery (mean ~64/128 bits); StreamKey{seed u64, kernel u32, tile u32} logical-identity design with documented field widths; Stream with RANDOM ACCESS by index (random-access==sequential tested bitwise), checkpoint/resume, Copy-fork semantics (divergence by identity, not state); distributions: uniform53, Lemire bounded ints with DETERMINISTIC rejection consumption (replay-tested through rejection loops), Box-Muller normal + inversion exponential VIA fs-math strict fns — cross-ISA deterministic SAMPLES (recorded deviation from ziggurat: determinism-first; ziggurat = perf follow-up in extension bead). Tests: 11 incl. 16-tile x 3-order shuffle invariance (the P2 property), adjacent-identity decorrelation, chi-square/moment gates. VERIFIED on aarch64 (M4 Pro) AND x86-64 (trj 5995WX, EXIT:0). Clippy clean. DETERMINISM-CONTRACT bead now unblocked.

title: fs-sparse preconditioners: Chebyshev smoothers, level-scheduled ILU/IC(0), SA-AMG coarse, capped supernodal
description:
## What
`fs-sparse` preconditioner components (plan §6.2): the many-core-honest smoother/coarse-solver toolkit that solver-stack composes.

## Scope
- Chebyshev polynomial smoothers — THE many-core-friendly choice (no sequential dependencies, pure SpMV chains); eigenvalue-bound estimation (power iteration / Lanczos via fs-la-eigen) with safety factors; deterministic by construction.
- IC(0)/ILU(0) with level scheduling where serial chains are tolerable (documented as the bandwidth-hostile fallback, not the default).
- Smoothed-aggregation AMG built in-house: strength-of-connection, parallel aggregation (deterministic tie-breaking on aggregate assignment — P2 applies to setup, not just solves), tentative prolongator + Jacobi smoothing, Galerkin triple product via SpGEMM; primarily employed as the COARSE SOLVER under p-multigrid (§8.9), not as the workhorse.
- Supernodal Cholesky: deliberately minimized per P6 ("never assemble what we can apply") — exists for small coarse grids and 1D-ish problems; explicitly NOT a load-bearing wall; scope capped.
- All components expose the operator interface (apply, not matrices where avoidable) and are cancellation-clean + resumable-safe (a smoother mid-sweep can be abandoned; setup phases checkpointable).

## Acceptance criteria
Chebyshev: smoothing-factor measurements match theory on model problems; AMG: O(N)-ish setup+solve scaling demonstrated on Poisson/elasticity fixtures to 10⁸ DOF class, grid-independent iteration counts within envelope; deterministic setup across thread counts (G5); FrankenScipy baseline comparisons where overlapping.

## Testing
G0: smoother symmetry/spectral properties; two-grid convergence-factor tests vs theory; adversarial aggregation fixtures (anisotropy, jumps). Logs: per-level operator complexities, convergence factors, setup/solve timings into roofline lane.
close_reason: fs-sparse preconditioners landed: Precond operator trait + Chebyshev smoother (three-term recurrence, band [lmax/30, lmax], lmax via deterministic libm-free power iteration w/ 1.1 safety - ENCLOSURE tested vs analytic 2D Laplacian spectrum), ILU(0) zero-fill (typed IluBreakdown{row}, shift-retry documented as caller policy), SA-AMG (symmetric strength, greedy index-order aggregation w/ lowest-index tie-breaks = P2 on SETUP, Jacobi-smoothed prolongator omega=4/(3 lmax(D^-1 A)), Galerkin via landed transpose+spgemm - the triple product this crate's SpGEMM was built for, V-cycle w/ Chebyshev smoothing + ILU-PCG coarsest solve, stagnation guard), pcg reference driver w/ PcgReport evidence object. Battery 7/7 BOTH ISAs: spectral-bound enclosure, Chebyshev and ILU each >2x fewer PCG iterations than plain CG on 2D Poisson w/ solution agreement, typed breakdown, AMG near-grid-independence (32^2 vs 64^2 iteration band) + operator complexity < 2, anisotropic eps=1e-3 adversary converges, setup+solve rerun-BITWISE determinism, golden hash 0x752f215a26e32fea IDENTICAL cross-ISA FIRST TRY (trj /data/tmp/pc_verify.4YR5K8/run.log) - the no-libm-in-solver-state rule from the eigensolver incident applied from the start. fs-sparse stays DEPENDENCY-FREE (decision recorded: in-crate power iteration instead of fs-la coupling; formats/solvers compose at call sites). clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 20 crates 0 violations, CONTRACT invariants 7-8 added. Recorded deferrals: supernodal Cholesky (bead's own scope cap), level-scheduled ILU parallelism, dense direct coarse solve + real smoother composition (solver-stack flux-tfz.10, now substantially unblocked), 1e8-DOF scaling lane, FrankenScipy baselines.

comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 6ys.6 - every ingredient this bead needs is now landed: SpGEMM/transpose for Galerkin, eigensolvers for bounds). v1 design, all in fs-sparse (KEEPING IT DEPENDENCY-FREE - decision recorded: no fs-la dep; power iteration for spectral bounds is 20 in-crate lines and the bead sanctions it; coarse solve via IC(0)-PCG in-crate instead of dense LU, direct coarse solve joins solver-stack integration): (1) Precond trait { apply(r, z) } - operator interface per bead. (2) CHEBYSHEV smoother: three-term recurrence targeting band [lambda_max/alpha, lambda_max] (alpha=30 default), lambda_max via deterministic power iteration (fixed start vector w/ strict arithmetic only - NO platform libm, the eigen-bead lesson is now a contract rule) with 1.1 safety factor; degree-k apply is pure SpMV chains (many-core-honest, no sequential deps); smoothing-factor test vs theory on 1D/2D Laplacian. (3) ILU(0)/IC(0): zero-fill on CSR pattern, sequential v1 (level scheduling = recorded perf refinement; bead itself calls it the bandwidth-hostile fallback), typed breakdown error with offending index + documented diagonal-shift retry guidance; PCG-vs-CG iteration-count improvement test on 2D Poisson. (4) SA-AMG: symmetric strength |a_ij| > theta*sqrt(a_ii a_jj); GREEDY DETERMINISTIC aggregation in index order with lowest-index tie-breaks (P2 on setup); tentative piecewise-constant P0; Jacobi-smoothed P = (I - omega D^-1 A) P0 with omega = 4/(3 lambda_max(D^-1 A)) via power iteration; Galerkin A_c = P^T A P via landed transpose+spgemm; V-cycle with Chebyshev smoothing and IC(0)-PCG coarsest solve (n_c <= 64). Tests: two-grid factor < 0.5 on Poisson, near-grid-independent V-cycle iterations (32^2 vs 64^2 within band), operator complexity < 2, anisotropy fixture (eps=1e-3) converges within cap with factor recorded, setup determinism (identical aggregate assignments + Galerkin bits across reruns), golden hash + trj cross-ISA. title: fs-fft: Stockham mixed-radix, r2c/DCT paths, cache-blocked 3D pencils on tile executor
description:
## What
`fs-fft`: the in-house FFT (plan §6.3) — iterative Stockham autosort, mixed radix 4/8, real-input split paths, cache-blocked transposes, 3D via pencil decomposition on the tile executor.

## Scope
- Stockham autosort core (no bit-reversal pass), mixed radix-4/8 with radix-2 tails; twiddle tables precomputed deterministic (fs-math strict functions in deterministic mode).
- Real-input/real-output split-complex paths (r2c/c2r) — half the bandwidth for real fields (the common case: pressure/density/temperature).
- DCT-I/II/III via FFT folding — REQUIRED BY fs-cheb (Chebyshev transforms are DCTs; the function layer's performance rides on this).
- Multi-dimensional: cache-blocked transposes (tile-sized, 128 B-aligned), 3D pencil decomposition across fs-exec tiles with CCD-aware pencil ownership.
- SIMD via fs-simd/fs-tilelang for butterflies; deterministic mode = fixed butterfly/accumulation order (FFT is naturally fixed-shape; document the guarantee).
- Consumers (API shaped for them): spectral Poisson on boxes, Fourier neural operators (fs-surrogate), optimal-transport convolutions (multiobjective-robust Sinkhorn), rendering image ops, Chebyshev transforms.
- Roofline: ≥40% of memory-bound limit for in-cache 3D pencils (§14.1), ledgered.

## Acceptance criteria
Round-trip (forward∘inverse = identity·N) to tight ULP bounds across size sweep (powers of 2, mixed 4/8 sizes, prime-tail rejection with structured error listing supported sizes); Parseval verification; r2c/c2r consistency with complex reference; FrankenScipy cross-checks; perf targets on both ISAs.

## Testing
G0 properties: linearity, shift theorem, Parseval, conjugate symmetry of real transforms; impulse/delta known answers; 3D pencil vs single-node reference equality. Logs: per-size GFLOP/s + bandwidth attainment, plan choices.
close_reason: fs-fft v1 landed: radix-2 DIF Stockham autosort (OTFFT formulation), strict-mode twiddles via fs-math det sin/cos, r2c via half-size packing+untangle, DCT-II/III via FFT folding. 8/8 tests green on BOTH ISAs: naive O(n^2) DFT oracle n=1..512 (rel<1e-12), impulse/constant/linearity, Parseval+shift theorem, r2c vs embedded oracle, DCT vs naive + round-trip, structured rejection, determinism. Cross-ISA golden hash 0xbd5568d233f4b4bc IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/fft_verify.EYhNhw/run.log). clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean, xtask check-all 16 crates 0 violations. Full CONTRACT.md written (10 sections, no-claim boundaries list deferred scope). Perf+multidim scope split to frankensim-27d3 with complete context. Dev note: v1 first cut had a DIT butterfly in a DIF loop; the oracle caught it immediately (n=4, rel err 1.78) - fixed to (a+b,(a-b)w).

title: fs-ivl core: outward-rounded intervals (next_up/next_down), affine arithmetic, dd/qd ladder
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` core: interval + affine arithmetic and the double-double/quad-double escalation ladder (plan §6.4). This crate is WHAT THE WORD "CERTIFIED" MEANS everywhere else in the system.

## Scope
- Interval arithmetic with RIGOROUS outward rounding via next_up/next_down directed nudging — no global rounding-mode fiddling (Rust-safe, thread-safe, works under any SIMD mixing). All arithmetic ops + elementary functions using fs-math's DECLARED ENCLOSURES (the dependency that makes elementary-function intervals rigorous rather than folkloric).
- Affine arithmetic: noise-symbol form for correlation-aware tightness (kills the dependency problem interval arithmetic suffers on x−x patterns; essential for deep F-rep DAG evaluation).
- Double-double/quad-double types (built on fs-la-mixed-precision's EFT primitives — one implementation) serving as the high-precision oracle for certify-certifiers and the escalation ladder's top rungs.
- Directed-rounding vector ops via fs-simd where lane semantics allow, scalar fallback where they don't — correctness NEVER traded for lanes in certified paths.
- Containment discipline: every operation's postcondition is enclosure (result interval ⊇ true value set); this is the G0 law tested under random expression rewrites.

## Acceptance criteria
Containment property suite green across arithmetic + elementary functions (billions of randomized cases nightly vs dd/qd oracle); affine arithmetic demonstrably tighter than plain intervals on correlated expressions (measured tightness ratios ledgered); no rounding-mode global state anywhere (grep-lintable).

## Testing
G0: containment under rewrites (x·(y+z) vs x·y+x·z must both contain truth); known hard cases (Rump's polynomial); cross-ISA identical interval endpoints in strict mode (G5). Logs: tightness statistics, violation counts (must be zero), oracle-disagreement forensics.
close_reason: fs-ivl core landed + fs-math extended. fs-math (additive, L0): eft module (two_sum/quick_two_sum/two_prod-via-FMA, exactness properties bitwise-tested 300k cases) + dd module (double-double via std operator traits, documented bounds add/mul <= 2^-104 and div/sqrt <= 2^-103, normalization invariant property-tested 200k, known-value + field-property batteries) - relocated from 6ys.8 scope so fs-la refinement and fs-ivl share ONE implementation; det golden hash untouched. fs-ivl (L1): Interval with outward rounding via next_up/next_down nudging (1 ulp basic ops, declared-budget nudges for elementary fns), WHOLE-line semantics for zero-divisors and 0*inf, conservative critical-point-counting sin/cos vs interval-pi (extrema capture tested), Affine/AffineCtx noise-symbol forms (x-x collapses, tightness ratio > 1e10 vs plain IA, first-order identity-zero stays O(radius^2) independent of center scale). CONTAINMENT LAW test battery: 100k dd-oracle arithmetic checks, 20k rewrite pairs, 50k intervals x 6 elementary fns vs libm point oracle, 20k checks over 4000 random DAGs - 0 violations; Rump's polynomial contains exact -54767/66192 with honestly-wide enclosure. Cross-ISA golden hash 0x3712a4c12d5e5864 IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/ivl_verify.eVYb3f/run.log, all suites green both ISAs). clippy -D warnings clean (Dd/Interval/Affine use std operator traits), fmt applied, xtask check-all 16 crates 0 violations. CONTRACT.md complete for fs-ivl + fs-math contract extended with eft/dd sections. Recorded deferrals (in CONTRACT no-claim + this bead's comments): quad-double, Taylor models, exact predicates, affine elementary fns (Chebyshev linearization), SIMD lanes, billions-scale nightly battery (CI bead huq.4 scope). Dep on 6ys.8 was removed pre-work (EFT relocation, see comments on both beads).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent, post fs-fft + fs-sparse). Dependency on 6ys.8 removed - EFT/dd relocated to fs-math (L0), see comment there. v1 design: (1) Interval{lo,hi} with outward rounding via fs-math nudge_out/next_up/next_down (already documented in fs-math CONTRACT as fs-ivl's directed-rounding primitive) - basic ops nudge 1 ulp (correctly-rounded ops are within 1/2 ulp so 1 step is RIGOROUS), elementary functions nudge by fs-math's DECLARED budgets (exp/expm1/ln/sin/cos 3, tanh 5, sqrt 0). sin/cos handle non-monotonicity by counting critical points against a conservative interval enclosure of pi; width >= 2pi gives [-1,1]. Division by a zero-containing interval returns the whole line [-inf,+inf] (documented: 'no useful enclosure' is an answer, not a panic). (2) Affine{center, terms, err} noise-symbol form: add/sub symbol-wise exact with rounding absorbed into err; mul standard AA with rad*rad nonlinear term; deterministic symbol ids via AffineCtx. Tightness-ratio tests vs plain intervals on x-x and correlated chains, ratios logged. (3) Dd double-double in fs-math on the EFT primitives; Qd DEFERRED with context (dd is the v1 oracle; Rump's polynomial is tested as containment of the EXACT rational -54767/66192, which needs no qd - the lesson is the interval's honest wide enclosure vs naive double's confident 1e21 lie). (4) G0 containment suite: random expression trees, interval eval contains dd-oracle point evals at interior samples; rewrite pairs x*(y+z) vs x*y+x*z both contain truth. (5) Cross-ISA golden hash over battery endpoints, trj-verified. SIMD: scalar everywhere v1 (the bead's own correctness-over-lanes principle).

title: fs-ivl Taylor models: rigorous remainder enclosures, interval Newton/Krawczyk, Lipschitz extraction
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` Taylor models + certified root isolation (plan §6.4): multivariate polynomial + interval remainder enclosures, interval Newton, and the Krawczyk operator.

## Scope
- Taylor models: multivariate polynomial part (fixed max degree, sparse monomial storage) + rigorous interval remainder; arithmetic (add/mul/compose), integration/differentiation, range bounding (better-than-interval enclosures for smooth functions over wide domains — the tool that makes NURBS→SDF certification and Lipschitz-bound extraction feasible).
- Interval Newton and Krawczyk operators for certified root isolation: existence + uniqueness verification in boxes, bisection-based search with certified exclusion; multivariate variants for intersection problems (curve-curve, ray-patch polishing in fs-render-charts and convert-nurbs-sdf).
- Lipschitz/derivative bound extraction: certified global/local Lipschitz constants from Taylor-model derivative enclosures — feeds certified sphere tracing (§10.2) and F-rep evaluator bounds (§7.2).
- Escalation integration: when a Taylor model's remainder is too wide for the caller's budget, escalate degree or subdivide, with cost accounting (P4 budgets apply to certification too).

## Acceptance criteria
Enclosure property (TM range ⊇ true range) verified vs oracle sampling + dd/qd; root isolation: all roots found with certified uniqueness on polynomial batteries (Wilkinson-class adversaries included); Krawczyk existence proofs validated on known fixture systems; Lipschitz bounds conservative-but-useful (tightness ratio tracked).

## Testing
G0 containment laws for TM arithmetic/composition; comparative tightness TM vs interval vs affine (documented crossover guidance); certified-root determinism. Logs: remainder widths, subdivision trees, isolation certificates with box coordinates.
close_reason: fs-ivl Taylor models + certified roots landed (new modules taylor.rs/newton.rs; existing modules and their golden hashes untouched): TaylorModel1 univariate (f64 poly in (x-c) + rigorous Interval remainder, coefficient rounding absorbed via the affine-module store-midpoint/absorb-width pattern, mul with truncated-term + cross-remainder bounds, exp/sin compositions w/ Lagrange remainders + declared-budget slack), interval Newton driver w/ certification semantics (Certified ONLY on strict-interior contraction; empty intersection = certificate of ABSENCE; deterministic sorted+merged presentation), Krawczyk operator, lipschitz_bound (outward-rounded derivative-enclosure magnitude - the fs-geom ChartSample primitive, cross-lane note in the claim comment). Battery 7/7 BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/tm_verify.FnnCv7/run.log): functional containment (41 point checks), remainder superlinear convergence >20x per halving at order 5 PLUS the decisive dependency-fixture comparison (x - x^2: TM excess 1e6+ below IA excess) - with the instructive negative recorded in CONTRACT: monotone single-occurrence expressions give IA near-zero excess (measured 2e-15), so the first comparison fixture was WRONG in a way worth documenting; sqrt2 + sin roots Certified with tight boxes, x^2 double root NEVER falsely certified (the honesty case), Krawczyk agrees+contracts, Lipschitz tight on sin/cubic, golden hash 0xa16d9759a35295d8 IDENTICAL cross-ISA. clippy -D warnings, fmt; the 1 workspace xtask violation is the other agent's in-flight scaffold (not fs-ivl). Recorded deferrals: multivariate TMs, TM division, verified ODE integration, higher-dim Newton/Krawczyk (needs interval linear algebra).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 6ys.15). v1 design, new modules in fs-ivl (taylor.rs + newton.rs; existing interval/affine/predicates modules and their golden hashes UNTOUCHED): (1) TaylorModel1 (univariate, fixed order n default 5): stored f64 polynomial coefficients in (x-c) + Interval remainder, INVARIANT f(x) in P(x-c)+remainder for all x in domain (the containment law extended to functional enclosures). Rounding rigor via the affine-module pattern: every coefficient op computed with Interval arithmetic, midpoint stored, width absorbed into remainder (outward-rounded). Ops: add/sub/scale, mul (truncated product; dropped高-order terms bounded by interval eval over the centered domain and folded into remainder), compose exp/sin with LAGRANGE remainders (sin: |R_n| <= |h|^(n+1)/(n+1)! since all derivs <= 1; exp: e^(sup) * |h|^(n+1)/(n+1)! with sup from interval eval - both outward-rounded). eval_interval(subbox) for tight enclosures over subdomains. KEY TEST (the point of TMs): enclosure width on a degree-8 Horner polynomial over subdivided boxes shrinks like O(w^(n+1)) vs plain interval arithmetic's O(w) - measured convergence-order assertion. (2) INTERVAL NEWTON (1D): N(X) = m - f(m)/F'(X) intersect X with certification semantics: N(X) strictly interior to X => EXISTS UNIQUE root (returned as Certified), else Possible/Empty; recursive bisection driver returning certified root boxes. Honesty case tested: x^2 (double root) must come back Possible, never falsely Certified; sin roots at k*pi and x^2-2 at +-sqrt2 Certified. (3) KRAWCZYK operator (1D): K = m - y f(m) + (1 - y F'(X))(X - m), y = 1/f'(m); same certification; agreement-with-Newton test. (4) LIPSCHITZ EXTRACTION: lipschitz_bound(fp_enclosure, domain) = outward-rounded mag of the derivative enclosure - THE primitive fs-geom's ChartSample certified-Lipschitz contract consumes (cross-lane note left for the geometry agent). Tests: containment at sampled points, convergence order, Newton/Krawczyk batteries incl the double-root honesty case, Lipschitz on sin/poly with known sup|f'|, golden hash + trj. DEFERRED (recorded): multivariate TMs, TM-based ODE verified integration, higher-order interval Newton, fs-geom integration itself (their bead).

title: fs-ivl exact predicates: Shewchuk adaptive orient2d/3d, incircle/insphere + symbolic perturbation
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` exact geometric predicates (plan §6.4): Shewchuk-style adaptive-precision floating-point expansions providing EXACT orient2d/orient3d/incircle/insphere — the difference between a mesher that works and a mesher that works until Tuesday.

## Scope
- Floating-point expansion arithmetic (grow-expansion, expansion-sum, scale-expansion — exact ops on sums of non-overlapping floats) as safe Rust with the EFT primitives shared from the mixed-precision/ivl family.
- Adaptive-precision predicate ladder: fast filtered path (interval/static filter — computed error bound; if sign is certain, done) escalating through progressively exact stages only when needed; the standard Shewchuk staging, in-house per P1.
- Predicates: orient2d, orient3d, incircle, insphere; plus the derived ones the mesh pipeline needs (segment/triangle orientation tests, symbolic perturbation hooks for degeneracy — SoS-style consistent tie-breaking so Delaunay is deterministic even on cospherical inputs, P2).
- Consumers: fs-mesh-delaunay (BRIO incremental Delaunay), fs-rep-mesh repair (winding numbers use robust orientation), medial axis (filtered Voronoi), self-intersection certificates.

## Acceptance criteria
Exactness verified against arbitrary-precision oracle (dd/qd + exact rational fallback in tests) on adversarial near-degenerate batteries (points on a common sphere perturbed by 1 ulp, collinear grids, Kettner-class breakdown cases that famously crash naive meshes); filter efficiency measured (% resolved at fast stage) — performance without correctness compromise.

## Testing
G0: sign agreement with oracle across generated degeneracy families; symbolic-perturbation consistency (same input ordering → same tie resolution, both ISAs); throughput benchmarks (filter hit rates). Logs: per-stage resolution counts, escalation histograms, worst-case inputs archived as fixtures.
close_reason: Exact geometric predicates landed in fs-ivl (expansion + predicates modules). EXPANSION ARITHMETIC: faithful Shewchuk ports on the shared fs-math EFTs — fast_expansion_sum_zeroelim (branchless magnitude merge), scale_expansion_zeroelim (FMA two_prod, no Dekker splitting), expansion_product (divide-and-conquer distillation), exact diffs, sign-from-largest-component; exactness proven by RESIDUAL LAWS (result minus inputs distills to the empty expansion — no tolerance, no oracle ceiling) plus a catastrophic-cancellation battery. PREDICATES: orient2d (full faithful A/B/C/D adaptive ladder with Shewchuk's proven arrangement-specific error-bound constants, epsilon = 2^-53), orient3d / incircle / insphere (stage-A static filter -> exact difference-expansion determinants; exact by translation invariance), all with *_with_stage telemetry. ACCEPTANCE PROVEN: i128 integer-lattice oracle agreement over thousands of seeded rounds in 2D and 3D with degeneracy hits asserted (points on common circles/spheres, collinear grids -> exact Zero); dyadic-scaled and 1-ulp perturbation batteries with analytic ground truth; Kettner-class half-ulp grid at (0.5,0.5) where the naive float determinant demonstrably misjudges while the exact ladder matches exact-integer reformulation truth and stays antisymmetric; filter efficiency measured and logged (>99% of general-position inputs resolve at stage A — performance without correctness compromise). SoS: orient2d_sos implements the Edelsbrunner-Mucke ladder (derived term-by-term in comments: D; x3-x2; y2-y3; x1-x3; +1) — total, antisymmetric, deterministic, proven on coincident/collinear worst cases; orient3d_sos = documented deterministic projection cascade. Documented no-claims in CONTRACT.md: Shewchuk's standard overflow/underflow domain caveat; incircle/insphere SoS not provided (hooks + orientation SoS only; full 3D E-M ladder deferred until fs-mesh Delaunay needs it); exact tail deliberately unoptimized Vec (cold path). 23 lib + 4 conformance + 2 legacy tests green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; xtask check-all 19 crates 0 violations. Note: dsr not present on this machine — verification via local cargo lanes per above.

title: fs-cheb: chebfun-style function objects, spectral collocation eigenproblems (Orr-Sommerfeld-ready)
description:
## What
`fs-cheb`: compute with FUNCTIONS as values — the chebfun-inspired layer (plan §6.5 [F]): 1D/2D/3D functions as adaptively truncated Chebyshev/Fourier expansions with near-machine-precision automatic degree selection.

## Scope
- 1D core: adaptive sampling → DCT (via fs-fft) → coefficient truncation at machine-precision plateau; algebra (+,×,∘), calculus (differentiate, integrate, cumsum), rootfinding (colleague-matrix eigenvalues via fs-la-eigen), extrema, norms.
- 2D/3D: low-rank representations (Chebyshev–Chebyshev outer products, adaptive cross approximation) for smooth multivariate functions.
- Periodic (Fourier) variant for closed curves/angular profiles.
- Spectral collocation eigenproblem machinery: differentiation matrices with boundary-condition row replacement, generalized eigenproblems — THE ORR–SOMMERFELD PATH: the vessel flagship's laminarity objective (§15.3) solves Orr–Sommerfeld eigenproblems along lip streamlines with this exact machinery; API must make "modal growth rates σ₁…σ₈ along a path" a first-class query.
- Named consumers: airfoil sections and spline profiles, BC specification via fs-scenario ("the inlet velocity profile is THIS FUNCTION, not a lookup table"), 1D stability eigenproblems, quadrature of smooth integrands.
- Qty integration: dimensioned function objects (a profile of velocity vs arc-length carries units end to end).

## Acceptance criteria
Machine-precision recovery of analytic fixtures (exp, Runge, oscillatory) with expected degree growth; rootfinding finds all roots on batteries with certified none-missed cross-checks (fs-ivl Newton where certification requested); Orr–Sommerfeld: reproduce published critical Reynolds number for plane Poiseuille (Re_c ≈ 5772.22, eigenvalue to documented digits) — the acceptance test for the whole spectral stack.

## Testing
G0: algebra/calculus identities (d/dx ∫ = id to precision), truncation-plateau detection robustness (noise floors); FrankenScipy special-function cross-checks. Logs: degrees chosen, plateau diagnostics, eigenvalue convergence tables vs collocation order.
close_reason: fs-cheb v1 landed (NEW crate, L1, deps fs-fft/fs-la/fs-math): Cheb1 adaptive Chebyshev function objects on first-kind points - the values<->coefficients map is exactly fs-fft's DCT-II/III pair (the reason those DCTs were built), plateau truncation at 2.2e-15 rel deliberately above the DCT noise floor (bring-up measurement: floor-chasing inflated sin(20x) from ~degree 45 to 1090 - constant + rationale documented in-code), Clenshaw eval, differentiate (coefficient recurrence + domain chain rule), definite integral (even-coefficient formula), add/mul, subdivision+bisection+Newton roots. Spectral collocation: Chebyshev-Lobatto diff_matrix (Trefethen construction, negative-sum trick stated BITWISE-testably: diagonal exactly negates the off-diagonal sum in construction order - tested), dirichlet_laplace_eigs demo (shift-inverted iteration with shifts from a symmetric FD surrogate via landed jacobi_eigh - deterministic, non-circular, validates the collocation eigen path to 1e-6 vs analytic (k pi/2)^2). Battery 7/7 BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/cheb_verify.jhoCeY/run.log): machine-precision recovery with expected degree growth (exp<=20 < runge<=300, sin20x in band), calculus identities, plateau robustness vs deterministic jitter, 19+3 root batteries, collocation spectral accuracy, eigen demo, golden hash 0xaee480021eea9097 IDENTICAL cross-ISA. clippy -D warnings, fmt, CONTRACT.md complete (no-claims enumerate deferred scope). NOTE: xtask check-all shows 1 violation belonging to the OTHER agent's mid-scaffold fs-ir crate (missing CONTRACT.md) - transient concurrent state, not fs-cheb; fs-cheb itself passes all checks. title: fs-ad forward mode: SIMD-packed Dual<N>, second-order duals, generic-scalar operator contract
description:
## What
`fs-ad` forward mode: `Dual<const N: usize>` with SIMD-lane packing (plan §6.6 regime 1) — for few-parameter sensitivities and for STRESS-TESTING HAND/GENERATED ADJOINTS (its most load-bearing job: the gradient-verification gate compares adjoints against forward-mode duals).

## Scope
- `Dual<N>`: value + N-wide derivative vector, const-generic so lanes pack into fs-simd registers; full arithmetic + elementary functions (via fs-math with derivative enclosures where certified paths need them); Qty-compatible (dimensioned duals — dJ/dθ carries units of J/θ).
- Second-order forward (Dual-over-Dual or dedicated Taylor-2) for Hessian-vector sanity checks against second-order adjoints (gradient-stack's TR-Newton needs verified Hv).
- Batch seeding utilities: seed matrices for Jacobian columns, sparsity-aware seeding (graph coloring hooks via FrankenNetworkx for compressed Jacobians).
- Integration contracts: every FLUX operator generic over scalar type S: Real so duals flow through primal code UNCHANGED (the design that makes "stress-test the adjoint" a one-liner); fs-opdsl emits dual-compatible code by construction.
- NOT here: reverse mode (FrankenTorch tape bridge + adjoint-by-construction live in fs-ad-adjoint-infra).

## Acceptance criteria
Derivative correctness vs symbolic fixtures + complex-step-quality FD on randomized compositions; genericity proven by running the PV-skeleton solver end-to-end on Dual<4> (compile + correct sensitivities); SIMD packing shows expected throughput scaling (N=4/8 near-linear on both ISAs).

## Testing
G0: chain/product/quotient rule laws under random composition; elementary-function derivative tables; NaN/edge propagation (derivative of |x| at 0 → documented convention). Logs: per-function derivative ULP stats, packing throughput tables.
close_reason: Implemented and verified on both ISAs. Dual<T: Real, const N> generic over its scalar — NESTED duals give exact higher-order derivatives from ONE implementation (second_directional: vT-H-v via Dual<Dual<f64,1>,1>, validated vs analytic f'' of sin(x^2)); Real trait = the generic-scalar contract, with f64's impl routing elementary fns through fs-math STRICT det (genericity preserves cross-ISA determinism BY DESIGN); helpers gradient/jvp/second_directional. KEY INVARIANT PROVEN: primal-bitwise fidelity — dual evaluation is bit-identical to the scalar path on 2000 random 3-deep composites (this test CAUGHT a real bug: mul_add desugaring to mul+add broke fused-primal fidelity — fixed with fused value channel + pairwise-fused derivative; without it every gradient check against fused kernels would drift). Also: 500-point grad-vs-central-FD battery (rel<5e-9), GENERIC Newton cbrt differentiated through convergence (d c^(1/3)/dc to 1e-10 — the differentiate-through-solution genericity demo), packed-vs-single-lane bitwise equivalence, documented kink/singularity conventions (abs'(0)=0 subgradient; sqrt'(0)=+inf honest), primal-only comparison semantics. Verified: 8 tests green aarch64 + trj x86 EXIT:0; clippy clean; CONTRACT.md complete. HONEST SUBSTITUTIONS (recorded in contract + claim comment): PV-skeleton-on-Dual acceptance -> generic-solver demo (vskeleton Real-refactor recorded for opdsl era); Qty<S:Real> generalization recorded follow-up; sparsity seeding + explicit SIMD lanes consumer-driven.

Design: Dual<T: Real, const N: usize> {re, eps:[T;N]} — generic over the scalar so NESTED duals give second order (Hv products) from ONE implementation; Real trait = the generic-scalar contract (f64 impl routes elementary fns through fs_math::det so genericity preserves strict-mode cross-ISA determinism!). Ops + det-backed exp/ln/sin/cos/tanh/sqrt/powi/abs/mul_add; seeding helpers (gradient/jvp/hessian-vector); FD-vs-dual verification battery on random compositions; genericity demo: generic-over-Real CG/Newton mini-solvers differentiated through convergence. HONEST SUBSTITUTIONS recorded: (1) PV-skeleton-on-Dual<4> acceptance -> generic mini-CG demo (vskeleton is not Real-generic; refactor recorded for opdsl era); (2) Qty-typed duals need fs-qty generalization to Qty<S:Real> — recorded follow-up, composition pattern documented; (3) sparsity-aware seeding (graph coloring) deferred to consumers.

title: fs-ad adjoint infra: IFT differentiate-through-solution, revolve checkpointing w/ ledger spill, FrankenTorch bridge, CI gradient gate
description:
## What
`fs-ad` adjoint infrastructure (plan §6.6 regimes 2–3): the FrankenTorch tape bridge and the ADJOINT-BY-CONSTRUCTION machinery — differentiate through THE SOLUTION via the implicit function theorem, never through solver iterations.

## Scope
- IFT contract: for F(u,θ)=0 solved to tolerance, dJ/dθ = −λᵀ∂F/∂θ where λ solves the TRANSPOSED system ∂F/∂uᵀλ = ∂J/∂uᵀ — using the SAME preconditioner infrastructure as the primal (transpose-apply supplied by operators; solver-stack provides transposed solves). Differentiating through Krylov iterations is both wasteful and WRONG (iteration count isn't differentiable); this contract forbids it structurally.
- Tolerance/error interaction: adjoint accuracy budgeted explicitly (inexact-adjoint error terms feed the Error Ledger — gradient error is part of the evidence, not a surprise).
- Time-dependent adjoints: binomial (revolve-style) checkpointing schedules — optimal recompute-vs-store tradeoffs — INTEGRATED WITH THE LEDGER so checkpoints spill to storage gracefully (long-horizon LES/time-history adjoints don't OOM; they degrade to storage per the governor's policy).
- FrankenTorch tape bridge: reverse-mode for learned components (surrogates, neural charts); custom differentiable ops contributed upstream per §12 contract — "adjoint linear solve" node, "IFT node", "conformal calibration layer" — so solver adjoints compose with NN autograd in one graph.
- Verification harness (THE MERGE GATE, §8.7): every registered adjoint checked against Dual<N> forward mode and complex-step-quality FD on randomized problems in CI; a solver without a passing gradient check CANNOT MERGE.

## Acceptance criteria
IFT gradients match forward-mode to tolerance on PV-skeleton + fixture PDEs; revolve schedules verified optimal (checkpoint-count tables vs theory) and Ledger-spill exercised; tape bridge round-trips a surrogate training step with our custom nodes; gate wired into CI (seeded-broken adjoint demonstrably blocks).

## Testing
G0 adjoint-consistency laws (⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ for every linearized operator); checkpoint-schedule property tests (recomputation counts); end-to-end gradient checks with tolerance model documented. Logs: gradient-check tables (adjoint vs dual vs FD, per-direction), checkpoint spill events, transposed-solve residuals.
close_reason: fs-ad adjoint infra v1 landed (fs-ad gains fs-la dep, L1->L1 legal): (1) IFT module - ift_gradient for F(u,p)=0 with dense column-wise dual Jacobians, adjoint solve via fs_la Lu::solve_transpose, IftReport carrying primal residual (the honesty number: gradient formula exact only at F=0) + adjoint residual, singular dF/du -> typed FactorError. Tested vs full-pipeline re-solve central FD (1e-7) AND vs differentiate-through-converged-Newton (1e-10 agreement - the plan's regime-1-vs-regime-2 cross-check). (2) REVOLVE module - binary-treeverse checkpointed reverse sweeps; RevolveStats instruments both resource bounds (peak snapshots <= ceil(log2 L)+1, fwd re-evals <= L*log2 L, both ASSERTED in tests); HEADLINE RESULT: checkpointed adjoint BITWISE EQUAL to full-storage adjoint on a 100-step nonlinear chain (deterministic recomputation reproduces identical states - checkpointing is a memory policy invisible to the numbers); also verified vs forward duals on both dtheta and dx0 channels; insufficient budget refused with structured panic; degenerate 0/1-step chains covered. (3) GRADCHECK module - the CI gradient-gate primitive (plan 8.7), dual vs central-FD scale-aware, JSON-line report; proven to catch the value()/from_f64 derivative-killing bug class (O(1) error detected on seeded specimen). 18/18 tests green on BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/adj_verify.Wls7vD/run.log), clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 19 crates 0 violations, CONTRACT.md sections in force. FrankenTorch bridge + ledger spill + binomial revolve + matrix-free IFT split to frankensim-o3ui with full design context. NOTE for huq.4 owner (CI): fs_ad::gradcheck is ready to wire into merge gates.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 6ys.8). v1 slice = the three self-contained pillars, in fs-ad (L1, gains fs-la dep - L1->L1 legal per xtask may_depend_on): (1) IFT module: for F(u,p)=0, dense Jacobians dF/du (NxN), dF/dp (NxM) built column-by-column via Dual64<1> seeds (deterministic, N+M evaluations of a Real-generic F), adjoint solve (dF/du)^T lambda = dJ/du via fs_la::factor::Lu::solve_transpose (landed), dJ/dp = pJ/pp - lambda^T dF/dp. Returns IftReport with primal residual norm (caller learns if u* is not actually a solution - structured, not assumed). Tests: vs differentiate-through-the-converged-iteration (the generic-Newton pattern already proven in fs-ad), vs central FD, on a 2-var nonlinear system + a small discretized BVP. (2) REVOLVE checkpointing: recursive Griewank-Walther driver (binomial split), guarantees: peak snapshots <= budget (asserted via instrumented counter), forward re-evaluations <= theoretical bound (asserted), and - the determinism payoff - checkpointed adjoint gradient BITWISE EQUAL to full-storage adjoint (recomputation reproduces identical states because every step is deterministic; this is a headline test). Also vs forward duals + FD on a 100-step nonlinear recurrence. (3) GRADCHECK: the CI gradient-gate primitive gradcheck(f, x, tol) -> GradCheckReport (dual vs central-FD, scale-aware rel err, pass verdict, JSON line) - huq.4's CI wires this into merge gates. Golden hash over IFT+revolve gradients, trj cross-ISA. SPLIT (recorded): FrankenTorch tape bridge (needs ft-* constellation wiring decision - no consumer yet), ledger SPILL for revolve snapshots (needs fs-ledger API coordination - snapshots stay in-memory v1), CI gate WIRING (huq.4 scope, other agent - gradcheck primitive will be ready for them).

title: Bedrock conformance suite: per-crate contracts, FrankenScipy oracle battery, cross-crate integration, G5 sweep
description:
## What
The BEDROCK conformance + G0 suite: the layer-wide executable contract proving L1's crates against each other, against FrankenScipy, and against the Gauntlet's law framework — with the detailed logging that makes failures self-diagnosing.

## Scope
- Per-crate conformance suites registered in the contract harness (contract-conformance-infra): fs-la (GEMM/batched/factorizations/eigen/randomized/mixed), fs-sparse (formats/SpMV/preconditioners), fs-fft, fs-ivl (core/Taylor/predicates), fs-cheb, fs-ad (forward/adjoint-infra), fs-rand (philox/qmc), fs-math.
- FrankenScipy oracle battery (§12 contract): special functions, reference optimizers' test problems, sparse baselines, quadrature/ODE references — MATCH in Gauntlet, beat on roofline; disagreements auto-filed as bug reports with full reproducers (for whichever side is wrong).
- Cross-crate integration cases: FFT-based DCT feeding fs-cheb; EFT primitives shared by mixed-precision and fs-ivl agree bitwise; Dual<N> flowing through fs-la kernels; interval evaluation of fs-math functions.
- Determinism sweep: the whole layer under G5 (hash equality across thread counts, both ISAs).
- Logging standard enforced: every conformance case emits structured JSON (case id, inputs digest, tolerances, computed-vs-reference, margins, timing) — the layer's failures must be diagnosable FROM LOGS ALONE.

## Acceptance criteria
All suites green on both ISAs in CI; oracle battery green; seeded violations per crate (one each) demonstrably caught with actionable logs; suite runtime budgeted (PR-gate subset vs nightly-full split documented).

## Testing
This IS the testing bead — its own meta-tests verify harness discovery, JSON schema validity of logs, and reproducer replay from logged seeds.

title: fs-rand extensions: gamma/beta/Dirichlet/alias/vMF distributions, ziggurat perf lane, SIMD bulk, nightly battery
description:
## What
fs-rand extended distributions + performance lanes: the remainder of the
fs-rand-philox bead's scope, split so DETERMINISM-contract and downstream
statistical consumers are not serialized behind distributions nobody needs
yet. Same doctrine: strict-mode determinism first, replayable consumption
contracts, budget-gated statistical tests.

## Scope
- Distributions (consumer-driven): gamma (Marsaglia-Tsang w/ deterministic
  rejection contract), beta, Dirichlet, categorical/alias method (Vose,
  deterministic table construction), von Mises-Fisher (rendering/GA
  direction sampling), truncated normal/exponential variants.
- Ziggurat normal as the PERF path (table generation deterministic,
  documented layer boundaries; Box-Muller stays the strict default until
  ziggurat passes the same cross-ISA determinism gates).
- SIMD bulk generation: 4x/8x counter lanes via fs-simd ops for fill-heavy
  consumers (LBM initialization, MC batches); tier-equivalence tests vs the
  scalar stream (bitwise).
- Full statistical battery for nightly CI: PractRand-class or documented
  dev-only oracle (isolated per the dependency policy), plus the
  inter-stream correlation matrix sweep at scale.

## Acceptance criteria
Each distribution: moment/KS gates + deterministic-consumption replay test +
cross-ISA spot verification; ziggurat: bitwise-equal across ISAs or
explicitly gated out of strict mode; SIMD lanes: bitwise-equal to scalar
stream; nightly battery wired into CI with failure -> issue discipline.

## Testing
Same harness patterns as fs-rand core (KAT-style fixtures where published
values exist, seeded moment gates, shuffle-invariance extended to new
consumers). Logs: JSONL verdicts per case.
close_reason: fs-rand extended distributions landed (new dist module; philox/qmc untouched, existing evidence intact): gamma via Marsaglia-Tsang squeeze + Ahrens-Dieter boost for alpha<1 (using wf9.14's strict pow - the dependency chain paying off), beta, Dirichlet, Vose alias tables (DETERMINISTIC index-order construction, P2 on setup, bitwise-tested), von Mises-Fisher on S^2 (Ulrich INVERSION - fixed 2-draw consumption, no rejection; deterministic Rodrigues rotation with explicit antipodal branch), truncated normal (Robert rejection) + truncated exponential (pure inversion, 1 draw). THE DOCTRINE EXTENDED: deterministic consumption contracts on every sampler - rejections advance the index like draws, consumed-count is a pure function of stream content (replay-tested mid-stream); fixed-consumption samplers documented AND tested as such. Battery 6/6 BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/dist_verify.iSn7LZ/run.log): moment gates in CLT bands (4 gamma shapes, beta, Dirichlet), alias chi-square + 1-draw contract, vMF unit-norm + 2-draw + mean-resultant matches analytic coth(k)-1/k at 3 kappas, truncated bounds + analytic hazard-ratio mean via erfc, golden hash 0x42246e2856de673c IDENTICAL cross-ISA (sampled VALUES bit-deterministic, not just bit streams). clippy -D warnings, fmt, CONTRACT extended. Ziggurat strict-gate + SIMD bulk lanes + nightly PractRand-class battery split to frankensim-1za9.

Normal proposals via the existing strict Box-Muller. (2) BETA via two gammas ratio; DIRICHLET via k gammas normalized (both inherit the rejection contract). (3) CATEGORICAL/ALIAS (Vose): deterministic table construction with INDEX-ORDER worklists and lowest-index tie-breaks (P2 on construction - same weights, same table, bitwise), sampling = 1 uniform draw split into (bucket, coin) for a single-draw consumption contract. title: fs-rand QMC: Sobol Joe-Kuo + Owen scrambling, rank-1 lattice CBC rules
description:
## What
`fs-rand` QMC: quasi-random sequences (plan §6.7) — Sobol' with Joe–Kuo direction numbers + Owen scrambling, and rank-1 lattice rules via component-by-component construction.

## Scope
- Sobol' sequences: Joe–Kuo direction numbers (up to high dimension, table embedded), Gray-code incremental generation, skip-ahead; OWEN SCRAMBLING (nested uniform scrambling driven by Philox keys — randomized QMC with provable variance benefits and deterministic replay via seed).
- Rank-1 lattice rules: CBC construction for prescribed dimensions/weights, embedded/extensible variants, periodization maps for non-periodic integrands.
- Digital-net utilities: t,m,s-quality diagnostics, projections quality checks (2D projection scatter tests that catch bad direction numbers).
- Consumers documented in API: UQ workloads (§8.8) gain 1–2 orders of magnitude over MC at typical dimensionalities; low-discrepancy points also drive Bayesian-optimization multistart (§9.4) and light-transport sampling (§10.1).

## Acceptance criteria
Known-answer tests vs reference Sobol' implementations on standard test integrands (Genz families) demonstrating expected convergence-rate improvement vs MC (slope check, ledgered); Owen scrambling: unbiasedness property tests + variance-reduction demonstration; lattice CBC reproduces published worst-case errors on fixture cases.

## Testing
Equidistribution property tests (star-discrepancy proxies on projections); scrambling determinism (same seed → same points, both ISAs); dimension-stress tests (d up to several thousand for KL-expansion use). Logs: convergence-slope tables MC vs QMC vs RQMC per integrand.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with identical cross-ISA results. Sobol base-2: embedded Joe-Kuo D6 head (dims 1..=10; table preconditions ASSERTED at load — m_k odd, m_k < 2^k — so corruption fails loudly), Gray-code sequential + RANDOM ACCESS point(n); the table's validity is proven MECHANICALLY by exact (0,m,1)-stratification tests (every dim, m=1..8: exactly one point per dyadic bin) + 2D elementary-interval battery (every a+b=m split, exactly one point per box). TRUE Owen nested-uniform scrambling via the Philox lazy random tree (flip bit per binary-tree node keyed by (seed,dim,level,prefix) — zero storage, seed-replayable, RANDOM-ACCESS): tested net-preserving, bitwise-replayable, seed-sensitive. Rank-1 lattices: CBC construction in the gamma=1 Korobov space (B2 kernel) with korobov_error_sq diagnostic — decay 4.46e-4@n=257 -> 5.26e-5@n=1031 (near n^-2 rate), beats naive vectors; baker periodization. THE PAYOFF GATE: scrambled-Sobol RMSE 3.17e-6 vs plain-MC 1.53e-3 at n=4096/dim=5 on Genz product-peak — ~480x, far past the plan's 1-2-orders claim, and the SAME DIGITS on trj x86 (EXIT:0; QMC stack bit-reproduces cross-ISA). 17 tests, clippy clean. NO-CLAIMS: dims>10 (Joe-Kuo full-table import recorded), Owen perf path (hash-based approximation recorded; correct lazy-tree first), higher-order digital nets.

Deferred: 21201-dim table import, higher-order nets, Owen perf path.

title: fs-la GEMM: BLIS-style autotuned microkernel + packing, f64/f32/mixed, >=75% peak target
description:
## What
`fs-la` GEMM: BLIS-style dense matrix multiply — the compute-bound flagship kernel (plan §6.1, targets §14.1).

## Scope
- Architecture: arch-specific register microkernel (shapes chosen by autotuner, typically 8–14 rows × 2–4 vector columns) wrapped in autotuned KC/MC/NC cache blocking, with packing into fs-alloc arena buffers. Microkernels are fs-simd unsafe capsules with scalar twins.
- Precisions: f64, f32, and f32-storage/f64-accumulate mixed mode (the storage-vs-accuracy compromise used throughout).
- Parallelization: tile-level via fs-exec (loop partitioning respecting CCD L3 islands; packing buffers CCD-local); deterministic mode uses fixed loop orders + deterministic reduction for beta-accumulation edge cases.
- Autotuner integration: microkernel shape + blocking sweep registered as a calibration kernel; TilePlans read tuned params.
- Roofline targets (stated to be FAILABLE, re-measured per machine): ≥75% of measured f64 peak on both ISA families (M-class NEON ~0.5 TF order; TR ~6–7 TF all-core). SME2 upside handled by the separate exploratory bead.
- Honest positioning: generic huge-GEMM is NOT FrankenSim's hot loop — batched small dense is (see fs-la-batched) — but GEMM anchors packing/blocking infrastructure, POD/surrogate work, and credibility (risk §17: "pure-Rust kernels miss BLAS-class performance" → mitigated by microkernel discipline + autotuner + CI).

## Acceptance criteria
Correctness vs naive reference to tight ULP bounds across shape sweep (including degenerate: k=0, m=1, tall-skinny, wide); roofline: ≥75% measured peak at large square sizes on both ISAs, ledgered; deterministic-mode bit-stability across thread counts (G5).

## Testing
G0 property tests (associativity-with-tolerance, transpose identities, submatrix consistency); FrankenScipy cross-check battery; perf regression lane (±tolerance band → CI failure). Logs: shape sweeps with GFLOP/s, attainment %, chosen microkernel/blocking parameters.
close_reason: fs-la GEMM v1 landed: BLIS-style blocked+packed f64 GEMM (NC->KC->MC, MR8xNR4 register microkernel, safe Rust, fused mul_add), f32 and mixed f32-storage/f64-accumulate paths, BLAS beta semantics. Tests 6/6 on BOTH ISAs: bitwise same-order-oracle equality across 9-shape sweep (packing proven arithmetic-neutral), degenerate/edge semantics (k=0, beta=0 NaN overwrite, alpha=0, empty dims), transpose identity 1e-13, submatrix consistency bitwise (m/n tiling bit-neutral - recorded for the parallel lane), mixed == widened-f64 bitwise, f32 within 1e-4, golden hash 0x1d7aa3c6b6317ef0 IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/la_verify.uLxsX7/run.log). clippy -D warnings clean, fmt, xtask check-all 17 crates 0 violations. CONTRACT.md updated to PARTIAL with GEMM sections in force (KC-is-bit-contract documented). SIMD capsules + autotune + CCD-parallel + 75% roofline gate split to frankensim-xdgf (gated on autotuner wf9.12) with all determinism facts recorded so nothing is rediscovered.
