DIFFERENTIATION & REALITY e2e SUITE — a runnable Layer-3 battery, the coupling-axis analog of the Layer-2 fs-epi-e2e capstone. run_battery() -> DiffRealReport {stages: Vec<StageLog{stage, passed, events}>} runs 4 stages, each emitting structured log events AS DATA (never stdout) with a load-bearing assertion: (1) DIFFERENTIATION: the adjoint (reverse-mode chain-rule) gradient of a composite AGREES with finite differences within a conditioning-aware tolerance; a full-VJP-coverage path (sdf/spline/solve) differentiates; a forced-remesh path with a MISSING VJP raises a STRUCTURED error that BLOCKS the gradient — never a silent zero (differentiate_path). (2) AS-BUILT LOOP (fs-asbuilt + fs-assimilate, my bk0o.4/bk0o.5): register a scanned fixture (residual carried forward), compute the as-built δ colored VALIDATED + anchored to a calibration cert, LOCALIZE a seeded 0.3 defect (argmax deviation = the seeded index), and run registration-free point-sensor 4D-Var assimilation that REDUCES the model-data misfit. (3) TOLERANCE ALLOCATION (fs-toleralloc, my bk0o.6): a GD&T report on a known-sensitivity fixture TIGHTENS the high-sensitivity feature + LOOSENS the low one, every loosened tolerance carries a certified sensitivity, and the band-extremes robustness check confirms P(in-spec). (4) GATED SPACETIME: the temporal-complex stage (bk0o.7) is not activated — reported GATED, not silently passed. Covers acceptance criteria (battery runs as one script; gradient agreement within tolerance; a missing-VJP/transpose issue blocks; the as-built δ is validated + anchored; the tolerance allocation satisfies P(in-spec) at sampled extremes). Tests (tests/e2e.rs, 6): full battery passes (4 stages logged), differentiation agrees-with-FD + blocks-missing-VJP, as-built localizes-defect + reduces-misfit, tolerance tighten-high/loosen-low + robustness confirmed, spacetime honestly gated, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-diffreal-e2e 6/6, fs-diffreal-e2e COMPLIANT under xtask check-all. No-claim: stage 1 uses a self-contained analytic-adjoint + FD check + VJP-coverage gate (production seam-crossing SDF->mesh->solve gradient runs on fs-adjoint's certified adjoints); stage 4 gated (bk0o.7 unbuilt); log events returned as data (tracing/ledger sink wiring is harness integration).

title: ADDENDUM L2 EPISTEMIC TYPE SYSTEM: three-color ledger, objective epistemics, falsifiers, Goodhart guard, evidence packages
description:
## Mission
ADDENDUM LAYER 2 — THE EPISTEMIC TYPE SYSTEM. The discipline that prevents the certificate culture's one fatal failure mode — **being precisely wrong with a pedigree** — and then turns that discipline into the product regulators buy. A pipeline that is watertight to ε, cohomologically consistent, and interval-certified end-to-end can still compute the wrong answer with great precision, because model-form error (wrong turbulence closure, wrong material law, fictional hazard model, folklore cost spreadsheet) dominates discretization error in most industrial practice and does NOT compose algebraically. A certificate culture that is not explicit about this boundary manufactures false confidence.

## The five proposals
- **Proposal 3 — The three-color ledger** (Mean 810). Every quantity carries a color: **verified** (interval-certified numerics), **validated** (anchored to experimental data in a stated regime), or **estimated** (cross-model discrepancy probes, surrogates). Composition rules are type-checked at ledger-write time so an estimate can never be laundered into a certificate. Load-bearing schema — Phase 0 spine.
- **Proposal F — Objective epistemics** (Mean 790). The three colors, applied to the *goal itself*: loads, missions, hazards, and cost models enter as typed evidence with their own colors and validation anchors — because in "cheapest earthquake-resistant building," the structural mechanics is the easy half and the ground-motion ensemble + cost spreadsheet + loss function determine the answer. No optimization may run against an un-colored objective.
- **Proposal 6 — Falsifier pairing** (Mean 790, Pract 900). No certificate ships without an attached independent falsifier — a different algorithm on a different code path that would catch the certificate being wrong — and agents carry a falsification budget allocated by consequence×doubt. Popper as infrastructure. Phase 0 spine.
- **Proposal D — The Goodhart guard** (Mean 770, Pract 850). Treat every optimization endpoint as an adversarial example by default: designs at optimizer convergence auto-trigger escalated falsification before their certificates are honored. An agent is, in the limit, a maximally creative adjoint optimizer that will find the cracks in the model and call them design. Phase 0 spine (pure policy over existing machinery).
- **Proposal 12 — Evidence packages** (Mean 720, Ex-A 775 — top of the *sell* ranking). Export any result as a signed, machine-checkable evidence package a third party can re-verify without rerunning the pipeline; make the auditor's checker open-source. The pitch to a regulator: "don't trust us; here is the checker."

## Why this layer is the spine
Three of these (3, 6, D) plus E-tombstones and 13's type checker constitute Phase 0 — weeks-scale schema and policy work over existing machinery (Pract ≥ 800 throughout). They ship first because the flywheel's cheap, aggressive machinery is only safe *inside* a type system that refuses to launder guesses into guarantees.

## Members
color-schema (3), color-probes (3), objective-epistemics (F), falsifier-pairing (6), goodhart-guard (D), evidence-packages (12).

## Success criteria
Laundering tests pass (adversarial pipelines that try to upgrade estimated→verified fail the type check); no-falsifier-no-ship gate live; the budget pie ("where is the error budget actually spent, by color") ships on every report; the first evidence package is engaged with by an external auditor as at least supplementary evidence.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- NOTE: `add-layer:Ln` here is the addendum's THEMATIC layer (economic engine / epistemics / coupling / self-knowledge / GTM), NOT the base L0–L6 architecture stack. Member crates still sit at their true base layer and must respect lower-knows-nothing-of-higher dependency direction.
close_reason: ADDENDUM LAYER 2 — THE EPISTEMIC TYPE SYSTEM — is COMPLETE. All ten member beads closed: qmao.1 color schema + no-laundering write gate; qmao.2 fidelity ladders + discrepancy probes + budget pie; qmao.3 objective epistemics (weakest-input rule, colored fragility); qmao.4 falsifier pairing + consequence-x-doubt budget + no-falsifier-no-ship [MINE]; qmao.5 Goodhart guard escalation ladder; qmao.6 evidence-package Merkle format; qmao.7 fidelity-ladder registry; qmao.8 the standalone solver-free checker; qmao.9 the regulatory crosswalk (ASME V&V / FAA-EASA CbA); qmao.10 the Layer-2 e2e suite (fs-epi-e2e by a fellow agent + my complementary ledger-sink battery in fs-checker). SUCCESS CRITERIA VERIFIED BY RE-RUNNING THE SUITES NOW: (1) LAUNDERING TESTS PASS — three independent batteries all green this minute: the spine gate (fs-probe, 5 tests: estimated->verified refused with teaching text), the fs-epi-e2e suite (7 tests), and my ledger-sink battery (fs-checker epi_e2e: write-gate refusal + regime auto-demotion + signed-waiver provenance); (2) NO-FALSIFIER-NO-SHIP GATE LIVE — fs-evidence falsify suite green (12 unit + conformance): unpaired classes blocked by name, the standard registry pairs six certificate classes including adjoint-gradient, budget allocator monotone in consequence x doubt with cold-start max-doubt; (3) THE BUDGET PIE SHIPS ON REPORTS — fs-probe BudgetPie renders error-by-color with the operator verdict (the 'refining the mesh will NOT help' teaching line) and the solver-free checker renders it from evidence packages independently; (4) the fourth criterion (an external auditor engaging the first evidence package) is a REAL-WORLD milestone outside the repo — the machinery for it is complete (signed Merkle bundles, the open checker, the regulatory crosswalk) and the criterion transfers to commercial governance (jwq8/xpck tracking). The layer does what the mission demanded: it prevents 'precisely wrong with a pedigree' — model-form honesty is now typed, gated, falsified, guarded, and exportable. Three of five proposals plus the spine shipped through MY beads this session (falsifiers, tombstone-adjacent gating, the spine milestone gate, gradient certificates extending the colors to Proposal 1).

title: Proposal 3 — Three-color ledger: SCHEMA + type-checked composition rules (verified/validated/estimated; auto-demotion on regime exit; no laundering)
description:
## What
Proposal 3's THREE-COLOR SCHEMA — the type discipline at the heart of the epistemic engine. Every quantity in the ledger carries a COLOR — **verified** (interval-certified numerics), **validated** (anchored to experimental data in a stated regime), or **estimated** (cross-model discrepancy probes, surrogates) — with composition rules ENFORCED BY THE TYPE SYSTEM, so an estimate can never be laundered into a certificate. Load-bearing schema (Phase 0 spine); Pract 800 because it is schema + policy, not research.

## Why (the failure mode it prevents)
A pipeline that is watertight to ε and interval-certified end-to-end can still compute the wrong answer with great precision, because MODEL-FORM error (wrong turbulence closure, wrong material law) dominates discretization error in most industrial practice and does NOT compose algebraically. A system that KNOWS AND DISPLAYS the boundary between its numerical certainty and its modeling uncertainty would be unique in the industry — and it defuses "precisely wrong with a pedigree" before a customer discovers it for you.

## Scope — schema
- `color ∈ {verified, validated, estimated}` on every ledger node, plus color-specific payloads: verified → the interval bound; validated → a REGIME TAG (a region in a declared feature space: Reynolds range, strain range, temperature range…) + the anchoring dataset's identity; estimated → the estimator's identity + its own dispersion.
- This EXTENDS the base plan's Evidence<T>/Certified<T> and the Error Ledger from one error type to three colors. Aligns with fs-evidence's existing ModelEvidence/validity-domain fields — the colors are the type-level surface of that machinery.

## Scope — composition rules (type-checked at ledger-WRITE time)
- verified ⊕ verified → verified (bounds compose by the existing ledger algebra / fs-ivl).
- validated ⊕ X → validated ONLY while execution stays inside the regime tag; the moment a computed state EXITS the declared regime → AUTOMATIC DEMOTION to estimated + a flag. Validation is a REGIONAL property and the type system enforces the region.
- anything ⊕ estimated → estimated. No exceptions, no overrides without a SIGNED WAIVER that itself appears in the provenance.

## Acceptance criteria
Colors + payloads on every ledger node; composition rules enforced at write time; a computed state exiting its regime is auto-demoted validated→estimated with a flag; a laundering attempt (mark an estimated result verified) FAILS the type check; waivers appear in provenance.

## Testing
Gauntlet LAUNDERING tests: adversarial pipelines that attempt to upgrade estimated→verified must fail the type check (this is a Phase-0 spine exit test). Property: composition-rule totality (every color pair has a defined, conservative result); regime-exit demotion correctness. Logs: per-node colors, payloads, demotion events, waivers.

## Kill criterion
Not applicable in the usual sense — this is load-bearing schema. The controllable risk is probe compute (see color-probes): cap discrepancy probing at a fixed % of fleet budget and audit quarterly whether probe-derived model-form maps actually changed downstream decisions.

## Dependencies
Base plan fs-evidence + fs-ledger (extends both). No hard blocker (Pract 800). Foundational for F, 6, D, 12, C, A, and the colors on every flywheel result. Amends the Error Ledger spec + the Gauntlet (laundering tests).

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Layer discipline:** the color enum + payloads + pairwise composition algebra live in fs-evidence (usable by L1+ without layer violation, per the fs-evidence contract), so ANY layer can attach a color to a Certified<T> without depending on HELM. The write-time enforcement + auto-demotion-on-regime-exit is HELM-side (fs-ledger) over already-colored values. No FLUX/MORPH kernel calls fs-ledger to color a result (lower layers must not call HELM).
- Tiers: composition-rule totality + regime-exit demotion -> G0; laundering suite (adversarial estimated->verified) -> G3, tagged a Certifying-the-Certifiers security-critical gate.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Signed-waiver path (security-critical bypass): a valid signed waiver for an anything(+)estimated override is honored AND its signer identity recorded in provenance; an absent/malformed waiver is rejected; a state exactly on the declared regime boundary demotes conservatively (validated -> estimated).
close_reason: Shipped Proposal 3's THREE-COLOR SCHEMA (bead qmao.1) exactly per the polish-note layer split: the color enum + payloads + pairwise algebra live in fs-evidence (L1, usable by every layer), the write-time gate lives in fs-ledger (L6/HELM). SCHEMA: Color::{Verified{interval}, Validated{regime: ValidityDomain, dataset}, Estimated{estimator, dispersion}} with the ColorRank lattice. ALGEBRA (G0, col-001): TOTAL and conservative over 600 random pairs — result rank = min of operands, NEVER higher; verified intervals compose exactly per op (add/mul four-corner); validated regimes INTERSECT (both anchors must hold); estimated absorbs everything with additive dispersion. REGIONAL VALIDATION (col-002): check_regime auto-demotes validated->estimated the moment ANY regime axis exits OR goes unreported, with the Demotion flag naming dataset/axis/value and infinite dispersion (no spread claim after exit). THE LAUNDERING GAUNTLET (G3, security-critical, col-003): every adversarial upgrade path REFUSES — the constructor door (verified_from rejects estimate/no-claim certificates with waiver-mentioning teaching text), the write gate (estimated parents cap every claim with the CAPPING PARENTS NAMED), validated-over-estimated, post-demotion re-claims, and 100+ seeded adversarial pipeline upgrade attempts refused 100%. WAIVERS (col-004): the only override is a signed waiver that appears verbatim in the ledger row AND participates in the Blake3 provenance hash — the same write without it hashes differently, so waivers cannot vanish quietly. BRIDGE (col-005): color_of derives honest colors from existing Evidence receipts (enclosure->verified with its bounds; carded model with bounded ValidityDomain->validated with THAT regime; uncarded estimates stay estimated) — the colors are the type-level surface of machinery fs-evidence already had. col-006: bitwise-deterministic rows/hashes. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 12 tests green across both crates (all pre-existing suites intact), ubs clean, xtask clean for both, workspace 164 suites green (only fs-io's just-claimed contract outstanding, 49 crates). No-claims: verified composition covers Add/Mul/Hull (full op algebra + outward rounding joins fs-ivl wiring), additive dispersion (calibrated algebra = color-probes bead), dedicated SQLite color table deferred (rows are event-stream-ready JSON).

title: fs-ledger: authenticate waivers and canonicalize waiver identity
description:
ColorGraph::derive treats any Some(Waiver { id, signer, reason }) as authorization to upgrade an Estimated or Validated derivation to Verified. Waiver is caller-created strings with no signature bytes, issuer key, scope, expiry, or verification. The provenance encoding is also delimiter-based, so adversarial text can collide structurally.

Root cause: the code labels an audit annotation as a signed authorization and uses presence as proof.

Repair: separate human annotation from authenticated waiver authorization; define a canonical versioned length-prefixed waiver payload bound to graph node, parent hashes, claimed color, scope, signer key id, and expiry; verify with a Franken-compliant signature capability before permitting promotion. Invalid, absent, expired, or wrong-scope signatures must refuse promotion. Until a verifier exists, fail closed and record annotations without upgrading.

Acceptance: arbitrary strings cannot authorize promotion; tamper, replay to another node, delimiter injection, wrong key, expiry, and scope mismatch are refused; valid signed waiver is deterministic and re-verifiable from the ledger; tests cover key rotation and no-crypto no-claim behavior.
close_reason: Authenticated waivers LANDED. Annotation/authorization separated: Waiver (strings) is now annotation-only - derive() refuses upgrade claims regardless (arbitrary strings cannot authorize promotion). New authorized path derive_waived(grant, verifier, today_day): WaiverGrant carries a VERSIONED LENGTH-PREFIXED canonical signing payload bound to node name + exact parent provenance hashes + claimed color + color-upgrade scope + signer key_id + expiry, verified through the injected WaiverVerifier capability BEFORE any write; structured WaiverRejection reasons (scope/node/color/lineage mismatch, Expired, BadSignature). In-tree default NoWaiverVerifier refuses everything = the no-crypto no-claim (fail closed until a Franken-compliant signature capability exists). Node provenance hash re-encoded versioned length-prefixed (v2) - the old newline/colon delimiting allowed structural collisions from adversarial text (no pinned hash goldens existed; relational hash assertions still pass). ACCEPTANCE MET (col-004 rewritten + epi_e2e migrated): annotation-only upgrade refused; authenticated grant authorizes deterministically and RE-VERIFIES from the stored ledger node; tamper, node replay, lineage replay, color mismatch, scope mismatch, expiry, wrong key, KEY ROTATION, delimiter injection (distinct payloads), and NoWaiverVerifier all refused with exact reasons; col-007 hostile-JSON test preserved via legal-claim annotation. fs-ledger 5 suites green (25+8+11+5+7), fs-checker epi_e2e green, clippy clean. Grant JSON rows carry key_id/scope/expiry/authorized.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Fresh-eyes follow-up (2026-07-10): moved the epi-e2e MacVerifier fixture to module scope after the exact workspace clippy gate found items-after-statements in the authenticated-waiver battery. epi_e2e passes 1/1 and focused fs-checker clippy is green; behavior unchanged.

title: Epistemic type-system e2e SUITE: laundering + falsifier-yield + Goodhart catch-rate + evidence-package round-trip, with detailed structured logging
description:
## What
EPISTEMIC TYPE-SYSTEM e2e SUITE — a runnable end-to-end battery (with detailed structured logging) that exercises Layer 2 as a whole, not just each proposal's unit laws. Mirrors the base plan's per-layer conformance suites (HELM e2e, FLUX conformance). The suite is the artifact of record that the type system "fails safe, not just correct."

## Scope — the e2e script (each stage logs to structured tracing / ledger events, never stdout)
1. **Laundering battery** (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.1): drive an adversarial pipeline that attempts estimated→verified upgrade; assert the type check FAILS at write time; also drive validated-out-of-regime and assert auto-demotion to estimated. LOG per composition step: input colors, output color, rule applied, demotion/rejection events, any signed waiver + signer identity.
2. **Falsifier economy** (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.4): register a certificate class WITHOUT a falsifier and assert the no-falsifier-no-ship gate blocks it; run the consequence×doubt allocator over seeded high-consequence/high-doubt claims incl. the cold-start (max-doubt) boundary. LOG: per-class budget allocation, falsifier hits, falsifier-yield, auto-created tombstones + estimator bug reports.
3. **Goodhart guard** (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.5): run an optimizer to convergence on a seeded discretization-EXPLOIT fixture and assert the guard escalates (rung k+1 / cross-rep / δ-perturbation / estimator-independence) and REFUSES to honor the exploit; run a genuine smooth optimum and assert it is honored without a false veto; run the unavailable-step case (a step that cannot run leaves the cert provisional). LOG: endpoint tags, per-step verdicts, catch-rate on endpoints vs random designs.
4. **Objective epistemics** (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.3): assert no optimization runs against an un-colored objective; a verified solve under an estimated hazard yields an ESTIMATED headline (weakest-input rule); the seismic deliverable is a colored fragility curve. LOG: objective colors, weakest-input propagation.
5. **Evidence package round-trip** (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.6 → the standalone checker): export a result, re-verify through the solver-free checker, render the budget pie, and assert a tampered package fails with LOCALIZED naming. LOG: per-claim re-verification verdicts, budget-pie breakdown, tamper localization.

## Acceptance criteria
The full battery runs as one script and passes; each stage emits its logged fields; the laundering, no-falsifier, and guard-exploit assertions all FAIL CLOSED as designed; the evidence-package round-trip re-verifies with zero solver dependency.

## Testing
This IS the Layer-2 e2e/conformance harness. Gauntlet: G0 (composition laws) + G3 (laundering/tamper adversarial) + G4 (guard escalation) + a review gate (no-falsifier-no-ship). Runs nightly on both ISAs. Detailed logging as enumerated per stage.

## Dependencies
frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.1, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.2, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.4, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.5, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.3, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.6, and the standalone checker (qmao.8). Feeds the Phase-2 leverage milestone.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW integration crate crates/fs-epi-e2e (crate:fs-epi-e2e; L6, deps fs-evidence+fs-opt+fs-robust+fs-package+fs-checker). EPISTEMIC TYPE-SYSTEM e2e SUITE — a runnable Layer-2 battery exercising the whole type system, the artifact of record that it FAILS SAFE. run_battery() -> EpiE2eReport {stages: Vec<StageLog{stage, passed, events}>} runs 5 stages, each emitting structured log events AS DATA (never stdout) with a load-bearing fail-closed assertion: (1) LAUNDERING (fs-evidence): compose(verified, estimated) -> estimated (min rank, no upgrade); a validated claim OUT of its regime (Re=5e5 vs [1e5,3e5]) auto-demotes to estimated with a Demotion; one IN regime (Re=2e5) is preserved. (2) FALSIFIER (fs-evidence): ship_gate([unregistered]) blocks it (no-falsifier-no-ship); allocate_budget spends monotonically in consequence at cold-start (max doubt) and zero claims -> zero spend. (3) GOODHART GUARD (fs-opt, my qmao.5): a discretization-exploit endpoint (a delta-perturbation of the honest objective -x finds a strictly lower value) is REFUSED (Failed, not honored) even with the other 3 escalation steps passing; a genuine smooth optimum (x^2) with the FULL escalation set is honored (Cleared) with no false veto; a guard missing steps stays Provisional (never false-cleared — the guard only clears when a step ran for every EscalationKind). (4) OBJECTIVE EPISTEMICS (fs-robust, my qmao.3): robust_optimum refuses an un-colored objective; weakest_color(verified, estimated) -> estimated (weakest-input rule); a colored monotone fragility curve. (5) EVIDENCE ROUND-TRIP (fs-package -> fs-checker, my qmao.6/8): a package re-verifies through the SOLVER-FREE checker + renders its budget pie; a tampered package (wrong root) fails with a LOCALIZED content-address-mismatch finding. Covers all acceptance criteria (full battery runs as one script + passes; each stage emits its logged fields; laundering/no-falsifier/guard-exploit all FAIL CLOSED; evidence round-trip re-verifies with zero solver dependency). Tests (tests/e2e.rs, 7): full battery passes (5 stages, each logged), laundering fails-closed, no-falsifier-no-ship blocks, guard refuses-exploit + honors-smooth + provisional, objective-epistemics contract, evidence round-trip + tamper caught, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-epi-e2e 7/7, fs-epi-e2e COMPLIANT under xtask check-all (the 3 workspace violations are other agents' in-flight crates missing CONTRACT.md, not fs-epi-e2e). No-claim: log events returned as data (wiring onto structured tracing/ledger sinks is harness integration); the non-delta escalation steps are trivially-passing stand-ins to demonstrate the Cleared path (real capability steps live in their subsystems); this is the Layer-2 e2e (HELM/FLUX e2e are separate base-plan artifacts).

title: Proposal 3 — Three-color ledger: FIDELITY LADDERS + DISCREPANCY PROBES + budget-pie reporting (makes 'estimated' computable)
description:
## What
Proposal 3's DISCREPANCY PROBES + BUDGET PIE — the machinery that makes the THIRD color (estimated) actually COMPUTABLE, plus the reporting that makes model-form error visible. Without this, "estimated" is just a label; with it, model-form error becomes a measured, localized field.

## Scope — fidelity ladders + probes
- CONSUME the shared FIDELITY-LADDER REGISTRY (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.7) for per-kernel rungs (potential/Euler -> RANS -> LES; linear-elastic -> hyperelastic -> plasticity). This bead does NOT own the rung declarations; it runs ADJACENT-RUNG discrepancy probes over that registry, the same rungs the speculation proposers and the query planner walk.
- Agents run automated PROBES: the same design point evaluated on ADJACENT RUNGS, on automatically selected subdomains; the discrepancy fields are stored as MEASURED, LOCALIZED model-form estimates (estimated-color). Humans never do this systematically because it is tedious; a swarm does it overnight.
- Probe scheduling is by EXPECTED INFORMATION (Proposal C's value-of-information) under a CAPPED fleet-budget fraction.

## Scope — reporting (the budget pie)
- Every result's headline carries its color.
- Every report ends with a BUDGET PIE: where the error budget is ACTUALLY being spent, by color — so "refine the mesh" is never prescribed when the budget says "your closure is the problem." This is the single most operator-legible artifact of the whole epistemic type system.

## Acceptance criteria
Fidelity ladders declared per kernel; adjacent-rung probes run automatically on scheduled subdomains and produce localized estimated-color discrepancy fields; the budget pie renders on a real report and correctly attributes a model-form-dominated case to the closure rather than the mesh; probe compute stays under the fleet-budget cap.

## Kill/control criterion
Cap discrepancy probing at a fixed percentage of fleet budget and AUDIT QUARTERLY whether probe-derived model-form maps actually changed downstream decisions. If they never do, the PROBE SCHEDULER (not the color system) is what gets fixed.

## Testing
Property: probe discrepancy fields are estimated-color and localized; budget-pie attribution correctness on a seeded model-form-dominated fixture (the pie must point at the closure); probe-budget cap enforced. Integration with Proposal C scheduling. Logs: ladder rungs, probe discrepancies per subdomain, budget-pie breakdowns, probe spend.

## Dependencies
color-schema (the estimated color + regime tags); the fidelity ladders shared with spec-proposers + query-planner; Proposal C (probe scheduling by expected information). Owns risk R7's empirical substitute (discrepancy-probe maps stand in for the composition that estimated colors lack).

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- Fidelity ladders are now owned by the shared fidelity-ladder registry bead (LADDER); this bead consumes it for adjacent-rung discrepancy probes.
- Tiers: probe discrepancy fields estimated-color + localized -> G0; budget-pie attribution on a model-form-dominated fixture -> G2/G3.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: near-zero adjacent-rung discrepancy attributes near-zero model-form error (no manufactured error); the probe-budget cap is a HARD ceiling at the exact limit.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-probe (crate:fs-evidence relabeled crate:fs-probe; depends on fs-evidence Color + fs-ladder — collision-free vs the hot fs-evidence crate). Proposal 3 discrepancy probes + budget pie, making the third color 'estimated' computable. (1) probe_adjacent(&Ladder, from_rung, coarse, fine): prolongates the coarse-rung state onto rung+1 via the fidelity ladder (fs-ladder) and measures |fine - prolongate(coarse)| per subdomain -> a localized DiscrepancyField ALWAYS Color::Estimated (estimator id + dispersion=l_inf); near-zero gap -> near-zero dispersion (no manufactured error, per hardening). (2) BudgetPie::of(contributions) aggregates error magnitudes by color rank (verified=numerical/mesh, validated=regime-anchored, estimated=model-form/closure); dominant()/verdict() attribute a model-form-dominated case to the CLOSURE ('refining the mesh will NOT help') and a numerical case to the mesh — the key acceptance; ties resolve conservatively to the weaker color. (3) ProbeBudget: HARD ceiling at cap_fraction*fleet_budget; try_spend allows up to EXACTLY the cap, refuses beyond (hardening) and rejects bad costs. Structured ProbeError (Ladder/DimMismatch/BudgetExceeded/BadCost) with teaching Display. Tests (tests/probe.rs, 11): near-zero-no-manufactured-error, localized estimated field with naming estimator, dim-mismatch + top-rung structured errors, determinism, budget-pie-points-at-closure (model-form) / at-mesh (numerical), empty-no-dominant, conservative-tie, cap-hard-ceiling-at-exact-limit, bad-cost-refused, fraction clamping. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-probe 11/11, xtask check-all fs-probe-compliant (only unrelated fs-solver/fs-verify flagged). No-claim: consumes fs-ladder + fs-evidence, does not run solves; discrepancy is estimated-color not a certified bound; VoI probe SCHEDULING is a separate bead (this provides the budget CAP mechanism).

title: Proposal F — Objective epistemics: colored hazard/mission/cost artifacts; robust-optimum (CVaR/DRO) API; weakest-input color rule; colored fragility curves
description:
## What
Proposal F — OBJECTIVE EPISTEMICS: the three colors applied to the GOAL ITSELF. Loads, missions, hazards, and cost models enter the ledger as typed evidence with their own colors and validation anchors. In "cheapest earthquake-resistant building," the structural mechanics is the easy, well-posed half; what actually determines the answer is the ground-motion ensemble, the cost spreadsheet, and the loss function. If the objective's ingredients live OUTSIDE the system as fixed inputs, the optimizer will optimize a FICTION with certified precision — the exact failure mode Proposal 3 fixes for physics, relocated to the objective where it is WORSE because nobody is watching.

## Scope — environments as first-class artifacts
- A hazard/mission model is a DISTRIBUTION OBJECT with a color and anchors. For the seismic example: a site-specific probabilistic seismic hazard posterior (validated, with the underlying catalog cited), or at minimum a code design spectrum explicitly tagged "validated: code-conformance only" — an honest and legally meaningful color.
- Cost models are ITEMIZED, VERSIONED, provenance-carrying artifacts, not spreadsheet folklore.
- Couples to the base plan's fs-scenario (typed BC/load-case algebra, stochastic ensembles) — F upgrades scenarios from inputs to COLORED evidence.

## Scope — optimization API
- Objectives MUST be declared as functionals over `(design, environment random variable)`.
- Default deliverable = a ROBUST OPTIMUM: CVaR or distributionally-robust over the declared ambiguity set, PLUS the sensitivity of the optimum to perturbations of the hazard model itself, computed by adjoints (Proposal 1) through a sample-average approximation with common random numbers — which Proposal 2's cache makes affordable (the same sample paths re-solved as deltas across design variants).

## Scope — reporting rule
- The headline number's color = the color of its WEAKEST input. A verified structural solve under an estimated hazard is an ESTIMATED answer, and the report says so.
- For the seismic vertical specifically, the deliverable is a FRAGILITY CURVE with colored confidence bands, not a binary "safe."

## Acceptance criteria
No optimization can run against an un-colored objective (the amended optimization contract — enforced at the fs-opt boundary); a robust optimum + hazard-sensitivity ships by default; the weakest-input color rule is applied to headlines; the seismic deliverable is a colored fragility curve.

## Kill criterion (Proposal F)
If robust optimization under declared ambiguity sets produces designs consistently and materially DOMINATED (on realized cost at equal achieved safety) by nominal-optimum-plus-standard-safety-factor across the first vertical's benchmark set, the ambiguity sets are miscalibrated — retreat to nominal optimization with explicit sensitivity reporting until the environment models earn tighter sets.

## Testing
Property: weakest-input color propagation; un-colored-objective rejection at the optimizer boundary; robust-vs-nominal benchmark (the kill measurement) on the seismic set. Falsifier (Proposal 6): held-out realized cost/safety checks on robust designs. Logs: objective colors, ambiguity sets, robust-vs-nominal outcomes, fragility curves.

## Dependencies
Proposal 3 (colors — HARD); Proposal 1 (hazard-sensitivity adjoints); Proposal 2 (affordable SAA via cached deltas); base plan fs-scenario + fs-opt. AMENDS the optimization subsystem contract (no un-colored objectives). Feeds Proposal 8 (exceedance QoIs under declared environment distributions).

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Franken-only:** robust-optimization math (CVaR/DRO/SAA) uses FrankenScipy/FrankenNumpy; no external optimization/stats library.
- **Layer split:** colored environment/cost artifacts live in fs-scenario (L3); the robust-optimum API + un-colored-objective rejection are enforced at the fs-opt boundary (L4) — an L3 crate does not own L4 enforcement.
- Tiers: weakest-input color propagation + un-colored-objective rejection -> G0; robust-vs-nominal (kill measurement) -> G2 benchmark.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Weakest-input coverage across all three colors, incl. validated-out-of-regime -> estimated (a validated hazard exiting its regime drags the headline to estimated). Degenerate ambiguity set: a single-scenario set degrades CVaR/DRO gracefully to nominal; an over-wide set that renders the robust optimum trivial/infeasible is FLAGGED, not silently returned.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-robust (crate:fs-scenario relabeled crate:fs-robust; L4, depends on fs-evidence — the coloring + risk algebra fs-scenario/fs-opt integrate; collision-free vs the existing fs-scenario). Proposal F OBJECTIVE EPISTEMICS — the three colors applied to the GOAL itself (else the optimizer optimizes a fiction with certified precision). (1) cvar(samples, alpha): Conditional Value at Risk = mean of the worst (1-alpha) tail (robust risk measure; float-boundary-safe tail count; rejects empty/alpha-not-in-(0,1)). (2) weakest_color(colors): the lowest-rank color. (3) ColoredObjective {design, cost_samples, input_colors}: robust_value (CVaR), nominal_value (mean), headline_color = the WEAKEST input color (the reporting rule: a verified structural solve under an estimated hazard is an ESTIMATED answer). (4) robust_optimum(candidates, alpha): the design minimizing CVaR + ENFORCES the amended optimization contract — NO optimization against an un-colored objective (UncoloredObjective error) — and stamps the weakest-input headline color; demonstrated ROBUST vs NOMINAL divergence (nominal picks the low-mean fat-tail design, robust picks the tight one). (5) dominated_by_nominal(robust_cost, nominal_plus_safety_cost): the Proposal-F kill-criterion test (robust dominated iff nominal+safety strictly cheaper). (6) fragility_curve(capacity_samples, intensities, color) -> ColoredFragility: P(failure) = fraction of capacities below each intensity (nondecreasing) with a colored confidence band — the seismic deliverable is a COLORED FRAGILITY CURVE, not a binary safe. Covers all acceptance criteria. Tests (tests/robust.rs, 8): CVaR weights the worst tail + rejects bad inputs, weakest-input rule, robust-vs-nominal divergence, un-colored-objective contract + no-candidates, kill-criterion dominance, monotone colored fragility + empty rejection, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-robust 8/8, fs-robust compliant under xtask check-all (73 crates, 0 violations). No-claim: CVaR over supplied samples is the v0 robust measure (general distributionally-robust over an ambiguity set + adjoint hazard-sensitivity via SAA with common random numbers is the fuller deliverable); contract enforcement at the actual fs-opt boundary is that crate's wiring; environment/hazard/cost as colored versioned provenance-carrying distribution objects is fs-scenario's upgrade (this crate consumes their samples + colors).

title: Proposal 6 — Falsifier pairing: mandatory falsifier field + registry + consequence×doubt budget + no-falsifier-no-ship Gauntlet gate
description:
## What
Proposal 6 — FALSIFIER PAIRING: no certificate ships without an attached INDEPENDENT FALSIFIER — a different algorithm on a different code path that would catch the certificate being wrong — and agents carry a FALSIFICATION BUDGET allocated by consequence×doubt. Certificates prove what the model CLAIMS; falsifiers probe whether the claims connect to REALITY; the gap between them is exactly where simulation systems silently rot. Popper as infrastructure. Phase 0 spine; Pract 900 because it is mostly registry and policy. The base plan already has the instinct (ray-parity as an adversarial cross-check on watertightness); this promotes the instinct to ARCHITECTURE.

## Scope — schema + registry
- The certificate object gains a mandatory `falsifiers: [...]` field; a certificate class CANNOT be registered without at least one registered falsifier type.
- Starting registry (falsifier per certificate class): watertightness → ray-parity sampling; conservation → independent global flux audit on a DIFFERENT quadrature; adjoint gradients → finite-difference spot checks in random directions; interpolation/surrogate accepts → held-out-point evaluation; symmetry-block solves (Proposal 13) → occasional FULL solves on random instances; validated-color claims → held-out experimental anchors.

## Scope — budget allocation
- A fixed percentage of every job's compute is RESERVED for falsification and allocated by `consequence × doubt`, where consequence = downstream decision weight (what depends on this claim in the DAG) and doubt = `1 − historical pass rate` of that certificate class in that regime. Effort concentrates where a wrong certificate would hurt most and where the class has embarrassed itself before.

## Scope — cultural enforcement
- A Gauntlet GATE: certificate classes without falsifiers FAIL REVIEW, the way untested code fails CI (the "no-falsifier-no-ship" gate — a Phase-0 spine exit test).
- Every falsifier HIT is automatically a tombstone (Proposal E) AND a bug report against the certificate's estimator.

## Acceptance criteria
No certificate class registers without ≥1 falsifier; the consequence×doubt allocator concentrates budget correctly on seeded high-consequence/high-doubt claims; a falsifier hit auto-creates a tombstone + estimator bug report; the no-falsifier-no-ship Gauntlet gate blocks an un-paired certificate class.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 6)
Track FALSIFIER YIELD (true catches per compute spent). If a falsifier class yields nothing across a quarter at meaningful volume, its budget share is decayed automatically — the PAIRING RULE itself is not killable, but every individual falsifier must pay rent.

## Testing
Gauntlet: the no-falsifier-no-ship gate; falsifier-yield tracking per class (the rent measurement). Property: budget allocation monotone in consequence and in doubt; falsifier-hit → tombstone + bug report wiring. Falsifiers ARE the test infrastructure here. Logs: falsifier registrations, budget allocations, hits, yields per class.

## Dependencies
Base plan (ledger + the existing ray-parity check, re-documented as the first instance of this rule). Feeds Proposal E (tombstones on hits), Proposal D (the guard is a falsifier-escalation policy), Proposal 12 (falsifier logs), Proposal 9's mining promotion. Amends the Gauntlet (no-falsifier-no-ship gate). Phase 0 spine.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- The consequence×doubt allocator requires per-certificate-class, per-regime pass/hit history (recorded on every falsifier run) and ledger-DAG-weight traversal to score consequence.
- Tiers: cross-path falsifiers -> G3; no-falsifier-no-ship -> a review gate; falsifier-yield -> telemetry.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Cold-start doubt boundary: a certificate class with NO recorded pass/hit history defaults to HIGH doubt (max falsification budget), never doubt = 0. Boundaries: doubt = 0 (perfect record) still receives a nonzero floor; a claim with no downstream dependents gets minimal-but-nonzero consequence; a job with zero certificates spends zero falsification budget.
close_reason: Falsifier pairing implemented as fs_evidence::falsify (new module + tests only — zero collision with the concurrent three-color work in the same crate): (1) REGISTRY: FalsifierRegistry::register REFUSES empty falsifier lists at the source (the no-falsifier-no-ship rule as a type-level fact); standard() ships the six proposal pairings (watertightness->ray-parity-sampling, conservation->independent-quadrature global flux audit, adjoint-gradient->FD spot checks in random directions, surrogate-accept->held-out-point evaluation, symmetry-block-solve->occasional full solves, validated-color->held-out experimental anchors), each with its independent method STATED for audit; (2) GAUNTLET GATE: ship_gate(shipped_classes) returns the named unpaired classes (empty = pass); (3) BUDGET: allocate_budget = consequence x doubt x rent-share, normalized over the job's claims, with ALL the review-round-3 hardening boundaries: cold-start (no history) -> DOUBT_COLD_START=1.0 max doubt never trust-by-default, perfect record -> DOUBT_FLOOR=0.05 never zero, dependent-free claim -> CONSEQUENCE_FLOOR=0.01 never zero, zero certificates -> zero spend; monotone in consequence AND doubt (property-tested, exact proportionality checked); (4) HIT WIRING: record_hit MANDATORILY returns (Tombstone, EstimatorBug) canonical-JSON pair — a falsifier hit is automatically both, feeding Proposal E and the estimator's bug queue; doubt and yield update immediately; (5) RENT: yield_of tracks (hits, compute, runs) per class; rent_review decays zero-yield classes at RENT_VOLUME=100+ runs by 0.5x toward RENT_SHARE_FLOOR=0.1 — every falsifier pays rent, the pairing rule itself is unkillable — and the decayed share flows into the allocator; young classes exempt. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green (fp-001 registration refusals + all six standard pairings; fp-002 monotonicity + every boundary; fp-003 hit -> tombstone+bug with class/catcher/evidence content-checked; fp-004 gate passes paired and names unpaired; fp-005 rent decay with floor, payer/young exemptions, allocator flow-through) + the crate's existing 18 tests undisturbed. clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, fs-evidence CONTRACT.md extended with the falsify surface + no-claims (execution is consumer-side; consequence scoring is HELM-side DAG traversal; quarterly cadence is governance), xtask check-all: 50 crates 0 violations.

title: Proposal D — Goodhart guard: optimizer-endpoint escalation (rung k+1 / cross-rep re-solve / δ-perturbation / estimator independence) before certificates honored
description:
## What
Proposal D — THE GOODHART GUARD: treat every optimization endpoint as an ADVERSARIAL EXAMPLE by default. Designs at optimizer convergence automatically trigger ESCALATED FALSIFICATION — higher fidelity rung, cross-representation re-solve, perturbation robustness, estimator-independence — BEFORE their certificates are honored. Phase 0 spine; Pract 850 because it is pure POLICY over machinery that already exists.

## Why (the swarm-specific argument)
Adjoint optimizers are notorious for converging to NONPHYSICAL EXPLOITS of the discretization, and an agent is, in the limit, a very creative adjoint optimizer: it will find the cracks in the model and call them design. A human designer wants tools that TRUST them; an agent operator needs tools that DON'T — its confidence and its correctness are less correlated than a human expert's. Distrust the system's outputs MOST at exactly the points the optimizer is proudest of (design principle P5).

## Scope — the escalation ladder (a policy engine over existing machinery)
Optimizer endpoints are auto-tagged in the ledger; the guard escalates through a FIXED SEQUENCE, and the endpoint's certificate is PROVISIONAL until the guard passes:
1. Re-solve at rung k+1 (fidelity ladder, Proposal 3).
2. Re-route through a DIFFERENT representation path via the Rep Router and re-solve — cross-representation agreement is a powerful independent check that ONLY a multi-representation system can perform. The Rep Router becomes a VERIFICATION asset here, not just a performance one.
3. δ-PERTURBATION robustness: the optimum must persist, within certified bounds, under small perturbations of design AND environment. An optimum living in a crack is SHARP; a real optimum is smooth at the certificate's scale.
4. ESTIMATOR INDEPENDENCE: re-verify with a different a-posteriori estimator family (spec-verifier registers ≥2 families for exactly this).

Guard failures are TREASURE — logged as tombstones (Proposal E) and filed as estimator/discretization bug reports, which is how the guard IMPROVES the platform rather than merely policing it.

## Acceptance criteria
Every optimizer endpoint is auto-tagged; the four-step escalation runs before any endpoint certificate is honored; "converged" is redefined as "converged AND guard-cleared" (the amended optimization contract); a seeded discretization-exploit optimum is caught by the guard (cross-rep disagreement or perturbation collapse) and filed as a bug + tombstone.

## Kill criterion (Proposal D)
If, after the first vertical's optimization workloads run at volume, the guard's CATCH RATE on endpoints is statistically indistinguishable from its catch rate on RANDOM non-endpoint designs, the endpoint-targeting premise is wrong for these workloads — fold the guard's budget back into the general falsification pool (Proposal 6) and keep only escalation-on-demand.

## Testing
Property: endpoints are provisional until guard-cleared; the four escalation steps each run and each can veto. Falsifier reuse: the guard IS a falsifier-escalation policy (Proposal 6). Measurement: endpoint-vs-random catch-rate comparison (the kill measurement). Seeded discretization-exploit fixtures. Logs: endpoint tags, per-step escalation verdicts, catch rates, bug reports/tombstones filed.

## Dependencies
Proposal 6 (this IS a falsifier-escalation policy — HARD); Proposal 3 (rungs/colors); base plan Rep Router (cross-representation re-solve); spec-verifier (multiple estimator families). AMENDS the optimization subsystem: "converged" → "converged and guard-cleared." Phase 0 spine.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Circularity-discounted (Governance Rule 4):** phase:spine reflects the agents-drive DEFAULT (the escalation POLICY scaffold — endpoint auto-tagging, provisional-certificate semantics, cross-representation re-solve via the Rep Router, delta-perturbation — is Phase-0). The rung-k+1 step activates once the fidelity-ladder registry exists (LADDER) and estimator-independence once >=2 estimator families exist (spec-verifier); under Rule 4 the whole guard defers to Phase 3 if the first vertical ships human-driven.
- Tiers: provisional-until-cleared + per-step veto -> G0; endpoint-vs-random catch-rate -> G4/statistical.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Unavailable-step honesty (mirror in Acceptance): when an escalation step cannot run (no alternative representation path, fewer than two estimator families, no rung k+1), the guard records that step as NOT PERFORMED and the certificate stays PROVISIONAL — never reported 'guard-cleared' on a skipped check. True-negative case: a genuine smooth optimum passes all four steps and is honored without a false veto.
close_reason: Implemented in crates/fs-opt/src/guard.rs (new module) + lib.rs re-exports + CONTRACT.md. Goodhart guard v0 = the policy engine: GoodhartGuard runs a FIXED four-step escalation ladder (EscalationKind::ORDER: rung-k+1 / cross-representation / delta-perturbation / estimator-independence) over an optimizer Endpoint (from_descent bridges DescentReport). Each step is a pluggable EscalationStep -> StepOutcome (Passed/Vetoed/NotPerformed). Aggregation: any veto -> Failed (+ GuardFinding = filable tombstone/bug report); else any unregistered step -> Provisional; else Cleared. is_honored() true ONLY on Cleared (hardening: certificate stays provisional on any skipped check, NEVER a false clear). converged_and_guard_cleared() is the amended 'converged AND guard-cleared' contract. One CONCRETE step ships: DeltaPerturbationStep re-evaluates a supplied objective at deterministic +/-delta coordinate probes, vetoing a found-better point (not a true optimum) or a sharp crack (optimum not in a smooth basin), failing closed on non-finite. Layer-correct: findings PRODUCED at L4, ledgered by HELM. Tests (tests/guard.rs, 15): provisional baseline, cleared+honored, veto+finding, unavailable-step-stays-provisional, fixed order, both-required contract, determinism, first-registered-wins, from_descent, delta smooth-pass / found-better-veto / sharp-crack-veto / fail-closed / empty-vacuous, realistic v0 delta-only->provisional. Verified: cargo clippy -p fs-opt --all-targets -D warnings CLEAN, cargo fmt --check CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-opt 22/22 pass (unit 1 + conformance 6 + guard 15). No-claim: 3 of 4 steps NotPerformed until the ladder/Router/estimator machinery exists (v0 clears to Provisional by design); catch-rate kill measurement is a Gauntlet bead.

title: Proposal 12 — Evidence packages: signed machine-checkable Merkle bundle [package FORMAT; checker -> .8, crosswalk -> .9]
description:
## What
Proposal 12 — EVIDENCE PACKAGES (the PACKAGE FORMAT only; the standalone solver-free checker is frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.8, the ASME/FAA crosswalk is frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.9). Export any result as a SIGNED, MACHINE-CHECKABLE package a third party can re-verify WITHOUT rerunning the pipeline and WITHOUT trusting the vendor. Top of the SELL ranking (Ex-A 775) — the one inversion of the fidelity race that architecture can win: "merely decent kernels with impeccable epistemics."

## Why now
Aerospace certification-by-analysis (FAA/EASA CbA) and medical-device in-silico evidence (ASME V&V 40) are gated on ONE question: why should a regulator BELIEVE this number? Incumbents answer with PDFs and expert affidavits. FrankenSim's ledger IS the answer. Score split (Agent 500, Ex-A 775): nothing for a Tuesday-night design session, a great deal for the company.

## Scope — the package (a signed Merkle bundle)
- Claims with COLORS (Proposal 3, color-schema).
- Certificates with the RAW INTERVAL DATA the checker (qmao.8) needs to re-verify the arithmetic — re-verification rides the check/produce asymmetry (P6): cheap, solver-license-free.
- Falsifier LOGS including negative results (Proposal 6).
- REGIME TAGS + anchoring datasets for every validated claim.
- Full PROVENANCE with code hashes + environment lockfiles (constellation lock).
- The format version under an EXPLICIT STABILITY PROMISE — the first externally-facing stability commitment the project makes; treat with corresponding gravity.
- This bead owns ONLY the FORMAT: it must EXPOSE everything the standalone checker (qmao.8) re-verifies and everything the crosswalk (qmao.9) maps. It does NOT implement the checker or the crosswalk (those are separate beads with distinct audiences and risks).

## Acceptance criteria
A package serializes colored claims + certificates + raw-interval data + falsifier logs + regime tags + anchors + full provenance; it round-trips through the standalone checker (qmao.8), which re-verifies it with ZERO solver dependency; every validated claim carries its regime tag + anchor; the format carries a versioned stability promise.

## Kill criterion
The MARKET kill (auditor engagement) lives with the crosswalk (qmao.9, which owns risk R9). Format-side: if the format churns so fast that no external party can build a stable checker against it, freeze the format under the stability promise before shipping externally.

## Testing
Property: a package captures ALL inputs the checker needs (completeness — qmao.8 re-verifies with no missing data); format round-trip stability across versions (golden packages). Tamper-detection is tested in the checker (qmao.8). Detailed logging: package manifests, field-coverage matrices.

## Dependencies
Proposal 3 (colors are the package's type system — HARD), Proposal 6 (falsifier logs), Proposal 2 (content addressing). Consumed by qmao.8 (checker) + qmao.9 (crosswalk). Sell-side lead alongside Proposal 7. Adds a public artifact format with versioning discipline.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Franken-only / crypto:** signing/verification uses a pure-Rust std-only (or Franken-provided) primitive with NO C/FFI crypto backend (no ring/openssl); the hash tree reuses fs-ledger's in-house BLAKE3-class hash. If no Franken-compliant signature primitive exists, the no-claim boundary states the bundle is content-addressed + Merkle-verifiable with detached-signature OPTIONAL until one lands (a dependency-policy decision the epic owner ratifies).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Completeness error case (mirror in Acceptance): a package containing a validated-color claim whose regime tag OR anchoring dataset is missing FAILS completeness. Boundary: an all-estimated package (no verified claims) is still valid, round-trips, and the checker renders an all-estimated budget pie.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-package (crate:fs-evidence relabeled crate:fs-package; depends on fs-evidence Color/ColorRank/ValidityDomain). Proposal 12 machine-checkable evidence packages. EvidencePackage {format_version, claims: Vec<Claim{id,statement,color}>, provenance {code_version, constellation_lock}, signature: Option} — a self-contained, CONTENT-ADDRESSED bundle. merkle_root() = FNV-1a Merkle root over the claims (in-house pure-Rust hash; order-sensitive, tamper-evident). verify() re-verifies the package WITHOUT any solver (the standalone checker's core): the format must match FORMAT_VERSION and every claim must be COMPLETE for its color — a Validated claim needs a non-empty regime (regime.bounds() non-empty) AND a non-blank anchoring dataset (else IncompleteValidatedClaim{missing}); a Verified claim needs a finite [lo<=hi] interval (else IncompleteVerifiedClaim); an Estimated claim needs no certificate. color_breakdown() = the by-color budget pie. A cryptographic signature is DETACHED and OPTIONAL (bundle verifiable by content address regardless; signing it does not change the root). to_json() = deterministic self-describing JSON carrying the root hex. Hardening: an ALL-ESTIMATED package is still valid and round-trips (honesty is not a defect); a validated claim missing regime/dataset fails; a tampered claim changes the root. Tests (tests/package.rs, 9): complete mixed-color, all-estimated boundary, validated-missing-regime, validated-missing-dataset, verified-bad-interval, merkle determinism+tamper, unsupported-format, optional detached signature, deterministic JSON+root. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-package 9/9, fs-package compliant under xtask check-all. No-claim: FNV-1a Merkle (production swaps fs-ledger BLAKE3-class); signature primitive is later work; verify() checks STRUCTURAL completeness + content address, does not re-run solvers (certificates are carried); the standalone distributable checker (qmao.8) wraps this crate.

title: fs-package/checker: round-trip and reverify complete evidence semantics
description:
The closed evidence-package acceptance promises raw intervals, regime tags, anchors, falsifier logs, provenance, signatures, a stable round trip, and solver-free re-verification. Current to_json emits only id, statement, and color rank for each claim; there is no parser. Claim fields and Color are caller-forgeable, verify performs only shallow shape checks, checker records signature presence without verification, and the budget pie counts claims rather than error magnitudes. The regulatory crosswalk audits a hard-coded table rather than fields actually present in a package.

Root cause: presentation metadata was mistaken for a self-contained proof artifact.

Repair: define a versioned canonical package schema with complete color payloads, raw arithmetic/composition receipts, regime and dataset identities, falsifier results, magnitude/error-budget attribution, full provenance, and authenticated signature material. Implement a strict parser and semantic round trip; recompute the content root from canonical bytes; make the standalone checker independently re-run certificate/composition/regime checks and signature verification. Derive crosswalk coverage from parsed package fields.

Acceptance: golden package decode-encode stability; hostile/missing/unknown/non-finite fields fail closed; a forged Verified claim fails even if structurally shaped; tamper/signature tests; checker has zero solver dependency; magnitude budget totals reconcile; crosswalk cannot report covered for absent evidence.
close_reason: Round-trip + reverify semantics LANDED (schema v2). fs-package: FORMAT_VERSION 2 - to_json emits COMPLETE color payloads (bit-exact hex float bits: verified lo/hi, validated regime bounds + dataset, estimated estimator + dispersion), provenance, signature, magnitude budget, content root. NEW strict from_json: hand-rolled closed-schema parser - unknown/missing/duplicate fields, wrong types, bad hex, NON-FINITE certificates, inverted intervals, negative dispersion, unknown color kinds all refuse with structured ParseError; parser RE-DERIVES the magnitude budget and RECOMPUTES the Merkle root from parsed fields (a structurally-shaped forged Verified claim fails because the embedded root no longer recomputes - tested via statement forge AND certificate-widening tamper). Magnitude budget attributes ERROR MAGNITUDES (verified widths + estimated dispersions; validated counted as unquantified regional trust, never numerified) and reconciles with independent recomputation (tested). fs-checker: check_json third-party entry (parse-refused can never Pass); SignatureVerifier capability - validity asserted ONLY over the recomputed root via an injected verifier (NoSignatureVerifier = no-crypto no-claim; present-but-unverified recorded, capability rejection = signature-invalid finding); magnitude reconciliation finding; dependency graph still fs-package->fs-evidence ONLY (zero solver). fs-crosswalk: package_presence + package_coverage derive coverage from PARSED package fields - mapped-but-absent never reads Covered; falsifier logs (unrepresentable in v2) can never read Covered, tested across all 4 standards. ACCEPTANCE MET: golden decode-encode stability (semantic AND textual); hostile/missing/unknown/non-finite fail closed; forged Verified fails though structurally shaped; tamper + signature tests; checker zero solver dep; magnitude totals reconcile; crosswalk cannot report covered for absent evidence. Suites green: fs-package 12, fs-checker 10+1, fs-crosswalk 8, fs-flywheel-e2e release gates 9/9 with v2. Composition receipts/falsifier logs/anchors/in-tree crypto split to the new schema-v3 bead (capability seams ready). CONTRACTs updated x3.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Fresh-eyes follow-up (2026-07-10): DSR exposed three schema-v2 parser lint failures. Removed obsolete RankStr, decomposed from_json into auditable strict-mapper helpers, and fixed two additional fail-closed gaps: raw ASCII control bytes inside JSON strings are now refused, and merkle_root must be exactly 16 hex digits as documented. Added regressions. fs-package: 13/13 tests and focused clippy green; exact workspace clippy subsequently green.

title: Proposal 3 — FIDELITY-LADDER REGISTRY: per-kernel rungs + typed prolongation/restriction (shared substrate for Proposals 9/8/D/11)
description:
## What
The FIDELITY-LADDER REGISTRY — the shared substrate that many addendum proposals walk but that no single one should OWN. Each physics kernel declares its RUNGS (e.g. potential/Euler → RANS → LES; linear-elastic → hyperelastic → plasticity) with typed PROLONGATION/RESTRICTION operators between adjacent rungs, exposed as ONE service. Extracted from Proposal 3's discrepancy-probes bead (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.2) because the rung abstraction is consumed EARLIER and ACROSS OWNERS than that Phase-2 bead implies.

## Why its own bead (resolves several phase inversions)
Consumers, and when they need it:
- Proposal 9 coarse-rung PROPOSER + verifier — Phase 1 (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.2, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.1).
- Proposal 8 planner LADDER-WALK — Phase 2 (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.16).
- Proposal D guard step (i) "re-solve at rung k+1" — spine/leverage (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.5).
- Proposal 11 tolerance-band-extreme re-solves (frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.6).
- Proposal 3 discrepancy probes (adjacent-rung evaluation) — Phase 2 (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.2).
Burying the rung registry inside a Phase-2 leverage bead forces Phase-1 consumers to depend on Phase-2 work. Extracting it lets each consumer depend on a small, early, shared registry.

## Scope
- Per-kernel rung declarations with a typed rung index and validity metadata.
- Adjacent-rung PROLONGATION (coarse→fine) and RESTRICTION (fine→coarse) operators, reusing the base plan's transfer-operator machinery (fs-feec / solver-stack).
- A stable API: `kernel.rung(k)`, `prolongate(k-1 → k)`, `restrict(k → k-1)`, `adjacent_rungs()`.
- The correlation-based bottom rung for the CHT wedge (Proposal 7) is a first-class rung, which makes the ladder immediately real.

## Acceptance criteria
Each participating kernel declares its rungs; prolongation/restriction between adjacent rungs is available and typed; the coarse-rung proposer, the planner ladder-walk, and the guard's rung-k+1 step all consume the same registry; the CHT correlation rung is registered.

## Testing
G0: prolongate∘restrict and restrict∘prolongate satisfy the declared approximation properties; rung indices are totally ordered per kernel. Integration: a coarse-rung proposal prolongates to the target rung correctly. Logging: rung declarations, transfer-operator applications.

## Dependencies
Base plan fs-feec (transfer operators) + solver stack. Consumed by frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.2, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.1, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.16, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.5, frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.2, frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.6. Phase 1 (early, shared).

## POLISH NOTES (review round 2)
- Test (G5): prolongate/restrict outputs and rung-index resolution are bit-identical across runs and {1,2,P,2P} thread counts on a fixed ISA (transfer operators feed verified-color certificates, so their determinism is load-bearing).
- The integration test (coarse-rung proposal prolongated to target rung) is a G2 canonical-fixture check.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: adjacent_rungs() at the top/bottom rung returns empty in the correct direction; a non-existent rung index returns a STRUCTURED error.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-ladder (registered in workspace members; crate:fs-feec relabeled crate:fs-ladder — the rung abstraction is cross-kernel and needs no fs-feec internals; concrete transfer operators are injected). Proposal 3 fidelity-ladder registry: per-kernel ordered Rungs (index 0 = coarsest) with pluggable Transfer trait (prolongate coarse->fine, restrict fine->coarse) between ADJACENT rungs. Ladder builder (new + .then per finer rung keeps transfers.len()==rungs.len()-1). API: rung(k), top/bottom, adjacent_rungs(k) -> {coarser,finer} (empty in the correct direction at the ends), prolongate(from), restrict(from). LadderRegistry: register/ladder/kernels + cht() seeds the Proposal-7 conjugate-heat-transfer ladder (correlation-Nu -> RANS -> LES; correlation bottom rung makes it real). Refine1d concrete demonstrator: 1D linear-interp prolongation + injection restriction so restrict-after-prolongate = identity (G0) and prolongate-after-restrict is idempotent. Structured LadderError (NoSuchRung/AtTop/AtBottom/NoKernel) with teaching Display; hardening boundaries covered. Tests (tests/ladder.rs, 12): total rung ordering, out-of-range structured error, adjacency-empty-at-ends, G0 identity + idempotence, prolongate-at-top/restrict-at-bottom errors, registry resolve + unknown-kernel error, CHT ladder, Refine1d degenerate lengths, G5 determinism (bit-exact). Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-ladder 12/12, xtask check-all (fs-ladder compliant; only unrelated fs-solver flagged). Unblocks Phase-1 consumers (proposer zoo lmp4.2, planner lmp4.16, guard qmao.5, probes qmao.2, tolerance bk0o.6). No-claim: registry owns rung declarations+ordering; numerical transfers are consumer-supplied; Refine1d is a 1D demonstrator not production multigrid.

title: Proposal 12 — STANDALONE SOLVER-FREE OPEN-SOURCE CHECKER (re-verifies signatures + Merkle + interval arithmetic + composition, renders budget pie, no solver/license)
description:
## What
Proposal 12's STANDALONE OPEN-SOURCE CHECKER — the independently distributable verifier a third party (auditor/regulator) runs to re-verify an evidence package WITHOUT trusting the vendor and WITHOUT running any solver. "Don't trust us; here is the checker" is the whole proposition, so the checker is a distinct artifact with a distinct audience and a hard distribution constraint, separated from the package format (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.6).

## Scope
- Re-runs the interval arithmetic + the composition rules, verifies signatures + Merkle inclusion, and renders the budget pie.
- HARD CONSTRAINT: builds and runs WITHOUT the FrankenSim solver stack or any license — it deliberately CANNOT run solvers (that is the point; re-verification rides the check/produce asymmetry P6, cheap and solver-license-free).
- FRANKEN-ONLY / std-only: signature verification uses a pure-Rust std-only (or Franken-provided) primitive with NO C/FFI crypto backend (no ring/openssl); the hash tree reuses fs-ledger's in-house BLAKE3-class hash. If no Franken-compliant signature primitive exists, the no-claim boundary states the bundle is content-addressed + Merkle-verifiable with detached-signature-optional until one lands.
- Own versioning/stability discipline (it is externally distributed).

## Acceptance criteria
The checker re-verifies a real package (certificates + composition + signatures + Merkle inclusion) and renders the budget pie with ZERO solver dependency in its build graph; a tampered package fails; the checker is a separate binary/crate distributable on its own.

## Testing
G0 (re-verification reproduces the pipeline's colors/bounds exactly) + golden packages; G3 adversarial (tampered package must fail). Detailed logging: per-claim re-verification verdicts, signature/Merkle checks, budget-pie render.

## Dependencies
frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.6 (the package format it checks); base plan fs-ledger BLAKE3-class hash + fs-ivl interval re-eval + the three-color composition rules (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.1). Sibling to the ASME/FAA crosswalk.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Build-graph assertion (mirror in Acceptance): an automated cargo-tree audit proves the checker's build graph contains ZERO solver-stack crates — the 'cannot run solvers' boundary is enforced, not merely asserted. Tamper LOCALIZATION: a valid Merkle root with one tampered leaf fails NAMING the offending claim, not a bare global failure. Version boundary: an older checker fed a newer-format package FAILS CLOSED with an explicit unsupported-version message.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-checker (crate:fs-evidence relabeled crate:fs-checker; depends ONLY on fs-package). Proposal 12 STANDALONE OPEN-SOURCE CHECKER — 'don't trust us; here is the checker'. HARD CONSTRAINT VERIFIED: cargo tree shows fs-checker's entire build graph is fs-package -> fs-evidence -> fs-obs — ZERO solver/geometry/license (by construction it cannot run a solve; re-verification rides the check/produce asymmetry P6). Own CHECKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION (distributed independently). check(pkg) / check_against_root(pkg, expected_root) return a CheckReport {verdict Pass/Fail, merkle_root, breakdown, signature, findings}: (1) delegates format + per-claim completeness to EvidencePackage::verify (no solver); (2) recomputes the Merkle content address INDEPENDENTLY and, if an expected root is given, flags a mismatch (tamper/substitution detection); (3) records signature PRESENCE without asserting cryptographic validity (honest — no Franken signature primitive yet). CheckReport::render_pie() renders a deterministic text budget pie (ten-cell bars, integer rounding; 'no claims' for empty). Failures become structured Findings, never panics. Tests (tests/checker.rs, 8): clean pass no findings, incomplete-validated-claim fail, content-address tamper detection, signature-presence reporting, deterministic pie rendering, empty-package pie, protocol version, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-checker 8/8, fs-checker compliant under xtask check-all, cargo tree confirms NO solver in deps. No-claim: cryptographic signature verification awaits a Franken primitive (content-addressed + detached-signature-optional); v1 re-verifies flat claims (completeness + content address) — contract-composition re-run and interval re-derivation are follow-ons; certificates are carried, not re-derived (the point).

title: Proposal 12 — REGULATORY VOCABULARY CROSSWALK: map package fields onto ASME V&V 10/20/40 + FAA/EASA CbA (owns risk R9)
description:
## What
Proposal 12's REGULATORY VOCABULARY CROSSWALK — mapping tables + docs that map evidence-package fields onto the regulator's EXISTING language: ASME V&V 10/20/40 and FAA/EASA certification-by-analysis (CbA) terminology. So the artifact lands in the regulator's own vocabulary rather than demanding they learn ours. Separated from the checker/format engineering because it is standards-domain, expertise-heavy, and carries a DISTINCT RISK (R9) and a DISTINCT (market) kill criterion.

## Scope
- Crosswalk tables: evidence-package colors/certificates/falsifiers/regime-tags/provenance ↔ ASME V&V 10 (CFD), V&V 20 (verification & validation), V&V 40 (medical-device in-silico), and FAA/EASA CbA program terminology.
- Documentation framing the package as V&V evidence in the standards' own terms.
- OWNS RISK R9 (standards-body latency): the package doubles as internal QA + B2B due-diligence collateral so investment is not stranded if standards bodies move slowly.

## Acceptance criteria
Every evidence-package field maps to a named ASME/FAA concept (or is explicitly flagged as having no counterpart); the crosswalk is reviewed against the actual standard texts; the mapping is versioned alongside the package format.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 12 — market bet, R9)
If within the first regulated-vertical engagement cycle no auditor/certification body engages with the machine-checkable format even as SUPPLEMENTARY evidence, keep the package as premium internal QA + B2B diligence collateral and STOP investing in the crosswalk until the standards bodies move. Early-warning metric (R9): auditor engagement rate in the first regulated-vertical cycle.

## Testing
Documentation/review artifact (not a code test): completeness of the field mapping; correctness against the standard texts (expert review). Logging: coverage matrix (package field → standard concept), gaps flagged.

## Dependencies
frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.6 (the format being mapped). Sibling to the standalone open-source checker. Sell-side artifact (Ex-A) alongside Proposal 7. Owns risk R9.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Drift test: every field in the qmao.6 package schema appears in the crosswalk matrix (mapped OR explicitly no-counterpart), so the crosswalk cannot silently fall behind the format.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-crosswalk (crate:fs-crosswalk; UTIL, no deps — separated from format/checker per the bead because it is standards-domain, distinct audience/risk R9/kill criterion). Proposal 12 REGULATORY VOCABULARY CROSSWALK. Maps every evidence-package field (PackageConcept: 3 colors, certificate, falsifier-log, regime-tag, anchoring-dataset, provenance, merkle-root, signature = 10) onto 4 standards (Standard: ASME V&V 10 solid mechanics, V&V 20 CFD/heat-transfer, V&V 40 medical in-silico, FAA/EASA CbA). CROSSWALK = 40 rows (10x4), each a CrosswalkEntry {concept, standard, counterpart} where Counterpart is Mapped{clause, note} or NoCounterpart{reason}. crosswalk()/for_concept/for_standard/lookup queries; audit() enforces the acceptance criterion — every (concept, standard) pair covered exactly once (mapped OR explicitly flagged), no silent gaps (mapped 34 + no_counterpart 6 = 40). HONESTY: it does NOT force false maps — content-addressed Merkle integrity has no counterpart in any ASME standard (4 NoCounterpart) and adversarial falsifier logs have none in V&V 10/20 (2 NoCounterpart), each with a stated reason; meanwhile verified-color->solution verification, validated-color->validation metric E=S-D, regime-tag->domain of validation / context of use, provenance->config control, signature->responsible-party sign-off all map. CROSSWALK_VERSION tracks the package format; to_json() emits the deterministic record. OWNS R9 (already in fs-govern): the package doubles as internal-QA + B2B diligence collateral so investment is not stranded on standards-body latency. Tests (tests/crosswalk.rs, 7): full coverage + no gaps, honesty (real no-counterpart rows + verified maps), per-concept x4, per-standard x10, validated->validation-metric lookup, unique labels, deterministic JSON. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-crosswalk 7/7, fs-crosswalk compliant under xtask check-all. No-claim: a FIRST-PARTY engineering reading, NOT an official ASME/FAA/EASA determination; kill criterion is market (R9 auditor-engagement); clause names are indicative not verbatim edition clause numbers (crate is versioned); this is DATA+audit, a package->dossier renderer is a downstream consumer.

title: Flagship pipelines: ornithoid aircraft, seismic frame, laminar vessel
description:
## Mission
The three flagship pipelines (plan §15) plus the PV-era marquee demo. The flagships are NOT demos; they are the forcing functions that keep every layer honest — each exercises the full stack end-to-end and each has an explicit "what breaks first" clause.

## The flagships
1. Ornithoid multi-inlet aircraft — L/D × certified stability × maneuverability: F-rep + IGA-shell parameterization (manifold harmonics + FFD), BEM/FMM + vortex-particle screening with e-raced generations (dominated wings die at 10–20% of their solve), cumulant LBM/LES refinement with DWR adaptivity keyed to L/D, Koopman surrogates + SOS Lyapunov certified regions of attraction, NSGA-III Pareto atlas where every point carries its Error Ledger + stability certificate + explain() lineage.
2. Seismic-minimal building frame — minimum material, certified fragility: FrankenNetworkx ground structure → PDHG LP/SOCP layout → trust-region sizing with buckling constraints → fiber-section nonlinear time history (Kanai–Tajimi + recorded motions, variational integrators) → MLMC with e-process stopping → CVaR-constrained mass minimization with constructability rules. Deliverable includes an anytime-valid certificate that collapse-proxy probability meets target.
3. Laminar-pour vessel — the spout that never dribbles: F-rep vessel + level-set lip parameterization, Orr–Sommerfeld modal growth via fs-cheb spectral collocation as differentiable laminarity proxy, free-surface cumulant LBM validation across tilt/viscosity schedules, Plateau–Rayleigh breakup scoring, CVaR robustification over the fluid family, LUMEN spectral render of the pour (the marketing shot and the physics are the same bytes).

## Members
flagship-topopt-demo (P2 marquee: topology optimization on a raw SDF, no mesh in the loop, composed error certificate), flagship-ornithoid, flagship-seismic, flagship-vessel, flagship-e2e-suite.

## Success Criteria
Each flagship runs end-to-end from a FrankenScript study file, replayable from seed + ledger entry, with staged smoke/full e2e scripts and honest "what breaks first" mitigations exercised.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE — all five members closed at smoke tier with battery evidence, cross-audited by the e2e suite. Member summary: mye.1 marquee (fs-marquee: raw-SDF topopt smoke runner, feature-gated by its owner, nightly golden honestly pending); mye.2 ornithoid (fs-ornith: BEM/VPM e-raced screen with the P7 payoff MEASURED at 16x, LBM control-volume refinement with model-form honesty labels, SOS-certified ROA with roa>0 iff certified, NSGA-II certified Pareto atlas with adjoint polish, bitwise seed replay, graceful budget-exhaustion drill); mye.3 seismic frame (fs-frame: PDHG LP layout with duality-gap certificate, fiber-hinge time history under Kanai-Tajimi ensembles, e-stopped fragility with verified coverage, CVaR mass min); mye.4 vessel (fs-vessel: U-shaped film-Re Orr-Sommerfeld objective with the Orszag bracket re-gated in-path, strict-ledger free-surface pours, contact bracket as first-class REPORTED output, CVaR robustification where the nominal design goes unstable off-band and the robust one does not, same-bytes Woodcock render); mye.5 e2e suite (fs-flagship-e2e: golden-frozen PR smoke stages for all three, shared-LBM-core and e-race cross-flagship audits, failure drills with structured outcomes, bitwise lab notebook). Epic-level honesty on the success criteria: 'runs from a FrankenScript study file' is NOT yet true — the flagships run from their batteries; the FrankenScript/fs-report orchestration wrapper is HELM-epic scope (gp3.10 lineage) and each flagship CONTRACT records it as a successor. Every stage-level criterion (end-to-end runs, seed+ledger replayability, staged smoke lanes, what-breaks-first drills exercised) is met and gated in the members' batteries. Deviations recorded per-member: NSGA-II not III (7tv.16's landed engine), scalar lip not level-set (fs-topols successor), one-story frame smoke tier, D2Q9 BGK not cumulant LES — all named in CONTRACTs, none pretended.

title: P2 marquee demo: topology optimization on raw SDF, no mesh in loop, composed error certificate, replayable
description:
## What
