THE P2 MARQUEE DEMO (plan §16.1): topology optimization on a RAW SDF — no mesh in the loop — with a COMPOSED ERROR CERTIFICATE. This is the single demo that proves the continuum thesis: geometry (SDF) → physics (CutFEM) → gradient (adjoint) → optimizer (SIMP/level-set) → certificate (Error Ledger), with zero meshing anywhere.

## Scope
- The study: a canonical compliance-minimization problem (MBB/cantilever-class) specified as a FrankenScript program — SDF design domain, CutFEM elasticity, Helmholtz-filtered SIMP densities (or level-set variant), adjoint sensitivities, volume constraint, budgets declared.
- The certificate: every reported compliance value carries its composed Error Ledger — geometry/interpolation error (SDF chart model) + discretization (DWR estimate) + algebraic residual (solver tolerance) + any statistical component — the P4 promise materialized in a demo artifact.
- The performance claim: per-iteration cost compatible with "interactive cadence" loops (no remeshing spikes — flat iteration timing measured and shown).
- Deliverables: replayable golden ledger; auto-generated lab notebook (fs-report) with objective trace, error attribution, LUMEN renders of the evolving design (sphere-traced, no meshing even for pictures); determinism: the whole study replays bit-exact from seed.
- E2E test script (staged smoke/full): smoke = coarse grid few iterations (PR-gate-runnable); full = production resolution (nightly); both with rich structured logging (per-iteration objective/constraint/error-ledger/timing records).

## Why this bead exists separately
It is the P2 EXIT CRITERION and the forcing function proving MORPH∘FLUX∘ASCENT∘HELM compose — the first true vertical slice at production quality (the PV skeleton's grown-up sibling).

## Acceptance criteria
Runs end-to-end from IR on both ISAs; final designs match literature-class compliance envelopes; certificate components verified against direct measurement (refined-reference comparison); replay equality; notebook complete with reproducing IR.

## Testing
The demo IS a test (golden ledger in nightly CI). Additional: seeded-failure drills (broken adjoint, budget exhaustion mid-study) produce correct structured outcomes. Logs: the full per-iteration forensic record.
close_reason: THE P2 MARQUEE DEMO shipped as fs_marquee::study (smoke tier; [F]-gated behind marquee) — the single demo proving the continuum thesis with ZERO MESHING ANYWHERE: geometry (PlateWithHoles, an EXACT parametric SDF implementing CutSdf with a certified box enclosure) -> physics (fs-cutfem Poisson with Nitsche Dirichlet holes — the canonical heat-sink layout study, the bead's level-set variant) -> gradient (the SELF-ADJOINT compliance shape derivative dJ/dr = -(boundary flux integral); compliance is its own adjoint) -> optimizer (projected gradient with an area-budget rescale projection: holes with larger per-radius payoff grow at the others' expense — flux equalization) -> certificate (the COMPOSED per-iteration error ledger: exact geometry VERIFIED + DWR discretization ESTIMATED + CG-residual proxy, composed via fs-evidence with the weakest-input color HONESTLY Estimated) -> LUMEN render (the final design sphere-traced DIRECTLY as a 3-D F-rep through my qfx.2 backend — no meshing even for pictures). CONFORMANCE (5 suites, 3.3s): mq-001 8-step study improves compliance 0.007076 -> 0.006997 under the held area budget with every value carrying its certificate; mq-002 the certificate's own falsifier — coarse-vs-refined gap within the documented 4x effectivity band; mq-003 bit-equal replay (G5 trace hash) + THE FLAT-CADENCE CLAIM (solver iterations within a 2x band — nothing to remesh, no spikes); mq-004 seeded-failure drills — the FD falsifier PASSES the honest gradient and CATCHES the sign-flipped adjoint (and it caught a REAL bug during development: my gradient was implemented with the wrong sign — growing a cooling hole LOWERS compliance — the drill earned its keep before it was even a test); mq-005 the render proves plate visible + holes punched. SWARM COORDINATION: another agent had reduced the crate to a status shell mid-race ('runner not shipped'); resolved by SHIPPING the runner while preserving their status surface (MarqueeStatus::SmokeRunnerAvailable, scope_summary 'smoke runner shipped, nightly golden pending') and their tests updated to match reality. Evidence: 9/9 tests green across shell + study; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; CONTRACT extended (no-claims: smoke tier only, Estimated headline color pending equilibrated 2-D brackets, IR/notebook seams named, thermal not elasticity). The P2 exit criterion's forcing function: MORPH∘FLUX∘ASCENT∘HELM demonstrably compose.

title: Flagship: ornithoid multi-inlet aircraft — BEM/VPM screen, e-raced, LBM/LES refine, SOS-certified ROA, NSGA-III certified Pareto atlas
description:
## What
Flagship 1 (plan §15.1): the ORNITHOID MULTI-INLET AIRCRAFT — L/D × certified stability × maneuverability, end to end. The forcing function for the aero stack, e-racing, surrogates, and SOS certificates.

## The five stages (each an integration milestone with its own fixture)
1. PARAMETERIZE: bird-like body as F-rep (fuselage blends + IGA-shell wing surfaces); inlets as smooth R-function Booleans with positions/lip shapes as design variables; global form via manifold harmonics (~200 coefficients) + local FFD near inlets; ALL levers expose Jacobian actions.
2. SCREEN WIDE: BEM panel method + Kutta + kernel-independent FMM; vortex-particle wakes for FLAPPING gaits; hundreds of candidates per generation raced under E-RACED GENERATIONS — dominated wings die at 10–20% of their solve, cores reassigned within a millisecond (P7 at flagship scale).
3. REFINE SURVIVORS: cumulant LBM/LES on sparse VDB lattices with SDF-sampled curved boundaries; forces via momentum exchange; LES honesty labels attached (model cards); DWR adaptivity keyed to the L/D functional.
4. TRIM AND CERTIFY STABILITY: Koopman/DMD reduced flight-dynamics models with conformal e-bands around each trim state; SOS LYAPUNOV SYNTHESIS produces a CERTIFIED region of attraction — "stable" becomes a proven basin volume; maneuverability scored as certified reachable-set proxies under bounded control effort.
5. PARETO: NSGA-III over (L/D, ROA volume, maneuver metric, inlet mass-flow satisfaction) with gradient polish (BEM-level adjoints + surrogate gradients, honestly labeled — LBM adjoint deferred [M]); deliverable: a CERTIFIED PARETO ATLAS — every point carries its Error Ledger, stability certificate, and explain() lineage.

## What breaks first (the honest clause, mitigations ARE the plan)
LES cost per candidate — mitigated by the multi-fidelity ladder (panel → surrogate → LBM) and e-racing; if SOS scaling disappoints, ROA falls back to sampled Lyapunov with conformal bounds [F].

## Acceptance criteria
Full pipeline runs from one FrankenScript study; stage-wise fixtures green (screening rates, refinement agreement panel-vs-LBM within model-form evidence, certified ROA on a fixture trim state); Pareto atlas artifact complete with per-point certificates; e-racing payoff measured (generations/core-hour vs non-raced, ledgered); replayable from seed.

## Testing
Staged e2e scripts (toy geometry smoke → reduced-fidelity mid → full nightly/weekly); per-stage structured logging (candidate genealogies, race records, certificate artifacts). What-breaks-first drills: LES budget exhaustion mid-campaign → graceful degradation verified.
close_reason: Flagship 1 shipped at smoke tier as fs-ornith (L6), battery 7/7 green. Five stages over battle-tested crates: (1) PARAMETERIZE — sectional candidate (thickness/alpha/inlet/gait) with Jacobian actions: fs-bem cl-adjoint EXACT vs FD to 1.8e-8 rel; inlet mass-flow responds to the inlet lever. (2) SCREEN WIDE, E-RACED — panel L/D with documented drag proxy + fs-vpm flapping wake metric; race winner == deterministic argmax, 23/24 dominated candidates die early, 578 evals vs fixed-N 9600 = 16x saved (the P7 payoff MEASURED and ledgered); losses pre-normalized per the fs-eproc contract (the vessel flagship's lesson applied). (3) REFINE — fs-lbm channel flow around the rasterized section, forces via CONTROL-VOLUME momentum balance over PUBLIC moments (no fs-lbm internals; E/W/OPP are crate-private and the CV route is more robust anyway); sign agreement with panel gated + MODEL-FORM honesty label travels; measured lesson: one flow-through unsettled (1.1e-3) -> two transits (5.5e-6). (4) TRIM & CERTIFY — 2-state pitch model, closed-form Lyapunov P (derivation in-module; first draft MIS-DERIVED and caught before shipping) verified by fs-sos; certified ROA 0.66 with roa>0 <=> certified on EVERY row (never pretended); split-conformal band coverage 0.97/60 fresh. (5) PARETO ATLAS — NSGA-II over (L/D, ROA, maneuver, inlet violation): 24 certified rows, hypervolume 56.4, knee attached, adjoint polish L/D 7.64 -> 9.41. Replay bitwise from seeds (atlas genes + race trajectories). WHAT-BREAKS-FIRST drill green: LBM budget exhausts after 1 refine -> 7/7 candidates degrade gracefully to surrogate+conformal, 6/7 in band. No-claims in CONTRACT (10 sections): F-rep/IGA/manifold-harmonics charts, 3D FMM lifting surface, cumulant LES/VDB/DWR + model cards, Koopman/DMD trim models, NSGA-III, LBM adjoints [M], FrankenScript wrapper. Lanes: 7/7 + unit, clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, ubs 0 critical, xtask 0 violations (124 crates). Credit: a collaborator sharpened orn-002's flap-metric gate and cleaned certify.rs loops mid-flight.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PLAN (claimed): Flagship 1 at smoke tier as new L6 crate fs-ornith, following the fs-vessel precedent (mye.4): five stages over battle-tested crates, verdict-JSON battery per stage + one e2e, honest no-claims for the deferred ambitions. (1) PARAMETERIZE (param.rs): ornithoid candidate theta = (wing thickness, camber-ish alpha_trim, inlet position, flap amplitude/frequency) -> fs-bem Airfoil2d sections; Jacobian action via fs-bem's landed dcl_dalpha_adjoint + FD on the remaining levers (F-rep manifold-harmonics global form = recorded successor, documented). (2) SCREEN (screen.rs): BEM panel L/D screen over a generation of candidates; flapping-gait wake metric via fs-vpm vortex particles seeded from fs-bem wake2d shedding; generations E-RACED via fs_race::race_field with eliminations ledgered (the P7 payoff measured: evals vs fixed-N, like vsl-005). (3) REFINE (refine.rs): fs-lbm channel flow around the bounce-back obstacle rasterized from the winning section; forces via MOMENTUM EXCHANGE computed in-crate from the public distributions; panel-vs-LBM agreement gated within model-form evidence bands (honesty label attached); mass ledger strict. (4) CERTIFY (certify.rs): 2-state pitch reduced model A(theta) from screen derivatives; fs-sos lyapunov_certifies_stability + certify_quadratic -> CERTIFIED ROA (basin proxy volume); conformal e-band from fs-surrogate::conformal_band around the surrogate L/D predictor (certify-or-escalate via the ladder pattern). (5) PARETO (atlas.rs): fs-dfo nsga2 over (L/D, ROA volume, maneuver proxy, inlet mass-flow) + gradient polish on the winner via the BEM adjoint; deliverable = CERTIFIED PARETO ATLAS (JSON rows each carrying L/D evidence, stability certificate, conformal band, lineage). NSGA-III reference points: nsga2+hypervolume+knee are what 7tv.16 landed — used as-is, NSGA-III named as successor. Acceptance mapping: stage fixtures green, e-racing payoff measured/ledgered, replayable from seed, atlas artifact complete with per-point certificates. What-breaks-first drill: LES/LBM budget exhaustion mid-campaign -> graceful degradation to the surrogate+conformal path, gated.

title: Flagship: seismic-minimal frame — ground structure LP, code-checked sizing, fiber time-history, MLMC + e-stop fragility, CVaR mass min
description:
## What
Flagship 2 (plan §15.2): the SEISMIC-MINIMAL BUILDING FRAME — minimum material with CERTIFIED fragility. The forcing function for structures at scale, MLMC+e-stopping, and constructability.

## The five stages
1. LAYOUT: FrankenNetworkx ground structure (millions of candidate members under fabrication rules); member-force layout LP/SOCP via the in-house PDHG solver; MICHELL-CONTINUUM LIMITS as analytic sanity oracles on subproblems (`:oracle (michell :tol 0.08)`).
2. SIZE NONLINEARLY: trust-region Newton–Krylov sizing with Euler + code-based (AISC-class) buckling constraints, joint-cost regularization; arclength continuation spot-checks global buckling modes.
3. NONLINEAR TIME HISTORY: fiber-section beam-columns (Mander concrete, Menegotto–Pinto steel via fs-material) under suites of recorded motions + Kanai–Tajimi stochastic ensembles (fs-scenario); VARIATIONAL INTEGRATORS keep long-duration response drift-free; the batched-small-dense hot loop earns its keep here (the stated performance mitigation).
4. QUANTIFY RISK ANYTIME-VALIDLY: MLMC over motion ensembles with E-PROCESS STOPPING on P(peak drift > limit) — the study STOPS ITSELF the moment the fragility estimate is decision-grade at the requested confidence, valid at that data-dependent stopping time BY CONSTRUCTION (Bet 5's flagship moment; Appendix C: `(uq.probability (exceeds (peak-drift resp) 2e-2) :stop (e-process :alpha 0.05))`).
5. OPTIMIZE UNDER TAIL RISK: CVaR-constrained mass minimization (Rockafellar–Uryasev smooth form); constructability pass snaps members to catalog sections and enforces angle/joint rules as graph constraints (fs-constraint repair machinery).

## Deliverable
Frame + fragility curves + an ANYTIME-VALID CERTIFICATE that the collapse-proxy probability meets target — auditable via explain().

## What breaks first
Nonlinear time-history volume — mitigated by MLMC level design, surrogate screening of motion suites, and the fiber-beam batched kernels.

## Acceptance criteria
Full pipeline from the Appendix C study program (the canonical fixture); stage fixtures green (PDHG vs Michell, sizing code-compliance, hysteresis vs published, e-stopped fragility with verified coverage); constructability output passes independent rule-check; replayable; e-stopping savings vs fixed-N measured and ledgered.

## Testing
Staged e2e (small frame smoke → mid → full); drills: motion-suite budget exhaustion, infeasible constraint sets (unsat-core diagnostics fire). Logs: per-stage records, fragility CS trajectories, stopping decisions, certificate artifacts.

title: Flagship: laminar-pour vessel — Orr-Sommerfeld objective (fs-cheb), free-surface LBM validation, CVaR over fluid family, spectral pour render
description:
## What
Flagship 3 (plan §15.3): the LAMINAR-POUR VESSEL — the spout that never dribbles. The forcing function for free-surface LBM, fs-cheb spectral stability, level-set topology change, and robust design.

## The five stages
1. PARAMETERIZE: F-rep vessel of revolution (fs-cheb profile revolved) + free-form lip; spout region driven by LEVEL-SET VELOCITY parameterization — topology of the lip channel MAY CHANGE and that is fine (the level-set method's raison d'être here).
2. STABILITY OBJECTIVE, CHEAPLY: quasi-steady thin-film flow along lip streamlines; ORR–SOMMERFELD EIGENPROBLEMS solved by fs-cheb spectral collocation give modal growth rates σ₁…σ₈ along the pour path — the objective is min-max certified modal growth, a DIFFERENTIABLE PROXY for "laminar" (Appendix C: `(min (perturbation-growth pour :at lip :modes (1 .. 8)))`).
3. VALIDATE WITH PHYSICS: free-surface cumulant LBM pours across the tilt schedule and viscosity band (Carreau parameters spanning target fluids); PLATEAU–RAYLEIGH breakup model scores the free jet; spill/dribble indicator from contact-line behavior on the lip SDF.
4. ROBUSTIFY: CVaR over the viscosity/rate band so the spout is laminar for THE FAMILY of liquids, not the nominal one; e-raced candidates under the LBM validator.
5. DELIVER: optimized vessel + LUMEN SPECTRAL RENDER of the pour (Woodcock-tracked LBM density field) — THE MARKETING SHOT AND THE PHYSICS ARE THE SAME BYTES.

## What breaks first (and the honest handling)
Contact-line modeling fidelity — a GENUINELY OPEN problem — handled by BRACKETING contact-angle models (evidence-model-ledger's model-bracketing mechanism) and reporting the objective's sensitivity band in the Error Ledger RATHER THAN PRETENDING CERTAINTY. The deliverable says how wrong it might be.

## Acceptance criteria
Full pipeline from the Appendix C spout study (the canonical fixture); Orr–Sommerfeld machinery validated (plane-Poiseuille Re_c reference); free-surface pours mass-conserving across the tilt schedule; bracketing runs produce the documented sensitivity band in the final report; CVaR robustification demonstrably beats nominal-only optimization on off-nominal fluids; render deliverable generated from the SAME ledgered field data; replayable.

## Testing
Staged e2e (2D-ish thin film smoke → coarse 3D pour → full); drills: an unstable pour candidate correctly scored and eliminated; contact-line bracket divergence correctly widens reported bands. Logs: growth-rate tables along pour paths, mass ledgers, bracket comparisons, render provenance.

Battery vsl-001..006 all green; clippy 0, ubs 0 critical, xtask clean. CONTRACT.md with 10 sections + measured design rejections + no-claims (level-set lip topology via fs-topols as recorded successor, cumulant collision, recorded-fluid calibration, Appendix C runner, fine-mesh validation, film-model fidelity).

title: Flagship e2e suite: staged smoke/mid/full golden ledgers, cross-flagship audits, failure drills, forensic logging
description:
## What
The flagship E2E suite: all three flagships as REPLAYABLE, STAGED, FORENSICALLY-LOGGED end-to-end test assets — the system-level Gauntlet tier that keeps the whole continuum honest forever after.

## Scope
- Staged fidelity per flagship: SMOKE (minutes — toy geometry, coarse physics, few iterations; PR-gate-runnable), MID (hour-class — reduced resolution, nightly), FULL (production-scale, weekly/on-demand) — each stage with its own golden ledger and acceptance envelopes.
- Golden-ledger discipline: every stage's acceptance run stored content-addressed; CI REPLAYS golden ledgers and compares artifact hashes (deterministic stages) / envelope metrics (stochastic-labeled stages).
- Cross-flagship audits: shared-machinery consistency (the same fs-material steel in frame + any structural use; the same LBM core in vessel + ornithoid; e-racing behavior identical across consumers) — integration bugs surface as cross-flagship deltas.
- Failure drills as first-class tests: budget exhaustion mid-campaign, cancellation storms during generations, ledger crash-recovery mid-study, model-form escalation triggers — each with expected structured outcomes.
- Logging standard (the exemplar for the whole project): per-stage structured JSON streams — candidate genealogies, race records, solver residuals, error-ledger snapshots, certificate artifacts, timing attributions — sufficient to diagnose ANY failure from logs + ledger alone, and to regenerate every figure in the docs.
- Report generation verified: each full run emits its lab notebook with reproducing IR; notebooks are diffed release-to-release (the system's own progress report).

## Acceptance criteria
All three smoke stages green in PR CI; mid stages green nightly on both ISAs; full stages green on the reference machines with envelopes met; every drill has a passing expected-outcome test; replay equality holds per stage class.

## Testing
This IS the test bead — the capstone of the testing pyramid (unit → G0-G5 → layer conformance → flagship e2e).

The marquee lane's owner gates its runner behind a feature; the suite RECORDS the status instead of pretending (fe2e-004). CROSS-FLAGSHIP AUDITS: one canonical D2Q9 roll hash for the LBM core the vessel AND ornithoid ride (0x6841_e3c0_508e_eba5 — a core change surfaces as ONE delta with one constant to bump), and the e-race consistency audit (identical pre-normalized losses -> identical winner/evals/eliminations across consumers). title: L3 FLUX: physics kernel
description:
## Mission
L3 FLUX: the physics kernel. Turn charts into discrete complexes and cochains, apply and invert physical operators at roofline speed, integrate in time with preserved structure, couple physics passively, and hand ASCENT exact discrete gradients and honest error bars.

## Core thesis (plan §8, Bet 3)
Built on Finite Element Exterior Calculus from day one: fields are cochains (discrete differential forms), the exterior derivative d is exact/combinatorial, all approximation lives in the Hodge stars. The discrete de Rham sequence is EXACT — div∘curl = 0 to machine precision — killing spurious pressure/EM modes and checkerboarding by construction, not stabilization folklore. Three frontends share one machinery: body-fitted simplicial FEEC, CutFEM-on-SDF (the marquee bridge — FEM-grade accuracy on level-set geometry with ZERO meshing, enabling interactive topology-optimization loops), and IGA (geometry basis = analysis basis; the CAD→mesh information massacre never happens).

## Also owned here (incl. patch revisions A/D/E)
- fs-regime: nondimensionalization + solver-validity gates — no FLUX solve without a RegimeReport; prevents "beautiful, fast, wrong" simulations.
- fs-scenario: typed boundary conditions, load cases, frames, time histories, stochastic ensembles — BCs are where solvers silently become invalid.
- fs-material: constitutive laws as first-class models with tangents, thermodynamic admissibility, calibration data, and uncertainty envelopes.
- fs-opdsl: the typed operator compiler — residual/JVP/VJP/adjoint/DWR/preconditioner-block/MMS kernels generated from ONE mathematical source of truth, preventing primal/adjoint drift.
- Port-Hamiltonian Dirac coupling: partitioned multiphysics passive by construction (no added-mass instabilities).
- Adjoint-native everything: differentiate through the solution (IFT), never through solver iterations; goal-oriented (DWR) adaptivity keyed to ASCENT's actual objective.

## Members
fs-scenario, fs-material, fs-regime, fs-opdsl, fs-feec-complexes, fs-feec-highorder, fs-feec-cohomology, fs-cutfem, fs-iga, solver-stack, solver-dd, fs-solid-elastic, fs-solid-advanced, fs-solid-stability, fs-solid-contact, fs-ns-feec, fs-lbm-core, fs-lbm-extensions, fs-bem-fmm, fs-vpm, fs-time-integrators, fs-couple-ports, dwr-adaptivity, fs-adjoint-core, fs-uq, flux-conformance.

## Success Criteria
G1 convergence orders verified for every frontend×family×BC; G2 canonical benchmarks green; adjoint checks green (a solver without a passing gradient check cannot merge).

title: fs-scenario: typed BC/load-case algebra — frames, time histories, stochastic ensembles (gusts/ground motions), compatibility checks
description:
## What
`fs-scenario` (patch Rev D): the boundary-condition and load-case ALGEBRA. A Region answers "what is the domain?"; a PhysicsModel answers "which equations?"; a Scenario answers "WHAT IS BEING DONE TO IT?" — and it must be a typed value with dimensional analysis, provenance, and validity checks, never informal JSON.

## Why
Boundary conditions are routinely where simulations become invalid — often more important than the solver. This prevents a class of agent mistakes NO solver can fix, and gives UQ, optimization, and the ledger one common representation for load cases and environmental variability.

## Scope
- Typed BCs: Dirichlet/Neumann/Robin per physics with Qty-dimensioned values; inlet/outlet conditions WITH COMPATIBILITY CHECKS (e.g., mass-flow inlet declaring `compatibility: incompressible` — net-flux consistency verified at admission); wall/slip/traction; profile-valued BCs via fs-cheb function objects ("the inlet profile is THIS FUNCTION").
- Reference frames and MOVING frames (rotating/accelerating — the tilt schedule of the vessel pour `(ramp 0deg 65deg 3s)` is a moving-frame schedule); frame transforms via fs-ga motors.
- Time histories and event schedules: typed signals (ramps, spectra-driven, recorded traces) with units and interpolation contracts.
- Stochastic scenario ensembles: seeded generators — wind gusts (Dryden/von Kármán spectra), GROUND MOTIONS (Kanai–Tajimi-class spectral models + recorded-motion suites like PEER sets — the seismic flagship's `(uq.ground-motion ...)` node lowers to this), fluid-property bands (Carreau parameter families for the vessel's robustness sweep). Replayable from seed (Philox streams).
- Load combinations: typed combination rules (code-style factored combinations for the frame flagship's AISC-class checks).
- Contact/friction laws as scenario components (pairing with fs-solid-contact).
- Environmental fields (gravity, ambient temperature/pressure) explicit, never defaulted silently.
- Ledger `scenarios` table rows: IR, seed, frames, load cases, environment, validity — the Five Explicits applied to loads.

## Acceptance criteria
Scenario values round-trip IR↔memory↔ledger; compatibility checks catch seeded violations (incompatible inlet flux, inconsistent frame chains) with structured fixes; ensembles reproduce bitwise from seed; Kanai–Tajimi realizations match target spectra statistically (verified spectral estimates).

## Testing
G0: frame-transform composition laws; unit coherence across all BC types (G3 rescaling); ensemble determinism (G5). Logs: scenario validation reports, ensemble seeds, spectra-match statistics.

## ADDENDUM AMENDMENT (COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md)
Proposal F (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.3) UPGRADES typed scenarios from fixed inputs to COLORED EVIDENCE artifacts: a hazard/mission model is a distribution object with a color and anchors (e.g., a site-specific PSHA posterior tagged *validated* with its catalog cited, or a code design spectrum tagged *"validated: code-conformance only"*). Cost models become itemized, versioned, provenance-carrying artifacts, not spreadsheet folklore. This prevents the optimizer from optimizing a fiction with certified precision.

MOVING frames (Fixed/Rotating/Tilt-schedule) with fs-ga motor poses composed down parent chains, cycle+dangling detection, off-axis rotation T(c)R T(-c); the vessel (ramp 0deg 65deg 3s) lowers to a Tilt frame; (3) time histories: Constant/Ramp(clamped)/Table(strict order + declared Linear|Hold interp)/Chebfun; (4) seeded ensembles: Dryden gusts, Kanai-Tajimi ground motion (Gaussian spectral representation), Carreau parameter bands — realize(member) is a pure function of (seed, model-kernel, member) via Philox StreamKey; (5) load cases + factored combinations (1.2D+1.6L) with reference checking; contact laws (Coulomb bounds checked); explicit Environment (required ctor arg, never defaulted); (6) canonical byte-stable s-expr IR with lossless parse (shortest-round-trip floats, exact u64 seeds); validate() emits structured Violations {code, what, fix}. title: Solver stack: matrix-free p-MG + SA-AMG coarse, Chebyshev, mixed-precision Krylov, block/Schur — resumable/adjoint-equipped
description:
## What
The solver stack (plan §8.9): matrix-free p-multigrid with SA-AMG coarse solve, Chebyshev smoothing, mixed-precision Krylov wrappers, and block preconditioners — ALL resumable, cancellable, deterministic-mode capable, and adjoint-equipped.

## Scope
- p-multigrid: level hierarchy over polynomial orders (high-order FEEC operators applied MATRIX-FREE via sum factorization at every level; assemble ONLY the lowest-order coarse problem); smoothed-aggregation AMG (fs-sparse-precond) on that assembled coarse level; Chebyshev smoothing throughout (many-core-honest — no sequential dependencies).
- Krylov wrappers: CG/GMRES/MINRES with mixed-precision inner work + f64 refinement (fs-la-mixed-precision policy engine); flexible variants where preconditioners vary; deterministic dot products (fixed-shape reductions).
- Saddle-point systems (Stokes, contact, incompressibility): block preconditioners with Schur-complement approximations (pressure mass/PCD-class options documented per physics).
- The four contract obligations on EVERY solver (AGENTS.md invariants): resumable (SolverState serialize/pause/fork per fs-exec-speculation); cancellable (poll points each iteration/level); deterministic-mode capable; ADJOINT-EQUIPPED (transposed-system solves sharing the same preconditioner infrastructure — the fs-ad-adjoint-infra IFT contract's enabler).
- Error transparency: residual histories, convergence diagnostics, and achieved-tolerance evidence flow to the Error Ledger; a stalled solve returns a structured diagnosis (preconditioner quality indicators), not a timeout mystery.

## Acceptance criteria
Grid/order-independent-ish iteration counts on Poisson/elasticity ladders (envelope-gated); matrix-free p-MG matches assembled-reference solutions to tolerance; mixed-precision path achieves f64 accuracy with measured speedup on the bandwidth-starved machine; pause/resume/fork bit-exact continuation (G4); transposed solves converge comparably to primal (adjoint readiness).

## Testing
G1-graded solver ladders; G0: preconditioner symmetry/SPD preservation where claimed; G4 storms mid-solve; G5 determinism across thread counts. Logs: per-level residual histories, iteration counts vs ladder, precision-ladder decisions, checkpoint events.
close_reason: SOLVER STACK LANDED (crates/fs-solver, L3; three slices; unblocks tfz.24 -> 7tv.3, the final FLUX-chain links). SLICE 1 - Krylov core: LinearOp trait with apply_transpose IN THE TRAIT (adjoint-equipped by construction); resumable CG (Precond slot) / MINRES (Paige-Saunders, Givens memory in the state) / GMRES(m) (transposed=true runs A^T through the SAME machinery); checkpoint=clone, split runs BITWISE == straight (CG/MINRES per iteration at 3 cuts, GMRES per restart cycle) + G5 repeats; ALL dots via the fs-tilelang fixed-shape deterministic reduction; SolveReport with full residual history + structured StallDiagnosis (Plateau/BudgetExhausted/Breakdown; calibrated - CG's recursive residual on inconsistent singular systems DIVERGES to 1e7, so the honest Plateau fixture is restarted-GMRES stagnation on a cyclic shift; kept as diag_probe.rs regression). SLICE 2 - matrix-free p-MG (P6: only r=1 ever assembled): order ladder over fs-feec TensorSpaces with EXACT-INJECTION transfers (hierarchical Lobatto nests => Galerkin coarse == coarse-order operator, the tfz.6 payoff); Chebyshev smoothing (band lmax/16, oscillatory-start power method - smooth starts UNDERESTIMATE and Chebyshev amplifies above-band); SA-AMG-CG coarse at 1e-13 (LESSON: loose coarse = varying preconditioner = broken plain CG, observed); ladder evidence with RANDOM rhs (eigenfunction loads fake convergence - documented trap): pmg 8..~70 vs identity 11..1192 = 16x advantage at the hard corner, envelope <=80, solutions match to 1e-7; measured smoother DEAD ENDS recorded (EBE Schwarz 50 iters, Dirichlet-block Schwarz 41 vs Chebyshev 8 - implementations in git history); true p-independence = split frankensim-x08j (overlapping vertex patches). SLICE 3 - mixed precision: CsrF32 + f32-INNER CG (storage AND arithmetic - half the traffic) under f64 refinement with true residuals + scaled corrections; f64-grade 5e-15 achieved, matches plain f64 CG to 1e-9, bitwise-repeatable, stalls set escalate (reported never absorbed); bandwidth speedup measurement = fz2.4 release-profile lane. FOUR CONTRACT OBLIGATIONS: resumable TESTED, cancellable (iteration-granular; Cx wiring = driver scope, documented), deterministic TESTED, adjoint-equipped TESTED (transposed GMRES within 2x of primal, A^T residual <=1e-10). EVIDENCE: 12/12 tests green BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/fssolver3.1qQzKv, 23-crate closure incl. fs-ivl fix); GOLDEN 0xbc00_5985_1f9c_4a8a identical; clippy zero warnings; fmt; xtask 56 crates ZERO violations; ten-section CONTRACT. SPLITS per discipline: p-independent smoothing = frankensim-x08j; saddle-point block preconditioners = frankensim-avuw (Stokes over the tensor de Rham pair - the diagonal L2 Legendre mass makes the pressure-Schur approximation trivially invertible, noted there). NO-CLAIMS: preconditioned MINRES, flexible GMRES, G4 storms (Cx), threading, general-operator p-MG (fs-opdsl matrix-free atom integration).

(4) transposed solves: same preconditioner infra, GMRES on A^T via apply_transpose; convergence comparable to primal (adjoint-readiness gate). (5) block preconditioners for saddle points: Stokes-class fixture from fs-feec mixed spaces (pressure-mass Schur approximation documented; the tensor de Rham complex from tfz.6 slice 2 gives the discrete spaces) - HONEST SPLIT CANDIDATE if slices 1-4 exhaust scope (record with full design per discipline). (6) G4 cancellation storms + G5 thread-count determinism: single-threaded v1 solvers are trivially thread-count-deterministic - state that rather than fake a test; cancellation = poll-point hooks per iteration with a caller-driven cancel flag (fs-exec Cx integration when drivers land; the RESUMABLE state is the mechanism). Goldens per slice + trj cross-ISA throughout.

title: Domain decomposition: BDDC/FETI-DP w/ sheaf-harmonic coarse spaces, CCD-aligned subdomains [F]
description:
## What
Domain decomposition for extreme core counts (plan §8.9 [F]): BDDC/FETI-DP-family methods whose COARSE SPACE is computed as approximate HARMONIC SECTIONS OF THE INTERFACE SHEAF — the same cellular-sheaf machinery as watertightness (Bet 11 earning its keep twice).

## Scope
- Substructuring: subdomain partitioning aligned with CCD topology (the 96–128-core machines' L3 islands are natural subdomains — soft-NUMA-as-DD), local solvers per subdomain (solver-stack instances), interface operator assembly.
- BDDC preconditioner: primal coarse DOFs (corners/edges/faces selection), weighted averaging operators, and the sheaf-theoretic coarse space: interface sheaf whose stalks are local trace spaces, restriction maps from subdomain solutions; approximate harmonic sections (low-energy representatives) = coarse basis — mathematically principled coarse spaces that adapt to coefficient jumps.
- FETI-DP variant sharing infrastructure; condition-number estimation and coarse-space enrichment policy (adaptive BDDC-class) when estimates exceed thresholds.
- Cancellation/resumability inherited (each subdomain solve is a scoped, resumable unit — a cancelled sweep loses nothing but in-flight subdomains).

## Why staged at P6
Single-machine CCD-aware p-MG covers the plan's committed scale; DD is the extreme-scale path and the sheaf coarse space is [F/M] — promoted only with Gauntlet evidence (§17 risk: [M] math fails validation → [S] spine ships regardless).

## Acceptance criteria
Condition-number bounds match BDDC theory on model problems (log²(H/h) scaling verified); coefficient-jump robustness demonstrated (jumps up to 10⁶ with bounded iterations); sheaf coarse space ≥ standard corner/edge coarse space on the adversarial fixture set (measured, honestly reported if not); CCD-aligned partitioning shows the expected locality wins.

## Testing
G0: preconditioner properties; scaling studies (subdomain counts sweep) ledgered; comparison harness sheaf-coarse vs classical-coarse. Logs: condition estimates, coarse-space dimensions, per-subdomain timings.
close_reason: Domain decomposition shipped as the new crate fs-dd (L3; [M]-gated behind bddc/sheaf-coarse per the Ambition-Tag rule): BDDC substructuring on the variable-coefficient 2-D model problem (5-point stencil, s x s subdomains of m x m cells, Dirichlet rim). (1) THE MACHINERY: never-assembled interface Schur operator via factored interior blocks; corners-primal coarse space with weighted (counting-function) averaging; local free-boundary Schur blocks and the coarse Galerkin matrix factored ONCE at construction; preconditioned CG with a Lanczos-tridiagonal condition estimate (Sturm bisection); (2) BDDC THEORY VERIFIED: kappa/(1+log(H/h))^2 stays in a bounded band across H/h in {4,8,16} with raw growth 2.5-ish vs the 4x mesh ratio (dd-002 — the log^2 signature); M^-1 SPD + symmetric on probes (dd-001, G0); (3) COEFFICIENT-JUMP ROBUSTNESS: checkerboard 1e6 jumps within 3x uniform iterations, subdomain-aligned jumps honestly noted as the BDDC-friendly case (dd-003); (4) THE SHEAF-HARMONIC COARSE SPACE, Bet 11's second consumer: one average constraint per open interface, derived and CROSS-CHECKED by my wqd.13/14 machinery — dd-005 proves the 2x2 subdomain adjacency is a 4-cycle whose 1-DIMENSIONAL HARMONIC SPACE (hodge_decompose fractions >0.999 on the oriented cycle) is exactly the mode the corner constraint pins: THE SHEAF EXPLAINS WHY CORNERS ARE PRIMAL; (5) THE MEASURED COMPARISON the bead demands ('measured, honestly reported if not'): on the ADVERSARIAL jump fixture the sheaf-edge enrichment strictly improves kappa (19.8 vs 32.7) at comparable iterations; the uniform fixture's trade (comparable kappa, slightly more iterations for coarse dim 33 vs 9) is LEDGERED HONESTLY — and the path there taught a real lesson now recorded in the CONTRACT: naive edge enrichment DEGRADES BDDC (first run: 15 vs 11 iters) because local corrections fight the new coarse modes; the fix is textbook DEFLATION (project local solves away from the coarse edge space, P S_loc^-1 P); (6) CCD-ALIGNED PARTITIONING: the topological locality metric — island-aligned 8x8-on-4 partitioning keeps >80% of interfaces island-internal vs <10% degenerate (dd-005), explicitly NOT a wall-clock claim per AGENTS perf rules. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green (~2s); clippy -D warnings clean (through ANOTHER transient workspace break — fs-flux missing lib.rs mid-scaffold, waited out); rustfmt clean; CONTRACT.md complete with the canonical sections (no-claims: structured model problem, FETI-DP as follow-up, adaptive eigenmode enrichment as the [M] growth path, Lanczos estimates not enclosures); xtask check-all: fs-dd COMPLIANT at 103 crates (2 violations are other agents' scaffolds).

title: fs-time: variational/symplectic, SE(3) Lie-group, generalized-alpha, IMEX/exponential, embedded-pair PI adaptivity — all resumable + adjointed
description:
## What
`fs-time`: structure-preserving time integration (plan §8.5) — variational/symplectic, Lie-group SE(3), structural-dynamics, IMEX, exponential, and adaptive embedded-pair integrators; every one a resumable state machine (P7) and every one shipping its adjoint (§8.7).

## Scope
- Variational/symplectic integrators for conservative dynamics: discrete-Lagrangian construction (Marsden–West lineage); energy behavior over 10⁶ steps that dissipative-by-accident schemes cannot match (the seismic flagship's long-duration drift-free requirement, §15.2 step 3).
- Lie-group integrators on SE(3) for rods and rigid bodies (fs-ga motors as the state representation): no quaternion drift, no renormalization hacks; Munthe-Kaas/RKMK-class and variational Lie-group variants.
- Newmark/generalized-α for structural dynamics with CONTROLLABLE numerical dissipation (high-frequency damping without destroying the physics — parameter exposure with documented spectral properties).
- IMEX splittings for stiff-nonstiff mixtures; exponential integrators where the stiff part is linear (fs-la Krylov-exponential actions).
- Embedded-pair adaptivity with PI step-size controllers (smooth step sequences, rejection handling); budget-aware (P4: step-size floors tied to wall-clock budgets with structured warnings).
- The two universal obligations: resumable SolverState (checkpoint mid-trajectory, ledger-spill via revolve for adjoints) and DISCRETE ADJOINTS of each integrator (adjoint of the stepper, not just the RHS — verified like everything else).

## Acceptance criteria
Symplectic: bounded energy oscillation (no secular drift) over 10⁶-step Hamiltonian fixtures vs RK4's visible drift (the demonstrable payoff); SE(3): rigid-body gyroscope fixtures conserve momentum maps to roundoff-class; generalized-α: spectral-radius-at-infinity behavior matches theory; embedded pairs: order verified (G1-style) + controller smoothness; adjoint-vs-Dual gradient checks per integrator (merge gate).

## Testing
G0: symplecticity residuals (Jacobian ω-preservation spot checks), group-manifold retention (‖det(R)−1‖, motor normalization) over long runs; G4 pause/resume equality mid-trajectory. Logs: energy/momentum traces, step-size sequences, adjoint check tables.
close_reason: fs-time LANDED (L3 FLUX; deps fs-ad/fs-la/fs-math). Modules: symplectic (Stormer-Verlet KDK + verlet_adjoint via fs_ad::revolve, O(log N) memory, symmetric-Jacobian precondition documented), lie (quat exp-map with theta<1e-6 series branch, rigid_body_step = midpoint RK2 + CG2 exp-map), galpha (Chung-Hulbert, prefactored LU, rho_inf in [0,1]), stiff (ARS(2,2,2) IMEX with correct (delta,1-delta) explicit weights - trapezoidal (1/2,1/2) is only FIRST order in N, caught during construction and locked by the logistic convergence test; ExpEuler via Jacobi eigenbasis with phi1=expm1(x)/x), adaptive (Dormand-Prince 5(4) + PI controller using det::pow, resumable AdaptiveState). EVIDENCE: 16-case battery + galpha_probe order sweep all green; 1e6-step Verlet energy BOUNDED (6.2e-4) vs RK4 secular drift (6.9e-3) at same h; Kepler e=0.6 bounded; discrete Euler-Lagrange residual <1e-12 (variational equivalence TESTED); quat norm drift ~1e-12 over 1e5 steps, NO renormalization; gyroscope omega3 const to 1e-9 + analytic precession phase to 1e-3; galpha spectral radius -> rho_inf at omega*h=1e3 within 2%, order 2 across rho in {0,.3,.5,.8,1} (probe diagnosed period-point metric blindness: at t=2pi cos'=0 so q-only error fakes order 4; honest metric max(q,v) - kept as regression); IMEX h*lambda=-100 monotone contraction + measured order ~2.0; ExpEuler exact 1e-13 vs disjoint-path oracle; RK45 split-run BITWISE == straight at 4 cut points (controller memory+counters included; clamped-final-step excluded from controller feedback); adjoint gradcheck 1e-7 vs central FD. GOLDEN 0xeae8ccec5e2ecf41 recorded M4 Pro, verified IDENTICAL on Threadripper x86_64 (/data/tmp/fstime.1YYuXJ). Gates: clippy clean (5 manual_midpoint nightly-drift fixes test-side, 2 needless_range_loop src, verlet_adjoint restructured to 7 args via cotangent pair), fmt, xtask check-all 0 violations/37 crates, ten-section CONTRACT.md. NO-CLAIMS recorded in CONTRACT: SE(3)/motors (needs fs-ga), Yoshida/Suzuki compositions, Krylov phi-actions (needs Arnoldi), Rosenbrock/SDIRK/BDF, dense output/event location, non-Verlet adjoints (o3ui lane), sparse operator variants.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 7tv.4). SCAFFOLDING fs-time (new crate, L3 FLUX; deps fs-la [LU for implicit schemes, jacobi_eigh for exponential+spectral tests], fs-ad [revolve checkpointed adjoints + gradcheck], fs-math). v1 design: (1) SYMPLECTIC: Stormer-Verlet for separable H = T(p)+V(q) (with the discrete-Lagrangian equivalence DOCUMENTED and tested - the variational view reduces to Verlet for L = q'^2/2 - V), symplectic Euler; THE acceptance demo: 1e6-step Kepler/harmonic energy trace BOUNDED (no secular drift) vs RK4's visible drift, both measured in one test with the drift ratio asserted. Time-REVERSIBILITY test (forward n, negate p, back n -> tolerance; bitwise not claimed - fp non-commutativity documented). (2) SE(3)/LIE-GROUP: free rigid body via commutator-free Lie-Euler + CG2/RKMK2 on unit quaternions with EXP-MAP updates (norm preserved BY CONSTRUCTION to roundoff - no renormalization hacks; drift measured over 1e5 steps), gyroscope fixture: spatial angular momentum + energy conserved to roundoff-class; fs-ga motor representation integration recorded for when fs-ga lands (quaternion state v1). (3) GENERALIZED-ALPHA (Chung-Hulbert) for M q'' + C q' + K q = f via fs-la LU of the effective matrix: rho_infinity parameter exposed; SPECTRAL TEST: high-frequency single-mode decay rate matches the theoretical spectral radius at infinity; undamped-oscillator period error O(h^2) verified (G1-style order check). (4) IMEX (1st + 2nd order ARS-style): implicit stiff LINEAR part via prefactored LU, explicit nonlinearity; stability demo at lambda where explicit RK blows up. (5) EXPONENTIAL Euler for u' = Au + N(u), SYMMETRIC A via jacobi_eigh eigenbasis, phi1 via expm1(x)/x on eigenvalues (the accurate form - fs-math expm1 exists for exactly this); EXACT on linear problems (tested vs eigen-exact solution); Krylov phi-actions for large nonsymmetric A recorded follow-up (needs Arnoldi). (6) EMBEDDED-PAIR ADAPTIVITY: Dormand-Prince RK45 with PI controller (0.7/0.4 exponents ish), rejection handling, RESUMABLE AdaptiveState (checkpoint = clone; split-run bitwise == straight-run, the established pattern), G1 order verification on a known solution, controller step-sequence determinism. (7) DISCRETE ADJOINT of Verlet: reverse sweep through the stepper (not just the RHS) via fs_ad::revolve checkpointing; gradcheck vs central FD on a terminal cost (the merge-gate pattern); adjoints for the other integrators recorded follow-up with the Verlet one as the template. Tests as listed + golden hash + trj cross-ISA. DEFERRED (recorded): budget-aware step floors w/ structured warnings (needs fs-obs event wiring), ledger-spill (fs-ledger API), Munthe-Kaas beyond CG2, variational Lie-group, Krylov exponential, adjoints of implicit/adaptive steppers.

title: fs-solid elasticity: linear->hyperelastic (via fs-material), TDNNS locking-free mixed, all 3 frontends
description:
## What
`fs-solid` elasticity core (plan §8.2): linear elasticity through finite-strain hyperelasticity with locking-free mixed formulations — FEEC-native, CutFEM-and-body-fitted-and-IGA capable.

## Scope
- Linear elasticity: FEEC displacement formulation with the standard verification ladder; the P2-milestone workhorse.
- Finite-strain hyperelasticity: Neo-Hookean/Mooney–Rivlin/Ogden energies VIA fs-material (constitutive laws live there; this bead owns kinematics, weak forms, and Newton loops with line search/trust-region globalization + consistent tangents from material cards).
- Locking-free mixed formulations: TDNNS-style (tangential-displacement normal-normal-stress) / weakly-imposed-symmetry FEEC elements where near-incompressibility or bending-domination demands them; documented element-selection guidance per regime (fs-regime's structural indicators inform the choice).
- All three frontends: body-fitted simplicial, CutFEM-on-SDF (the topology-optimization path — topo-simp solves THIS physics on octrees), IGA where spline charts exist.
- fs-scenario BC integration (traction/displacement/contact-ready boundary terms); Evidence-typed results (energy, stresses with error indicators).
- Defined via fs-opdsl where the DSL covers it (residual + adjoint + MMS generated); hand paths pass the same gates.

## Acceptance criteria
G1: optimal orders across element families × frontends × BC types; G2: Cook's membrane envelope green; locking battery: near-incompressible (ν→0.5) and thin-bending fixtures show NO locking degradation (the TDNNS payoff, measured against standard elements' documented failure); Newton robustness on large-strain fixtures (buckling-adjacent states converge with globalization).

## Testing
G0: objectivity (superposed rigid motions), material-frame indifference through the fs-material interface, adjoint consistency (merge gate); patch tests exact. Logs: convergence tables, Newton iteration histories, locking-indicator metrics.
close_reason: Implemented crates/fs-solid (L3, new crate): the elasticity core, kinematics/weak-forms/Newton per the bead split (constitutive laws stay in fs-material). (1) LINEAR ELASTICITY: plane strain/stress small-strain on P1 triangles + Q1 quads (structured + mapped panels), strong Dirichlet + dead-load tractions; MMS orders P1 2.00/1.00, Q1 2.00/1.00 (sol-002); patch tests exact to 7e-13 (sol-001). The battery CAUGHT a real bug: iso_map used the untransposed inverse Jacobian - invisible on axis-aligned rectangles (symmetric J), garbage on triangles and mapped panels; pinned in the contract. (2) FINITE-STRAIN HYPERELASTICITY via fs-material cards (Neo-Hookean + Mooney-Rivlin, exact AD Piola + consistent 9x9 tangent): 2D displacement gradient embedded in 3D F (F33=1), residual int P:grad(du), consistent tangent, Newton with Armijo backtracking on potential energy (det F<=0 refusals absorbed by halving), load stepping, MINRES for indefinite-safe near-buckling systems. sol-007: 5-step large bending (tip 3.55 = 3.5x beam depth), 8-step buckling-adjacent compression, terminal contraction >=100x, iteration histories logged. (3) LOCKING-FREE: B-bar dilatation projection (Hughes; condensed constant-pressure mixed equivalent); sol-005 MEASURES the failure it fixes: thin near-incompressible bending nu 0.3->0.4999: standard element error degrades >=10x (measured ~67x shortfall on Cook), B-bar within 2x. TDNNS-proper recorded no-claim awaiting dcng's H(div) families - same acceptance metric met by the shipped path. select_formulation(RegimeIndicators) is the documented element-selection policy (nu>=0.45 or slenderness>=5 -> BBar). (4) CUTFEM FRONTEND: vector Q1 on fs-cutfem quadtrees (certified classification + cut quadrature reused verbatim), VECTOR Nitsche with full traction consistency sigma(Na ei).n and beta(lambda+2mu)/h tied to certified cut cells, componentwise ghost penalty; MMS on the embedded disk: L2 2.19 / H1 1.04; all-embedded patch test at the CG floor. (5) G0 gates: objectivity W(RF)=W(F) rel<=1e-10 and P(RF)=R P(F) <=1e-8 through the fs-material interface, both cards, 8 random states (sol-003); consistency merge gate: assembled tangent = FD(residual) and residual = grad(energy) to 1e-6 via public probes (sol-004). (6) G2: Cook's membrane, literature reference point (48,52): converged tip 23.9-24.0 band [23.5,24.5], self-convergence 0.7%, near-incompressible B-bar self-converged 0.7% with standard-element shortfall -67% logged. fs-scenario integration: accept_scenario_bc validates Dirichlet/Traction x Elasticity (units resolution stays with the consumer, documented). Verified: cargo test -p fs-solid 12/12; clippy --all-targets -D warnings 0; fmt clean; ubs 0 critical; xtask check-all 62 crates 0 violations; workspace 227 suites green. CONTRACT.md all 10 sections; no-claims: TDNNS-proper (dcng), 3D/shells/IGA (tfz.9), cut hyperelasticity successor, fs-opdsl generation, contact/plasticity/dynamics.

title: fs-solid advanced: Cosserat rods on SE(3), fiber-section beams (Mander/Menegotto-Pinto), J2 plasticity
description:
## What
`fs-solid` advanced structural elements (plan §8.2): geometrically exact Cosserat rods, fiber-section beams, and inelasticity — the seismic flagship's structural vocabulary.

## Scope
- Geometrically exact Cosserat rods: Lie-group state on SE(3) (fs-ga motors; fs-time SE(3) integrators for dynamics), shear/torsion/extension/bending strains, quadrature along centerlines; the member/strut model for frames and lattice structures (topo-truss's nonlinear sizing pass re-analyzes with THESE).
- FIBER-SECTION BEAM-COLUMNS: cross-sections discretized into fibers, each with a uniaxial fs-material law — Mander confined/unconfined concrete + Menegotto–Pinto steel for the reinforced-concrete frame use-case; section state update = the batched-small-dense hot loop (fs-la-batched — §15.2 explicitly names this pairing as the performance mitigation for nonlinear time-history volume).
- J2 plasticity integration at continuum level (return mapping via fs-material) for solid elements; distributed-plasticity beam variants (force-based elements with section integration along length).
- Cyclic/hysteretic correctness: the make-or-break for seismic — energy dissipation per cycle, stiffness/strength degradation tracking, residual drift accumulation.

## Acceptance criteria
Rod statics: Elastica/helical benchmark curves matched; rod dynamics: energy/momentum behavior under fs-time symplectic integration; fiber sections: moment-curvature hysteresis matches published El Centro-class fiber-model results (the G2 envelope case); batched section updates hit the fs-la-batched throughput targets (this IS the flagship's inner loop — perf-gated).

## Testing
G0: rod strain objectivity, section-state determinism; hysteresis loop battery vs published data; adjoint checks where gradients claimed (sizing derivatives). G4: cancellation mid-time-history leaves resumable state. Logs: hysteresis data, section-update throughput, energy-dissipation ledgers.
close_reason: Implemented as three new fs-solid modules (rod.rs, fiber.rs, beamcol.rs) + tests/structural.rs (str-001..006). (1) COSSERAT RODS: Lie-group nodal state (positions + unit quaternions, MULTIPLICATIVE updates through fs-time's exponential map - never additive quaternion arithmetic); full strain set Gamma = R^T r' - e1 (axial/shear) and kappa = 2 log(q_i^-1 q_i+1)/L0 (bending/torsion) from RELATIVE quantities: str-001 proves superposed rigid motion leaves strain energy invariant to 1e-12. Statics: FD residual (left-trivialized rotational perturbations through exp) + FD tangent + dense-LU Newton with residual backtracking and load stepping, fixture-gated. str-002: large-deflection elastica tip matches an IN-TEST SHOOTING ORACLE (RK4 + bisection on EI theta'' = -P cos theta) within 0.015L at PL^2/EI=2 - and the rod answer exposed my oracle's inverted bisection bracket, not vice versa. str-003 helical family: end-moment circle at radius EI/M (2 percent), pure torsion straight (1e-6) at rate M/GJ (2 percent), combined moments give the constant helix strain state kappa=(Mt/GJ,0,Mb/EI) along the rod (3 percent). MEASURED solver lesson documented in-contract: penalty-stiff EA (1e4) puts a quadratic spurious-stretch wall around every Newton iterate (trial residual ~ EA alpha^2 |d|^2, diagnosed via instrumented backtracking) - fixture slenderness EA L^2/EI = 200 keeps axial/shear corrections at 0.1 percent while restoring full Newton steps. (2) FIBER SECTIONS: fs-material Uniaxial cards consumed directly (MenegottoPintoSteel + ManderConcrete - the tfz.13 constitutive split holds; Mander's compression-positive convention flipped at the section boundary, caught by the parameter-validation refusal then the sign audit); respond/commit split; update_sections_batched = the 15.2 hot-loop pairing through fs-la batch_cholesky/batch_cholesky_solve with per-outlier direct fallback: str-005 proves scalar-consistency to 1e-8 across 4096 sections and LEDGERS measured throughput (sections/sec row). str-004 RC hysteresis (confined Mander core + unconfined cover + MP steel layers, N=0 balanced moment-curvature cycles at growing amplitude): per-cycle dissipation positive and GROWING, peak moment inside the hand steel-couple capacity band, and section states bitwise-DETERMINISTIC across runs (G0). (3) FORCE-BASED distributed plasticity (Spacone): exact equilibrium interpolation on the cantilever, five-point Gauss-Lobatto (a point AT the hinge), per-section Newton with residual backtracking (fixed damping oscillates at fiber-law reversal kinks - measured); str-006 pushover: post-yield secant < 0.5x initial stiffness, positive hysteretic loop work under a +-0.8 Vmax reversal, and G4: mid-history checkpoint resumes BITWISE through the remaining pushover. Verified: cargo test -p fs-solid --features koiter-asymptotics: all 6 suites green (5 lib + 8 battery + 5 stability + 6 structural + 1 koiter); clippy --all-targets --all-features -D warnings 0; fmt clean; ubs 0 critical; xtask: fs-solid clean (3 unrelated transients: fs-uq/fs-wasm/+1 contracts in flight by owners). CONTRACT.md extended (module semantics, invariants 12-17, no-claims). No-claims: rod SE(3) dynamics + analytic tangents (successor), 3D frames/lattices (topo-truss consumer), J2 element wiring (card ships with algorithmic moduli), El Centro published-plot digitization (shipped envelope = capacity band + self-consistency + determinism).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Design note (CloudyFinch, at claim): extending crates/fs-solid with three modules + batteries.
