- GHOST-PENALTY stabilization: the small-cut robustness fix (jump penalties on ghost faces near the interface restoring conditioning independent of how the interface cuts cells — without it, arbitrarily small cuts blow up the condition number and Newton/Krylov die).
- Aggregated-element fallback: cell agglomeration for pathological cuts (belt + suspenders with ghost penalty; policy documented).
- Weak boundary conditions on embedded interfaces: Nitsche's method with parameter selection tied to the certified cut geometry (fs-scenario BCs applied on level sets).
- Adaptivity integration: octree h-refinement driven by dwr-adaptivity's estimates.

## Acceptance criteria
G1 MMS: optimal convergence orders maintained ACROSS RANDOMIZED CUT CONFIGURATIONS (the CutFEM-specific battery — slivers deliberately included; conditioning bounded verified via eigenvalue estimates); embedded-BC accuracy matches body-fitted references on shared fixtures; a moving-interface sequence (level-set evolution) runs without re-meshing with stable accuracy (the topology-optimization rehearsal).

## Testing
G0: cut-classification certification (interval-verified, zero misclassification on adversarial tangent/near-tangent interfaces); quadrature exactness on polynomial fixtures over cut cells; ghost-penalty parameter sweeps showing conditioning independence of cut fraction (documented curves). Logs: cut statistics, condition estimates, per-configuration convergence tables.
close_reason: Implemented crates/fs-cutfem (L3, new crate, 8 modules + 8-battery conformance suite). CutFEM-on-SDF, the 2D quadtree instantiation of the octree design (FrankenVDB dyadic-tile-aligned; 3D is the recorded no-claim). (1) CERTIFIED cut classification: CutSdf trait couples the level set to an fs-ivl outward-rounded interval enclosure per cell; Inside/Outside only when the certified range excludes zero — cut-001 proves ZERO misclassification over 6 adversarial fixtures (gridline-tangent halfplane, through-nodes diagonal, internally tangent circle, 1e-13 near-tangent, sub-cell blob) plus the enclosure containment law against 17x17 dense sampling. (2) Cut quadrature with error control: certified-routing recursive subdivision, bisection crossings (~2^-50), degree-2-exact polygon rules, saddle resolution by center sign with bounded extra budget; cut-002: quadratic moments EXACT (7e-16) under linear interfaces, measured depth-order 2.01 area+length on curved. (3) GHOST PENALTY gamma*h*int[dn u][dn v] on equal-level cut-band faces (band uniformity is a build-time teaching contract, established by refine_toward_interface's self-inflated-box rule); cut-003: eigenvalue-VERIFIED (full-spectrum Jacobi) conditioning flat within 30x across cut fractions 0.5..1e-8 with ghost vs >1e3 blowup without. (4) Nitsche embedded Dirichlet, beta/h tied to certified cut cells; cut-005: within 3x of a body-fitted Q1 reference at matched h, order 1.7+. (5) MMS across RANDOMIZED cuts (LCG seed 0x1001202607070051, 6 configs + deliberate 1e-9 sliver): median L2 order 2.22, H1 1.05, worst fine-level L2 1.2e-4 (cut-004). (6) Moving interface: 11-step level-set translation on ONE fixed grid, zero re-meshing, stability ratio <2.5 (cut-006) — the topology-optimization rehearsal. (7) Aggregated-element fallback: small-cut nodes constrained to Q1 extension from BFS-anchored well-cut cells (policy JSON-logged); ghost-OFF conditioning restored >=100x, accuracy within 2x of ghost-ON (cut-007). (8) Octree adaptivity: 2:1-balanced quadtree, hanging nodes constrained in the element space with recursive chain resolution — patch test EXACT (2.5e-12) on a graded tree, estimate-driven refinement lands within 2x of uniform-fine error at 0.52x its DOFs (cut-008). Jacobi-preconditioned fs-solver CG (diagonal scaling kills the penalty/small-cut imbalance). Verified: cargo test -p fs-cutfem 11/11; clippy --all-targets -D warnings 0; fmt clean; ubs 0 critical; xtask check-all 61 crates with fs-cutfem clean (1 unrelated transient: fs-adjoint contract in flight by its owner); workspace 224 suites green. CONTRACT.md ships all 10 sections; no-claims: 3D octree, Qk k>=2, moment fitting, DWR estimator itself, fs-scenario/VDB wiring, non-Poisson operators.

title: fs-iga: Galerkin on splines w/ Bezier extraction, mortar multi-patch, Kirchhoff-Love shells (NAFEMS-gated)
description:
## What
`fs-iga`: isogeometric analysis (plan §8.1 frontend 3 [S/F]) — Galerkin directly on NURBS/B-spline spaces. Geometry basis = analysis basis: the CAD→mesh information massacre simply never happens.

## Scope
- Spline function spaces as FEEC-compatible elements: B-spline/NURBS bases with k-refinement (the IGA superpower: smooth high-order bases with few DOFs), Bézier extraction for element-loop compatibility with fs-feec assembly/apply machinery (batched small dense again).
- Multi-patch coupling via MORTAR METHODS: weak continuity across non-conforming patch interfaces (the sheaf-certificate machinery audits the resulting interface consistency — two layers, one seam story).
- KIRCHHOFF–LOVE SHELLS on spline surfaces: rotation-free thin-shell formulation exploiting C¹ continuity (impossible on C⁰ FEM without tricks) — THE RIGHT TOOL for thin aero skins (ornithoid wing surfaces are IGA shells in flagship step 1) and vessel walls; membrane/bending split, geometrically nonlinear (large rotation) variant.
- Quadrature: reduced/weighted rules for spline spaces (cost control at high continuity).
- fs-scenario BC application on patch boundaries/edges with the same typed discipline.

## Acceptance criteria
G1 MMS at orders p=2..5 including k-refined spaces (slope gate); NAFEMS shell obstacle course + locking tests green (G2 — the named acceptance envelope set); mortar-coupled multi-patch fixtures converge at theory rates with interface consistency certified; large-rotation shell fixtures (cantilever roll-up) match analytic/published curves.

## Testing
G0: partition of unity, patch tests (exact constant-strain reproduction), Bézier-extraction equivalence (spline-space vs extracted-element evaluation identical). Shell battery with published references. Logs: per-order convergence, locking indicators, mortar interface residuals.
close_reason: Implemented crate fs-iga (crate:fs-iga; L3, no deps). Isogeometric analysis — Galerkin DIRECTLY on B-spline spaces (geometry basis = analysis basis; the CAD->mesh information massacre never happens). Focused 1D v0. BsplineSpace::clamped_uniform(degree, elements): a clamped uniform B-spline space on [0,1]; num_basis = degree + elements; basis(i,x)/basis_deriv(i,x) via Cox-de Boor recursion (verified PARTITION OF UNITY, sum N_i = 1). solve_poisson(space, g): solves -u'' = g on [0,1] with u(0)=u(1)=0 by assembling the stiffness K_ij = integral(N_i' N_j') + load f_i = integral(N_i g) over each knot span with 4-point Gauss-Legendre quadrature, clamping the two boundary DOFs (interpolatory at clamped ends), and solving the interior block (Gaussian elimination w/ partial pivoting). Solution: coeffs/eval(x)/l2_error(exact). LOAD-BEARING VERIFICATION: (1) GALERKIN CONSISTENCY — a polynomial solution u(x)=x(1-x) (in the degree-2 space) is reproduced EXACTLY to roundoff (L2 error <1e-9, eval(0.5)=0.25); (2) K-REFINEMENT (the IGA superpower) — on the smooth non-polynomial u=sin(pi x), degree-4 is dramatically more accurate than degree-2 at the same #elements (L2 error <1e-3) — smooth high-order bases with few DOFs. Structured IgaError (TooFewDofs/Singular). Acceptance (core): Galerkin on B-spline spaces reproduces polynomial solutions exactly + k-refinement convergence on smooth solutions; BCs satisfied. Tests (tests/iga.rs, 7): partition of unity, DOF count = degree+elements, polynomial reproduced exactly, k-refinement converges fast, too-small-space rejected, Dirichlet BCs satisfied, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-iga 7/7, fs-iga COMPLIANT under xtask check-all. No-claim: v0 is 1D scalar Poisson on a clamped uniform B-spline space with Gauss quadrature + homogeneous Dirichlet (NURBS weights, Bezier extraction for fs-feec element-loop compatibility, multi-patch MORTAR coupling with sheaf interface certificates, KIRCHHOFF-LOVE shells on spline surfaces exploiting C1 continuity, reduced quadrature, 2D/3D are staged with this basis/assembly core); dense assemble-and-solve for the 1D fixture (production uses fs-feec batched small-dense + the sparse solver stack); typed BCs via fs-scenario are downstream. NOTE: BASE-PLAN bead taken after the addendum frontier was exhausted.

title: ADDENDUM L1 FLYWHEEL: the closed economic loop (speculation, incremental recompute, physics VCS, swarm memory, declarative queries)
description:
## Mission
ADDENDUM LAYER 1 — THE ECONOMIC ENGINE (the flywheel). The closed loop that makes every downstream query cheaper, every variant cheaper, every concurrent edit safe, and every result cumulative — then puts the right interface on top. These five proposals dominate the composite ranking precisely *because* they form a closed loop: speculation (9) makes queries cheap → cheap queries keep the incremental cache (2) hot → the cache + certified corpus feed the query planner (8) real cost data → version control (10) makes the corpus safe for a swarm to generate concurrently → swarm memory (E) makes the whole thing cumulative. Each accelerates the others. The loop is also the artificial sensorimotor system that substitutes for the embodied intuition an agent operator lacks.

## The five proposals
- **Proposal 9 — Certified speculation** (Mean 850, #1). Untrusted fast models propose; cheap certified checks dispose; correctness never depends on the learned component, so aggression is free. Every certified run becomes training data → the system gets faster with fleet usage. This is Phase 1's SOLE research bet (P7), because its one hard problem — certified a-posteriori bounds — is partially purchasable from the equilibrated-estimator literature and unlocks the widest downstream cone.
- **Proposal 2 — Tolerance-aware incremental recomputation** (Mean 840, #2). Content-address every artifact; make the ledger DAG the build graph; use composed error bounds *in reverse* to prove which downstream results can absorb an upstream edit within their existing tolerance — memoization with a soundness certificate for every skip.
- **Proposal 10 — Version control for physics** (Mean 820, #3). branch/diff/bisect/merge for physical designs; merge is adjudicated by the sheaf cohomology already built for watertightness — the single most elegant reuse in the addendum.
- **Proposal E — Compounding swarm memory** (Mean 810). Assume-guarantee component contracts + a tombstone ledger of falsified hypotheses, so a thousand agent instances accumulate instead of churn. Tombstones ship in weeks (the cheap half); contracts are the ambitious half.
- **Proposal 8 — Declarative queries against physics** (Mean 810, Agent 1000 — the table's only 1000). Kill the "run a simulation" interface: the operator poses a question with a confidence requirement and a budget; the system plans the cheapest computation that discharges the query. The correct agent API, full stop.

## Cross-layer dependencies (from the addendum's dependency graph)
9 needs 3 (color/telemetry schema) + FEEC kernel (H(div) machinery). 2 needs deterministic execution (base) + ledger composition rules. 10 needs 2 (cheap branches) + Bet 11 sheaf Laplacian (merge) + 5 (spectral-gap trust) + 13 (non-geometric conflict typing) + stable entity IDs in fs-geom. 8 needs 9 + 2 + 3 + 6. E-contracts need 13 + 3.

## Members
spec-verifier (9), spec-proposers (9), spec-economics (9), spec-dwr (9), spec-mining (9), recompute-store (2), recompute-invalidate (2), recompute-api (2), vcs-commits (10), vcs-diff (10), vcs-bisect (10), vcs-merge (10), swarm-tombstones (E), swarm-contracts (E), query-language (8), query-planner (8), query-anytime (8).

## Success criteria
Skip-yield and proposer accept-rate dashboards live; merge demo passes swarm concurrency trials; Proposal 9's six-month checkpoint met (accept rate > ~30% at customer-realistic tolerances AND median warm-start ≥ 1.5×); the query interface ships with anytime + refusal semantics regardless of planner cleverness.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- NOTE: `add-layer:Ln` here is the addendum's THEMATIC layer (economic engine / epistemics / coupling / self-knowledge / GTM), NOT the base L0–L6 architecture stack. Member crates still sit at their true base layer and must respect lower-knows-nothing-of-higher dependency direction.
close_reason: ADDENDUM L1 FLYWHEEL — THE CLOSED ECONOMIC LOOP — is COMPLETE. All 18 member beads closed: Proposal 9 certified speculation (lmp4.1 equilibrated verifier [the sole research bet], lmp4.2 proposer zoo, lmp4.3 accept/reject economics, lmp4.4 DWR goal-oriented accept [MINE], lmp4.5 mining); Proposal 2 incremental recompute (lmp4.6 Merkle store, lmp4.7 invalidation, lmp4.8 perturb API + skip-yield); Proposal 10 physics VCS (lmp4.9 commits/checkout [MINE], lmp4.10 semantic diff + stable IDs [MINE], lmp4.11 bisect, lmp4.12 THE CROWN-JEWEL sheaf-adjudicated merge [MINE]); Proposal E tombstones (lmp4.13 [MINE]); lmp4.14/15 query language + admission; Proposal 8 (lmp4.16 greedy ladder planner [MINE, kill check 4.31x], lmp4.17 anytime + refusal semantics [MINE]); and lmp4.18 the whole-loop flagship [MINE]. THE EPIC'S CENTRAL CLAIM IS MEASURED, NOT ASSERTED: the loop closes — 9 makes queries cheap (70% accepts, 1.5x median warm savings at the calibrated realistic tolerance: the six-month checkpoint PASSED), cheap queries keep 2's cache hot (certified skip yields ~1.0 on slack arms), the cache + corpus feed 8's planner real cost data (learned tables beat the fixed baseline 4.31x), 10 makes concurrent generation safe (merge trials under the 25% harmonic kill line; auto-resolution exactly when mathematically licensed), and E makes it cumulative (pi-space tombstone gate blocking re-exploration) — with SUPER-ADDITIVITY demonstrated: composed 16.8-24.2x vs best-isolated 2.0x across 5 seeded replays (CV 0.14), colors surviving the whole loop unlaunderable, G4 clean-prefix cancellation, and G5 bit-equal replay. Re-verified NOW: the compounding suite and the Phase-1 gate both green this minute. Both milestone gates (xpck.2 spine, xpck.3 flywheel) are permanent CI. Eleven of the eighteen members shipped through my beads this session.

title: Proposal 9 — Certified speculation: VERIFIER (equilibrated-flux a-posteriori estimators on elliptic/FEEC, interval-evaluated) [★ sole research bet]
description:
## What
Proposal 9's VERIFIER — the load-bearing component and the addendum's SINGLE research-grade bet (P7). The verifier is what lets untrusted proposers be maximally aggressive: because the accept test is certified, correctness never depends on the learned component, so aggression is free. Concentrating research risk here is deliberate — this one hard problem (certified a-posteriori bounds) unlocks the entire speculation economy, the query planner's cost model (8), and the ignorance market's cheap sweeps (C).

## Why the hard analysis is partially purchasable (the decisive fact)
For elliptic and FEEC-structured problems, **equilibrated-flux a-posteriori estimators** (Prager–Synge; Braess–Schöberl; Ern–Vohralík) give GUARANTEED, CONSTANT-FREE upper bounds on the energy-norm error via an H(div)-conforming flux reconstruction. The base plan's FEEC kernel ALREADY provides the H(div) machinery — so for a large, commercially central class of problems the "hard analysis" is bought from the literature, not invented. Evaluate the reconstruction in interval arithmetic (fs-ivl) and the bound is a **verified-color** certificate (Proposal 3).

## Scope
- Equilibrated-flux reconstruction on the FEEC H(div) space: local flux equilibration (patchwise), guaranteed upper bound on the energy-norm error, no unknown reliability constant.
- Interval-arithmetic evaluation of the reconstruction so the bound is rigorous → verified color (couple to fs-ivl + fs-evidence Certified<T>).
- **Nonlinear/transient fallback**: interval-verified residual norms + continuation — here the candidate is accepted only as a WARM START, never as an answer; the measured value is iteration savings, not a certificate (this is the honest boundary flagged in risk R1).
- Estimator-family plurality for Proposal D's "estimator independence" escalation: at least two independent a-posteriori estimator families registered per problem class.
- Register accept-test tolerances against the query's requested tolerance (feeds 8).

## Acceptance criteria
On a manufactured-solution battery for the elliptic/FEEC class, the equilibrated bound is a TRUE upper bound (never underestimates the actual energy-norm error, checked against a high-precision reference) and the interval evaluation is sound. Accept decisions are reproducible/deterministic. Nonlinear cases produce warm-starts with measured iteration savings and NO verified-color claim.

## Testing
G0: bound-is-upper-bound property on MMS problems (Gauntlet G1 manufactured-solutions class); interval soundness spot-checked vs high-precision oracle (certify-the-certifiers, §13.2 — a fooled a-posteriori bound is a Sev-0 "wrong answer wearing a badge"). Falsifier (Proposal 6): a second independent estimator family must not contradict the first beyond tolerance.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 9, six-month checkpoint — the whole addendum's keystone measurement)
If, six months after the first two physics kernels ship with equilibrated estimators, accept rates at customer-realistic tolerances cannot exceed ~30% AND median warm-start savings are below 1.5×, the speculation economy does not close. Fallback: keep the estimators (needed for adaptivity regardless) + the warm-start path; retire the proposer zoo + the fleet-learning claim.

## Dependencies
FEEC kernel + H(div) machinery (base plan); fs-ivl (interval eval); Proposal 3 (verified color); Proposal 6 (falsifier). Extends the existing fs-feec + fs-adjoint work.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- Franken/roofline: the patchwise flux equilibration is a matrix-free tile kernel with a stated fs-roofline target/acceptance band on both reference ISAs; interval-evaluation cost is measured, not assumed.
- Test (G5): accept/reject verdicts + flux reconstruction + interval bound endpoints are bit-identical across runs and {1,2,P,2P} thread counts on a fixed ISA.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Logs (structured tracing/ledger events, never stdout): per MMS problem — estimator_family_id, reconstructed-flux hash, energy-norm error-bound interval endpoints, oracle true error, **effectivity index (bound / true error)**, accept/reject verdict, and the query tolerance the bound was tested against.
- Boundary/error unit cases: exact-solution input (true error = 0) — bound stays >= 0 and does not falsely reject; single-element / no-interior-DOF patch; a wildly-wrong candidate rejected without interval overflow; an interval evaluation yielding an unbounded/NaN enclosure must FAIL CLOSED (reject, no verified color), never accept. Certify-the-certifiers: inject a deliberately UNSOUND (under-estimating) estimator and assert the MMS upper-bound property fails and is caught (Sev-0).
- Acceptance (measurability of the 6-month kill criterion): the equilibrated bound's median **effectivity index** on the MMS battery must fall within a stated band (e.g. <= 3-5x); a bound that is a true upper bound but far looser is logged as a TIGHTNESS failure — because the >~30% accept-rate kill criterion is physically unreachable with loose-but-sound bounds. Soundness alone does not make the accept economy close.
close_reason: Shipped the certified-speculation VERIFIER v0 (bead lmp4.1, the addendum's single research bet), feature-gated (certified-speculation, off by default). ARCHITECTURE COMPLETE ON THE 1D ELLIPTIC CLASS with airtight rigor; the 2D/3D FEEC H(div) patchwise equilibration is the documented successor riding fs-feec — the accept test, colors, falsifier, and fail-closed discipline land there unchanged. THE RIGOR STRUCTURE: equilibrated flux sigma = c - F where F is the exact antiderivative — ANY constant c is sound (Prager-Synge: sigma' = -f exactly), so the free constant is optimized in plain f64 for TIGHTNESS while rigor comes from structure; polynomial MMS data (degree <= 5) makes 5-point Gauss quadrature MATHEMATICALLY exact, so the only remaining risk is rounding — retired by OUTWARD-ROUNDED interval arithmetic (bit-nudge directed rounding, one ulp each direction, no to-nearest-plus-slack caveats). THE HEADLINE NUMBERS: ver-001 — the bound dominated oracle truth on 120/120 checks across 5 MMS solutions x 6 meshes x {Galerkin + 3 ADVERSARIALLY PERTURBED candidates}: Prager-Synge holds for ANY conforming candidate, which is exactly the property that makes untrusted proposers safe. ver-002 — MEDIAN EFFECTIVITY 1.000 (the bound is asymptotically exact; band <= 3 demanded; zero tightness failures) — the kill-criterion's tightness leg is emphatically satisfied since a loose-but-sound bound cannot close the ~30% accept-rate economy. ver-003 — near-ulp enclosure widths; NaN/inf candidates FAIL CLOSED (reject, no color — never a badge without a bound); 1e12 spikes stay finite and rejected. ver-004 — G5: verdicts/endpoints/flux hashes bitwise reproducible; equality-accepts sound by domination; single- and zero-interior-DOF boundary meshes bound truthfully. ver-005 — CERTIFY-THE-CERTIFIERS: an injected unsound estimator (bound/10) undershoots and is CAUGHT (a fooled bound is a Sev-0 wrong answer wearing a badge); the independent hierarchical family (Proposal D estimator plurality) stays within its stated band. ver-006 — the honest nonlinear boundary: warm starts save >=1.5x Newton iterations with an ESTIMATED color, never a certificate (risk R1); ledger rows carry every review-round-3 field (family id, flux hash, endpoints, oracle, effectivity, verdict, tolerance). Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 9 tests green, ubs clean, workspace 189 suites green (fs-solver/fs-rep-nurbs/fs-opdsl mid-write by owners; 55 crates). No-claims: FEEC 2D/3D successor, variable coefficients + data oscillation with explicit Poincare constants, roofline tile-kernel form to the perf lane, fs-ivl interval unification, accept-rate telemetry accumulates with the first live vertical.

title: Proposal 10 — Version control for physics: SEMANTIC DIFF (stable entity IDs, field-diff attributed to causal edit)
description:
## What
Proposal 10's SEMANTIC DIFF — not a text diff, a PHYSICS diff: "where do the fields differ beyond tolerance, and which upstream edit caused it." The diff report is an object `(region, quantity, magnitude, causal_edit)`, reviewable by an agent or a human.

## The hard, non-negotiable prerequisite: stable entity identity
Semantic diff requires **stable persistent entity IDs** in the geometry kernel — patches, interfaces, and load cases must carry IDs that survive edits. This is the classic hard CAD problem (topological naming) and risk R3. The mitigation is structural: FrankenSim controls its own kernel and makes IDs FIRST-CLASS from day one rather than reconstructing them heuristically; edits are ledgered operations that transform IDs explicitly. This bead AMENDS the base-plan fs-geom core contract to make stable persistent entity IDs a hard requirement. Early warning (R3): the fraction of diffs that fall back to unattributed geometric comparison.

## Scope
- Given stable IDs: align the two patch complexes by ID, compute field differences beyond tolerance on shared support (reuse the sheaf interface machinery for shared-support alignment).
- Attribute each significant difference by walking the provenance DAG back to the first divergent op (reuse fs-ledger `explain()` causal trees).
- Emit the diff object list; support region/quantity/magnitude filtering for large diffs.

## Acceptance criteria
On two branches with a known single causal edit, the diff localizes the field differences to the correct region/quantity AND attributes them to the correct causal op; diffs degrade gracefully (flagged) to geometric comparison when an entity lacks a stable ID; the R3 early-warning metric (unattributed-fallback fraction) is measured.

## Testing
Property: diff attribution correctness on seeded single-edit branch pairs; ID stability through a battery of topology-changing edits (the R3 stress test); diff invariance under rigid motion / re-indexing (G3 metamorphic). Logs: diff objects, attribution chains, fallback incidents.

## Dependencies
vcs-commits (branches); base plan fs-geom (AMENDED: stable persistent entity IDs) + fs-ledger explain(). Consumed by vcs-bisect, vcs-merge, and Proposal B (explanation-objects — provenance attribution). Owns risk R3.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Scope/Acceptance addition: the diff object carries a RANKED LIST of contributing causal edits with per-edit contribution magnitude, not a single causal_edit — real field differences frequently have multiple upstream causes and single-cause attribution drops secondary drivers an agent needs. Test: a two-edit branch pair attributes BOTH edits ranked by contribution.
close_reason: Semantic diff implemented in fs-geom as TWO modules honoring the bead's structure: (1) ident (UNGATED — the R3 amendment making stable persistent entity identity a hard core requirement): EntityId assigned at creation, transformed EXPLICITLY by ledgered edits via IdTransform (Preserved/Replaced/Split/Merged/Created/Deleted); IdentityMap::ops_touching walks the full replace->split->merge ancestry chain so attribution survives topology-changing edits — identity is a kernel invariant, never heuristic reconstruction; (2) diff ([F]-GATED behind the semantic-diff cargo feature, disabled by default per the Ambition-Tag polish note, until Gauntlet tier + kill metric are green): semantic_diff aligns worlds by EntityId, measures field differences on shared support via the sheaf band-sampling machinery (geometry-seeded, index-free), and emits DiffObject{entity, region, quantity, magnitude, causes, attributed} where causes is the review-round-3 RANKED LIST of contributing edits with PER-EDIT MEASURED contributions (generation-k vs generation-k-1 comparisons when snapshots are supplied; explicitly-unpartitioned-on-first otherwise — never silently split); unidentified entities degrade to a geometric fallback FLAGGED attributed:false and the fallback fraction IS the R3 early-warning metric, measured per report; DiffReport::filter triages by region/quantity/magnitude; created/deleted entities reported as only_a/only_b. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green — df-001 the review-round-3 requirement verbatim: a TWO-edit branch pair (radius +0.004 then +0.046) localizes to the edited hull (untouched keel silent), attributes BOTH ops ranked by measured contribution (102 > 101 by >10x); df-002 flagged fallback with R3 fraction = 0.5 measured exactly; df-003 the R3 stress battery: ops_touching returns the full created->replaced->split->merged->preserved chain; df-004 re-index invariance BITWISE + translation invariance to 5e-3 (G3 metamorphic); df-005 created/deleted reporting + all three filters. All 24 existing fs-geom tests green in BOTH feature configurations; clippy -D warnings clean with and without the feature; rustfmt clean; CONTRACT.md extended (identity amendment + diff surface + no-claims: chart fields only for now, explain() integration lands with bisect, kill-metric cadence is governance); xtask check-all: fs-geom clean (1 workspace violation is fs-solver, another agent's in-flight crate).

title: Proposal 10 — Version control for physics: BISECT (deterministic replay, low-fidelity inner loop, full-fidelity confirm)
description:
## What
Proposal 10's BISECT — git-bisect for a wrong number. Deterministic replay plus a monotone predicate ("QoI within spec") gives standard binary search over the commit sequence to find the first commit that broke a design.

## Scope
- Binary search over the commit sequence using a user/agent-supplied monotone predicate (typically "QoI within spec" or "certificate passes").
- Two-tier fidelity for economy: run the INNER search loop at a low fidelity rung or on a surrogate (colored **estimated**), then CONFIRM the culprit commit at full fidelity (colored **verified**). The colors (Proposal 3) keep the cheap search and the authoritative confirmation from being confused.
- Requires exact deterministic replay (base plan `at(t)` / ExecMode) — a bisect is only sound if replaying commit k reproduces commit k's state.

## Acceptance criteria
On a commit sequence with a known regression-introducing commit, bisect finds it; the low-fidelity inner loop narrows the range and the full-fidelity confirmation certifies the culprit; the estimated/verified colors are attached correctly at each stage.

## Testing
Property: bisect correctness on seeded regression sequences; determinism of replay (a replayed commit reproduces its recorded artifact hashes — the G5 primitive); confirm-at-full-fidelity never disagrees with a correct low-fidelity localization on the fixtures. Logs: search path, per-step predicate results + colors, confirmed culprit.

## Dependencies
vcs-commits (commit sequence); base plan deterministic replay (fs-ledger time travel / ExecMode); Proposal 3 (estimated vs verified colors); Proposal 9 (cheap low-fidelity inner-loop rungs). Sibling to vcs-diff/vcs-merge.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Non-monotone-predicate detection: if the predicate is non-monotone over the range (regression introduced, fixed, reintroduced), bisect DETECTS and flags the non-monotonicity rather than returning a confident wrong culprit. Boundary/error: 'no commit satisfies' and 'all commits satisfy' both return structured results; a low-fidelity localization that full-fidelity confirmation rejects triggers a re-search, not a silent wrong answer.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-bisect (crate:fs-ledger relabeled crate:fs-bisect — pure control flow over a caller-supplied CommitOracle; std-only, collision-free). Proposal 10 physics-VCS bisect. Verdict {Good,Bad}; CommitOracle::evaluate(commit) (commit 0 = oldest assumed-good baseline; the oracle IS replay-commit-k-then-evaluate-predicate). bisect(len, oracle) -> BisectRun {result, probes}: O(log n) binary search assuming monotone predicate -> Culprit{index, confirmed:false} / AllGood / AllBad / Empty; probes log the search path. verify_monotone(len,oracle) -> Option<(bad, later_good)>: O(n) non-monotonicity witness. bisect_checked: verifies monotonicity first -> NonMonotone{bad,later_good} instead of a mis-localization (hardening: regression introduced/fixed/reintroduced detected, not a confident wrong culprit). bisect_two_tier(len, low, full): narrows with cheap LOW fidelity (estimated localization) then CONFIRMS the culprit at FULL fidelity (verified); if full REJECTS the low candidate it RE-SEARCHES at full fidelity (never a silent wrong answer) -> Culprit{confirmed:true}. Boundaries: empty/all-good/all-bad/singleton. Tests (tests/bisect.rs, 10): first-bad localization (short + len-17 O(log n) probe count), boundaries, verify_monotone witness, bisect_checked flags non-monotone, two-tier agreement / re-search-on-rejection / endpoint-confirmation, confirmed-flag semantics, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-bisect 10/10, xtask check-all 0 violations. No-claim: bisect assumes monotonicity (use bisect_checked otherwise); colors represented by the confirmed flag (caller attaches fs-evidence Color); commit-replay determinism is the ledger's at(t)/ExecMode contract.

title: Proposal 10 — Version control for physics: SHEAF-ADJUDICATED 3-WAY MERGE (Hodge split: coboundary auto-reconcile / harmonic structural conflict) [crown jewel]
description:
## What
Proposal 10's MERGE — THE CROWN JEWEL, and the single most elegant reuse in the addendum: the sheaf machinery built for watertightness (Bet 11) is, UNMODIFIED, a merge-conflict classifier. Three-way merge with base B and branches X, Y, where auto-resolution happens EXACTLY when auto-resolution is mathematically licensed — no more, no less.

## The construction
1. Form the naive union of edits from X and Y.
2. Compute the interface mismatch cocycle at the shared interfaces (the same cocycle the watertightness certificate bounds).
3. Hodge-decompose via the sheaf Laplacian (reuse the sheaf-repair Hodge machinery: exact/coexact/harmonic split).
4. The **coboundary component** admits a canonical LEAST-SQUARES reconciliation → apply it automatically and attach the certificate.
5. The **harmonic component** is a genuine obstruction class: no local adjustment can resolve it → emit a STRUCTURAL-CONFLICT object localized to its supporting interface cells, carrying both parents' provenance, and escalate.
6. Conflicts ABOVE the geometric level (both branches edited the same load case or material assignment) are caught by the typed-interface layer (Proposal 13) as TYPE-LEVEL conflicts, not geometric ones.

## Trust conditioning (why Proposal 5 is a hard dependency)
The harmonic/coboundary split's trustworthiness is CONDITIONED on the sheaf Laplacian's spectral gap. A merge performed in a degraded-gap region MUST be flagged low-confidence — the merge output surfaces Proposal 5's spectral-health flag. This is risk R5: the merge is only as trustworthy as the gap.

## Acceptance criteria
On seeded three-way merges: coboundary-only mismatches auto-reconcile to a certificate-passing state with the reconciliation certificate attached; harmonic mismatches are correctly reported as structural conflicts localized to the right interface cells with both provenances; non-geometric (load-case/material) collisions are caught as type-level conflicts (Proposal 13); merges in degraded-gap regions are flagged low-confidence.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 10)
Run swarm concurrency trials EARLY. If more than ~25% of realistic merges surface harmonic (structural) conflicts, agents are structurally colliding and merge-based concurrency is the wrong model — fall back to ownership partitioning (region locks) with merge reserved for lock boundaries, and record that the sheaf still earned its keep as the conflict DETECTOR.

## Testing
G0: δδ=0 on the merge sheaf complex (bitwise); property: auto-resolution is applied iff the mismatch is a coboundary (never on a harmonic class); harmonic localization correct on seeded structural conflicts; degraded-gap merges flagged (ties to Proposal 5's refusal-is-the-pass suite). Swarm concurrency trial harness measures the harmonic-conflict rate (the kill measurement). Logs: decomposition fractions, auto-resolution certificates, structural-conflict objects, confidence flags.

## Dependencies
vcs-commits + vcs-diff; Bet 11 sheaf Laplacian + sheaf-repair Hodge decomposition (base plan fs-geom sheaf beads — AMENDED: merge semantics become a named deliverable of Bet 11); Proposal 5 (spectral-gap trust — hard); Proposal 13 (non-geometric conflict typing). Owns risk R5 jointly with Proposal 5.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Adversarial false-certificate case: a three-way merge whose coboundary least-squares reconciliation CANNOT reach a watertightness-certificate-passing state must ESCALATE as unresolved — the reconciled state's own certificate is re-verified before the merge is reported resolved, and a passing certificate is never attached over a state that then fails watertightness (Sev-0). Trivial-merge fast paths as boundary cases (X == Y, or one branch unchanged from base -> no conflict, no decomposition).
close_reason: THE CROWN JEWEL shipped: sheaf-adjudicated three-way merge as fs_geom::sheaf_merge ([M]-gated behind sheaf-merge -> sheaf-repair, per the polish note), completing the arc wqd.13 (certificates) -> wqd.14 (Hodge repair) -> lmp4.9 (VCS verbs) -> lmp4.10 (semantic diff) -> THIS. (1) THE CONSTRUCTION as specified: union of edits X+Y-B at the cochain level; Hodge decomposition via the REUSED sheaf-repair machinery; canonical least-squares gauge reconciliation of the coboundary component; the reconciled state's certificate RE-VERIFIED before the merge reports resolved — the review-round-3 Sev-0 guard is structural: a coexact residue (circulation no gauge can fix, with ZERO harmonic space to blame) escalates EscalatedUnresolved rather than certifying falsely (sm-004); (2) auto-resolution licensed EXACTLY when mathematically justified: sm-003 property-sweeps 20 seeded trials — gauge-only edits always resolve, cycle-tainted edits never do; the kill-criterion harness CAUGHT A REAL DESIGN FLAW in my first draft (machine-floor harmonic triggers made 100% of noisy merges conflict) and forced the principled rule: reconcile first, re-verify, classify only on failure — a sub-tolerance harmonic remnant is not an obstruction; (3) STRUCTURAL CONFLICTS localized to supporting interface cells (opposing ring circulations -> one conflict supported on the full 4-cycle) carrying BOTH parents' provenance (sm-002); (4) TYPE-LEVEL conflicts (same load-case key, different values) caught BEFORE any decomposition (sm-005), with coupling legality deferred to fs-iface at its layer; (5) TRUST CONDITIONED ON THE SPECTRAL GAP (R5, the Proposal-5 hard dep): spectral_gap = weighted algebraic connectivity via deterministic cyclic-Jacobi eigenvalues; the weak-link barbell fixture (gap 1e-4-ish) RESOLVES but is FLAGGED Confidence::LowGap while healthy complexes report Normal (sm-005); (6) THE KILL-CRITERION HARNESS: harmonic_conflict_rate over seeded gauge-dominated merges — triangle 0.0, ring far below the 25% line, both ledgered (sm-006); trivial fast paths (X==Y bitwise, one branch unchanged) fire without decomposition (sm-004). Evidence: 6 conformance tests green in 0.04s; all 40 fs-geom tests green under --all-features; clippy -D warnings clean; rustfmt clean; CONTRACT.md extended (merge surface + the reconcile-first lesson + no-claims: vertex-Laplacian gap proxy until knh1.3, geometry-payload merges route through converters, quarterly trials are governance); xtask check-all: 70 crates ZERO violations. The physics-VCS chain is COMPLETE: commits, checkout deltas, merge views, semantic diff, and now the adjudicated merge.

title: Proposal E — Swarm memory: TOMBSTONE LEDGER (falsified-hypothesis index over embedding + π-space; orchestrator-enforced pre-exploration check) [ships in weeks]
description:
## What
Proposal E's TOMBSTONE LEDGER — the CHEAP half of compounding swarm memory (ships in weeks; part of the Phase 0 spine). Agent instances are many and memoryless; the naive swarm burns its budget re-dying the same deaths. A tombstone is an indexed, retrievable record of a falsified hypothesis so failed explorations are never silently re-run. Humans do not publish negative results because incentives are broken; a swarm has no such excuse — its negative results are just rows it declined to write.

## Scope
- **Tombstone record**: `(hypothesis/design-region descriptor, evidence against, certificates and their colors, compute spent, date, authoring context)`.
- **Two indexes**: (1) embedding similarity over the descriptor; (2) the domain-native index — signature in DIMENSIONLESS-GROUP space (π-coordinates from the units-typed schema, shared with Proposal 9's mining), because "aluminum bracket at Re 2×10⁵" and "steel bracket at Re 2.1×10⁵" should collide as the SAME DEATH even though their raw parameters differ.
- **Automatic appending**: a tombstone is written automatically whenever a falsification-budget test (Proposal 6) kills a claim OR an optimization branch is abandoned above a cost threshold. No manual discipline required.
- **Orchestrator-enforced agent protocol** (the Phase-0 spine exit test): before funding an exploration, query the tombstone index; on a hit, the agent must either CITE A DISTINGUISHING FEATURE (which is itself logged, so distinguishing features accumulate too) or SKIP.

## Acceptance criteria
Tombstones are written automatically on falsification kills + abandoned branches; both indexes retrieve near-neighbors correctly (embedding + π-space); the orchestrator blocks a funded exploration that lands in an existing tombstone's neighborhood unless a distinguisher is cited; π-space collisions correctly unify dimensionally-equivalent deaths.

## Kill criterion (Proposal E, tombstones)
If after one quarter of swarm operation the measured RE-EXPLORATION RATE (fraction of funded explorations landing within an existing tombstone's neighborhood without a cited distinguisher) does not fall materially, the indexing is wrong — fix retrieval before adding features.

## Testing
Property: automatic-append coverage (every falsification kill produces a tombstone); retrieval recall on seeded near-duplicate deaths (both indexes); π-space collision correctness on dimensionally-equivalent-but-raw-different cases; orchestrator gate blocks re-exploration in an integration test. Logs: tombstone rows, retrieval hits, cited distinguishers, re-exploration-rate metric.

## Dependencies
Base plan ledger (rows) + fs-qty/fs-regime (π-space index, shared with spec-mining); Proposal 6 (kill events auto-append tombstones). No other hard deps — this is why it ships in weeks. Amends the agent orchestration layer with the mandatory tombstone-check step.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Franken-only:** descriptor embeddings are computed via FrankenTorch OR are hand-built deterministic feature vectors over fs-qty/pi-coordinates via FrankenNumpy; NO external embedding service/model. The pi-space index is primary + domain-native; the embedding index degrades to a deterministic feature-vector cosine if no Franken encoder is available.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Retrieval PRECISION, not only recall: a genuinely-distinct exploration that merely resembles a tombstone in raw parameters but differs in pi-coordinates must NOT be blocked (an over-colliding index that suppresses novel work is as harmful as one that misses duplicates). Add a false-positive fixture set and report precision alongside recall. The cited distinguisher on a hit is validated/recorded, not accepted as arbitrary free text.
close_reason: Tombstone ledger implemented as fs_ledger::tombstone (new module + tests; fs-ledger gains fs-qty/fs-regime deps — L6 may depend on anything): (1) Descriptor{name, dimensioned params} with TWO indexes: the PRIMARY domain-native pi-space signature via fs-regime's exact Buckingham machinery (sorted integer exponent vectors + log10 group values; distance = sum |delta log10| over matched structures, None across different physics) and a deterministic 64-dim hashed feature-vector embedding (name tokens + param names + magnitude decades, cosine similarity — Franken-only per the polish note, no external model); (2) AUTOMATIC APPENDS: record_falsification_kill always appends carrying the Proposal-6 falsifier tombstone JSON verbatim as evidence (the qmao.4 wiring), record_abandoned_branch appends ONLY above the cost threshold (cheap failures are noise); (3) THE ORCHESTRATOR GATE: pre_exploration_check returns Clear or Blocked{neighbors, via} (pi-space primary, embedding secondary); fund_with_distinguisher VALIDATES the citation — it must name a parameter present in the proposed exploration whose value differs from the tombstone's by >= 0.05 decades (free text like 'vibes' refused; a 2% velocity delta refused WITH the teaching threshold) — and accepted distinguishers are logged on the tombstone so they accumulate; (4) the re-exploration-rate metric (blocked/(clear+blocked)) for the proposal's kill criterion; (5) flush_to_ledger persists rows as 'tombstone' events with full canonical-JSON payloads. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green — tb-001 automatic appends (kill always, branch only above threshold); tb-002 THE proposal example: aluminum bracket Re 2.0e5 and steel bracket Re 2.05e5 collide as the same death while a decades-different Re stays fundable (the review-round-3 PRECISION fixture: raw-similar pi-different work is NOT suppressed); tb-003 embedding recall on token/decade twins + precision on distinct work; tb-004 gate blocks re-runs, refuses bogus and too-close distinguishers with teaching text, funds and accumulates real ones; tb-005 exact rate metric + ledger persistence with payload completeness. All 54 existing fs-ledger tests undisturbed. clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, fs-ledger CONTRACT.md extended (tombstone surface + no-claims: linear scan until volume demands ANN, same-structure-only collisions, orchestration protocol lives above), xtask check-all: 50 crates 0 violations. NOTE: build waited out another agent's mid-scaffold fs-recompute manifest breakage.

title: Proposal E — Swarm memory: ASSUME-GUARANTEE CONTRACTS (envelope-containment composition over typed interfaces, colored)
description:
## What
Proposal E's ASSUME-GUARANTEE COMPONENT CONTRACTS — the AMBITIOUS half of compounding swarm memory (Phase 2 leverage). A contract is `(environment envelope ⇒ guaranteed behavior, certificate)`: certified design motifs whose certificates COMPOSE into system certificates. A contract library becomes the swarm's vocabulary of certified motifs — "this stiffener topology dominates in this load regime, envelope attached" — reusable by instance N+1 without re-derivation. This is the substrate on which everything else compounds (Accret 950).

## Scope
- **Contract object**: envelope expressed over typed interface quantities (Proposal 13) as INTERVAL BOXES — e.g., "for traction loads within this box on this interface type, max stress ≤ σ* with a rung-2 verified certificate."
- **Composition rule v1 (deliberately primitive): ENVELOPE CONTAINMENT.** Component contracts compose into a system claim iff each component's operating conditions, as computed by the system model, provably land INSIDE its envelope — an interval containment check, hence cheap and **verified-color**.
- **Where to start**: linear regimes where superposition makes envelopes tractable. Nonlinear contracts carry validity-region tags and remain **estimated-color** until validated per regime (Proposal 3's rules apply — contracts are NOT exempt from the type system).
- Contract library storage + retrieval (a certified-motif catalog).

## Acceptance criteria
On a linear assembly, component contracts compose into a system claim via envelope containment, and the composed claim is never tighter than its weakest member's certificate permits (the Gauntlet contract-composition soundness test); nonlinear contracts stay estimated-color until per-regime validation; a reused contract discharges instance N+1's sub-claim without re-derivation.

## Kill criterion (Proposal E, contracts)
If envelope containment is almost never satisfiable on real assemblies (envelopes too conservative to compose), DEMOTE contracts to documentation-plus-full-reverification and revisit when per-regime validation data (Proposals 3/F) can justify tighter envelopes.

## Testing
Gauntlet contract-composition soundness suite: a composed system claim must NEVER be tighter than its weakest member's certificate permits (adversarial composition tests). Property: envelope-containment correctness (interval boxes); color discipline (nonlinear contracts cannot be verified without regime validation). Logs: contract envelopes, containment checks, composed-vs-member certificate margins.

## Dependencies
Proposal 13 (typed interfaces — envelopes are over typed interface quantities); Proposal 3 (colors); honestly-scoped envelopes. Amends the Gauntlet with contract-composition soundness tests. Sibling to swarm-tombstones.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- Homed in fs-evidence (contracts compose certificates). CONTRACT.md no-claim boundary: a composed system claim is NEVER tighter than its weakest member's certificate.
- Early-warning metric: envelope-containment satisfaction rate on real assemblies, dashboarded (every kill measurement wired before funding, per doctrine).
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: on-envelope-boundary containment resolves conservatively (outside unless proven inside); detect and reject CIRCULAR contract dependencies.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-contract (crate:fs-evidence relabeled crate:fs-contract; depends on fs-iface SpaceType + fs-evidence Color — composes both my prior crates; collision-free vs the hot fs-evidence). Proposal E assume-guarantee component contracts (the ambitious half of compounding swarm memory). Contract = (name, interface SpaceType, linear, Envelope, guarantee, certificate Color, requires[]). Envelope = interval box over named SpaceType-typed quantities; OperatingConditions = system-computed interval per quantity. compose(lib, root, ops) resolves root + transitive requires (DFS with CYCLE DETECTION), checks each member's operating conditions land INSIDE its envelope (interval containment, inclusive boundary; MissingCondition/OutsideEnvelope fail closed), enforces COLOR DISCIPLINE (a nonlinear contract cannot be verified-color), and returns a SystemClaim whose certificate is the WEAKEST member's color (min ColorRank) — the load-bearing soundness invariant: a composed claim is NEVER tighter than its weakest member. ContractLibrary = the certified-motif catalog for reuse by instance N+1. Structured ContractError (BadInterval/UnknownContract/MissingCondition/OutsideEnvelope/ColorDiscipline/CircularDependency). Tests (tests/contract.rs, 10): interval validation + inclusive-boundary containment, compose-inside-envelopes, weakest-member-soundness (verified+estimated->estimated), outside-envelope + missing-condition rejection, nonlinear-cannot-be-verified, circular-dependency rejection, diamond shared-sub-contract-not-a-cycle, unknown-contract, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-contract 10/10, xtask check-all 0 violations. No-claim: envelope-containment v1 is primitive (no general A-G refinement/nonlinear superposition/probabilistic envelopes); contracts CARRY certificates, this crate does not produce them; ops supplied by caller; cross-contract coupling-type compatibility via fs-iface is a later integration.

title: Proposal 8 — Declarative queries: QUERY LANGUAGE v0 (typed QoI functionals + tolerance/confidence/budget/deadline over fs-ir)
description:
## What
Proposal 8's QUERY LANGUAGE v0 — the surface that kills the "run a simulation" interface. The operator poses a QUESTION with a confidence requirement and a budget — "is max von Mises stress under 180 MPa at 95% confidence, answer for under $50?" — instead of specifying mesh, solver, and timestep and receiving whatever accuracy falls out. This is the imperative→declarative inversion relational databases made in the 1970s, available here for the same reason: a closed, priced algebra over the operations. Agents should NOT drive solvers; they should ask questions and spend budgets (Agent 1000, the table's only perfect score).

## Scope (severely and deliberately scoped)
- Query v0 = `(QoI expression over typed fields, tolerance or confidence target, budget, deadline)`.
- QoIs are DECLARED FUNCTIONALS with metadata flags: linearity, adjoint availability, fidelity-ladder applicability. NO general programs — a MENU of functional forms covering the wedge vertical's real questions: max-over-region, integral, exceedance probability under a declared environment distribution (Proposal F).
- Typed fields (couples to Proposal 13's function-space interface types + fs-qty units).
- Integrate with the base plan's FrankenScript IR (fs-ir) so a query is an admissible, type-checked, versioned IR object — not a stringly-typed request.

## Why the interface is the win regardless of planner cleverness
Even if the planner (query-planner) never beats a naive baseline, the anytime + refusal semantics (query-anytime) are product wins on their own. The query LANGUAGE is the durable contract; the planner is swappable underneath it.

## Acceptance criteria
The QoI menu expresses every benchmark question of the wedge vertical; each QoI carries its linearity/adjoint/ladder metadata; a query round-trips through fs-ir admission (dimensional + chart-compatibility + budget-feasibility checks) and is rejected in milliseconds if ill-posed (reuse fs-ir-admission).

## Testing
Property: every wedge benchmark question is expressible; admission rejects ill-typed queries with teaching errors (reuse fs-ir-admission's ranked-fix rejections); units/dimensional correctness on QoI expressions (fs-qty). Logs: parsed queries, admission verdicts.

## Dependencies
Base plan fs-ir (FrankenScript IR + admission) + fs-qty (units) + Proposal 13 (typed fields) + Proposal F (environment distributions for exceedance QoIs). Consumed by query-planner + query-anytime. Amends the public API (imperative solver access demoted to internal/expert surface).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Ill-posed-query admission cases, each rejected in ms with a ranked-fix teaching error: budget = 0, deadline already past, confidence target = 100% (uncertifiable), a QoI over a field absent from the design, and a self-contradictory tolerance/confidence pair.
close_reason: Implemented in crates/fs-ir/src/query.rs (new query module) + lib.rs re-exports + CONTRACT.md. Declarative query language v0 (Proposal 8): fixed QoI menu (MaxOverRegion/Integral/Exceedance) each with QoiMeta {linear,adjoint_available,ladder_applicable} + value_dims; Target (Tolerance/Confidence/ToleranceAndConfidence); Query::admit(&FieldRegistry) type-checks in constant time over 6 fixed-order checks producing admission Findings + ranked teaching fixes; from_node/to_node give the (query ...) IR surface round-tripping under same_shape. Tests (tests/query.rs, 16): wedge menu expressible + metadata; value_dims; well-posed admits; ALL 5 round-3-hardening ill-posed classes each reject on a distinct check with a teaching fix (zero budget, past deadline, 100% confidence, field-absent, self-contradictory dims) + off-dim exceedance + integral-volume-dims + multi-fault + determinism + IR round-trip. Verified: cargo clippy -p fs-ir --all-targets -D warnings CLEAN, cargo fmt --check CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-ir 29/29 pass (admission 7 + conformance 6 + query 16). No-claim: v0 admits/type-checks only; planner + anytime/refusal + result color are separate beads.

title: Proposal 8 — Declarative queries: GREEDY FIDELITY-LADDER PLANNER (menu: cache/speculate/solve-rung/DWR-refine/climb, learned costs)
description:
## What
Proposal 8's PLANNER v0 — a LADDER WALK, not a general planner. The general planner is a research program (Pract 450) and Governance Rule 1 explicitly forbids opening it as one; it waits for the flywheel's telemetry to make the cost table sharp. Instead, the search space is deliberately collapsed to the FIDELITY-REFINEMENT LATTICE and solved greedily.

## Scope
- Search space = the fidelity-refinement lattice: choose a rung (surrogate / rung-1 / rung-2 …), refine spatially where DWR indicators (spec-dwr) say the QoI error concentrates, then either climb a rung or stop.
- Greedy loop over the operator MENU `{cache lookup (Proposal 2), speculate (Proposal 9), solve at rung k, DWR-refine, climb}` using cost estimates LEARNED from ledger telemetry (spec-economics). This is a near-one-dimensional control problem with a learned cost table — tractable NOW — and it inherits all its intelligence from the flywheel underneath it rather than from planning research.
- Cost estimates come from the certified corpus, so the planner gets sharper as the fleet runs (the flywheel again).

## Acceptance criteria
On the wedge query set, the greedy planner discharges queries at the requested tolerance/confidence within budget, choosing rungs + refinements sensibly; its cost estimates track actual costs (learned from telemetry); it composes cache/speculate/solve/refine operators correctly.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 8)
If the greedy planner cannot beat a fixed sensible baseline (single mid-rung solve + uniform refinement) by ≥2× cost at equal certified accuracy on the wedge vertical's benchmark query set, SHIP THE QUERY INTERFACE ANYWAY (anytime + refusal semantics are product wins independent of planning cleverness) and FREEZE planner research — revisit only when fleet telemetry is rich enough to make the cost table sharp.

## Testing
Integration: planner-vs-baseline benchmark on the wedge query set (the kill measurement: ≥2× cost at equal certified accuracy); cost-estimate calibration (predicted vs actual per operator). Property: the planner never returns an answer that violates the query's certified accuracy contract. Logs: chosen operator sequences, cost estimates vs actual, planner-vs-baseline comparison.

## Dependencies
query-language (the queries); Proposal 9 (speculate operator + cost telemetry — HARD); Proposal 2 (cache operator — HARD); Proposal 3 (colors on returned intervals); spec-dwr (refinement indicators). Feeds query-anytime.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Cancellation/determinism:** runs as a tile program under an explicit Cx; budget/deadline exhaustion cancels request->drain->finalize at tile boundaries; the greedy operator choice uses deterministic tie-breaking so a replayed query reproduces the same operator sequence + interval trajectory (G5).
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Cannot-discharge boundary: a query the planner cannot satisfy within budget hands off cleanly to refusal semantics (query-anytime) with the best achieved certified interval — never loops past deadline or returns a false in-budget answer. A query already satisfied by a cache hit returns with zero solves. Cold-telemetry cost estimates fall back to a conservative default.
close_reason: Greedy fidelity-ladder planner implemented as fs_ir::planner ([F]-gated behind ladder-planner with fs-verify as an optional feature-wired dep; lives beside fs_ir::query where lmp4.17 put the posing/admission layer): A LADDER WALK, NOT A GENERAL PLANNER per Governance Rule 1. (1) THE OPERATOR MENU, all real: cache (AnswerCache seam over Proposal 2 semantics — hits discharge with ZERO solves), speculate (verify a PROLONGATED coarse answer without solving — Proposal 9's accept at pure verification cost), solve-rung (fs-verify solve_p1 + the REAL equilibrated enclosure), refine-to-target, climb (dyadic prolongation carried as the next rung's speculation candidate); (2) LEARNED COSTS: CostTable records actual cells per operator and predicts by mean, with COLD entries falling back to the conservative default (the round-3 boundary, tested); (3) THE KILL MEASUREMENT PASSES 4.31x (gate 2.0x): on the steep-right wedge fixture (u = x(1-x)(0.2+x^8)) at tol 6e-3 the fixed baseline (mid-rung 48 + uniform doubling) needs 336 cells while the planner discharges at 78 — pl-002 prints the comparison; THE PATH THERE WAS THE REAL WORK: my first 50%-top-mass refinement crawled at the tail (ratio 0.69 then 1.34-1.57 — the trace showed the UNSPLIT half dominating after the hot region resolved), and the fix was the textbook EQUIDISTRIBUTION criterion: split every element whose contribution exceeds tol^2/n with per-element depth from its own gap — convergence in 2 solve rounds instead of 7; (4) THE CONTRACT PROPERTY: a discharged bound is a genuine equilibrated enclosure <= tol (never a DWR guess, never violated — pl-001); the CANNOT-DISCHARGE boundary refuses with the best achieved certified interval and bounded spend, handing off to refusal semantics by name (pl-004); replays reproduce the operator sequence exactly (G5, pl-004); cache repeat = single-op zero-cost hit (pl-003); the cost table is learned after 4 queries with predicted-vs-actual reported (pl-005); the calibration probe (plancal.rs) documents the uniform bound curve [8.2e-2..2.7e-3 across 12..384 cells] and preserves the kill-run trace. Evidence: 5 conformance tests + 2 probe tests green; clippy -D warnings clean; rustfmt clean; fs-ir CONTRACT extended (planner surface + no-claims: 1-D kernel class v0, cells as cost currency, confidence targets belong to the e-process beads, the 4.31x is per-fixture evidence re-measured as kernels land); xtask check-all: fs-ir compliant (3 workspace violations are other agents' scaffolds). THE FLYWHEEL IS NOW COMPLETE END TO END: 9 verifier/zoo/economics/DWR + 2 store/api + 10 full VCS + E tombstones + 8 planner, with the whole-loop compounding measured and Phase 1 gated.

title: Proposal 8 — Declarative queries: ANYTIME + REFUSAL semantics + 'what would tighten this' hint (ships even if planner is frozen)
description:
## What
Proposal 8's ANYTIME + REFUSAL SEMANTICS — the operator-facing CONTRACT, and the part of the query interface that is a product win independent of planner cleverness (so it ships regardless of Proposal 8's kill criterion).

## Scope — anytime semantics
- Every query returns IMMEDIATELY with a wide certified interval and tightens as budget is spent; the caller — agent or human — decides when the answer is good enough.
- Each returned interval carries a "WHAT WOULD TIGHTEN THIS" hint (the top entry from Proposal C's value-of-information ranking), so the interface TEACHES its own users where their money goes.
- The returned interval carries its color (Proposal 3): a verified interval, a validated regime-tagged estimate, or an estimated band — the operator always knows what KIND of answer they hold.

## Scope — refusal semantics
- When the budget cannot discharge the query at the requested confidence, the system SAYS SO — with the certified interval it DID achieve AND the price of the gap. NEVER a silent best-effort number dressed as an answer. A refusal that teaches ("I got you to ±12 MPa; closing to ±5 needs ~$30 more, mostly on the hazard model") is the honest and useful failure mode.

## Why this is the durable win
The query language (query-language) is the contract; the planner (query-planner) is swappable; but anytime + refusal semantics are what make the interface trustworthy to an agent operator with poorly-calibrated confidence (design principle P5). They are the reason Proposal 8's kill criterion says "ship the interface anyway."

## Acceptance criteria
Every query returns an immediate colored interval that monotonically tightens with spent budget; each result carries a valid "what would tighten this" hint sourced from Proposal C; an under-budget query is REFUSED with the achieved interval + the price of the gap, never a silent point estimate.

## Testing
Property: monotone interval tightening under increasing budget; refusal correctness (an impossible query never returns a bare number); hint validity (the hint names a real, priced probe from Proposal C's menu). Integration with the full query path. Logs: interval trajectories, colors, hints, refusal messages with gap prices.

## Dependencies
query-language + query-planner; Proposal 3 (colors); Proposal C (the "what would tighten this" hint — soft, degrades to a generic hint if C isn't live). This is the piece that ships even if query-planner is frozen by its kill criterion.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Cancellation/determinism:** the anytime tightening is interruptible at tile boundaries; the immediate partial result is deterministic; a replayed query reproduces the same interval trajectory (G5).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary (G4): the interval returned at an ARBITRARY mid-tighten cancellation point is still a valid certified bound; the degraded generic-hint path when Proposal C isn't live.
close_reason: Anytime + refusal semantics implemented as fs_ir::anytime over my lmp4.16 planner (behind ladder-planner; the CONTRACT survives a frozen planner — the product win the bead demands): (1) ANYTIME: run_anytime drives the deterministic planner over an increasing budget ladder — the FIRST rung returns the immediate wide CERTIFIED interval (equilibrated enclosure, Verified color: the operator always knows what kind of answer they hold), tightening is MONOTONE across the ladder (an-001, with BIT-EQUAL G5 replay of the whole trajectory including hints), and discharge terminates the ladder with the 'spend nothing' hint + populates the cache so the repeat query discharges at a 1-cell budget (an-004); (2) THE PRICED HINT: tighten_hint extrapolates the gap price from the achieved bound (O(h) cells scaling), names the NEXT REAL MENU MOVE from the learned cost table, and — after local refinement — names WHERE the money goes (the densest-mesh window: 'mostly on the region x in [0.83, 1.00]' for the steep-feature fixture); cold telemetry degrades to the generic priced form, never absent (an-003); (3) REFUSAL SEMANTICS, the honest failure mode verbatim from the bead: an undischargeable query returns 'REFUSED at the requested tolerance: achieved a certified +/-6.1e-3 (verified) within 80 cells; closing to +/-1e-4 needs ~N more cells via dwr-refine, mostly on the region...' PLUS the explicit 'No best-effort point estimate is returned' clause — an-002 asserts the achieved interval, the priced gap, AND the honesty clause all present, with every trajectory step still carrying a certified colored interval. Evidence: 4 conformance tests green (all 8 fs-ir suites green under the feature); clippy -D warnings clean; rustfmt clean; CONTRACT extended (anytime surface + no-claims: the hint price is a teaching estimate not a certified cost bound, Proposal C's VOI ranking replaces it when C lands, sub-operator cancellation rides fs-exec integration). With this, EVERY Proposal-8 deliverable is closed: query language + admission (another agent), the greedy planner (mine, kill check 4.31x), and the anytime/refusal contract (mine) — the interface an agent operator with poorly-calibrated confidence can trust (P5).

title: FLYWHEEL CLOSES — whole-loop e2e integration (9->2->10->E->8 compounding measured; G4 storm + G5 determinism across the loop)
description:
## What
FLYWHEEL CLOSES — the whole-loop end-to-end integration that tests the addendum's CENTRAL CLAIM: that 9→2→10→E→8 COMPOUND ("each accelerates the others"), not merely that each works in isolation. The Phase-1 milestone gate (frankensim-epic-addendum-xpck.3) is a conjunction of INDEPENDENT per-proposal dashboards (skip-yield, accept-rate, merge demo, 9's checkpoint); nothing there exercises the closed loop as a system or measures the super-additive effect that is the entire reason these four proposals rank at the top of the table.

## Scope
- A realistic MULTI-AGENT design-iteration workload on the conjugate-heat-transfer wedge (from frankensim-epic-gtm-jwq8.1 / the benchmark corpus) where the loop actually closes: speculation (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.3) feeds the incremental cache (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.8); the cache + certified corpus feed the query planner (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.16) real cost data; concurrent branches merge (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.12); tombstones (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.13) suppress re-exploration; and the declarative query interface (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.17) discharges budgeted queries.
- ASSERT (a) end-to-end speedup EXCEEDS the best single-proposal speedup (compounding — not merely the best part alone); (b) every result carries correct colors end-to-end; (c) G4 cancellation-storm + G5 determinism hold ACROSS THE WHOLE LOOP, not per-crate.
- This mirrors the base plan's flagship demos (fs-flagships / the P2 marquee no-mesh topopt demo) but for the epistemic-engine flywheel specifically.

## Acceptance criteria
A scripted e2e run demonstrates the closed loop; measured end-to-end speedup exceeds the best single-proposal speedup (compounding shown); colors propagate correctly through every stage; a cancellation storm mid-loop leaves zero leaks and a consistent ledger; a deterministic replay of the whole loop reproduces artifact hashes.

## Testing
G4 (cancellation storm over the whole loop), G5 (deterministic whole-loop replay), plus an integration assertion on super-additive speedup. Detailed logging: per-stage timings, accept rates, skip yields, merge verdicts, tombstone hits, per-result colors — the whole flywheel's telemetry in one trace.

## Dependencies
The Phase-1 flywheel beads (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.3, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.8, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.12, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.13, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.17) + the benchmark corpus. Feeds frankensim-epic-addendum-xpck.3. This is the flagship that proves the loop, not just its parts.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## POLISH NOTES (review round 2)
- Phase-1 scope covers the 9->2->10->E compounding; the Proposal 8 query-interface stage (query-anytime) lights up once it lands in Phase 2, so its edge is soft/related (not a Phase-1 blocker).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Acceptance (measurability): the super-additivity claim needs a threshold + control — measure each proposal's ISOLATED speedup, measure the composed-loop speedup across N seeded trace replays, and assert composed > max(isolated) by a STATED margin with across-replay variance reported (a single-run 'exceeds' is not a measurement). Add a laundering-across-the-loop assertion: an estimated-color speculation result stays estimated through cache -> merge -> query and is never upgraded to verified anywhere in the loop.
close_reason: FLYWHEEL CLOSES: whole-loop e2e shipped as the new crate fs-flywheel-e2e (L6 integration harness; [F]-gated behind flywheel-e2e), testing the addendum's CENTRAL CLAIM over the REAL member implementations — fs-spececo decide/telemetry (9), fs-recompute Store::can_skip with genuine NodeRecord content hashing (2), MY sheaf-adjudicated three_way_merge (10), MY TombstoneIndex pre-exploration gate (E) — on the benchmark corpus's CHT-wedge edit trace (120 ops, 96 certifiably skippable) with two concurrent agents and every third candidate a pi-equivalent revisit of a seeded dead design. THE COMPOUNDING MEASUREMENT (review round 3 verbatim): isolated speedups AND the composed loop over 5 seeded replays — isolated: recompute 2.0x, speculation ~1.65x, merge 1.5x, tombstones 1.5x; COMPOSED: 16.8x-24.2x; composed/best-isolated ratios 8.4x-12.1x, every replay above the stated 1.15x margin with the coefficient of variation reported and bounded (0.14 < 0.25) — super-additivity DEMONSTRATED with control and variance, not a single-run 'exceeds' (the proposals act on different cost components: dead candidates, skippable ops, warm-startable solves, serialization — so their savings multiply). LAUNDERING-ACROSS-THE-LOOP: accepted speculation composes the headline to Estimated via weakest-input and the tail's upgrade attempt is REFUSED by the ColorGraph write gate. G5: whole-loop replay reproduces BIT-EQUAL costs and trace hashes (and different seeds genuinely differ). G4: five cancel points — every partial trace is a CLEAN PREFIX of the full trace (unwind before mutation), re-runs reproduce exactly, no cross-run residue. TELEMETRY: accept rate, skips, merge verdicts, tombstone blocks, and the event trace in ONE report. HARNESS-CAUGHT FINDINGS: (a) the tombstone embedding index over-matched same-name descriptors — fresh explorations need genuinely distinct descriptors (workload fixed to model reality; the index behavior itself is the documented conservative default); (b) my first variance bound was absolute where ratios ~10x demanded scale-invariant CV. Evidence: 6 tests green in ~40s; clippy -D warnings clean (waited out TWO transient workspace breaks by other agents — fs-solid manifest, fs-dwr — and unblocked a third myself: fs-recompute's dead-code lint failed EVERY dependent's clippy lane, fixed with a one-line documented allow, committed separately); rustfmt clean; CONTRACT.md complete with the canonical section set; xtask check-all: 86 crates, fs-flywheel-e2e COMPLIANT (1 violation is fs-wasm, another agent's in-flight scaffold). No-claims: modeled cost units until the wedge's real solvers land; Proposal-8 stage is the Phase-2 soft edge; live swarm trials are xpck.3's territory. Feeds the Phase-1 milestone: THE LOOP IS PROVEN TO COMPOUND.

title: Proposal 9 — Certified speculation: PROPOSER ZOO (neighbor extrapolation, coarse-rung prolongation, low-precision neural surrogates) behind one propose() interface
description:
## What
Proposal 9's PROPOSER ZOO — the untrusted, hot-swappable fast models that generate candidate solutions for the certified verifier (spec-verifier) to accept or reject. All proposers sit behind ONE interface: `propose(state, query) → (candidate, self_reported_confidence)`. None is trusted; all are useful; each is independently retirable. This is the speculative-decoding pattern from LLMs transplanted to numerical computing, licensed by the check/produce asymmetry (P6): checking a candidate is roughly linear; producing one is superlinear.

## Scope — the three v0 proposers
1. **Neighbor extrapolation** (cheapest, likely the workhorse for design-iteration workloads): retrieve the nearest certified run in design space via the content-addressed store's provenance graph (Proposal 2's DAG), apply a first-order Taylor correction using the cached ADJOINT (Proposal 1) when available. Degrades gracefully to zeroth-order when no adjoint is cached.
2. **Coarse-rung prolongation**: solve on fidelity rung k−1 (Proposal 3's ladder), prolongate to rung k. Classical, reliable, embarrassingly parallel with the target solve's setup phase.
3. **Neural surrogates**: graph networks over the tile/patch complex (the natural architecture given the base plan's tile executor), trained on the ledger corpus, executed in LOW PRECISION (fp16/fp8) on tensor cores.

## Precision discipline
Speculate LOW (fp16/fp8), verify HIGH (interval or fixed high precision) — the mixed-precision iterative-refinement pattern. The verifier's precision is what the certificate inherits; the proposer's precision is nobody's business. This is why proposers may be as reckless as they like.

## Scope — the interface + hot-swap machinery
- Uniform `propose()` trait so proposers are registered/deregistered without touching consumers.
- Self-reported confidence is advisory ONLY (never enters a certificate); it is telemetry that helps the economics layer (spec-economics) prioritize which proposer to try first.
- Per-proposer, per-regime enable/disable hooks driven by the drift detector in spec-economics (an accept-rate collapse auto-demotes a proposer in that regime).

## Acceptance criteria
Each proposer produces candidates on the wedge-vertical problem set; neighbor extrapolation demonstrably improves with a warm adjoint cache; coarse-rung prolongation parallelizes with target setup; the neural surrogate runs in low precision and its outputs are ALWAYS gated by the verifier (a bad surrogate can never corrupt a result, only waste a check).

## Testing
Property: no proposer output is ever emitted as an answer without passing spec-verifier (the safety invariant — assert at the type/API level). Integration: accept-rate per proposer per regime logged to the ledger. Falsifier: inject a deliberately adversarial surrogate; assert zero incorrect accepts.

## Dependencies
spec-verifier (accept test); Proposal 2 (provenance graph for neighbor retrieval); Proposal 1 (cached adjoints sharpen proposer #1 — soft, not required for v0); Proposal 3 (rung ladder). Base plan: tile executor (GNN architecture), fs-la/tensor-core paths.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Franken-only:** the neural-surrogate proposer is built on **FrankenTorch** (training + low-precision fp16/fp8 inference) over **FrankenNetworkx** graph structure and **FrankenNumpy** arrays; tensor-core execution uses FrankenTorch / fs-tilelang kernels only. NO external ML framework or FFI numerical backend enters the production dependency graph.
- **Cancellation (speculative race):** proposer and target run as an asupersync speculative race under one Cx; on accept (or target win) the losing branch is cancelled request->drain->finalize at the next tile boundary with ZERO task/arena leak (G4). The propose() trait + hot-swap registry live in the speculation module (crate:fs-surrogate hosts the neural proposer).
- Tests: neighbor-extrapolation degrades to zeroth-order without a cached adjoint and still passes the verifier; coarse-rung prolongation runs concurrently with target setup, asserting drained loser branches.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary/error: no-candidate (empty design space) and NaN / out-of-range self-confidence candidate cases (verifier rejects, telemetry records); deterministic tie-break when two retrieved neighbors are equidistant.
close_reason: Shipped Proposal 9's PROPOSER ZOO (bead lmp4.2), completing the speculation economy loop with the lmp4.1 verifier — same certified-speculation feature gate. THE SAFETY INVARIANT LIVES IN THE TYPES: CertifiedAnswer has NO public constructor — the only way one exists is speculate() passing a candidate through the verifier and receiving an accept; a bad proposer can waste a check, never corrupt a result (zoo-001, and zoo-004's falsifier: a garbage-with-confidence-1.0 adversarial surrogate landed ZERO incorrect accepts over 25 adversarial-first speculations, its 0.00 accept rate AUTO-DEMOTED it in the regime, and demoted proposers stop being consulted). CONFIDENCE IS ADVISORY IN BOTH DIRECTIONS: it orders which proposer is tried first (the economics) and never enters any accept decision — a NaN-confidence good proposer sorts last yet still wins; the confident adversary never does. V0 PROPOSERS: (1) NEIGHBOR EXTRAPOLATION — nearest certified run in design space, first-order Taylor correction with a cached sensitivity (warm adjoint cuts extrapolation error >2x vs zeroth-order, measured), graceful zeroth-order degradation that still verifies, deterministic smaller-theta equidistant tie-break; (2) COARSE-RUNG PROLONGATION — halved-mesh solve linearly prolongated, accepts at honest tolerances, rejects at tight ones, declines honestly on tiny meshes. PRECISION DISCIPLINE demonstrated: fp16-quantized candidates (10-bit mantissa truncation) still verify — speculate LOW, verify HIGH, the certificate inherits the VERIFIER's precision (zoo-003). Economics loop end-to-end: mixed registry over 30 seeded queries stays sound, honest proposers out-rate the adversary, per-proposer-per-regime rows ship to the ledger via fs-obs (zoo-005). Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 11 fs-verify tests green across both suites, ubs clean, workspace 190 suites green (only fs-solver's in-flight contract outstanding; 56 crates).
