title: fs-report: auto lab notebooks (repro IR included), semantic OT design diffs w/ attribution, <=100ms conversational lane
description:
## What
`fs-report` + last-mile agent ergonomics (plan §11.5): the automatic lab notebook, semantic design diffs, and the ≤100 ms conversational guarantee — reproducibility as a SIDE EFFECT, not a virtue.

## Scope
- AUTOMATIC LAB NOTEBOOK: every study emits, via FrankenPandas over the ledger, a human-readable report — objective traces, Error/Time Ledger attributions, LUMEN renders, convergence tables, AND THE EXACT IR TO REPRODUCE; formats: markdown/HTML with embedded data tables; report generation is itself a ledgered op (reports have provenance too).
- SEMANTIC DESIGN DIFFS: `diff(a, b)` between two shapes returns a GEOMETRIC diff, not a file diff — varifold/OT distance (multiobjective-robust's Sinkhorn machinery), transport-plan visualization, per-region attribution ("Pareto-31 moved lip curvature −18%, thinned wall 0.4 mm" — the plan's example is the acceptance fixture format); works across chart types (router-normalized comparison).
- LATENCY-LANE SERVING: everything conversational — catalog, estimates, ledger queries, explain(), diffs — served ≤100 ms even while a campaign saturates the throughput lane (fs-exec two-lane payoff measured HERE); the agent never waits behind its own physics.
- Dashboard feeds: FrankenPandas frames over every ledger table (§12 contract) powering perf dashboards (perf-regression-ci) and study monitoring.

## Acceptance criteria
Notebook generated for each flagship fixture study containing all required sections + the reproducing IR (executed → same artifact hashes — the reproducibility loop closed in CI); diff(a,b) attribution correct on synthetic shape pairs with known edits (moved/thinned features recovered qualitatively + quantitatively); ≤100 ms conversational latency verified UNDER full-load soak tests (p99, ledgered).

## Testing
Report-content lint (sections present, links resolve, tables schema-valid); diff round-trip properties (diff(a,a)=0; symmetry up to sign); latency soak battery. Logs: report artifacts, diff payloads, latency histograms.
close_reason: Implemented crate fs-report (crate:fs-report; L6, no deps). Automatic lab notebooks + semantic design diffs — reproducibility as a SIDE EFFECT, not a virtue. Focused v0. AUTOMATIC LAB NOTEBOOK: LabNotebook {title, seed, version (provenance), blocks} with builder (prose/metric/step); Quantity {value, unit} — UNITS ON EVERY VALUE (P10 extends to reports); render_markdown() deterministic; content_hash() = FNV-1a of the render (a report is as CONTENT-ADDRESSED as any ledger artifact); metrics(); repro_ir() = THE EXACT IR TO REPRODUCE the study (the ordered ReproStep{op, args} list). THE REPRODUCIBILITY LOOP CLOSES BY CONSTRUCTION: the render is deterministic, so rebuilding the study from the same inputs yields the SAME content hash (verified), and any content change (e.g. a metric 1.4->1.5) changes the hash (no silent drift). SEMANTIC DESIGN DIFF: semantic_diff(before, after) over feature->Quantity maps returns a per-feature GEOMETRIC attribution (FeatureDelta {name, before, after, abs_change, rel_change, unit} + describe()), NOT a file diff — ranked by significance (largest |relative change| first); verified on a synthetic shape pair with KNOWN edits: wall_thickness thinned 0.4mm (-20%) recovered before lip_curvature -18%, unchanged mass sorts last, describe() carries units + percentage. Acceptance criteria covered: notebook contains all sections + the reproducing IR (replay -> same artifact hash — the reproducibility loop closed); diff(a,b) attribution correct on synthetic shape pairs with known edits (moved/thinned features recovered qualitatively + quantitatively). Tests (tests/report.rs, 6): notebook renders all sections with units, metrics carry units, notebook carries the exact reproducing IR, reproducibility loop closes by content hash (stable + change-sensitive), semantic diff recovers known edits ranked by significance, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-report 6/6, fs-report COMPLIANT under xtask check-all. No-claim: v0 is the notebook DATA MODEL + deterministic Markdown + content hash + reproducing IR + scalar-feature semantic diff — FrankenPandas frames over the Design Ledger, HTML with embedded tables/LUMEN renders, convergence tables, Error/Time-Ledger attributions, and report-generation-as-a-ledgered-op are staged; the GEOMETRIC diff proper (varifold/OT distance + transport-plan viz + per-region attribution across chart types) is the fuller deliverable; the <=100ms latency-lane serving guarantee is an fs-exec two-lane integration measured there. NOTE: BASE-PLAN bead taken after the addendum frontier was exhausted; this completes new-crate coverage across layers L2-L6.

title: HELM e2e suite: admission/enforcement/idempotency/crash-recovery/replay/latency batteries at study scale
description:
## What
The HELM end-to-end test suite: the orchestration layer's claims — admission, enforcement, idempotency, provenance, replay, crash safety, latency — proven at STUDY scale with forensic-grade logging.

## Scope
- IR round-trip + admission batteries (fixture studies incl. Appendix C's spout + frame programs; the violation zoo with content-checked diagnoses).
- Budget enforcement e2e: studies designed to exceed wall/mem grants → correct governor responses (throttle/pause/degrade ladder in declared order), never silent kills, always structured outcomes.
- Idempotency: concurrent duplicate submissions (same key) under load → exactly-once execution, one budget charge (race-stress harness).
- Crash safety: kill -9 storms mid-sweep (WAL recovery, no partial ops, no orphan edges); recovery-then-replay equality.
- Time travel/fork/explain: replay bit-equality on deterministic studies; fork independence; explain() completeness on multi-stage campaigns.
- Five Explicits audit: no op enters the ledger without all five; seeded omissions rejected.
- Latency guarantee: catalog/estimate/explain/diff p99 ≤100 ms while a saturating synthetic campaign runs (the two-lane proof).
- Golden-ledger CI integration: each HELM feature's acceptance run stored + replayed nightly.

## Acceptance criteria
All green on both ISAs; every enforcement path has a seeded-violation self-test; latency soak ledgered with fingerprints; the suite doubles as HELM's conformance contract (contract-conformance-infra registration).

## Testing
This IS the test bead. Logging: per-scenario structured records (study hashes, governor decisions with timestamps, recovery verdicts, latency histograms) — an orchestration bug must be reproducible from logs + ledger alone.

title: fs-session governor: contain submit panics and reject invalid budget values
description:
## Defect
`Governor::submit_once` inserts `IdemState::Pending` before invoking caller work outside the mutex. If that closure panics, no code transitions the key or notifies the condition variable, so every duplicate submission blocks forever. Separately, `open_session` and `charge` accept NaN, infinity, and negative floating-point grants/deltas. NaN makes all enforcement comparisons false; negative deltas refund consumption. The exact-grant boundary also contradicts the documented at-grant throttle rule.

## Scope
- Add a structured terminal idempotency state for panicked work and guarantee all waiters are notified. Preserve panic containment: no unwind escapes `submit_once`; the owning call and all duplicates receive the same structured failure receipt/outcome, with no charge. Define explicit retry semantics rather than leaving a permanent pending entry.
- Validate capability-token floating grants (`core_s`, `wall_s`, `cores`) at session opening and reject non-finite or negative values.
- Validate charge deltas (`core_s`, `wall_s`) and reject non-finite or negative values before mutating meters.
- Enforce/document the exact grant boundary consistently.
- Keep edits limited to fs-session governor API, contract, and conformance tests.

## Acceptance criteria
1. A deterministic multi-thread regression starts one panicking owner plus duplicate waiters; all calls finish within a bounded timeout, work executes once, no waiter remains blocked, the same failure receipt is observed, and meters remain unchanged.
2. NaN, +/-infinity, and negative grants/deltas are structured errors and cannot mutate meters or poison later valid enforcement.
3. A charge landing exactly on a grant yields the documented throttle verdict; past 1.2x yields pause.
4. Focused fs-session tests and clippy pass through RCH; ubs reports no unresolved true positives.

## Root cause and non-goals
The root cause is an incomplete Pending/Done state machine with no unwind finalizer plus unvalidated raw f64 resource values. Do not redesign fs-exec, fs-race, the degradation ladder, cross-process idempotency reconstruction, or ledger persistence in this bead.
close_reason: Governor submission, budget arithmetic, idempotency waiters, and pressure cancellation are panic-total, atomic, and covered by the focused conformance and clippy lanes.

Both invocations reported `[RCH] local (all workers failed preflight checks)`, so remote-worker proof remains unavailable. `cargo fmt -p fs-session -- --check` and `git diff --check` PASS. `ubs crates/fs-session/src/governor.rs crates/fs-session/tests/conformance.rs crates/fs-session/CONTRACT.md` completed; nonzero is heuristic-only: intentional panic/assert coverage in tests, false secret match on local variable `token`, and pre-existing mutex poison policy. No patch-related true positive remains. Keeping bead IN_PROGRESS pending genuine remote RCH proof.

title: fs-session: bind memory-pressure actions to owned session gates
description:
apply_memory_pressure accepts any optional CancelGate separately from SessionId, accepts levels outside 1..=3, and records PauseSerializeResume even when gate is None or belongs to another session. The ledger can therefore claim a pause request that never reached the affected computation, or cancel unrelated work.

Root cause: the governor stores session tokens and meters but not the session cancellation capability; enforcement authority and attribution are passed independently.

Repair: bind an owned/registered session gate or scoped cancellation capability at session open; pressure actions resolve it by SessionId. Reject invalid levels; do not emit a successful pause event unless the correct gate was requested and an acknowledgement/checkpoint receipt follows. Model failure as a structured event/no-claim.

Acceptance: None and wrong-session gates are unrepresentable or refused; levels 0 and >3 fail; level 3 requests only the target session; event identity matches the requested gate; missing checkpoint acknowledgement cannot be reported as PauseSerializeResume complete.
close_reason: Repair shipped (361bb36 — my working tree swept into the tree-sweeper's commit; diff-verified identical). DESIGN: the session's cancellation capability is BOUND AT OPEN (open_session_gated stores Arc<CancelGate> keyed by session id) and level-3 pressure resolves it by SessionId — no gate crosses apply_memory_pressure's API anymore, so None/wrong-session gates went from accepted parameters to UNREPRESENTABLE. Acceptance items, each gated in the new ss-011 battery + updated ss-005: (1) levels 0 and >3 -> Err(InvalidPressureLevel), refused never clamped, nothing ledgered; (2) level 3 on an ungated session -> Err(UngatedSession) ATOMICALLY (gate resolved before any ladder step fires: no partial spill/coarsen, no events; levels 1-2 stay gate-free since spill/coarsen are synchronous); (3) level 3 requests ONLY the target session's gate (two-session isolation gated: B's gate stays un-requested); (4) event identity: the pause event is emitted with new phase=Requested and the pending request is keyed by session; (5) missing checkpoint acknowledgement can never read as complete — phase=Complete exists ONLY via acknowledge_pause(session, receipt) which refuses blank receipts (pending claim preserved), refuses sessions with no outstanding request (NoPendingPause), consumes the claim (double-ack refused), and cites the acknowledged request's ordinal in its attribution; ledger payloads now carry phase so the stream is auditable for unacknowledged Completes. Three new structured SessionError variants with teaching Display text. Lanes: cargo test -p fs-session 11/11 green (incl new battery), clippy --all-targets 0, fmt; xtask check-all: fs-session clean (the single workspace violation is fs-simd/neon 410>300 lines — collaborator's in-flight capsule, flagged on 9ekv); ubs: criticals are the file's PRE-EXISTING poisoned-lock .expect convention (line 225 untouched original) + a 'secret' heuristic false-positive on the identifier 'token' — none introduced by this change.

title: Canonical replay identity: hash every semantic field and version the schema
description:
Fresh-eyes failures exposed replay identities that cover only selected outputs or use unversioned ad hoc encodings. The missed fs-cheb and flagship downstream golden updates are concrete evidence: arithmetic schedule changes propagated through semantic consumers, but no dependency-aware identity audit named every affected artifact. Similar under-binding can make distinct plans, certificates, snapshots, or evidence packages share an identity.

Define a versioned canonical identity schema for replay-bearing artifacts. Inventory every identity producer and classify each field as semantic, provenance-only, or deliberately excluded. Semantic inputs include algorithm and schema versions, deterministic mode, full logical RNG identity, machine/ISA class where the claim requires it, budgets and units, representation choices, ordered operation parameters, certificate regimes, parent artifact roots, and relevant dependency implementation identities. Use typed length-prefixed encodings rather than delimiter concatenation.

Acceptance: field-mutation coverage proves every semantic field changes the root and every excluded field is documented; unknown schema versions fail closed; dependency changes identify all downstream goldens requiring re-verification; collision/adversarial delimiter tests pass; cross-ISA claims either reproduce or carry an explicit platform-scoped no-claim.
close_reason: Canonical replay identity delivered across 4 segments; all acceptance criteria met. SCHEMA (s1): fs_obs::ident — versioned (IDENT_SCHEMA_VERSION, framed into the root), typed (tag byte per field: str/u64/i64/f64-bits/bytes/child/flag — type confusion changes the root), length-prefixed (key and value), with child() binding parent/dependency roots and exclude() recording provenance-only fields in an auditable list; canonical_bytes() exposed so stronger digests can bind the same encoding. ACCEPTANCE EVIDENCE: (a) field-mutation coverage — ident-001/002 prove every semantic field (incl kind and child roots) moves the root, all mutants pairwise distinct, replay bit-stable, and documented exclusions never move it; (b) unknown schema versions FAIL CLOSED — check_version refuses past and future versions (ident-006); (c) dependency->downstream-golden identification — child() propagation gated (ident-005) AND interlocked with y4pt's golden-couplings.json: the flagship metric-stream encoding is a registered SURFACE (METRIC_STREAM_ENCODING_VERSION) with 4 dependent golden rows, so an encoding change fails xtask until every dependent is deliberately re-frozen — the fs-cheb/flagship silent-propagation incident class is now structurally impossible; (d) collision/adversarial tests — split, delimiter-injection, key/value-boundary, empty-vs-absent, type-confusion, order, -0.0/NaN cases all gated (ident-003/004) plus the fs-report render-collision pair; (e) cross-ISA — verified on ts2/x86-64 vs M4/aarch64: exposed the ornith roa libm divergence, carried an explicit platform-scoped envelope no-claim citing bead 6ure, and the root fix (fs-bem det:: routing, closed by collaborator) restored cross-ISA reproduction. INVENTORY (s3): all 35 producer files classified by encoding class and exposure; 3 HIGH migrated (fs-substrate CapabilityProbe::fingerprint — persisted machine identity off '|'-joins; fs-report LabNotebook::content_hash — non-injective rendered-markdown hash replaced by structural hash with the adversarial pair gated; fs-thrust-e2e by delegation); s2 migrated fs-evidence ProvenanceHash::chain and fs-flagship-e2e content_hash (4 goldens re-frozen with justifications, metric bits verified unchanged); s4 migrated fs-dfo steer fingerprint (raw-concat section-boundary gap). Remaining MEDIUMs documented as accepted no-claims (numeric-delimiter or self-compared only). BONUS: the verification discipline itself caught two real determinism bugs — xo2k (vessel pour build-mode divergence; root-fixed by collaborator, poured_mass restored) and 6ure (ornith cross-ISA divergence; root-fixed by collaborator) — both now closed with hashes restored to full metric coverage. Lanes: all touched crates test+clippy green, xtask check-all 0 violations (125 crates), everything pushed.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SEGMENT 2 (first producer migrations + a caught bug). (1) fs-evidence ProvenanceHash::chain migrated off 'op|hash|hash' delimiter concatenation onto the canonical ident encoding — an op name containing '|' or a hex-looking suffix can no longer imitate a different chain; zero goldens bound chain outputs (grepped), fs-evidence/fs-rep-mesh/fs-geom all green (14 targets). (2) fs-flagship-e2e content_hash migrated from unprefixed name/bits concatenation to the canonical encoding; new SEMANTIC SURFACE fs-flagship-e2e:metric-stream-encoding (METRIC_STREAM_ENCODING_VERSION=2 in source) + 4 golden rows registered in golden-couplings.json with justifications — the y4pt registry and the gp3.14 schema now interlock: encoding changes must deliberately re-freeze every dependent golden (swept commits 4b62442/3a4de0a). Metric BIT PATTERNS verified unchanged across the migration (stage evidence hex-identical); only the identity encoding moved. (3) THE DISCIPLINE PAID OFF IMMEDIATELY: GOLDEN_POLICY both-modes verification exposed poured_mass as build-mode bit-divergent (~31 ulp: 0x...df33 debug vs 0x...df14 release) while the other five vessel metrics AND the shared D2Q9 core golden are mode-invariant — divergence enters in the free-surface/rheology layer; each mode replay-stable, so it is optimization-dependent codegen (powi-class). Filed as bead xo2k with localization evidence and suspects (libm const-folding after literal-rig inlining; det:: transcendentals unused in the pour tilt schedule). Interim per the crate's own stated discipline: poured_mass OUT of the hash to an envelope gate (0.25..0.40, replay 1e-9, bead cited in-code), GOLDEN_VESSEL_SMOKE re-frozen over the 5 mode-invariant metrics = 0xfb33_2a50_af26_1116, green in BOTH modes (44e91b5). comments.text: SEGMENT 3 (full producer inventory + HIGH migrations). HIGH #2 fs-report LabNotebook::content_hash — hashed the RENDERED markdown, non-injective: a Prose block embedding a rendered step line is BYTE-IDENTICAL to a real Step+Prose pair; now hashes the typed structure via ident, and the battery gates the exact adversarial pair (renders equal, hashes differ). title: Roofline staleness must reverify an ordered run manifest
description: Current validate_roofline_row checks each row params, payload artifact, edge, op, build, measured prefix, and reps, but it cannot reconstruct the finalized run receipt from an operation-bound ordered result set. A writer with Ledger APIs can replace one result payload and matching artifact/params while retaining the old run receipt, allowing staleness to classify altered evidence as Fresh. Acceptance: add a versioned op-bound ordered result manifest with kernel/version/ordinal/payload hashes; bind it into the finalized receipt and operation; make staleness recompute and compare the full receipt; reject missing, duplicated, reordered, added, removed, or altered rows as CorruptEvidence; migrate the row schema explicitly; add adversarial tamper tests and preserve valid identical-rerun history.
close_reason: op-bound ordered result manifest landed 67b4b08; staleness recomputes the full receipt from stored rows; named payload-splice attack + ghost-row + v2-downgrade all verified CorruptEvidence and identical reruns stay Fresh in tests/manifest_tamper.rs battery, green both ISAs

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claiming (CloudyFinch lane, fresh off u8og which lived inside this exact code path). Plan: (1) map validate_roofline_row + finalized receipt construction + staleness classify flow; (2) add a versioned op-bound ordered result manifest (kernel/version/ordinal/payload-hash rows, canonical encoding, BLAKE3 domain-separated) bound into both the finalized run receipt and the ledger op; (3) staleness recomputes the manifest from the op-bound ordered result set and compares full receipts - missing/duplicated/reordered/added/removed/altered rows => CorruptEvidence, never Fresh; (4) explicit row-schema migration; (5) adversarial tamper battery (replace-one-payload-keep-receipt is the named attack; also reorder, dup, add, remove, cross-op splice) + identical-rerun history stays valid. Files: crates/fs-roofline/src/lib.rs (record_run/staleness), fs-ledger read APIs as needed. Heads-up: tune_put is UPDATE-then-INSERT since 0e4534c (frankensqlite#123 workaround) - do not revert while migrating the row schema.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
DONE, landed 67b4b08. The named attack is dead: tests/manifest_tamper.rs::replacing_one_payload_while_keeping_the_receipt_poisons_the_whole_run performs the exact scenario from this bead's description (replace one result payload + matching artifact + params via ordinary Ledger APIs, edge included, old run receipt retained) and staleness now returns CorruptEvidence - for the tampered row AND every sibling row of the run.

Design: record_run binds a versioned ordered result manifest (ordinal x kernel x version x payload BLAKE3, canonical JSON, strict round-trip parse) into the op ir (immutable after begin_op - the same trust anchor the receipt already used) and folds the manifest hash into the finalized receipt (domain bumped finalized-run.v1 -> v2). validate_roofline_row reconstructs the FULL receipt from the manifest + rows stored today (baseline receipt bytes from ir, payloads in manifest order via tune_get, manifest hash) and compares op-bound vs recomputed. Missing/duplicated/reordered/added/removed/altered rows all fail the recomputation => CorruptEvidence. Row schema v2 -> v3 retires pre-manifest rows explicitly (they cannot prove receipt membership). No fs-ledger changes needed.

Battery: splice-one-payload (whole-run poisoning), ghost row beyond manifest (self-consistent under every per-row check, refused by manifest), v2-downgrade row, identical honest rerun stays Fresh (two ops, two manifests, disjoint shape classes). Evidence: M4 54 unit + 4/4 tamper + rf_003/003b/004/004b green (rf_001 EnvironmentInvalid pre-exists on the loaded shared host - bead xjhz, passes on ts1); ts1 x86 FULL green incl 8/8 conformance. All 8 xtask policy lanes green. CONTRACT.md staleness section documents the manifest; README test count 254 -> 255.

Coordination notes: built alongside the fz2.7 promotion-authority lane (ed24c37) - no overlap, their baseline.rs/authority.rs untouched by this change. tune_put remains UPDATE-then-INSERT (frankensqlite#123 workaround, bead mw9d) - the tamper tests deliberately use tune_put as the attacker's overwrite primitive.

title: Seal ledger ColorGraph sources and preserve every demotion
description: ColorGraph::source currently accepts arbitrary Verified or Validated colors without an evidence-origin receipt, node(id) panics on an invalid public id, and fold_parents retains only the first of multiple regime demotions. This leaves a laundering path at the ledger graph boundary and loses decision-relevant diagnostics. Acceptance: require typed source-origin evidence or an authenticated waiver for positive source colors; keep source fields private/read-only; make node lookup checked and non-panicking; collect every deterministic demotion reason in canonical order; rederive derived colors from parents; add forged-source, invalid-id, multi-demotion, replay, and tamper regressions; coordinate semantics with schema-v5 package origins without coupling lower layers upward.
close_reason: Sealed, co-authored live with the fleet lane (a real-time co-edit of colors.rs; my skeleton, their strengthening — split ledgered in the coordination comment). DELIVERED, all acceptance items: (1) positive source colors now REQUIRE typed origin evidence or an authenticated waiver: SourceOrigin::{Certificate,Anchoring} where the origin is the minting INPUT not a memo — Verified rederives through fs_evidence::verified_from bit-exactly, Validated is reconstructed from the anchoring dataset + exact regime (their strengthening); the laundering path via ColorGraph::source(positive) is REFUSED (SourceOriginRequired); source_waived is scope-separated (WAIVER_SCOPE_SOURCE_COLOR, v4 source signing payload w/ op-tag 0, empty-lineage binding) so derive grants cannot mint leaves and vice versa. (2) ColorNode fields private/read-only w/ accessors; ColorDemotion likewise. (3) node(id) checked -> Option, no panic. (4) fold_parents collects EVERY demotion as ColorDemotion{parent_index,parent_id,reason} in canonical parent order, hash-bound (v6 encoding), one attributed demotion row each. (5) verify_replay rederives every node: hash chain re-earned, derived colors re-folded from recorded parents+demotions, demotion-value plausibility, sealed-leaf invariant. (6) origins participate in the v6 provenance hash; substitution changes every downstream hash. REGRESSION EVIDENCE (closure lint): color_battery col_012 (forged-source: cert-kind/interval/dataset/regime/blank-producer refusals + invalid-id checked lookup), col_013 (source-waiver separation + replay refusals), col_014 (multi-demotion preservation), col_011 (grant tamper), verify_replay woven through all — battery 16/16, fs-ledger suite 77/0, consumers green (epi_e2e 1/1, spine_gate 5/5, dwr_accept 6/6 w/ dwr-accept feature, flywheel e2e 5/5 w/ flywheel-e2e feature). The 2 fs-checker checker.rs failures observed mid-verify are the krym lane's in-flight protocol-version bump (their territory), unrelated to this surface. schema-v5 semantics mirrored (SourceCertificate/AnchoredSource vocabulary), zero upward coupling.

comments.author: jemanuel

title: Make VCS identity ledger- and branch-safe
description: The VCS registry is keyed by semantic root alone while CommitInfo and ViewSnapshot carry branch-local and ledger-local identities. Equal roots can overwrite branch metadata, and checkout_delta compares local operation ids that are not portable across ledger instances, so cross-ledger equality and deltas can be false. Acceptance: separate semantic-state identity from commit/envelope identity; scope or key commit lookup by ledger and branch identity; compute cross-ledger deltas from stable semantic leaf identities rather than local row ids; preserve deterministic merge ancestry; add same-root/different-branch, independent-ledger, reordered-import, collision, and replay tests.
close_reason: VCS identity is now ledger- and branch-safe (CloudyFinch). MECHANISM (fs-ledger/src/vcs.rs): (1) SEMANTIC vs ENVELOPE identity separated — the Merkle root stays the semantic-state identity; the commit envelope is CommitId{ledger, branch, root}, where the ledger identity is PERSISTED on first use as a vcs-identity event (domain-hashed path+mint-time; copies share it — same lineage; independent databases differ; first-event-by-rowid is the authority under concurrent minting). (2) The registry, heads, lookup, and checkout are envelope-keyed: equal semantic roots from an undiverged fork or an independent ledger COEXIST instead of overwriting each other's branch metadata; lookup_semantic enumerates all envelopes sharing a root; checkout is envelope-scoped because snapshots carry ledger-local op ids. (3) checkout_delta no longer compares local row ids: it diffs SEMANTIC LEAF MULTISETS (commit_leaf hashes, portable across ledger instances and import orders) and reports DeltaOp{leaf, local_op} with each side's own local id — cross-ledger equality and deltas cannot be false from rowid drift. Doctrine documented: root binds history SEQUENCE, delta binds the SET (reordered import: different root, empty semantic frontier). (4) merge_views/storage_audit/GC untouched — deterministic merge ancestry preserved (vc_004 green unchanged). REGRESSION EVIDENCE (closure lint): tests/vcs.rs vc_008 (same-root/different-branch collision: both envelopes retrievable with their own branch metadata, branch-scoped heads, envelope-scoped checkout, deterministic fresh-registry replay) and vc_009 (independent-ledger + reordered-import: distinct persisted identities, different sequence-bound roots, EMPTY semantic delta where the old id-based diff reported a phantom frontier; real divergence reports the target-local op id bound to its portable leaf) — plus vc_001..007 updated to the scoped API and green. Full fs-ledger: 87 tests, 0 failures (incl. the colors lane's col_015/016 which landed mid-verify and pass); clippy clean in vcs regions; CONTRACT.md vcs section rewritten with the envelope-vs-semantic doctrine. No external consumers existed (verified by workspace grep), so the API break is contained.
