title: fs-render differentiable: edge-aware silhouette + reparameterized shading gradients, inverse-rendering objectives [F]
description:
## What
Differentiable rendering (plan §10.4 [F]): reparameterized/edge-aware gradient estimators for silhouette-and-shading derivatives — closing the loop where AESTHETIC constraints join physical ones in ASCENT.

## Scope
- Gradient estimators: reparameterized sampling for interior/shading terms (differentiable BSDF/light sampling through the FrankenTorch bridge where learned components appear); EDGE-AWARE handling of visibility discontinuities (silhouette integrals — the part naive autodiff gets silently WRONG; edge-sampling or reparameterization-class treatment, documented choice).
- Chart-level derivatives: image w.r.t. design parameters θ through chart perturbations (fs-xform Jacobian actions composed with rendering gradients — sphere-traced SDF derivatives via implicit differentiation of the hit equation).
- Inverse problems as objectives: match a target photograph, a sketch, or a legacy artifact's appearance as an objective TERM combinable with physics objectives in fs-opt-ir (aesthetic + aerodynamic Pareto — the plan's stated use).
- Bias/variance discipline: estimator bias characterized and documented (Evidence on rendering gradients like everything else); gradient-check battery vs FD-of-renders (expensive but definitive, run on fixtures).

## Acceptance criteria
Gradient checks: silhouette + shading derivatives match FD on fixture scenes (sphere position/radius, SDF blend parameters) within statistical tolerance; inverse-rendering fixtures converge (recover known shape parameters from target images); integration: one combined physics+appearance optimization fixture runs end-to-end.

## Testing
G0: estimator unbiasedness where claimed (interior terms), bias bounds where not (edge terms); variance tracking vs sample counts. Logs: gradient-check tables, inverse-problem convergence traces, per-term variance decomposition.
close_reason: Edge-aware differentiable rendering shipped at smoke tier, feature 'differentiable' in fs-render (fs-ad + fs-math optional deps, [F]-gate precedent). Design (documented): deterministic scanline renderer with ANALYTIC horizontal antialiasing — silhouette crossings of g(x,y)=min_z phi bisected per sub-row and rows integrated piecewise-exactly, so the image is SMOOTH in theta and the gradient is the exact derivative of the quadrature. Interior terms: shading differentiated through the CONVERGED sphere trace in fs-ad duals (differentiate-through-convergence), normals via NESTED spatial duals over the theta-dual scalar. Boundary terms: crossing velocities dx*/dtheta = -(dg/dtheta)/(dg/dx), dg/dtheta by Danskin at the converged z-argmin. Battery dr-001..006 all green FIRST RUN: gradient vs central FD worst rel 5.9e-6 over all 9 params (2 blended sphere centers/radii + blend k — the acceptance's parameter set); NEGATIVE CONTROL: naive frozen-crossing autodiff off by 1.4e-1 relative vs edge-aware 1.6e-8 — the silhouette boundary term measured at ~9e6x the honest error budget (the bead's 'silently WRONG' claim demonstrated with numbers); quadrature bias monotone 2.8e-1->1.6e-2 (deterministic estimator: no variance, bias measured); inverse rendering recovers position+radius to 7e-7; combined appearance+physics (image L2 + volume budget) 1.1e-2 -> 8.9e-12 through the shared gradient path; bitwise replay of render AND gradient. No-claims/recorded successors in CONTRACT: MC/reparameterized path-traced estimators + FrankenTorch-bridged learned BSDFs, charts::Backend unification, fs-xform chart perturbations, fs-opt-ir registration (term shape already compatible), sub-row tangency kink documented. Lanes: 6/6 battery + all-features suite green, clippy --all-features -D warnings 0, fmt, ubs 0 critical, xtask clean for fs-render (only violation is fs-wasm, another agent's crate).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PLAN (claimed): smoke-tier differentiable rendering as feature 'differentiable' in fs-render (dep fs-ad, L1<L5 ok), following the volumes/chart-backends [F]-gate precedent. DESIGN (documented choice): a deterministic SCANLINE renderer with ANALYTIC horizontal antialiasing — per sub-row, silhouette crossings of the closest-approach function g(x,y)=min_z phi are localized by bisection and the row is integrated PIECEWISE-EXACTLY in x, so the image is SMOOTH in theta and the edge-aware gradient is the exact derivative of the quadrature: interior terms flow through Dual sphere tracing (implicit differentiation through the converged trace, the fs-ad differentiate-through-convergence pattern; normals via nested spatial duals), boundary terms through explicit crossing velocities dx*/dtheta = -(dg/dtheta)/(dg/dx) with dg/dtheta by Danskin at the converged z-argmin. Scene: smooth-min blended SDF spheres (theta = centers, radii, blend k — the acceptance's parameters), orthographic camera, Lambertian + directional light. Gates: dr-001 full-image loss gradient vs central FD (rel 1e-5); dr-002 HONEST NEGATIVE CONTROL — same check with edge terms disabled fails by a large measured factor (naive interior-only autodiff is silently wrong, demonstrated); dr-003 deterministic-quadrature bias characterized (2nd-order shrink vs supersampling); dr-004 inverse rendering recovers known sphere params from a target image (GD + backtracking); dr-005 combined appearance+physics objective (L2 image + volume penalty) optimized end-to-end through the shared gradient path; dr-006 bitwise replay. No-claims/recorded successors: MC/reparameterized path-traced estimators + FrankenTorch-bridged learned BSDFs (the full [F] ambition), unification with charts::Backend, vertical antialiasing beyond sub-row averaging, fs-opt-ir wiring (term shape is compatible).

title: fs-img: in-house PNG/OpenEXR writers, AOVs, a-trous denoiser (labeled biased), film curves, deterministic encodes
description:
## What
`fs-img` (plan §10.5): in-house image plumbing — PNG and OpenEXR writers, denoising, film curves — pure Rust per P1.

## Scope
- PNG writer (8/16-bit, correct sRGB tagging) and OpenEXR writer (16f/32f, multi-channel: beauty/albedo/normal/depth AOVs, tiled/scanline) — in-house implementations of both formats' required subsets, spec-conformant.
- À-trous wavelet denoiser with feature guides (albedo/normal AOVs), CLEARLY LABELED AS BIASED (the honesty tag is part of the API — denoised images carry an Evidence marker distinguishing them from converged renders; a denoised image can never masquerade as ground truth in a comparison).
- Film response curves (tone mapping: filmic curve families + display transforms), white balance, exposure controls — deterministic pixel pipelines.
- Round-trip readers for our own outputs (testing + ledger artifact loading).

## Acceptance criteria
PNG/EXR outputs validated against external references (imagemagick/oiio comparisons in dev-only CI — allowed as isolated dev oracles per policy); bit-exact deterministic encodes (same image → same bytes, both ISAs); denoiser improves perceptual metrics on fixture renders while the bias label propagates; AOV round-trips lossless.

## Testing
G0: encode/decode round-trips; malformed-input rejection on readers (fuzzed); color-space conversion exactness vs fs-math reference implementations. Logs: encode timing/size stats, denoiser metric tables (with bias labels), byte-hash manifests.
close_reason: fs-img (L5, dep fs-math only) implemented from first principles: (1) PNG writer/reader — 8/16-bit Gray/RGB/RGBA, sRGB chunk, None filters, in-house CRC-32 + Adler-32 + stored-block zlib (byte-exact deterministic; no-claim on compression ratio); (2) OpenEXR writer/reader — single-part scanline v2, NONE compression, HALF/FLOAT AOV channels in spec alphabetical order, in-house f32<->f16 with RNE incl. subnormals (exhaustive round-trip over every finite half passes); (3) a-trous denoiser (5x5 B3 spline, edge-stopping albedo guide) whose output type PERMANENTLY carries PixelProvenance::BiasedDenoised — the honesty tag is in the type system; (4) deterministic film stack: exposure/white-balance/Hable filmic/sRGB via fs_math::det::pow/quantize8. Evidence: 9 unit + 4 conformance tests green (im-001 byte-determinism + lossless AOV round-trips; im-002 external sips/CoreImage oracle validates our PNG+EXR, skip-with-note where absent; im-003 denoiser MSE >2x improvement with bias label propagated; im-004 4000 seeded junk buffers + every truncation prefix rejected structurally). clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md with no-claims written, xtask check-all: 29 crates 0 violations.

title: LUMEN test suite: furnace/MIS/statistical batteries, tunneling audits, golden images, determinism + ray-rate gates
description:
## What
The LUMEN test + benchmark suite: rendering correctness is statistics + geometry + radiometry at once, and each needs its own harness — with logging that makes image bugs diagnosable without staring at pixels.

## Scope
- Radiometric: furnace tests, white-image tests, reciprocity checks, MIS weight-sum audits, analytic-scene batteries (spheres/planes with closed-form radiance).
- Statistical: sampler chi-square/KS batteries; convergence-rate verification (variance ∝ 1/N; QMC sampling where wired shows expected improvement); denoiser bias quantification.
- Geometric: tunneling audits (certified sphere tracing — zero misses), watertight ray-tri edge-case battery, BVH-vs-brute-force equivalence, mixed-chart consistency (same shape via SDF/mesh/NURBS backends → images within tolerance).
- Determinism (G5): render hashes across thread counts/runs on both ISAs; per-tile Philox stream audit.
- Performance (§14.1): ray-rate benchmarks (primary + secondary), volume-tracking throughput, ledgered with fingerprints; regression-gated.
- Golden images: content-addressed reference renders with the golden-evidence discipline (change requires semantic justification + verifier rerun); perceptual + numeric diff tooling.
- Live-integration: progressive-streaming correctness (tile updates arrive ordered, complete, cancellable mid-render with consistent partial images).

## Acceptance criteria
All batteries green both ISAs; every failure class has a seeded self-test; golden set curated + documented; ray-rate targets tracked with trend alerts.

## Testing
This IS the test bead. Logs: per-test structured records (scene hash, sampler stats, image hashes, diff metrics, ray rates) — a failed render bug is reproducible from seed + scene hash alone.

title: L2 MORPH: geometry kernel
description:
## Mission
L2 MORPH: the geometry kernel. Represent, construct, interrogate, convert, mesh, and parameterize geometry — with certified validity, native derivatives w.r.t. design parameters, and no privileged representation.

## Core thesis (plan §7, Bet 1)
The canonical object is an abstract Region presented through charts (SDF grid, F-rep CSG, NURBS patchwork, half-edge mesh, sparse voxel tree, point cloud, lattice graph, neural implicit). Conversions are functors carrying (cost, error-bound, certificate) labels; the Rep Router solves a Pareto shortest-path over the chart graph to pick the cheapest conversion chain that keeps composed error inside the caller's budget. This turns "which representation should I use" — the perennial unforced error of every CAD/simulation handoff — into a solved planning problem. "The same shape held three ways" is a normal, coherent state; agreement between charts is a checkable proposition.

## Also owned here
- Sheaf-theoretic gluing (Bet 11): watertightness as an interval-verified vanishing-cocycle certificate, H¹ obstructions reported with offending interface cells, plus constructive sheaf REPAIR (Hodge decomposition of mismatch: exact→local fixes, harmonic→true topological obstruction).
- Design parameterizations — the levers ASCENT pulls — each a differentiable map θ→Region with Jacobian action.
- Geometric algebra (PGA/CGA) as the internal kinematics substrate with Vec3/quat façades.
- Shape-program synthesis (patch Rev G): typed constructive-geometry programs, shape grammars, geometric e-graphs with equality saturation — discrete design invention beyond continuous perturbation.
- Import/export quarantine (patch Rev J): dirty external geometry never becomes a trusted Region without a certification receipt.

## Members
fs-geom-core, fs-rep-sdf, fs-rep-frep, fs-rep-mesh, fs-rep-nurbs, fs-rep-voxel, fs-rep-neural, rep-router, convert-mesh-sdf, convert-sdf-mesh, convert-nurbs-sdf, convert-sdf-nurbs, sheaf-certificates, fs-sheaf-repair, geom-queries, fs-mesh-delaunay, fs-mesh-remesh, fs-mesh-hex, fs-xform-params, xform-manifold-harmonics, xform-constraints, fs-ga, validity-certificates, fs-shapeprog, fs-io-quarantine, morph-conformance.

## Success Criteria
P1 exit criteria: chart round-trips certified; watertightness certificate validated against ray-parity oracle; router path independence proven metamorphically (G3).

title: fs-geom core: Region/Chart traits, ChartSample w/ certified Lipschitz, agreement checking, Convert receipts
description:
## What
`fs-geom` core: the Region/Chart abstraction (plan §7.1) — the geometry kernel's founding move. An abstract `Region` (measurable subset of R³ with piecewise-smooth boundary) is NEVER stored directly; it is PRESENTED through charts.

## Scope
- `Chart` trait (Appendix B): query interfaces — inside/outside, signed distance, closest point, ray intersection, curvature, integrals over region/boundary — via `eval(x, cx) -> ChartSample` (value + gradient + certified Lipschitz data), `support() -> Aabb`, `topology_hint() -> BettiBounds`; plus a DECLARED ERROR MODEL relative to the abstract region.
- `Region`: holds one or more charts + the provenance of how each was obtained. "The same shape held three ways" is a normal, coherent state instead of a synchronization-bug factory; AGREEMENT BETWEEN CHARTS IS A CHECKABLE PROPOSITION (sampled + certified agreement tests are API, not folklore).
- `Convert` trait: certified conversion as a functor with a receipt — `convert(budget, cx) -> Result<Certified<Dst>, Diag>` (Evidence-typed per fs-evidence); every conversion's error explicit and composable, feeding the Error Ledger (P4).
- Design-parameter plumbing: `Chart::Param` as the design-lever handle (fs-xform builds on it); charts advertise differentiability class.
- Cancellation + budgets: all queries take &Cx (interruptible geometry — huge queries poll at tile granularity).
- No privileged representation, ever (the anti-CAD-kernel stance): operations request capabilities ("a chart supporting operation X with error ≤ ε under budget B"), the Rep Router picks.

## Acceptance criteria
Trait suite compiles the whole chart zoo (proven by downstream beads); a two-chart Region with deliberate disagreement is DETECTED by the agreement checker with localized diagnostics; error-model composition matches fs-ivl bounds on fixture conversions.

## Testing
G0: trait-law suite (eval consistency: inside(x) ⇔ sd(x)<0 within declared tolerance; gradient vs FD on smooth charts; support() actually bounds). Fixture Regions (sphere/box/torus analytic charts) as the shared test vocabulary for ALL of MORPH. Logs: per-query evidence (values, bounds, chart id, budget spent).

## ADDENDUM AMENDMENT (COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md)
HARD NEW REQUIREMENT: **stable persistent entity IDs**. Patches, interfaces, and load cases must carry IDs that survive topology-changing edits, because Proposal 10's semantic diff/merge (frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.10, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.12) is gated on them (risk R3 — the classic CAD topological-naming problem). Mitigation the addendum mandates: FrankenSim owns its kernel, so IDs are first-class from day one rather than reconstructed heuristically; edits are ledgered operations that transform IDs EXPLICITLY. Early-warning metric: fraction of diffs falling back to unattributed geometric comparison.
Also NEW: **fiducial/datum primitives** in the geometry layer (design-for-verification), and the representation registry is explicitly OPENED to measured-data chart types, for Proposal 11's reality-as-a-chart ingestion (frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.4).
close_reason: Implemented fs-geom core (L2): the Region/Chart founding abstractions. Chart trait (object-safe: eval(x,&Cx)->ChartSample, support, topology_hint, name, differentiability, inside; plan Appendix B's type Param moved to the DesignChart subtrait so Region can hold Arc<dyn Chart> — same contract, object-safe core, fs-xform builds on DesignChart). ChartSample = signed distance + optional gradient + certified local Lipschitz + DECLARED error model (fs_evidence::NumericalCertificate). Region: multi-chart presentations with per-chart provenance; check_agreement = seeded deterministic sampling over the inflated union support where agreement means gap <= sum of declared error half-widths + slack — disagreements localize (point, chart names, gap vs allowed, first-K) and reports render canonical JSON (G5 replay proven). Acceptance landed: an undeclared 0.03 bias behind a LYING Exact error model is caught and gap-localized naming the liar (geo-003, LyingSphereChart fixture). Convert<Dst> trait -> Certified<Dst> receipts (fs-evidence): blanket Chart->SampledSdf conversion (dense trilinear, rigorous L*h in-box bound, outside-box enclosures leaning on the source's certified Lipschitz); receipt QoI bound CONTAINS the empirical error over 10k seeded points (geo-004); infeasible budgets refuse BEFORE sampling with ranked fixes (BudgetInfeasible computes needed resolution vs the 96-cap); NoLipschitzBound refusal for uncertified sources. Public fixtures module = MORPH's shared test vocabulary: exact sphere/box/torus SDFs (unit Lipschitz, exact error models, known Betti hints) + the lying sphere. G0 trait-law battery: 12k seeded queries proving inside<=>sd<0, support bounds, Lipschitz along random steps, unit-norm gradients matching central FD (geo-001). All geometry cancellable via Cx polls (geo-005). Lanes: fs-geom clippy -D warnings/fmt/tests green (8 lib + 5 conformance), xtask 0 violations (19 crates); full workspace green except two tests inside the 6ys.14 agent's UNCOMMITTED in-flight fs-ivl expansion work (theirs, untouched). No-claims: agreement is sampled evidence not proof (wqd.7 certificates); curvature/ray/integral queries arrive as capability traits with their consumers; router/cost models are the Rep Router bead's.

title: Converter SDF->mesh: dual contouring w/ QEF + manifold variant + interval bracket certificate
description:
## What
Certified converter: SDF → mesh via DUAL CONTOURING with QEF vertex placement (plan §7.3 edge 2) — sharp-feature capable, with a manifold variant, and INTERVAL VERIFICATION that the extracted surface brackets the zero set within tolerance [S/F].

## Scope
- Dual contouring core: Hermite data (edge crossings + normals) sampled from any SDF-capable chart; QEF minimization for vertex placement (SVD-based with regularization — fs-la small-dense); sharp features preserved where Hermite data indicates creases.
- Manifold variant: the manifold dual contouring family for guaranteed-manifold output when downstream (FEEC meshing) requires it; documented tradeoff vs feature sharpness.
- Adaptive: octree-based contouring on adaptive SDFs with crack-free stitching across levels.
- THE CERTIFICATE: interval verification that the extracted triangle surface BRACKETS the true zero set within tolerance — interval evaluation of the SDF over surface-triangle neighborhoods proves |φ(surface)| ≤ tol everywhere (not just at samples); failures localize the offending triangles.
- Output quality controls: min-angle/aspect bounds via post-passes that MAINTAIN the bracket certificate (re-verify after smoothing).

## Acceptance criteria
Analytic fixtures reconstructed within tolerance (Hausdorff measured); sharp-edge fixtures (box, CSG edges) show feature preservation vs marching-cubes baseline; manifoldness verified on the manifold variant across the fixture zoo; bracket certificate holds (interval check green) and DETECTS a seeded bad triangle.

## Testing
G0: watertightness of output on watertight inputs (Euler checks + sheaf certificate when available); G3: rigid-motion equivariance; crack-freedom across octree level changes (adversarial refinement patterns). Logs: QEF condition stats, feature-detection decisions, certificate margins per triangle batch.
close_reason: Implemented SDF->mesh dual contouring in fs-rep-mesh (plan 7.3 edge 2). Core: uniform-grid DC over any Chart — corner lattice sampling, secant edge crossings + chart gradients as Hermite data, regularized 3x3 QEF vertex placement (Schaefer mass-point regularization, Cramer solve, cell clamping; MassPoint baseline toggle for the feature comparison), axis-uniform quad stitching (cyclic (u,v) rings with circulation normal +d — a real orientation bug caught during construction: lexicographic cell ordering gave -x/+y/-z normals across axes). THE CERTIFICATE: bracket_certificate proves |phi| <= tol over the WHOLE extracted surface via the Lipschitz cone |phi(centroid)| + L*r with recursive 4-way subdivision — rigorous because the chart's Lipschitz bound is certified; uncertified charts refused (NoLipschitz teaching error); failures localize to triangles with margins. rmesh-008 acceptance: sphere reconstructed with all vertices within 0.06 of the zero set and the certificate proven; output manifold + closed + outward-oriented (winding +1 at center); translation-equivariant (G3, dyadic shift, 1e-9); QEF resolves the box corner >2x sharper than mass-point; a seeded bad triangle (vertex yanked to (3,3,3)) is DETECTED and localized. Stats ledgered via fs-obs. Lanes green: clippy -D warnings, fmt, 3 lib + 8 conformance, workspace 84 suites; xtask's single violation is fs-plan's in-flight missing contract (theirs). DOWNSCOPE recorded in contract no-claims: uniform grid v1 — adaptive octree contouring w/ crack-free stitching, guaranteed-manifold MDC for ambiguous topologies, and certificate-preserving quality post-passes are follow-ups; fs-ivl interval evaluation will tighten the conservative Lipschitz-cone bracket.

title: Converter NURBS->SDF: Bezier-clipping bounds + interval-Newton certified distance [F]
description:
## What
Certified converter: NURBS → SDF (plan §7.3 edge 3 [F]) — certified distance via convex-hull/Bézier bounding + interval Newton polishing.

## Scope
- Distance evaluation: Bézier-clipping recursion using convex-hull property for conservative distance bounds to patches; interval-Newton polish (fs-ivl-taylor Krawczyk machinery) for certified closest-point brackets; trimmed-patch awareness (trim classification determines which surface regions count).
- Sign assignment: consistent orientation from B-rep topology where available; winding-style fallback for imperfect shells with certificate downgrade (Evidence honesty — same pattern as mesh→SDF).
- Tiled generation: per-tile certified distance intervals (lower/upper bounds stored alongside f32 field where certification is requested); adaptive effort — tight bounds near surface, cheap bounds far away (budget-aware per P4).
- Edge label: certified where trim/orientation quality permits; measured-only otherwise — the label is data on the router edge, decided per-input.

## Acceptance criteria
Against exactly-representable fixtures (sphere/cylinder/torus as NURBS): certified intervals CONTAIN analytic distance everywhere sampled; near-trim behavior correct on adversarial trims (tangencies, slivers); throughput acceptable for optimization outer loops (target documented + ledgered).

## Testing
G0 containment battery vs dense oracle sampling; trim-classification consistency with fs-rep-nurbs's own tests; G3 frame invariance. Logs: per-tile bound widths, Newton iteration/failure stats, downgrade reasons.
close_reason: NURBS->SDF certified converter implemented as fs_rep_nurbs::sdf ([F]-gated behind the nurbs-sdf feature per the Ambition-Tag rule; fs-rep-nurbs gains fs-geom/fs-evidence/fs-exec deps following the rep-crate precedent): (1) CERTIFIED DISTANCE via convex-hull Bezier-clipping branch-and-bound reusing my closest_point_surface bracket machinery (hull distance = rigorous lower bound by the convex-hull property; evaluated points = rigorous upper bounds) plus a damped Gauss-Newton polish on the projection normal equations that only ever TIGHTENS the upper bound via accepted strict improvements — certification never depends on Newton converging; (2) TRIM AWARENESS: the winning (u,v) is classified against the TrimmedPatch (rationalized at 2^20); closest-point-in-hole or boundary-band hits DOWNGRADE the certificate to an infinite upper bound rather than lying — the router edge label (certified vs measured-only) is decided per input; (3) SIGN from declared B-rep orientation (du x dv outward normals; sign-aware enclosure and outward gradients) with the honest unsigned fallback NAMED in the chart ('nurbs-sdf/unsigned'); ShellSdfChart implements fs_geom::Chart with lipschitz=1; (4) TILED GENERATION, budget-aware per P4: two-pass adaptive effort (coarse everywhere, refinement within two cell diagonals of the surface), achieved bound widths and total B&B splits LEDGERED per tile as the throughput evidence. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green — ns-001 the G0 CONTAINMENT battery: 150 LCG-sampled points across EXACT revolution fixtures (unit sphere, cylinder side r=0.7 h=1.2, torus R=1 r=0.3 — the classic 9-point rational-quadratic circle construction) all satisfy lower <= analytic <= upper with worst bracket width < 5e-3; ns-002 sign follows declared orientation (inside negative, outside +0.5 exact to 1e-4, outward gradients) and Unknown stays unsigned by name; ns-003 adversarial trim: keep-northern-half trim downgrades a southern-closest query (infinite hi) while the kept region certifies at the exact analytic value; ns-004 G3: 25 translated queries agree to 1e-6; ns-005 adaptive tile: near-band widths bounded at the documented budget with near < far (effort concentrates at the surface) and splits ledgered. FIXTURE LESSONS: revolution profile direction determines normal orientation (south->north for outward); trim fixtures must avoid parameter seams and degenerate poles. Runtime kept at ~18s (the 8000-split tight-tile variant took 200s — documented as the budget trade in CONTRACT no-claims instead). clippy -D warnings clean both configs, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md extended (converter surface + no-claims: medial-axis/pole width trade, trusted sign, untrimmed lower bound), xtask check-all: fs-rep-nurbs clean (1 workspace violation is fs-cutfem, another agent's in-flight crate).

title: Converter SDF->NURBS: MLS/spline refit w/ thin-plate smoothing + measured/promoted Hausdorff bound [F]
description:
## What
Certified converter: SDF → NURBS (plan §7.3 edge 4 [F]) — MLS/spline re-fitting with thin-plate smoothing and a REPORTED, MEASURED Hausdorff bound (estimated, promoted to certified where the interval machinery can close it).

## Scope
- Fitting pipeline: extract dual-contoured surface (convert-sdf-mesh) → segment into quad-dominant patch layout → fit tensor-product B-spline patches per segment (least squares with thin-plate/bending-energy regularization, fs-la solvers) → enforce cross-patch continuity (G¹ approximate, with measured seam deviations).
- Error reporting: two-sided Hausdorff estimation via dense certified sampling (SDF gives exact distance-to-target for free — the reverse direction sampled on the fitted spline); PROMOTION path: where fs-ivl-taylor can bound spline-to-SDF distance over whole parameter boxes, the estimate upgrades to a certificate (Evidence records which).
- THE STRATEGIC ROLE: this edge is what makes the honest NURBS Boolean policy work — Booleans route through F-rep/SDF, then re-fit to splines when a spline chart is required (§7.2). It must therefore handle blend regions and near-sharp features gracefully (adaptive patch density, feature-aligned segmentation).
- Knobs as budget: patch count vs fidelity tradeoff exposed through the standard ErrBudget interface; router cost model reflects it.

## Acceptance criteria
Round-trip NURBS→SDF→NURBS on spline fixtures recovers shape within declared Hausdorff (and near-exactly for unblended regions); Boolean-then-refit end-to-end produces watertight-certified (sheaf) results on CSG fixtures; seam continuity measurements within tolerance; Evidence records estimate-vs-certificate status correctly.

## Testing
G3: refit(convert(x)) ≈ x metamorphic battery; adversarial: thin features below patch resolution → structured warning with locations (not silent smoothing). Logs: patch layouts, fit residual maps, Hausdorff tables (estimated + certified where available), seam deviation stats.
close_reason: SDF->NURBS refit implemented as fs_rep_nurbs::refit ([F]-gated behind nurbs-refit, which enables nurbs-sdf — the CSG acceptance closes the loop through my wqd.11 forward converter AND my wqd.13 sheaf certificate): (1) v1 pipeline for star-shaped domains: radial-projection sampling by BISECTION ON THE FIELD ITSELF (40 iters, each sample certified by the field's own sign change; non-star-shaped inputs get a structured teaching refusal), tensor-product B-spline LSQ with discrete thin-plate regularization (control-lattice Laplacian, hand-rolled SPD Cholesky on the control-net-sized normal equations), EXACT G0 seam closure by control-column tying with G1 measured; (2) ERROR HONESTY, the bead's core demand: the spline->SDF direction is PROMOTED to a certificate — sup|sdf(S(u,v))| <= max-sampled + (L_u+L_v)*h/2 with per-direction hodograph Lipschitz bounds L <= max||dC||*(n-p) (I initially wrote the bound with a spurious x-degree factor giving 0.63; the corrected derivative-control-point derivation closes at ~0.04) — while coverage (SDF->spline) stays a measured estimate, and the report records which is which; (3) thin features below patch resolution produce STRUCTURED WARNINGS with (u,v) + world locations and residuals, never silent smoothing; (4) patch-density knobs as the ErrBudget trade. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green in 14s — rf-001 the G3 ROUND-TRIP THROUGH THE REAL CONVERTER (exact NURBS sphere -> wqd.11 ShellSdf B&B distance -> refit): radius recovered to 5e-3 with the promoted bound closing under 8e-2 and dominating its sample; rf-002 THE STRATEGIC ACCEPTANCE: F-rep Boolean (two-sphere min-union) -> refit -> SHEAF WATERTIGHTNESS CERTIFICATE between the refit-as-ShellSdfChart and the source CSG chart PASSES at 2x the reported Hausdorff (a three-bead integration: wqd.11 + wqd.12 + wqd.13; the first run FAILED with mismatch 0.457 at coboundary_share=1.0 — the sheaf machinery itself diagnosed my parameterization's inward normals as a pure sign gauge, fixed with phi=pi(1-v) south->north); rf-003 seam G0 exact (<1e-9 gaps) + G1 < 1e-2; rf-004 a sub-patch-resolution capsule spur warns with azimuth-localized locations and max_residual > 0.1 (fixture lesson: the spur must be visible to the SAMPLE grid to be reportable — features no ray hits are invisible, documented as the coverage no-claim); rf-005 the budget knob trades monotonically (6x6 vs 16x16 control, ledgered). All fs-rep-nurbs tests green under --all-features; clippy -D warnings clean; rustfmt clean; CONTRACT.md extended (refit surface + no-claims: radial v1 with wqd.10 segmentation as upgrade path, one-sided promotion under stated SDF assumptions, sampled coverage honesty); xtask check-all: fs-rep-nurbs clean (1 workspace violation is fs-adjoint, another agent's in-flight crate). MORPH round-trip edge pair COMPLETE: NURBS->SDF->NURBS both certified.

title: Sheaf certificates: cellular-sheaf watertightness (interval-verified cocycle bound), H1 obstruction diagnostics [F/M]
description:
## What
Sheaf-theoretic consistency certificates (plan §7.3, Bet 11 [F/M]): the mathematically correct language for "locally represented, globally consistent" — and the WATERTIGHTNESS CERTIFICATE for multi-representation models.

## The construction (concrete, not decorative)
A model whose patches live in different charts is a CELLULAR SHEAF over the patch-adjacency complex: stalks = local chart/function spaces; restriction maps = trace/conversion operators to shared interfaces. Global consistency = existence of a section (H⁰). The watertightness certificate is an INTERVAL-VERIFIED bound ‖δs‖ ≤ tol on the interface mismatch cocycle. If the coboundary can't be driven below tolerance, the H¹ OBSTRUCTION is reported WITH THE OFFENDING INTERFACE CELLS ATTACHED — exactly the diagnostic an agent needs to fix a leaky model. Cellular sheaves are finite linear algebra over an adjacency complex — "the most galaxy-brained component that is nonetheless straightforwardly implementable."

## Scope
- Patch-adjacency complex construction from multi-chart Regions (interface discovery via chart-support overlap + boundary correspondence).
- Restriction-map assembly: discretized trace operators per interface (chart-pair-specific: mesh-edge sampling, spline trace, SDF interface band), assembled as sparse blocks (fs-sparse).
- Coboundary operator δ and mismatch cocycle computation; least-squares section solve (H⁰ candidate) via solver-stack/fs-la; residual → interval-verified per-interface bounds (fs-ivl).
- Certificate output: Evidence-typed watertightness verdict — PASS(‖δs‖≤tol, per-interface margins) | FAIL(H¹ obstruction, offending cells, mismatch magnitudes).
- Second consumer designed-in: the SAME machinery later computes BDDC-style domain-decomposition coarse spaces as approximate harmonic sections of the interface sheaf (solver-dd bead) — Bet 11 earning its keep twice.
- Cross-examination hook: certify-certifiers attacks this with brute-force ray-parity queries on adversarial seams.

## Acceptance criteria
On seeded multi-chart fixtures (known-watertight and known-leaky): verdicts correct with correct localization; interval verification sound (no PASS on a truly leaky seam — ray-parity cross-check); scaling to realistic patch counts (hundreds) within budget.

## Testing
G0: δδ=0 on the sheaf complex (bitwise); property: verdict invariance under patch re-indexing + rigid motions (G3). Adversarial seam zoo (near-tangent interfaces, T-junctions, mixed chart types). Logs: per-interface mismatch norms, obstruction reports with cell ids, solve diagnostics.

## ADDENDUM AMENDMENT (COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md)
Bet 11 gains **merge semantics** as a named deliverable (Proposal 10, frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.12): the SAME sheaf machinery is, unmodified, a merge-conflict classifier — the single most elegant reuse in the addendum. Three-way merge Hodge-decomposes the interface mismatch cocycle (via the sheaf Laplacian / sheaf-repair machinery frankensim-epic-flywheel-lmp4.12 reuses): the coboundary component auto-reconciles by canonical least-squares with a certificate; the harmonic component is a genuine structural conflict, localized and escalated. Merge trustworthiness is conditioned on the spectral gap (Proposal 5, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.3) — degraded-gap merges must be flagged low-confidence.
close_reason: Sheaf certificates implemented as fs_geom::sheaf (new module + tests; fs-geom gains fs-ivl/fs-sparse deps): (1) the CELLULAR SHEAF over the patch-adjacency complex — SheafComplex::from_charts discovers interfaces via support-overlap + shared zero-band rejection sampling (seeds derived from overlap-box GEOMETRY, not indices, so re-index invariance is bitwise-exact), stalks = per-patch sample values with each chart's OWN error certificate as the enclosure (NoClaim charts poison to infinite intervals -> honest Unknown only), triple junctions as 2-cells; (2) delta0/delta1 assembled as fs-sparse CSR with entries in {-1,0,+1} — delta1 * delta0 == 0 verified BITWISE on a genuine triple-junction fixture (small-integer f64 exactness); (3) the INTERVAL-VERIFIED watertightness certificate as Evidence<SheafVerdict>: PASS iff every sample's |mismatch| enclosure lies INSIDE [0,tol] decided by fs-ivl's sound encloses/contains predicates (Interval hides its bounds — no extraction, no rounding leak into the verdict; reported margins are documented midpoint±width reconstructions), FAIL = the H1 OBSTRUCTION with offending interface cells + magnitudes attached when an enclosure sits entirely above tol, else honest Unknown with straddling cells; (4) the SECTION SOLVE (per-patch gauge offsets via deterministic Gauss-Seidel on the adjacency Laplacian, gauge pinned) splitting mismatch into coboundary share vs structural residual — the merge-semantics split Proposal 10 (lmp4.12) reuses unmodified: a constant radius-offset seam reports >0.9 coboundary share and the recovered gauge equals the radius delta to 1e-3; (5) ray_parity_falsifier: the INDEPENDENT falsifier (sign-crossing counts on the union min-SDF, a different algorithm on a different code path — the registry pairing my qmao.4 standard() declares). Evidence: 5 conformance tests green — sh-001 identical charts PASS / radius leak FAILs at the named seam with ~delta magnitude; sh-002 delta-delta bitwise zero; sh-003 re-index invariance EXACT + rigid-motion invariance to 5e-3; sh-004 adversarial zoo (genuinely-overlapping distinct-box seams and near-tangent spheres never falsely certify; ray parity cross-examines the sound PASS at 4001 steps x 3 rays); sh-005 section split. Fixture debugging caught two real issues: exactly-touching supports produce an EMPTY open overlap (adversarial fixtures must genuinely overlap) and the gauge sign convention. All 24 existing fs-geom tests undisturbed. clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md extended with the sheaf surface + no-claims (point-sampler restrictions, BDDC/spectral-gap deferred to their beads), xtask check-all: fs-geom clean (the 1 workspace violation is fs-solver, another agent's in-flight crate).

title: Sheaf repair: Hodge decomposition of mismatch (exact/coexact/harmonic), bounded auto-repair, ranked proposals
description:
## What
Sheaf REPAIR (patch Rev L): upgrade sheaf machinery from diagnosis to CONSTRUCTIVE repair — H¹ obstruction reports become actionable automatic fixes where possible.

## Scope
- Hodge decomposition of the interface mismatch cochain m = exact + coexact + harmonic (discrete Hodge via the sheaf Laplacian; fs-la-eigen/solver-stack for the solves). Interpretation contract: EXACT component → repairable by local potential/patch adjustments; COEXACT → circulation-like inconsistencies, usually orientation/trace errors (points at converter bugs); HARMONIC → true topological obstruction or global inconsistency (no local fix exists — report honestly).
- Repair solvers: least-squares section projection (minimal patch-boundary adjustment killing the exact component); interval-bounded seam correction (repairs that provably stay within each chart's declared error model — a repair must never silently distort geometry beyond budget); minimal chart-conversion replanning (ask the Rep Router for an alternative chart for the worst-offending patch — "reroute SDF→mesh through interval dual contouring").
- Agent-facing repair proposals (the patch's example format): ranked actions with expected post-repair ‖δs‖ ("project NURBS patch P17 boundary control points → expected 4.1e-6 m"), costs from router models, applied only with explicit acceptance or auto-apply policy under budget.
- Obstruction localization refinement: harmonic-component support → minimal cut-set of interfaces that would need topology-level changes.

## Acceptance criteria
On seeded-defect fixtures: exact-component defects auto-repaired to certificate-passing state WITHOUT exceeding chart error budgets; coexact seeding (flipped orientation) correctly diagnosed as converter-side; harmonic seeding (genuinely mismatched topology) correctly declared unrepairable-locally with correct localization; repair proposals' predicted-vs-actual post-repair norms within stated accuracy.

## Testing
Property: repair idempotence (repairing a passing model is a no-op); repair safety (post-repair charts still agree with pre-repair within budgets — G3-style checks). Full decomposition correctness vs dense linear-algebra oracle on small complexes. Logs: decomposition fractions, per-action predictions vs outcomes, refusal reasons.
close_reason: Sheaf repair implemented as fs_geom::sheaf_repair ([M]-gated behind the sheaf-repair cargo feature until certifier trials, per the Ambition-Tag rule): (1) HODGE DECOMPOSITION m = exact + coexact + harmonic over a SheafSkeleton (extractable from any built SheafComplex or constructed directly for controlled fixtures) via deterministic Gauss-Seidel normal-equation projections onto im(d0) then im(d1^T) — VERIFIED against an independent dense Gaussian-elimination oracle (sr-001: exact component matches to 1e-8, harmonic vanishes on the contractible triangle, orthogonality residuals d0^T(h) and d1(h) < 1e-8, energy fractions partition to 1); (2) the INTERPRETATION CONTRACT with a seeded-defect fixture per component: EXACT seeding (patch gauge drift +0.012) auto-repairs to certificate-passing (<1e-9) with the predicted post-norm matching the re-measured actual to 1e-9, repair offsets provably within every chart's declared budget, IDEMPOTENT (repairing the repaired model is a no-op), and the identical defect under a tight budget is REFUSED for auto-apply with 'EXCEEDS a patch budget' teaching text (a repair must never silently distort geometry beyond budget); COEXACT seeding (pure circulation d1^T w, the flipped-orientation signature) is >99.9% coexact, diagnosed CONVERTER-side localized to the correct triple junction, and provably NOT gauge-fixable; HARMONIC seeding (circulation around a 4-patch ring with no 2-cells) is >99.9% harmonic, declared unrepairable-locally with the full-cycle interface cut-set and infinite local-cost honesty; (3) RANKED agent-facing proposals sorted by expected post-repair norm then cost, with the optional Rep-Router reroute proposal carrying the planned route and router-modeled cost (sr-005 exercises the wqd.8 dependency end-to-end: certified sdf->mesh dc-interval route at 2.0s appears in the ranking); (4) apply_gauge as the constructive step. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green (sr-001 dense oracle; sr-002 auto-repair + prediction + idempotence + budget gate; sr-003 converter-side; sr-004 honest obstruction; sr-005 router ranking); all 34 fs-geom tests green under --all-features; clippy -D warnings clean (fixed >100-line fn by extracting gauge_proposal/coexact_proposal helpers, loop-index lint via iterator); rustfmt clean; CONTRACT.md extended (repair surface + interpretation contract + no-claims: GS solves not eigen yet, constant-gauge repairs only, auto-apply policy is governance); xtask check-all: 57 crates ZERO violations. Feeds the lmp4.12 crown-jewel merge (coboundary auto-reconcile / harmonic structural conflict is exactly this split) and milestone P6.

title: Geometry queries: closest/ray/clearance/offset/Minkowski, medial-axis thickness oracle, curvature on all charts
description:
## What
Geometric queries and constructions (plan §7.4): the interrogation layer every consumer (FLUX embedding, ASCENT constraints, LUMEN) calls constantly.

## Scope
- Closest point / signed distance / ray casting uniformly across chart types (dispatching to chart-native implementations, router-converting where a chart lacks the query).
- Clearance fields: distance-to-nearest-other-body fields for assemblies (collision margins as first-class fields).
- Offsets: exact-ish via distance-field composition (φ−r on SDF charts); spline offsets via re-fit (convert-sdf-nurbs path) with reported error.
- Minkowski sums via SDF convolution / max-plus composition (tiled, FFT-assisted where applicable).
- MEDIAL AXIS via filtered Voronoi (λ- and angle-criteria filtering on the Delaunay/Voronoi machinery) — doubling as THE THICKNESS ORACLE for manufacturability constraints in ASCENT (xform-constraints' minimum-thickness primitive queries this; it must therefore be differentiable-friendly: thickness values with subgradient information where the axis is stable).
- Curvature (principal/mean/Gaussian) on EVERY chart type: analytic differentiation where the chart permits (F-rep, NURBS, neural), certified stencils otherwise (SDF grids, meshes via discrete operators) — with per-chart accuracy class documented.

## Acceptance criteria
Query results agree across chart representations of the same fixture Region within composed certificates (the multi-chart agreement discipline); medial axis on fixture shapes (rectangle, L-shape, dumbbell) matches analytic/reference within filtering tolerance; thickness oracle returns correct min-thickness on graded fixtures; curvature converges at documented order under refinement per chart type.

## Testing
G0 cross-chart agreement battery; G3 rigid-motion equivariance for all queries; adversarial: queries AT the medial axis (ties), zero-thickness necks, tangent rays. Perf: query throughput ledgered (these run in inner loops). Logs: per-query evidence, medial filtering stats, curvature convergence tables.
close_reason: Shipped fs-query, the geometry interrogation layer (plan 7.4), UNIFORM over &dyn Chart — the same queries run against analytic fixtures, F-rep CSG, tiled SDF grids, and mesh charts, held to the multi-chart AGREEMENT discipline with per-chart-certificate tolerances (gq-001: exact/frep at 2e-16, tiled within its own declared bound, mesh at faceting scale; the failing first cut taught the right lesson — the tiled chart converges to ITS zero set, so agreement tolerances ARE the charts' certificates). Surface: closest_point (damped Newton, residual MEASURED not assumed, FD-gradient fallback where charts honestly decline), raycast (conservative sphere tracing on certified Lipschitz bounds; tangent rays never tunnel; CSG never claims a hit past a dense oracle over 200 rays), OffsetChart/minkowski_ball (ball Minkowski IS the offset, bitwise; erosion exact on exact charts), ClearanceField + separation (grid+polish minimization with a RIGOROUS Lipschitz lower bound: truth provably in [lower_bound, observed], verified across gaps 1.0->0.05, field dominates separation everywhere), THE THICKNESS ORACLE (inward-normal bisection: graded slab read analytically at 1% rel, dumbbell neck = 2x neck radius exactly with 0 skips, per-sample failures counted; medial poles — lambda-filtered Delaunay circumcenters via my fs-mesh kernel — cross-check the slab core at 2r=0.4; and the DIFFERENTIABLE-FRIENDLY claim demonstrated: FD through the F-rep neck-radius design lever reads subgradient 2.0000 vs analytic 2), and curvature (mean/Gaussian/principal from tangent-restricted Hessian stencils) with a PER-CHART ACCURACY CLASS documented AND measured: order ~2 convergence on SecondOrder charts, torus principals hit 1/r and 1/(R+r), GridLimited lands within its own scale, rotation-invariant scalars. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 8 tests green, ubs clean, fs-query xtask-clean, workspace 125 suites green excluding fs-ir/fs-time (both actively claimed in-flight by gp3.5/tfz.12; fs-dfo and fs-scenario went green from their owners). No-claims: general Minkowski (non-ball) deferred, pole-based medial v1 (full filtered-Voronoi complexes follow), FD subgradients (exact adjoints = gradient stack), global-Lipschitz separation slack (fs-ivl would tighten), mesh curvature is Estimate class by design.

title: fs-mesh Delaunay: BRIO incremental on exact predicates, boundary recovery, Ruppert refinement, sliver exudation, deterministic parallel
description:
## What
`fs-mesh` Delaunay tet pipeline (plan §7.5): when body-fitted meshes are WANTED (final verification, shells, export) — remembering CutFEM-on-SDF exists precisely to make meshing optional inside optimization loops.

## Scope
- BRIO-ordered incremental Delaunay on EXACT PREDICATES (fs-ivl-predicates orient3d/insphere with symbolic perturbation — determinism on degenerate inputs): spatial-locality insertion ordering, point-location via jump-and-walk with history fallback.
- Constrained boundary recovery: edge/face insertion with Steiner points, conformity to input PLC (piecewise linear complex from chart boundaries).
- Quality refinement: Ruppert/Shewchuk-style with radius-edge bounds, local feature-size awareness (small-angle input handling documented).
- Sliver exudation via weighted-Delaunay perturbation (the standard post-pass that kills near-degenerate tets).
- Parallelism: fully tiled via DOMAIN COLORING (independent cavity insertions in non-conflicting regions; deterministic merge order per P2 — same input, same mesh, any thread count).
- Output: oriented tet complexes (fs-rep-mesh types) with boundary correspondence back to source charts (the mapping DWR/adaptivity needs).

## Acceptance criteria
Determinism: identical meshes across thread counts (G5, bitwise on connectivity + coordinates); quality: radius-edge/dihedral distributions within targets on fixture zoo (mechanical parts, thin features, CAD-ish inputs); robustness: the cospherical/collinear adversarial battery completes correctly (exact predicates earn their keep); boundary conformity verified exactly.

## Testing
G0: Delaunay property (empty circumsphere via exact predicates on final mesh — full audit mode); Euler/orientation invariants; G3 rigid-motion + relabeling invariance (up to symmetry). Scale tests (10⁷+ points) with timing into perf lane. Logs: insertion stats, recovery Steiner counts, quality histograms, exudation improvements.
close_reason: Shipped the fs-mesh Delaunay KERNEL (plan 7.5) — the bead's foundation — with remaining scope split to frankensim-uee3 (documented in CONTRACT no-claims, not silently absent). Kernel: BRIO-ordered (LCG shuffle -> doubling rounds -> Morton) incremental Bowyer-Watson on fs-ivl exact predicates with ghost tets carrying the hull. THE ROBUSTNESS CORE: conflict rules are exact, canonical, and SoS-FREE — real tets by strict insphere (cospherical Zero = not-in-conflict, the deterministic weak-Delaunay choice), ghosts by orient3d with exactly-coplanar cases delegated to in-plane exact incircle (a real bug found by the suite: the first cut used SoS for ghost conflicts, which absorbed real tets into cavities on coplanar hull cases and broke strict local Delaunay on the 4x4x4 grid — the geometric rule fixed it and made the growth-repair safety net fully dormant: 0 firings across the zoo). Exact SELF-AUDIT with the same predicates: local Delaunay on every internal facet (Delaunay lemma lifts to global), positive orientation, mutual adjacency, Euler=1, hull closed + EXACTLY convex, plus O(n*t) global empty-sphere mode. Acceptance evidence: tmesh-001 200-pt cloud 1127 tets FULL audit clean; tmesh-002 adversarial battery — integer grid, exactly-cospherical shell, collinear runs all clean, 30/30 bitwise duplicates receipted, all-coplanar refuses with teaching text (exact predicates earn their keep); tmesh-003 bitwise determinism + relabeling invariance + exact dyadic G3; tmesh-004 hull closed/2-manifold/outward (winding +1) + TetComplex dd=0; tmesh-005 radius-edge refinement: no interior-refinable offender remains, audit clean through every Steiner insert, deterministic, hull-escapers counted (450) for uee3's boundary handling; tmesh-006 10k points 66366 tets clean audits, 8.7 walk steps/insert (BRIO locality), 0 exhaustive fallbacks, suite total 1.4s. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt clean, 8 tests green, workspace 95 suites green, ubs clean; xtask single violation is fs-xform's in-flight contract (another agent). SPLIT to frankensim-uee3: constrained PLC recovery + chart correspondence, full Ruppert w/ lfs + encroachment splitting, sliver exudation, parallel domain coloring, 1e7 perf lane.

title: fs-mesh remeshing: isotropic + anisotropic-under-metric (DWR-supplied), chart-projected, deterministic
description:
## What
`fs-mesh` surface remeshing (plan §7.5): isotropic and ANISOTROPIC-UNDER-A-METRIC — the latter closing the goal-oriented adaptivity loop, because the metric tensor field is SUPPLIED BY FLUX'S ERROR ESTIMATOR (dwr-adaptivity).

## Scope
- Isotropic: incremental edge ops (split/collapse/flip/smooth) toward target edge length with feature preservation (dihedral-angle crease detection, boundary/feature-curve locking), projection back to source chart (remeshing a chart's surface must stay ON the chart within tolerance — chart-aware smoothing).
- Anisotropic: same op set measured in a Riemannian metric M(x) (SPD tensor field, interpolated from FLUX's DWR output or user-specified): edge lengths, quality, and smoothing all metric-aware; produces stretched elements aligned with solution anisotropy (boundary layers, shear bands) — the difference between 10⁶ and 10⁸ elements for the same accuracy.
- Metric handling: SPD interpolation (log-Euclidean), gradation control (bounded metric variation), intersection of metrics (multi-goal).
- Determinism: op scheduling by deterministic priority queues (P2).

## Acceptance criteria
Isotropic: on fixture surfaces, edge-length distributions concentrate at target, quality bounds met, Hausdorff-to-source within tolerance; anisotropic: on analytic metric fields (boundary-layer metric), element aspect ratios/alignments match metric prescription (measured conformity metrics), and a model adaptivity loop (metric from an analytic "error" field) demonstrably reduces interpolation error vs isotropic at equal element count.

## Testing
G0: mesh validity invariants under 10⁶ random op sequences; chart-projection drift bounds; G3 invariances. Logs: op counts by type, quality/conformity histograms, Hausdorff tracking per pass.
close_reason: Shipped fs-mesh surface remeshing (plan 7.5): the Botsch-Kobbelt split/collapse/flip/tangential-smooth loop measured in a RIEMANNIAN METRIC — isotropic IS the identity-metric special case (UniformMetric), anisotropic feeds the same four ops a spatially varying SPD tensor. Architecture: functional passes (BTree connectivity rebuilt per pass, canonical op order) — auditable and P2-bitwise-deterministic over raw throughput, documented as the right trade until the perf lane profiles it. Ops: split >4/3 with canonical orientation-preserving subdivision patterns, collapse <4/5 under link condition + no-new-long-edge + normal-flip guards, valence-6 flips with fold-over guard and live edge/valence/face bookkeeping, Jacobi tangential smoothing with area-weighted normals; EVERY placed or smoothed vertex Newton-projects onto the chart. Feature preservation: dihedral creases/boundaries/fins locked from flip+collapse, endpoints never smooth; split midpoints always project (no-op on straight creases — a real bug found by the suite: transiently mis-classified creases on smooth patches were baking in chord sagitta 3.2e-2; projecting all midpoints drove vertex drift to 1.1e-16). Evidence: tmesh-007 isotropic sphere — 96% of edges within [0.7,1.4] of unit metric length, vertex drift 1.1e-16, centroid sag 1.6e-2 (sagitta-bounded), closed/manifold/outward, BITWISE deterministic, translation-equivariant in QUALITY PROFILE (exact-topology G3 cannot hold for threshold-driven ops — honest statement in contract); tmesh-008 16 randomized remesh storms kept half-edge invariants + closed-manifold + Euler=2 after every round (11k+ ops); tmesh-009 cube — all 8 corners survive BITWISE, every crease-grade output edge lies on a cube edge line, drift <5e-3; tmesh-010 anisotropic boundary-layer metric REALIZED — metric-unit conformity, stretched equator-aligned layer elements, and the adaptivity win MEASURED: interpolation residual < 0.6x isotropic at comparable element count. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt clean, 11 fs-mesh tests green, xtask 0 violations across 27 crates, ubs clean on remesh.rs. Workspace note: fs-roofline rf_005 (another agent's timing-dispersion test) flaked once on the loaded shared worker, passes locally — commented on fz2.4. No-claims recorded: curved-crease rounding, locked boundary loops, metric gradation/log-Euclidean interpolation/intersection join FLUX's estimator bead.

title: fs-mesh hex-dominant: SH9 frame fields + MBO smoothing, polycube fallback [F, off critical path]
description:
## What
`fs-mesh` hex-dominant meshing via frame fields (plan §7.5 [F]) — HONESTLY FLAGGED as the hardest meshing deliverable in the plan (§17 risk register), OFF the critical path, mitigated by the fact that IGA and CutFEM cover most hex use-cases at higher accuracy anyway.

## Scope
- Octahedral frame-field computation: SH9 (spherical-harmonic) parameterization with MBO-style smoothing iterations; boundary alignment constraints; singularity-graph extraction and analysis.
- Volume parameterization seeded by the frame field; hex extraction from the parameterization; POLYCUBE FALLBACK for geometries where frame-field integration fails (documented decision criteria).
- Hex-dominant output: hexes + transition elements (prisms/pyramids/tets) with quality guarantees per element class; conformity to boundary features.
- Scope discipline: this bead ships behind `frontier-hexmesh`; it must NEVER block P0–P6 milestones; failure modes route users to IGA/CutFEM alternatives with an explanatory diagnostic (the honest-alternatives doctrine).

## Acceptance criteria
On the classic benchmark zoo (mechanical parts, CAD fixtures): hex-dominance fraction and scaled-Jacobian quality distributions meeting stated targets; singularity structures valid (no invalid singular curves); polycube fallback engages correctly on known-hard inputs; everything reproducible/deterministic.

## Testing
G0: frame-field smoothness/alignment energies decrease monotonically per MBO step; extracted-mesh validity invariants (positive Jacobians, conformity); comparison harness vs tet+IGA/CutFEM alternatives on shared problems (accuracy-per-DOF reported honestly — if hex loses, the report says so). Logs: singularity graphs, quality histograms, fallback decisions with reasons.
close_reason: Hex-dominant meshing shipped as fs_mesh::hexdom ([F]-gated behind frontier-hexmesh, OFF the critical path exactly as the bead demands — the honest v1 lattice tier of the plan's hardest meshing deliverable). (1) SH9 FRAME FIELDS: the band-4 parameterization realized as a FIXED SPHERICAL SAMPLING of the degree-4 octahedral polynomial — the sample vector is a LINEAR IMAGE of the SH9 coefficients so sample-space diffusion IS SH9 diffusion (exact Wigner-D machinery the named growth path); MBO iteration = graph diffusion + deterministic seeded projection to the octahedral variety — hd-001: the Dirichlet energy falls 1.064 -> 0.024 MONOTONICALLY over 3 steps with boundary frames pinned normal-aligned (G0), and the 24-element cube matching group generates exactly; (2) SINGULARITY EXTRACTION with the lesson that IS the mathematics: singularities are loop HOLONOMIES of nearest-octahedral matchings — the first fixture seeded isolated 45-degree cells and detected NOTHING because every matching snapped consistently; a quarter-turn WINDING (0/30/60/90 about z around a plaquette column) is what a singularity is, and hd-002 detects its 8 singular edges bitwise-deterministically while aligned fields stay singularity-free; (3) EXTRACTION + ROUTES (hd-003/004): the box extracts 100% hexes at scaled Jacobian EXACTLY 1.0 through the frame-field route; the twist-seeded field engages the POLYCUBE FALLBACK with its decision criteria documented in the diagnostic (singular count / energy per cell); and the hostile thin shell REFUSES with the diagnostic naming IGA and CutFEM — the honest-alternatives doctrine as a runtime string; (4) THE HONESTY HARNESS (hd-005): accuracy-per-DOF measured for trilinear hex vs linear tet at matched nodes (8.2e-4 vs 3.3e-3 on the smooth fixture — hex wins here, but the assert demands only the honest record, whichever way it falls). Evidence: 5 conformance tests green; fmt clean; clippy shows zero fs-mesh findings (the 4 reported errors are the wsbf agent's in-flight fs-sparse/perf.rs); check-all: 125 crates, zero violations; CONTRACT extended (no-claims: lattice tier only — general tet-domain fields, CubeCover parameterization, and curved-hex quality are the research core this tier deliberately does not claim).

title: fs-xform: parameterization contract (theta->Region + Jacobian action) — FFD, RBF, spline CPs, level-set velocity, SIMP fields
description:
## What
`fs-xform` design parameterizations (plan §7.6): the levers ASCENT pulls. A parameterization is a DIFFERENTIABLE MAP θ → Region shipped with its Jacobian action (dRegion/dθ as a boundary velocity field or density perturbation).

## Scope
- The Parameterization contract: apply(θ) → chart transform; jacobian_action(θ, δθ) → boundary velocity field / density perturbation (the object shape-gradient assembly consumes, §8.7); bounds/validity domain; provenance.
- Free-form deformation lattices: trivariate B-spline control lattices warping any chart (SDF sample-space warp, mesh vertex warp, NURBS control-point composition), with exact Jacobians.
- RBF morphs with compact support (Wendland kernels): scattered-handle deformation, locality control.
- Direct spline control points (NURBS charts' natural levers).
- Level-set velocity fields: θ = velocity DOFs on a narrow band (THE topology-optimization workhorse — `xform.level-set-velocity vessel :band 12mm :dof 4096` from Appendix C is this exact API).
- Density fields (SIMP): θ = per-cell densities with filtering handled downstream (topo-simp owns Helmholtz filtering; this bead owns the raw field parameterization + chain rule to charts).
- Constructive/procedural programs: F-rep DAGs with exposed parameters (fs-rep-frep integration); fs-shapeprog extends this to program synthesis.
- Composition: parameterizations compose (global manifold harmonics + local FFD near inlets — the ornithoid flagship's setup); Jacobian actions compose accordingly.

## Acceptance criteria
Every parameterization passes the Jacobian-action-vs-Dual<N> verification battery (the same gradient-gate discipline as solvers); composition Jacobians correct; level-set velocity path drives a working advection step (with topo-levelset machinery); FFD/RBF invertibility monitoring (fold-over detection with structured diagnostics).

## Testing
G0: linearity of jacobian_action in δθ; consistency apply(θ+εδθ) ≈ apply(θ)+ε·J(δθ) with convergence-order checks; G3 frame equivariance. Logs: per-lever Jacobian check tables, fold-over events, composition graphs.
close_reason: fs-xform parameterizations v1 landed (plan 7.6): the levers ASCENT pulls, as a new L2 crate. THE CONTRACT: Parameterization trait — dof / apply / jacobian_action (dT/dtheta·dtheta, the boundary velocity field shape-gradient assembly consumes) / spatial_jacobian (fold-over monitoring + composition chain rule); contract law T(theta+eps·dtheta)(x) − T(theta)(x) = eps·action + o(eps), exact for the linear levers. LEVERS: FfdLattice (trivariate Bernstein Sederberg-Parry FFD; exact basis-contraction Jacobians, analytic spatial gradients via Bernstein derivatives, pass-through outside the box with documented boundary-continuity responsibility); RbfMorph (Wendland-C2 compact support, radial hence frame-equivariant, smooth at handle centers); VelocityBand (narrow-band trilinear normal-velocity DOFs — Appendix C's xform.level-set-velocity — plus advect_sdf, a Godunov upwind step); DensityField (raw SIMP densities with clamp diagnostics naming the offending component; filtering stays downstream per the bead). COMPOSITION: Composed with chain-rule Jacobian actions through the spatial Jacobian and matrix-product spatial Jacobians (the ornithoid global+local setup). ACCEPTANCE PROVEN: Jacobian-action-vs-Dual battery — the FFD warp re-expressed over fs-ad Dual64 JVPs agrees with jacobian_action to 1e-12 (the solvers' gradient-gate discipline); exact-secant + delta-theta-linearity battery for FFD/RBF over seeded random configurations; composition chain rule verified with measured second-order FD convergence; the level-set velocity path DRIVES a working advection step (unit-speed band grows a sphere SDF at the expected rate); fold-over monitoring refuses structurally with location+determinant (probed through the compression side — where folds actually form); G3 rotation equivariance for the radial lever to 1e-14. Module unit tests: Bernstein partition-of-unity + derivative-sum laws, Wendland shape/support (including the −0.0 exact-zero subtlety), band masking, identity lattices, teaching diagnostics. 6 unit + 6 conformance green; clippy -D warnings clean (including the newer-nightly manual_assert_eq lints from CI drift); fmt clean; xtask policy OK across 27 crates. No-claims in CONTRACT.md: manifold-harmonic/neural/spline/F-rep levers are follow-ups on this trait; fold-over detection is sampling-based monitoring (certified invertibility needs fs-ivl interval Jacobians); advect_sdf is first-order full-grid (WENO/narrow-band/redistancing are topo-levelset's).

title: fs-rep-sdf: tiled SDF grids (f32/f64), FrankenVDB sparse tree, adaptive octree SDF, narrow-band
description:
## What
`fs-rep-sdf`: signed-distance-field charts (plan §7.2) — dense tiled grids, the FrankenVDB sparse hierarchy, adaptively-sampled octree SDFs, and narrow-band mode.

## Scope
- Dense tiled grids: Morton 8³ tiles (tile-layout crate), f32 STORAGE / f64 EVALUATION convention, tricubic/triquadratic interpolation with continuous gradients (C¹ matters: shape optimization differentiates through samples), certified interpolation-error model vs declared band.
- **FrankenVDB**: in-house sparse hierarchical tile tree — hash root → 32³ internal nodes → 8³ bitmasked leaves — for huge sparse domains; iteration, topology ops (dilate/erode of active set), coordinate transforms; THE shared spatial substrate also used by LBM lattices (§8.3), CutFEM background grids (§8.1), and voxel charts.
- Adaptively-sampled SDF: octree with per-cell polynomial fits, error-controlled refinement (fit residual bounds ledgered) — compact representation for smooth-but-feature-bearing shapes.
- Hash-grid variant for point-cloud-adjacent workflows.
- Narrow-band mode for level-set evolution (topo-levelset): band maintenance, reinitialization hooks, velocity-extension scaffolding.
- Chart impl: eval (distance + gradient + Lipschitz≈1 with quantified deviation from perfect SDF — the "how much is this NOT a distance field" certificate, feeding sphere-tracing safety), curvature via certified stencils, ray intersection via sphere tracing against own bounds.

## Acceptance criteria
Analytic-SDF fixtures (sphere/box/torus/blends) reproduced within declared interpolation error; FrankenVDB: random access O(1)-ish verified, sparse iteration matches dense reference on masked domains, memory footprint targets on 10⁹-voxel-class sparse domains; narrow-band evolution mass/shape drift bounded on advection fixtures.

## Testing
G0: gradient-vs-FD, eikonal-residual statistics (|∇φ|−1 distributions logged), tile-boundary continuity (no seams — exact equality of shared-face samples). Property: VDB set/get/iterate vs BTreeMap oracle. Perf: sample throughput into roofline lane. Logs: per-fixture error histograms, band statistics, VDB topology stats.
close_reason: Implemented fs-rep-sdf (L2): four SDF representations under one honesty discipline. (1) TiledSdf: dense Morton-tiled grids on fs_substrate::TiledField, f32 STORAGE/f64 EVALUATION, C1 triquadratic B-spline reconstruction (continuous analytic gradients — shape optimization differentiates through samples); constructed error enclosure 2*L*h+f32-quant, conservative and PROVEN by containment over 12k seeded fixture points (rsdf-001 worst err/bound <= 1.0); seamless across tile boundaries with gradients matching central FD (rsdf-002); measured eikonal |grad phi|-1 stats ledgered as EVIDENCE never certificate (rsdf-003); sphere-traced raycast stepping (sd-bound)/lipschitz — never tunnels by construction, hits match analytic within bound, cancellable (rsdf-006); teaching refusals (NoLipschitzBound, ResolutionTooFine w/ 512/axis cap). (2) FrankenVDB VdbGrid<T>: BTreeMap root -> 32^3 internal (sparse children) -> 8^3 bitmasked leaves; set/get/deactivate, DETERMINISTIC total iteration order, face-6 dilate/erode, layout-derived footprint stats ledgered; EXACT agreement with a BTreeMap oracle over ~12.5k clustered+stray actives incl. misses (rsdf-004). (3) NarrowBand on the VDB: cancellable build from any chart, trilinear interpolation, semi-Lagrangian advect with band dilate+trim, Godunov-upwind reinitialization w/ smoothed sign; sphere translated 6 steps -> zero-crossing drift 0.0025 (<0.15 bound), reinit recovers a deliberately distorted field better than halved (rsdf-005). (4) AdaptiveSdf octree: per-cell trilinear fits, residual-driven refinement, cells/depth/residual ledgered, Estimate-grade error honestly labeled (rsdf-006). ALSO repaired 5 committed-on-main clippy errors in fs-geom/src/router.rs (the wqd.8 agent's janitor-committed WIP: NaN-preserving comparison rewrites + associated-fn conversions) that blocked every dependent crate's clippy lane. Lanes green: clippy -D warnings (fs-geom+fs-rep-sdf), fmt, fs-rep-sdf 1 lib + 6 conformance, workspace 71 suites, xtask 0 violations (20 crates), ubs 0 criticals. No-claims: hash-grid variant deferred to wqd.6 (its consumer); WENO/fast-iterative redistancing are topo-levelset's; eikonal/adaptive residuals are measured statistics until fs-ivl integration; perf rooflines are the perf harness's; 10^9-voxel footprint scaled in CI with per-active overhead ledgered.

title: fs-xform manifold harmonics: Laplace-Beltrami eigenbasis shape spectrum for global search [F]
description:
## What
Manifold-harmonic shape bases (plan §7.6 [F]): eigenfunctions of the Laplace–Beltrami operator ON THE CURRENT SHAPE, giving an automatically smooth, hierarchical, low-dimensional "shape spectrum" ideal for CMA-ES-scale global search.

## Scope
- Laplace–Beltrami assembly on surface meshes (cotan weights, robust variants for bad triangles) and on SDF narrow bands (implicit surface Laplacian) — the basis must be available WITHOUT requiring a body-fitted mesh.
- Eigenbasis computation via fs-la-eigen LOBPCG (smallest-k eigenpairs, k ~ 50–300); deflation of rigid/constant modes; deterministic eigenvector sign/ordering convention (P2 — CMA-ES coordinates must mean the same thing across runs).
- Parameterization: θ = spectral coefficients; displacement field = Σ θᵢ ψᵢ · n̂ (normal displacement) or full vector variants; Jacobian action trivially the basis functions (the smoothness is FREE preconditioning — low-frequency exploration first, the reason this pairs with CMA-ES global search; the ornithoid flagship uses ~200 coefficients for global form).
- Basis refresh policy: recompute on large shape changes (drift criterion), with coefficient transfer between bases (projection) so optimizers survive refreshes; refresh events ledgered.

## Acceptance criteria
Spectra match analytic references on sphere/torus fixtures (within discretization error, convergence verified); basis smoothness (Dirichlet energy ordering) holds; coefficient-transfer preserves shape within tolerance across refresh; end-to-end: a 100-coefficient CMA-ES toy shape-matching study converges markedly faster than raw-vertex parameterization (the demonstrable payoff, ledgered).

## Testing
G0: eigenpair residuals, orthonormality in the right inner product (mass matrix); G3 isometry invariance of spectra (bent vs flat developable fixtures); refresh-transfer round-trip. Logs: spectra, refresh events, study comparison curves.
close_reason: Manifold harmonics shipped as fs_xform::harmonics ([F]-gated behind manifold-harmonics; fs-la becomes an optional dep): the Laplace-Beltrami shape spectrum for CMA-ES-scale global search. (1) ASSEMBLY: cotan Laplacian with the robust obtuse-triangle clamp + barycentric lumped mass; (2) EIGENPAIRS: lumped-mass symmetrization M^-1/2 L M^-1/2 fed matrix-free to fs-la LOBPCG via shift-and-negate (Gershgorin shift keeps the largest-mode path exact for smallest-k); numeric kernel deflated and the CONSTANT re-admitted ANALYTICALLY as mode 0 — found the hard way: the ES study stalled at a 0.18 misfit floor because uniform inflation (the mean of any bump field) was deflated away with the kernel; inflation is a design direction, so theta_0 = uniform normal offset; (3) DETERMINISM (P2): ascending-eigenvalue ordering + largest-|entry|-positive sign convention — CMA-ES coordinate j means the same thing across runs; (4) PARAMETERIZATION: displace = normal displacement sum theta_j psi_j n-hat; REFRESH: needs_refresh bbox-relative drift criterion + M-weighted coefficient transfer (project/transfer) so optimizers survive basis recomputation. CONFORMANCE (5 suites): mh-001 the icosphere spectrum hits the ANALYTIC l(l+1) ladder — 2 (x3), 6 (x5) — within 6% with residuals <1e-5, M-orthonormality to 5e-3, torus fundamental self-converges; mh-002 eigenvalues ascend and each mode's Dirichlet energy EQUALS its eigenvalue (smoothness ordering: low coefficients are provably the smooth directions); mh-003 G3 isometry — flat vs isometrically bent strip spectra match (to the documented ~1e-4 robust-clamp tolerance); mh-004 zero-field transfers to zero, self-transfer round-trips the displaced shape to 5e-3, drift criterion gates refreshes; mh-005 THE DEMONSTRABLE PAYOFF, ledgered: seeded (1+1)-ES shape matching at equal 500-eval budget with dimension-normalized mutations — 18 spectral coefficients reach misfit 0.0017 vs 66 raw vertex amplitudes at 0.0487, a 27.9x ratio: smoothness is free preconditioning, exactly the pairing-with-CMA-ES story the bead names. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green (~49s); clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; CONTRACT extended (no-claims: clamp trades consistency for stability, SDF narrow-band variant is follow-up, LOBPCG estimates not enclosures, transfer assumes refresh-in-place).

title: Geometric constraint primitives: min-thickness (medial), draft, symmetry-by-quotient, envelopes, volume/mass
description:
## What
Differentiable/certifiable geometric constraint primitives (plan §7.6): the manufacturability and design-intent constraints ASCENT enforces, each first-class and shipped with derivatives or certificates.

## Scope
- Minimum thickness via the MEDIAL ORACLE (geom-queries): differentiable-ish thickness constraint (smooth aggregation of medial radii with subgradient handling at axis instabilities) — the anti-paperclip constraint.
- Draft angle: surface-normal-vs-pull-direction constraints for casting/molding (smooth penalty + exact violation regions).
- Symmetry groups ENFORCED BY CONSTRUCTION — quotient the parameterization by the group (reflection/cyclic/translational): constraint violation is structurally impossible rather than penalized (design levers act on the quotient; the plan's stated preferred mechanism).
- Bounding envelopes: containment constraints (design stays inside allowed region / outside keep-outs) via SDF composition with certified margins.
- Volume/mass constraints: exact-ish integrals over charts (per-chart quadrature with error models) with shape derivatives (Hadamard formulas for volume are the textbook warm-up — also the validation case).
- Every primitive declares: differentiability class, certificate availability, and its fs-constraint (ASCENT) ConstraintKind mapping — geometry supplies the values+derivatives; ASCENT owns the constraint SEMANTICS.

## Acceptance criteria
Each primitive's derivative verified vs Dual<N>/FD; thickness constraint correctly flags and localizes violations on graded fixtures; symmetry quotient provably yields symmetric shapes for arbitrary θ (property test); volume derivative matches Hadamard analytic on fixtures.

## Testing
G0 per primitive; integration: a toy optimization drives each constraint to feasibility (the smoke test that derivatives point the right way). Logs: violation maps, derivative check tables, quotient-group audit.
close_reason: Shipped fs-geocon, the geometric constraint primitives (plan 7.6) — each FIRST-CLASS with a value, derivative story, certificate story, and fs-constraint kind mapping (GeoPrimitive::descriptor is the declared table, gcp-006). MIN-THICKNESS (anti-paperclip): mean p-norm aggregation of fs-query oracle samples — soft_min is the C1 optimizer value (over-approx converging DOWN, exact on uniform samples so lever derivatives stay clean: neck FD reads 2.000 vs analytic 2), hard_min + LOCALIZED violation list are the ledger verdicts, and the closed loop is demonstrated: toy descent drives the dumbbell neck from r=0.15 to feasibility >=0.5 (gcp-001). DRAFT: hinge^2 smooth penalty + exact violating regions; 10-degree wall passes 5 fails 15 fully localized; vertical walls fail any positive draft; a mushroom shoulder flags as UNDERCUT distinct from low draft; FD matches the analytic hinge slope (gcp-002). SYMMETRY BY QUOTIENT (the plan's preferred mechanism): fold-to-fundamental-domain charts make violation STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE — invariance verified for arbitrary asymmetric inner designs across 12 random levers x 3 groups (reflection bitwise, cyclic/periodic fp-scale) x full orbits, gradients chain through the fold (gcp-003). ENVELOPES: containment + keep-outs with a SUM-FORM LSE aggregate proven conservative within ln(n)/beta — a real bug found: the mean-form softmax UNDER-approximated and descent stopped 0.12 short of feasibility; sum-form drives the true worst to zero (gcp-004). VOLUME: rigorous enclosure (sure-inside vs Lipschitz band, truth in [lo,hi], tightens with h) over an EXPLICIT domain — second real bug: integrating over chart.support() made the grid realign with the lever, biasing dV/dr 17%; a fixed domain reads Hadamard 4pi r^2 within 2% — plus descent to a volume cap (gcp-005).
