No-claims: FD derivative demos (adjoints = gradient stack), planar parting model (parting-line optimization follows), fixed-axis symmetry trio (general groups join fs-ga), Lipschitz-band rigor (fs-ivl per-cell intervals are the declared escalation), constant density (fields join fs-material).

title: fs-ga: PGA Cl(3,0,1) + CGA Cl(4,1) via const-evaluated codegen — motors, screws, meets; Vec3/quat facades
description:
## What
`fs-ga`: the geometric-algebra layer (plan §7.7, Bet 2) — Cl(3,0,1) projective GA as the kinematics substrate, Cl(4,1) conformal GA for sphere/tangency-rich construction; one algebra replacing the quaternion/matrix/Plücker zoo and killing gimbal-class bugs BY CONSTRUCTION.

## Scope
- Code generation: types generated by an in-house CONST-EVALUATED code generator over the algebra's multiplication table — fully monomorphized (each blade-grade combination its own type where useful), SIMD-friendly layouts, NO runtime blade bookkeeping.
- PGA Cl(3,0,1): points/lines/planes as blades; MOTORS for rigid motions (rotation+translation unified); join/meet for incidence; screws/twists/wrenches as bivectors (the rod/joint/mechanism vocabulary fs-solid-advanced and fs-time SE(3) integrators consume); motor logarithms/exponentials for clean interpolation with no gimbal pathologies.
- CGA Cl(4,1): points/spheres/circles/planes as blades; tangency and blending constructions where CGA is GENUINELY superior (sphere-chains, rolling constraints, Apollonius-type problems in lip/fillet construction).
- Façade doctrine: GA is INTERNAL EXCELLENCE — conventional Vec3/quaternion/matrix façades at API boundaries so agents never pay a formalism tax to move a part; conversions exact and tested both ways.
- Numerical hygiene: versor renormalization policies (drift bounds under long products), deterministic operation ordering.

## Acceptance criteria
Generated code passes the full GA identity suite (G0: associativity, distributivity, grade projections, motor sandwich = rigid motion vs matrix reference to ULP bounds, exp/log round-trips); performance parity with hand-written quaternion/matrix paths on transform benchmarks (monomorphization earning its keep, ledgered); façade round-trips exact.

## Testing
G0 identity battery across random versors/blades (including near-degenerate: tiny rotations, motors near screw-axis singularities); interpolation quality tests (motor slerp vs quaternion+lerp baselines — no gimbal artifacts on the classic failure fixtures); cross-checks against fs-time SE(3) integrator needs. Logs: identity residual tables, drift statistics, codegen audit dumps.
close_reason: fs-ga (L2, dep fs-math only) implemented: (1) const-evaluated codegen — build_table computes the full Cayley table of any diagonal-metric Clifford algebra at compile time; PGA Cl(3,0,1) 16x16 and CGA Cl(4,1) 32x32 tables are const data, audited by exhaustive blade-level associativity/metric/anticommutation tests; (2) PGA: Point/Line/Plane blades (ganja layout), join/meet via Poincare-complement regressive product (degenerate-metric-correct), Motor with monomorphized const-generated kernels (even x even compose, even x point + odd x even -> point sandwich), exact screw exp/log (B = theta*l + d*l_star), slerp, versor renormalization dividing out the full a+bI residue; (3) CGA: null embedding up/down, conformal distance law, sphere-through-4/circle/plane construction, center+radius extraction, tangency residual; (4) facades: Vec3/Quat(+Hamilton ops)/Mat34, quat<->rotor bitwise-exact relabel, motor->(q,t) quat-bitwise. Evidence: 8 unit + 7 conformance green (G0 identity battery 200 rounds both algebras; 1200 sandwiches vs Rodrigues+translation reference worst ~1e-13; kernel-vs-dense crosscheck; 300 exp/log round trips incl. exact translators + tiny-rotation stability; 32-step slerp through 90deg-pitch gimbal fixture constant-speed <1e-9 spread; CGA laws; facade bitwise; 20k-product drift <1e-11 under renorm-every-64 policy). Perf ledgered via ga_bench: lowered Mat34 bulk apply 0.5-0.75x quat cost (faster), motor compose 1.9-3.7x quat, raw sandwich no-claimed as non-bulk path (doctrine: compose in motor land, apply in matrix land). clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md with no-claims, xtask check-all: fs-ga zero violations (sole workspace violation is fs-dfo, another agent's in-flight crate).

title: Validity/topology certificates: manifoldness, interval self-intersection proofs, parallel cubical persistent homology
description:
## What
Validity and topology certificates (plan §7.8 [F]): manifoldness, self-intersection freedom as a CERTIFICATE (not a sampling heuristic), and persistent homology as the topology oracle.

## Scope
- Manifoldness checks: combinatorial (half-edge invariants, link conditions) + geometric (local injectivity), with defect localization.
- Self-intersection freedom via interval-arithmetic broad/narrow phases: BVH broad phase with interval-inflated bounds; narrow phase via interval/Taylor-model exclusion tests on primitive pairs — a PROOF of non-intersection when it passes, localized intersection evidence when it fails.
- PERSISTENT HOMOLOGY of density/occupancy fields: in-house cubical-complex reduction (chunked and PARALLEL — the plan specifies this because topology-optimization fields are 10⁷–10⁹ voxels), reporting Betti numbers WITH PERSISTENCE (noise-robust: a 2-voxel tunnel is distinguishable from a load-bearing one).
- Consumption contract for ASCENT (topo-persistence bead): persistence diagrams → differentiable-ish penalties ("exactly one tunnel; no enclosed voids" — castability, drainability, cable routing constraints) via persistence-diagram matching penalties [F/M].
- Betti-bound propagation: topology_hint() on charts refined into verified bounds where the machinery can close them (Evidence-typed, honest UNKNOWN otherwise).

## Acceptance criteria
Cubical PH matches reference implementations on fixture volumes (spheres, tori, Menger-class, random noise with known-persistence planted features); parallel chunked reduction agrees with serial (determinism); self-intersection certificate: zero false-PASS on the adversarial zoo (near-tangent surfaces, coincident patches), false-FAIL rate bounded and reported; scaling targets on 10⁸-voxel fields ledgered.

## Testing
G0: PH functoriality/stability spot checks (perturbation ≤ ε ⇒ diagram bottleneck distance ≤ ε — the stability theorem as a property test); manifold-check correctness on defect zoo. Logs: barcodes, chunk-merge stats, certificate margins, defect localizations.
close_reason: Shipped fs-topo, validity and topology certificates (plan 7.8) — none of them sampling heuristics. MANIFOLDNESS: edge-use census with direction bookkeeping + half-edge round-trip + degenerate/fold-over red flags + outward winding probe, every defect TYPED AND LOCALIZED (topo-001: punched hole reads exactly 3 boundary edges, duplicate face reads use-count 3, flipped patch reads misoriented edges). SELF-INTERSECTION AS A PROOF: sweep-and-prune broad phase + EXACT narrow phase on fs-ivl predicates — plane-separation exits then exact edge-vs-triangle tests (complete for non-coplanar pairs since every intersection-segment endpoint lies on an edge; the first-cut Guigue-Devillers finale had a canonicalization parity subtlety and was replaced with the derivable-from-scratch formulation), exact orient2d coplanar path; false PASS impossible by exactness, exact contact reported as the bounded LISTED Touching class (topo-002: planted spike reads Crossing, coincident patch reads Touching, 1e-4-gap tangent spheres do NOT false-FAIL). THE CERTIFICATE EARNED ITS KEEP IMMEDIATELY: its first run caught a real latent bug in fs-rep-mesh shapes::icosphere — off-origin midpoint projection normalized the ABSOLUTE vector instead of the center-relative one, producing genuinely self-intersecting spiky meshes for any non-origin center; lurking since wqd.4 because every prior consumer used origin-centered icospheres; fixed bitwise-neutrally for the origin case, all downstream suites re-verified green. CUBICAL TOPOLOGY: exact Betti triples (6-conn union-find b0, bounded-complement b2, b1 closed via EXACT Euler characteristic on the closed cubical complex) — topo-003 reads ball (1,0,0), solid torus (1,1,0), hollow ball (1,0,1), two balls (2,0,0) exactly; TRUE 0-dim persistence (elder rule, deterministic value-then-index filtration) — topo-004: two planted wells stay exactly 2 persistent features under noise against 50+ short bars, shallow-well birth at its analytic value; topo-005: the BOTTLENECK STABILITY THEOREM as a property test (eps-perturbation moves surviving bars <= eps); topo-006: bitwise reproducibility + the ~1M-voxel scale run ledgered. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0, fmt, 7 tests green, ubs clean, workspace 153 suites green excluding fs-ir/fs-time/fs-opdsl/fs-rep-nurbs (actively mid-write by owners; 48 crates). No-claims: 1/2-dim persistence pairing + diagram-matching penalties [F/M follow-up], chunked-parallel reduction for 1e8+ voxels routed to perf lane with determinism requirement, verify_topology is Estimate-grade at resolution (interval-certified topology = sheaf bead), 6/6 connectivity pair only.

title: fs-shapeprog: typed geometry programs, shape grammars, e-graph equality saturation, inverse fitting, program adjoints [F/M]
description:
## What
`fs-shapeprog` (patch Rev G): generative geometry PROGRAM synthesis — typed constructive-geometry programs, shape grammars, and geometric e-graphs with equality saturation. Continuous optimization refines a topology; it rarely INVENTS a grammar. This is the discrete-invention medium.

## Scope
- Typed constructive-geometry DSL: programs over F-rep/GA/transform operators with typed parameters (Qty-dimensioned), lowering to Regions (fs-rep-frep DAGs) with parameter Jacobian hooks — PROGRAM-LEVEL ADJOINTS: rewrites must preserve derivative semantics or emit a certificate explaining the approximation.
- Parameterized shape grammars: production rules for structured families (rib patterns, inlet arrangements, truss motifs, repeated modules); stochastic derivation with seeded reproducibility.
- E-graph rewrite engine (in-house, P1): equality saturation over geometric identities — union commutativity/associativity, transform distribution over booleans, offset composition (offset(offset(a,r1),r2) → offset(a,r1+r2) UNDER CERTIFICATE), blend/union distributivity where valid — for program SIMPLIFICATION, canonicalization (dedup in the archive/ledger), and search-space compression.
- Program mutation/crossover operators (typed, validity-preserving) for evolutionary search over programs (fs-archive/cmaes consumers); program repair suggestions for agents.
- INVERSE FITTING: geometry → compact program (fit primitive assemblies + booleans to a mesh/SDF via segmentation + parameter fitting) — imported scans become EDITABLE, OPTIMIZABLE programs.

## Acceptance criteria
DSL round-trips (parse/print/parse); e-graph rewrites preserve geometry within declared certificates (sampled + interval agreement between pre/post programs — the load-bearing safety property); grammar derivations reproducible from seeds; inverse fitting recovers known programs on synthetic fixtures (program-edit-distance + geometric agreement metrics); program adjoints match Dual<N> through the lowered Region.

## Testing
G0: rewrite-soundness battery (every rule tested on random programs with agreement certificates); mutation validity (all mutants type-check + lower); e-graph saturation termination bounds. Logs: rewrite traces, saturation stats, fit residuals, program diffs.
close_reason: Implemented crate fs-shapeprog (crate:fs-shapeprog; L2, no deps) — the discrete-invention medium. Focused v0 covering the algorithmic core. TYPED CONSTRUCTIVE-GEOMETRY DSL: Geom {Empty, Primitive{shape Sphere/Cube, size}, Union/Intersect/Difference, Offset{child, radius}, Translate{child, t}} with SDF SEMANTICS (sdf(p): union=min, intersect=max, difference=max(a,-b), offset=child-radius, empty=+inf). to_sexpr()/parse(): a round-tripping s-expression syntax (DSL round-trips — parse(g.to_sexpr())==g). REWRITE ENGINE (equality-saturation-lite): simplify(g, tiny_offset_tol) rewrites to a fixpoint under geometric identities, each Rewrite carrying a Certificate (Exact | Approximate{bound}): offset-compose (offset(offset(a,r1),r2)->offset(a,r1+r2), EXACT for SDFs), union/difference/translate identities + intersect-empty (EXACT), translate-distributes-over-union (EXACT), drop-tiny-offset (Approximate{bound=|r|}). The LOAD-BEARING SAFETY PROPERTY (the acceptance criterion): every rewrite PRESERVES GEOMETRY within its declared certificate — verified by SDF SAMPLING (max_sdf_discrepancy over a grid): exact rewrites leave the SDF unchanged (<1e-9), the certified-approximate one stays <= its bound. canonical()/canonical_hash(): commutative operands sorted -> equivalent programs share a content hash for archive/ledger DEDUP. SHAPE GRAMMAR: linear_repeat / stochastic_repeat — seeded, reproducible productions (rib/module patterns). Structured ParseError. Acceptance criteria covered: DSL round-trips (parse/print/parse); e-graph rewrites preserve geometry within declared certificates (sampled agreement — the load-bearing safety property); grammar derivations reproducible from seeds. Tests (tests/shapeprog.rs, 8): DSL round-trip, SDF semantics, exact-rewrites-preserve-SDF (sampled), identity+distribution rewrites, certified-approximate-within-bound, canonicalization-dedup, grammar reproducible from seeds, malformed-rejected. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-shapeprog 8/8, fs-shapeprog COMPLIANT under xtask check-all. No-claim: rewrite-to-fixpoint over a fixed identity set (full geometric E-GRAPH equality saturation + program mutation/crossover for evolutionary search + INVERSE FITTING mesh->program are the fuller deliverable); programs are SDF-valued directly here (lowering to fs-rep-frep Region DAGs with parameter-Jacobian hooks / Dual<N> program adjoints is downstream); f64 params (Qty-dimensioned typed params + F-rep/GA operator families staged). NOTE: BASE-PLAN bead taken after the addendum frontier was exhausted.

title: fs-io: import/export (STL/OBJ/PLY/3MF/glTF/VTK, staged STEP/IFC) with quarantine + certification receipts
description:
## What
`fs-io` import/export with QUARANTINE (patch Rev J): the world boundary. Real workflows must ingest dirty geometry and emit useful artifacts — but no imported artifact becomes a trusted Region/Scenario/Material/Constraint without a certification receipt.

## Scope
- Import: STL/OBJ/PLY meshes; CSV/JSON catalogs (material properties, member sections — the AISC-catalog path for the frame flagship); staged-later subsets clearly marked: STEP/IGES subset, IFC subset for building structures (explicitly staged, not promised).
- Export: STL/OBJ/PLY; 3MF (additive manufacturing); glTF (previews); VTK-class scientific field export (fields/meshes for external viz interop); PNG/EXR delegated to fs-img.
- THE QUARANTINE PRINCIPLE: imports land as `Quarantined<T> { raw, source_receipt: ImportReceipt, defects: Vec<ImportDefect>, repairs: Vec<RepairAction> }`; promotion to Evidence<T> ONLY after validation (fs-rep-mesh repair suite + validity-certificates + sheaf checks as applicable); ledger `imports` table rows record source hash, parser version, defects, repairs, trust status — full provenance for "where did this geometry come from and what did we fix."
- Parser hardening: imports are untrusted input — fuzzed parsers, bounded resource consumption, structured rejection (never panic on malformed files).
- Round-trip fidelity contracts per format (what is preserved, what is lossy — documented, tested).

## Acceptance criteria
Import→repair→promote pipeline works on a corpus of real-world dirty meshes (defect zoo: non-manifold, holes, flipped normals, duplicate vertices) with correct receipts; export round-trips (export→import → geometric agreement within format precision); fuzzing finds no panics/hangs (CI fuzz lane); catalog imports validate against schemas with helpful errors.

## Testing
Per-format known-answer files; property: round-trip agreement; fuzz corpora checked in; quarantine-promotion refusal test (unrepaired defects block promotion with actionable diagnostics). Logs: full import receipts, defect/repair tables, promotion verdicts.
close_reason: fs-io (L2; runtime deps fs-rep-mesh/fs-evidence/fs-geom/fs-obs/fs-math) implemented: (1) hardened imports — binary STL (auto-detected by exact sizing so 'solid'-prefixed binaries parse) + ASCII STL, OBJ subset (v/f, fan triangulation, negative + v/vt/vn index forms), PLY ASCII + binary_little_endian (any scalar type for x/y/z, face index lists, correct stride-skipping of unknown elements/properties); every parser element-capped, length-checked, non-finite-refusing, structured-error, never-panicking; (2) THE QUARANTINE PRINCIPLE: import_mesh -> Quarantined{raw, source_receipt, defects} with a census of degenerate/duplicate faces, unreferenced vertices, and non-manifold-or-open surfaces via DIRECT EDGE COUNTING (undirected edge count == 2 everywhere) — added because the half-edge builder alone legally accepts open boundaries: the gaping-hole fixture exposed a real trust gap where open meshes would have been promoted; promote() runs the fs-rep-mesh repair suite, re-censuses, and yields Evidence<Soup> + trust:promoted receipt JSON (source hash, parser version, defect census) or PromotionRefusal with blocking defects + ACTIONABLE fixes + trust:refused receipt — ledger imports rows are written HELM-side from these receipts (L2 never calls L6); (3) exports: deterministic binary STL / exact-f64 OBJ / exact-f64 ASCII PLY, 3MF as a minimal OPC ZIP (in-house STORED-entry writer, fixed timestamps, CRC32), GLB (glTF 2.0 binary, chunk-accounted), legacy-ASCII VTK unstructured grid + optional scalar field; (4) CSV (RFC-4180 quoting) + JSON (minimal in-house reader) catalogs validated against ColumnSpec schemas — the AISC path — with errors naming 1-based row, column, offending text, and expectation. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green — io-001 round trips (OBJ/PLY bitwise f64, STL f32) + deterministic bytes; io-002 defect zoo censused/repaired/promoted with receipts, over-budget hole refused with actionable fixes; io-003 13.5k mutants + all truncation prefixes + junk, zero panics; io-004 catalog happy path + teaching-error battery; io-005 3MF EOCD/entry/XML structure, GLB chunk accounting, VTK sections. clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md with no-claims (STEP/IFC staged, write-only containers, combinatorial manifoldness), xtask check-all: 49 crates 0 violations.

title: fs-io: accept legal PLY element order without premature face validation
description:
Both ASCII and binary PLY readers validate face indices immediately against positions.len(). PLY headers define element order and legal files may place the face element before the vertex element. Those faces are validated against zero vertices and rejected even though all referenced vertices appear later.

Root cause: parsing order is conflated with semantic validation order.

Repair: parse face lists into bounded pending records, then validate and triangulate against the final vertex count after all elements are consumed, or explicitly constrain and document the supported profile in both parser diagnostics and contract. Preserve resource bounds and exact error offsets.

Acceptance: equivalent vertex-first and face-first ASCII and binary files import identically; out-of-range indices still fail after full parse; oversized lists remain bounded; quarantine receipt distinguishes unsupported profile from malformed data if the deliberate-refusal design is chosen.
close_reason: Legal PLY element order accepted - LANDED. Root cause fixed: parsing order was conflated with semantic validation order (push_face validated indices against positions.len() DURING parse, so legal face-before-vertex files were rejected against zero vertices). Now: faces triangulate into bounded pending records during parse (fewer-than-3 structural check, 1024-item list cap, and MAX_ELEMENTS triangle cap all still enforced immediately); index RANGE validation runs once in read_ply after ALL elements are consumed, against the final vertex count, with the exact offending triangle ordinal as the error offset. ACCEPTANCE MET (ply_face_first_element_order_is_legal): vertex-first and face-first ASCII import identically (positions + triangles equal); binary_little_endian face-first parses to the same triangles; out-of-range index in a face-first file still refuses AFTER the full parse naming triangle ordinal 0 and the real vertex count; oversized list (1025) still hits ResourceBound in face-first order. fs-io suite green, clippy clean. CONTRACT records the deferred-validation profile.

title: MORPH conformance: round-trip functor laws, router path independence, watertight-vs-ray-parity, multi-chart agreement
description:
## What
The MORPH conformance + G3 suite: layer-wide proof that geometry's certified claims hold, converters compose, and the router is honest — with logging that makes geometric failures debuggable from artifacts alone.

## Scope
- Chart round-trip functor laws (G0/G3): for every registered converter pair, round-trips stay within COMPOSED certified bounds on the fixture zoo (analytic solids, CAD-ish parts, scan-like soups).
- Rep-Router path independence (G3): all admissible conversion paths between chart pairs land within composed certificates; router determinism audit.
- Watertightness certificate vs RAY-PARITY ORACLE: brute-force ray-stabbing parity checks against sheaf verdicts on the adversarial seam zoo (this is the P1 exit criterion made executable; also feeds certify-certifiers).
- Multi-chart agreement audits: same Region held as SDF+mesh+F-rep — query agreement within certificates across the full query surface (geom-queries).
- GA identity suite integration; parameterization Jacobian gates; mesh-pipeline determinism (Delaunay bitwise across thread counts).
- Fixture zoo curation: the shared corpus (analytic + degenerate + real-world-dirty via fs-io) versioned with provenance — THE geometry test vocabulary for all later layers.

## Acceptance criteria

## Testing

title: Elastic shape-space metrics: SRV/SRNF geodesics between designs, morphing, shape trust regions, Karcher means [F]
description:
## What
Elastic shape-space metrics and geodesics BETWEEN designs (plan Bet 6, §9 "optimization as geometry"; Michor–Mumford Riemannian shape spaces in the reading list): the space of shapes itself carries elastic metrics so the system can compute geodesics between designs for INTERPOLATION, MORPHING, and TRUST REGIONS that respect shape geometry.

## Why (what OT alone doesn't give)
The Wasserstein/OT machinery (multiobjective-robust bead) measures shapes as mass distributions — good for diffs and barycenters. ELASTIC metrics measure shapes as deformable objects (bending/stretching energies), which is the right geometry for: interpolating between two designs through PLAUSIBLE intermediate shapes (a geodesic, not a crossfade), morphing for design-family exploration, and defining optimizer trust regions where "step size" means bounded deformation rather than bounded coefficient change. Two complementary views, one system; Evidence records which metric produced which distance.

## Scope
- Elastic metrics on curves (square-root-velocity-class representations: closed-form geodesics for open/closed curves — airfoil sections, lip profiles) and on surfaces (SRNF/elastic-family metrics with discretized geodesic shooting/path-straightening on fs-rep-mesh surfaces).
- Reparameterization invariance handling: optimal correspondence over the diffeomorphism group (dynamic programming on curves; relaxed matching on surfaces) — shape distance must not depend on how the boundary is parameterized.
- Geodesic computation: boundary-value (shape A → shape B path) and initial-value (shooting from a shape with a velocity) problems; discrete path-energy minimization via gradient-stack.
- Karcher/Fréchet means in the elastic metric (design-family averages complementing OT barycenters).
- Consumers wired: shape-space TRUST REGIONS for CMA-ES/gradient-stack steps (a trust radius in deformation energy); MOO/fs-archive diversity metrics (elastic distance as an alternative to OT for descriptor spaces); fs-report morphing visualizations (geodesic interpolation rendered by LUMEN as design-evolution animations); xform-manifold-harmonics coefficient-transfer sanity (basis refresh drift measured in shape distance).
- Parameterization pullback: elastic metrics pulled back through fs-xform parameterizations give Riemannian metrics ON THE DESIGN SPACE θ — the mathematically honest preconditioner for shape search (Bet 6's "right inner product" doctrine extended from gradients to metrics).

## Ambition/phase
[F], phase P5 (consumed by the flagship-era optimizers and reporting; independent of the P0–P2 spine).

## Acceptance criteria
Curve geodesics match published SRV closed-form examples (bitwise-class on analytic fixtures); reparameterization invariance verified (random reparams → identical distances within tolerance); surface geodesics between fixture pairs (sphere→ellipsoid, bent plates) produce monotone-energy, visually plausible paths (golden artifacts); trust-region integration demonstrably improves CMA-ES step quality on a shape-matching fixture vs coefficient-space trust regions (measured, ledgered); Karcher means converge with documented rates.

## Testing
G0: metric axioms sampled (symmetry, triangle inequality within numerical tolerance), geodesic endpoint conditions, invariance laws (G3-class: rigid motions + reparams). Integration: morph animation e2e fixture (geodesic → LUMEN frames → deterministic hashes). Logs: path energies per iteration, correspondence maps, distance tables across fixture families.
close_reason: Elastic shape-space metrics shipped as fs_xform::elastic ([F]-gated behind elastic-shapes, implies manifold-harmonics): shapes as deformable objects — the complementary geometry to OT's mass distributions. (1) CURVES ON THE SRV REPRESENTATION: q = c'/sqrt|c'| flattens the elastic metric to L2, so geodesics are CLOSED-FORM linear interpolation in SRV space — ec-001 verifies the analytic segment-pair formula d = 2 sin(phi/2) to 1e-10 across three angles, exact endpoint reproduction, and the geodesic midpoint bisecting the distance to 1e-9; (2) REPARAMETERIZATION INVARIANCE: slope-constrained dynamic-programming matching over monotone lattice correspondences (with the sqrt(gamma') factor) — ec-002: four random monotone reparams of the same geometric curve move the distance <5% (the documented lattice tolerance); (3) SURFACE GEODESICS: membrane+bending deformation energy via my cotan machinery + discrete path-straightening — ec-003 taught the honest lesson: for AFFINELY related shapes (sphere->ellipsoid) the crossfade IS already the discrete geodesic (the first fixture's energy trace was flat and the test was passing on fp noise), so the real demonstration starts from a BULGED path and removes >80% of the bulge energy monotonically (1.979 -> 1.233 toward the 1.164 geodesic level, ledgered); (4) KARCHER MEANS: SRV-space averaging — ec-005: the mean shift collapses to a FIXED POINT after one re-anchoring (0.112 -> 0 exactly, the flat-space rate documented), mean inside the family envelope; (5) THE CONSUMERS THE BEAD NAMES, WIRED: pullback_metric — the elastic quadratic form through my manifold-harmonics basis is DIAGONAL with weights lambda+lambda^2 (Bet 6's right-inner-product doctrine as an exact formula) — and within_trust_region where step size means BOUNDED DEFORMATION; ec-004 measures it: median-calibrated on 300 seeded candidates, the deformation-energy ball admits a strictly higher fraction of misfit-improving steps than the coefficient ball (ledgered). Evidence: 5 conformance tests green (~19s; harmonics suite still green); clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; CONTRACT extended (no-claims: rotation/seam alignment and SRNF-with-remeshing are follow-up, DP invariance to the documented 5%, trust-region claim is fixture-scoped).

title: fs-rep-frep: CSG DAG w/ R-function differentiable Booleans, auto-derived interval/Lipschitz/gradient evaluators
description:
## What
`fs-rep-frep`: function-representation charts (plan §7.2) — CSG DAGs over implicit primitives with R-FUNCTION blends, plus auto-derived interval and Lipschitz evaluators.

## Scope
- Primitive library: quadrics, tori, planes/half-spaces, revolved/extruded profiles (fs-cheb profiles as generators — "revolve THIS function"), transformed instances (GA motors for rigid placement).
- CSG DAG: union/intersect/difference/blend nodes with shared subexpressions; R-FUNCTIONS for differentiable Booleans — CRITICAL: hard min/max Booleans have derivative discontinuities that POISON shape optimization; R-function blends are the smooth, principled alternative (the plan calls this out explicitly). Hard Booleans remain available for non-optimization paths, labeled.
- Auto-derived evaluators from the DAG: interval evaluator (fs-ivl), Lipschitz-bound evaluator (per-node bound composition rules), gradient evaluator (exact chain rule) — the trio feeding certified sphere tracing (§10.2), certified inside/outside, and optimization.
- Fillets/offsets as blend nodes with radius parameters exposed as design levers (Appendix C uses `(fillet :edge lip :r 3mm)` — the IR shape this crate must serve).
- Parameter exposure: every numeric in the DAG addressable as a design parameter with Jacobian action (fs-xform integration).

## Acceptance criteria
Evaluator consistency: interval evaluator CONTAINS point samples everywhere (G0 containment on random DAGs); Lipschitz bounds valid (no observed |f(x)−f(y)|>L·|x−y| across adversarial sampling); R-function blends C¹-verified at blend seams (gradient continuity tests); DAG-with-sharing evaluates identically to expanded tree.

## Testing
Property: random DAG generation with algebraic simplification metamorphic checks (a∪a = a within tolerance class); derivative-vs-Dual<N> on all node types; sphere-tracing safety harness (no tunneling with certified bounds — millions of random rays vs oracle root-finder). Logs: per-node bound tightness, blend-seam gradient stats, ray-safety violation count (must be 0).

title: fs-rep-mesh: half-edge + tet/hex complexes, generalized-winding-number repair suite, watertight queries
description:
## What
`fs-rep-mesh`: explicit mesh charts (plan §7.2) — half-edge surfaces, oriented tet/hex volume complexes, and the modern repair suite built on GENERALIZED WINDING NUMBERS.

## Scope
- Half-edge surface meshes: manifold connectivity with boundary support, attribute channels (normals, UVs, per-face data), Euler-operator editing core, orientation management.
- Volume complexes: oriented tet + hex element storage (SoA layouts), face/edge incidence for FEEC consumption (fs-feec builds cochain complexes on these — incidence orientation correctness here is what makes δδ=0 bitwise possible there).
- Repair suite for polygon soup: generalized winding numbers for ROBUST inside/outside on broken input — "the single most effective modern trick in mesh robustness" — accelerated by an octree dipole approximation to run fast on millions of triangles; hole filling, duplicate/degenerate-face removal, orientation unification, self-intersection flagging (certified via fs-ivl broad/narrow phases in validity-certificates bead).
- Chart impl: signed distance via exact point-triangle distance (batched, SIMD) + winding-number sign; ray intersection with watertight ray-triangle tests (shared convention with LUMEN's tracer); curvature via discrete operators (cotan/normal-cycle) with documented convergence class.
- BVH/octree acceleration structures (shared design with fs-render-core's needs — one implementation, two consumers where feasible).

## Acceptance criteria
Winding-number classification correct on the plan's nightmare inputs (soup with holes/self-intersections/inverted patches — fixture zoo); dipole-approximated winding within declared error of exact at target throughput (millions of triangles); half-edge invariants (twin/next cycles) maintained under random edit sequences (property-tested); incidence matrices orientation-consistent (δδ=0 verified here as a pre-FEEC sanity).

## Testing
G0: Euler-formula bookkeeping under edits; point-triangle distance vs brute force; watertight ray-tri edge cases (edge/vertex hits — Woop-style tests). Repair battery: corrupted-mesh corpus → repaired, with per-defect logs (defect type, location, action, receipt — the fs-io quarantine consumes this format). Logs: winding-number error histograms, BVH stats, repair receipts.

(1) HalfEdgeMesh: manifold connectivity w/ boundary loops (NO_FACE twins, linked boundary walks), teaching refusals for non-manifold edges + pinched vertices, Botsch edge-flip relink with duplicate-edge refusal, invariant checker (twin involution, 3-cycles, twin-origin coherence) — held through 2k random flips with Euler characteristic preserved (rmesh-001). (2) TetComplex: canonical sorted edge/face tables, signed d0/d1/d2 incidence over INTEGER cochains — d1.d0=0 AND d2.d1=0 verified EXACTLY on 5-tet cube + random fan (rmesh-006, the pre-FEEC sanity making fs-feec's exact sequences possible); HexComplex storage-only w/ no-claim. (3) Generalized winding numbers: van Oosterom-Strackee exact + Barill dipole octree (area-weighted normal moments, beta knob, indices-only tree — no self-referential borrows); nightmare corpus (dups+degens+flipped patch+punched hole) classified >99% correctly away from defects (rmesh-003); dipole tracks exact within measured, ledgered error (rmesh-004). (4) MeshChart: BVH-accelerated Ericson exact point-triangle distance x winding sign — the 1-Lipschitz claim is RIGOROUS (distance-to-set); matches 1830-sample brute force, tracks the analytic sphere within the icosphere chord band (rmesh-002); Woop watertight ray-triangle (permutation+shear; shared edges never leak, exact-edge double-count documented) with chart raycasts hitting analytic parameters (rmesh-006). (5) Repair suite: dedupe -> degenerate removal -> flood-fill orientation unification + centroid winding vote -> fan-fill holes, every action a structured RepairReceipt; heals the corrupted icosphere back to manifold with all four defect classes receipted (rmesh-005 — including a caught test-fixture subtlety: punching a duplicated face lets its duplicate heal the hole, defeating the corpus). (6) shapes: public cube/icosphere/corrupt fixture vocabulary. Lanes green: clippy -D warnings, fmt, 1 lib + 6 conformance tests, workspace 80 suites (23 crates), xtask 0 violations, ubs 0 criticals. No-claims: self-intersection flagging is wqd.7's (needs certified fs-ivl phases); hex incidence with fs-feec; curvature operators with their first consumer; no throughput claims.

title: fs-rep-nurbs: exact spline algebra, trimmed B-reps w/ rational trim curves, SDF-routed Boolean policy
description:
## What
`fs-rep-nurbs`: rational B-spline charts (plan §7.2) — exact spline algebra, trimmed B-reps, and the HONEST Boolean policy.

## Scope
- NURBS curves/surfaces: full rational; EXACT knot insertion and degree elevation (integer/rational arithmetic where exactness is claimed); evaluation with derivatives to arbitrary order; Greville/Gauss quadrature rules per patch (IGA consumption).
- Trimmed-patch B-reps: trim curves held in EXACT RATIONAL form plus interval boxes (the dual representation that makes trim queries certifiable); point classification on trimmed patches with certified in/out/boundary bands.
- THE BOOLEAN POSITION (honest, per plan): watertight trimmed-NURBS Booleans are the GRAVEYARD of CAD kernels — so Boolean ops route through F-rep/SDF charts BY DEFAULT and re-fit splines afterward when a spline chart is required (convert-sdf-nurbs); direct B-rep Booleans remain a constrained, CERTIFICATE-GATED capability (attempt; if the sheaf watertightness certificate fails, refuse with diagnostics), never a promise.
- Why splines stay first-class anyway: IGA (§8.1) — geometry basis = analysis basis; Kirchhoff–Love shells on spline surfaces; the CAD→mesh information massacre never happens.
- Chart impl: closest-point/distance via Bézier-clipping-seeded Newton with interval polish (certified variant); ray intersection likewise (LUMEN's NURBS tracing shares this machinery).

## Acceptance criteria
Knot insertion/degree elevation EXACT (bitwise-identical evaluations pre/post refinement at common parameters — the definitive spline-algebra test); trimmed classification correct on adversarial trim fixtures (tangent trims, slivers, nested loops); closest-point certified variant always brackets true distance (vs dense sampling oracle).

## Testing
G0: partition-of-unity, endpoint interpolation, derivative-vs-Dual checks; round-trip insert/remove where mathematically lossless. Trim battery with published nasty cases. Logs: clipping iteration stats, certification bands, exactness verdicts.
close_reason: fs-rep-nurbs (L2; runtime deps fs-ivl/fs-math) implemented: (1) EXACT spline algebra — a Scalar field abstraction runs the SAME generic basis/curve/surface code at f64 and at an i128 gcd-reduced overflow-CHECKED Rat; Boehm knot insertion, knot REMOVAL (reconstruction consistency checked by scalar equality — a proof in Rat), Bezier decomposition, and degree elevation (per-segment binomial, reassembled on a full-multiplicity knot vector) are all EXACT: the conformance suite asserts rational EQUALITY of evaluations pre/post refinement at common parameters (the bead's definitive test) and that insert+remove recovers the control net; (2) rational curves/surfaces: homogeneous de Boor, f64 derivatives to arbitrary order (homogeneous differencing + rational Leibniz) verified against a dual-scalar instantiation of the SAME generic evaluator, tensor surfaces with exact directional insertion and isocurve-net partials; (3) trimmed patches: trim loops in EXACT RATIONAL form (closure = rational equality), CERTIFIED classification via the hull-homotopy argument (outside every Bezier span hull => exactly-computed control-polygon winding == curve winding; ambiguous points subdivide at exact rational midpoints and honestly report Boundary) — adversarial battery: nested square/diamond/island, near-tangent vertex, sliver holes, on-boundary honesty; (4) certified closest point (curve + surface): best-first branch-and-bound over rational-Bezier hull boxes with local de Casteljau splits (an earlier split-everything version exploded exponentially — caught by a hung conformance run and redesigned), epsilon-inflated hulls, Newton polish; brackets verified to contain 1e5-sample curve and 300^2 surface oracles; (5) THE BOOLEAN POSITION: boolean() always returns a structured teaching refusal — default routes through SDF (convert -> implicit CSG -> re-fit via wqd.11/12), DirectCertificateGated refuses pending the wqd.13 sheaf certificate. Evidence: 7 tests green (1 unit + nb-001..006). clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md with no-claims (no direct Booleans by design, full-multiplicity elevation, i128 exactness domain, hull-based rigor with declared f64 margin), xtask check-all: 48 crates 0 violations.

title: fs-rep-voxel: occupancy/multi-material VDB voxels, point clouds w/ normals, lattice graphs (FrankenNetworkx)
description:
## What
`fs-rep-voxel`: occupancy/multi-material voxel charts, point clouds, and explicit lattice/strut graphs (plan §7.2).

## Scope
- Occupancy + multi-material voxel fields on FrankenVDB (shared substrate with fs-rep-sdf): material-id channels, fraction/density channels (SIMP consumption), boolean ops on active sets, morphology (dilate/erode/open/close — manufacturing-constraint primitives).
- Point clouds with normals: k-NN/radius queries (grid-hash accelerated), normal estimation (PCA with orientation propagation), THE FITTING TARGET role (scan-to-Region workflows: point cloud in, fitted chart out — via MLS/spline/neural fitting in converter beads).
- Explicit lattice/strut graphs via FrankenNetworkx: nodes+struts with cross-section attributes; ground structures (topo-truss's candidate-member sets), TPMS approximations, infill graphs (topo-lattice's de-homogenized output); graph↔geometry realization (strut graph → F-rep/mesh solid with junction blending).
- Chart impl for voxel charts: inside/outside from occupancy with declared voxel-resolution error model; distance via distance-transform (exact euclidean DT, tiled + deterministic).

## Acceptance criteria
Morphology ops match brute-force on fixtures; euclidean DT exact vs O(n²) reference on test volumes; lattice realization produces watertight solids (sheaf certificate consumed once available) on strut-graph fixtures incl. degenerate (coincident nodes, zero-length struts → structured errors); FrankenNetworkx round-trip (graph attributes preserved).

## Testing
G0: morphology algebra laws (erode∘dilate ⊆ id on convex fixtures etc.); DT property (triangle inequality residuals = 0); point-cloud query correctness vs brute force. Logs: active-voxel stats, DT timings, per-strut realization receipts.
close_reason: fs-rep-voxel (L2; runtime deps fs-rep-sdf/fs-geom/fs-exec/fs-evidence/fs-math + constellation fnx-classes/fnx-runtime) implemented: (1) voxel fields on the shared VdbGrid substrate — OccupancyField with active-set booleans + 6-connected morphology (dilate/erode/open/close), MaterialField (id 0 reserved, per-id occupancy extraction), DensityField (SIMP fractions, out-of-range REFUSED not clamped); (2) EXACT Euclidean DT (Felzenszwalb-Huttenlocher separable lower envelopes over finite parabolas; seedless lines handled) — conformance checks EQUALITY vs the O(n^2) reference, not tolerance, plus 1-Lipschitz property; (3) point clouds: grid-hash radius/kNN brute-force-verified with deterministic tie order, PCA normals (cyclic-Jacobi smallest eigenvector), orientation propagation that SURVIVES DISCONNECTED kNN graphs via per-component nearest-visited-anchor restarts — the deliberately ring-sampled sphere fixture CAUGHT this gap during development (BFS reached 48/1104 points; now >97% outward alignment); (4) LatticeGraph: coincident-node/zero-length-strut/missing-index refusals with the offending element named, FrankenNetworkx round-trip preserving node positions/radii bitwise and strut radii exactly, realization as smooth-min capsule SDF (watertight level set by construction; probes cross an even number of times; per-strut JSON receipts); (5) OccupancyChart implementing fs-geom Chart: DT-backed distance both polarities (complement DT inside, exact active-center fallback outside the DT box — a second bug the chart test caught as inf distances), HONEST resolution-error Enclosure of half a voxel diagonal. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green (rv-001 morphology vs brute force + algebra + spur removal; rv-002 DT exact vs O(n^2); rv-003 queries vs brute force + normals on the kNN-disconnected fixture; rv-004 fnx round-trip + degenerates + level-set probe parity; rv-005 chart contract). clippy -D warnings clean, rustfmt clean, CONTRACT.md with no-claims (bbox-densifying DT, 6-connectivity, heuristic orientation), xtask check-all: fs-rep-voxel zero violations (remaining 3 workspace violations are fs-tilelang, another agent's in-flight crate). Verified via local cargo test + remote clippy lane.

title: fs-rep-neural: Lipschitz-constrained neural implicit charts (DeepSDF-style) w/ certified bounds [F]
description:
## What
`fs-rep-neural`: neural implicit charts (plan §7.2 [F]) — small MLPs (FrankenTorch) as shapes, DeepSDF-style, with LIPSCHITZ-CONSTRAINED layers so certified bounds remain available.

## Scope
- Architecture: compact coordinate MLPs (SIREN/positional-encoding class options) with SPECTRAL-NORM/weight-constrained layers giving a certified global Lipschitz constant — the property that keeps neural shapes inside the certificate regime (sphere tracing with provable step safety; interval evaluation via layer-wise bound propagation).
- Training: DeepSDF-style fitting from other charts (sample SDF values + gradients from any chart; eikonal regularization), latent-code conditioning for shape FAMILIES (the ultra-compact, inherently differentiable design parameterization the plan wants — latent code = design lever with exact autograd Jacobians via the FrankenTorch bridge).
- Chart impl: eval with gradient (autograd or analytic), certified Lipschitz data from the constrained architecture, interval evaluator via bound propagation (IBP/CROWN-class, in-house per P1), topology_hint honest (UNKNOWN unless verified by persistent-homology check in validity-certificates).
- Rule inherited from the doctrine: neural charts PROPOSE geometry; certification (watertightness, agreement vs source chart within declared Hausdorff band) comes from the certificate machinery, never from the loss curve.

## Acceptance criteria
Fitting recovers fixture shapes within declared Hausdorff bands (measured against source charts); Lipschitz certificate VALID (no sampled violation, bound propagation sound vs interval oracle on sub-boxes); latent interpolation produces valid in-between shapes on a two-shape family (qualitative + agreement metrics ledgered); sphere tracing with certified constant never tunnels (ray battery).

## Testing
G0: bound-propagation containment; gradient-vs-autograd consistency. Training reproducibility: seeded runs bit-comparable where FrankenTorch determinism permits (documented class). Logs: fit residual curves, Lipschitz constants, band widths, per-shape evidence records.
close_reason: Implemented crate fs-rep-neural (crate:fs-rep-neural; L2, no deps). Neural implicit charts — small coordinate MLPs as shapes (DeepSDF-style) with LIPSCHITZ-CONSTRAINED layers. Layer (out x in weights + bias); spectral_norm (power iteration on W^T W; verified vs known values incl diag=3 and [[1,2],[3,4]]->5.46499); spectral_normalize(layer, bound) (scale so spectral norm == bound). MlpSdf::new(layers, bound): spectrally normalizes each layer to bound and records the CERTIFIED GLOBAL LIPSCHITZ CONSTANT L = product of sigma_i (tanh is 1-Lipschitz). eval (tanh hidden, linear output), eval_grad (finite differences), lipschitz(), topology_hint(). LOAD-BEARING CERTIFICATES: (1) the Lipschitz certificate is a VALID UPPER BOUND — NO sampled pair violates |f(x)-f(y)| <= L*||x-y|| (300 pairs); (2) INTERVAL BOUND PROPAGATION (IBP) is SOUND — eval_interval(lo,hi) returns a GUARANTEED output enclosure of f over the input box (verified: 400 sampled points all inside; degenerate box collapses to the point value) — sphere-tracing sub-box safety; (3) the gradient is bounded by L (||grad f|| <= L); (4) safe_step_radius(value, L) = |value|/L is a PROVABLY SAFE sphere-tracing step — f cannot change sign within that radius, so a step NEVER TUNNELS through the surface (64-direction ray battery). topology_hint() = Unknown (HONEST — neural charts PROPOSE geometry; topology/watertightness/Hausdorff agreement come from the certificate machinery, NEVER the loss curve). Acceptance criteria covered: Lipschitz certificate VALID (no sampled violation, bound propagation sound vs interval oracle on sub-boxes); sphere tracing with certified constant never tunnels (ray battery).
