title: Rep Router: Pareto shortest-path conversion planning over chart multigraph, learned costs, explainable routes
description:
## What
The Rep Router (plan §7.3, Bet 1): conversions form a directed multigraph — nodes are chart types, edges are converters annotated with (cost model, error model, certificate availability) — and requests ("give me a chart supporting operation X with error ≤ ε under budget B") are solved as a PARETO SHORTEST-PATH problem over that graph.

## Scope
- Conversion graph registry: converters self-register with their edge labels; cost models read the autotune table (machine-specific ON PURPOSE — kernel-level winners flip between the M-class and TR-class machines, §14.1); error models declare composition rules (additive Hausdorff, multiplicative relative, exact).
- Pareto shortest-path planner (cost × composed error, certified edges PREFERRED): FrankenNetworkx label-correcting/multi-objective search; deterministic tie-breaking (P2 — router decisions must be reproducible and explainable, per AGENTS.md invariant).
- Execution engine: run the winning chain, produce Evidence-typed result with the COMPOSED certificate; record ACTUAL cost/error back into tune tables so the models improve (the router learns).
- Explainability: `route_explain()` returns the considered paths, their Pareto labels, and why the winner won — an agent-facing diagnostic, and the hook fs-ir-admission uses for chart-compatibility checking.
- Failure semantics: no admissible path within budget → structured error listing the BINDING constraint (error vs cost) and the cheapest relaxations (errors-as-guidance doctrine).

## Why
This turns "which representation should I use" — the perennial unforced error of every CAD/simulation handoff — into a solved planning problem, and it is the load-bearing mechanism behind "no privileged representation."

## Acceptance criteria
On a seeded graph with known Pareto fronts, planner returns exact fronts; composed certificates verified against direct measurement on executed chains (G3 path independence: ANY admissible path lands within the composed certificate of the direct edge); learned-cost feedback demonstrably improves subsequent plans; determinism of decisions across runs.

## Testing
G0: planner correctness on synthetic multigraphs (oracle enumeration); G3 metamorphic path-independence battery across the real converter set as it lands; budget-infeasible diagnostics content-checked. Logs: full route explanations, per-edge predicted-vs-actual cost/error tables (the model-improvement evidence).

## ADDENDUM AMENDMENT (COMPREHENSIVE_ADDENDUM_TO_FRANKENSIM_PLAN.md)
The Rep Router's fitness function gains TWO new terms:
- a **differentiability term** (Proposal 1, frankensim-epic-coupling-bk0o.2): when a query requests gradients, PREFER naturally-smooth SDF/spline paths — converting multi-representation from a complication into the asset that provides differentiable paths where mesh-locked pipelines have none.
- a **conditioning term** (Proposal 5, frankensim-epic-selfknow-knh1.3): route by END-TO-END conditioning (composed per-op amplification factors), not just per-op cost/error — "which path through representation space keeps the whole pipeline well-posed."
The Router also becomes a VERIFICATION asset: the Goodhart guard (frankensim-epic-epistype-qmao.5) re-routes optimizer endpoints through a DIFFERENT representation path as an independent cross-check.
close_reason: Rep Router v1 landed as fs-geom's router module (Bet 1: no privileged representation). GRAPH REGISTRY: ConverterSpec edges with (a-priori cost, declared error model with composition rule — Exact / AdditiveAbs / MultiplicativeRel grounded against the request scale, certificate availability); duplicate/invalid specs refused structurally. PLANNER: exact Pareto label-correcting search over (cost, composed abs error, uncertified-edge count), simple-paths (non-simple dominated under nonneg weights), deterministic by construction (name-sorted expansion + total winner order: certified-preferred -> min cost -> min error -> lexicographic path). PROVEN against a brute-force DFS Pareto oracle on a seeded multigraph with parallel edges, a cycle, and mixed certificates, across three targets — exact front match (bead acceptance). EXPLAIN: every Pareto candidate with admissibility + winner index + reason (the fs-ir admission hook). REFUSALS TEACH: binding constraint named (Error/Cost/Both/NoPath) with best-achievable relaxation values, content-tested. COST ORACLE: machine-specific costs read through the CostOracle trait (L2 must not depend on L6 fs-ledger — HELM wires the tune table behind it; MemoryCostOracle in-process); learned-cost feedback demonstrably reroutes plans (acceptance); learned error magnitudes replace declared ones ONLY on uncertified additive edges — certificates are never learned away. EXECUTION: chains run through EdgeRunners; per-edge Evidence receipts composed via fs-evidence Op::Add over achieved-error enclosures; measured composed error verified inside the plan's composed certificate (G3); actuals recorded back into the oracle (the router learns); failures attributed to their edge. Real integration proven: fs-geom's actual Convert<SampledSdf> conversion (sphere F-rep fixture) executed under a routed plan with its achieved error inside the plan bound. 6 router tests + full fs-geom suite green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; xtask check-all 20 crates 0 violations. Scope notes: single-objective-per-request routing (operation-capability matching lands with more chart kinds); FrankenNetworkx label-correcting swap-in deferred until fnx exposes multi-objective search (in-crate planner is exact and oracle-proven meanwhile).

title: Converter mesh->SDF: exact point-triangle distance + winding sign, per-tile fp-exact certificate
description:
## What
Certified converter: mesh → SDF (plan §7.3 edge 1) — exact point-triangle distance + generalized-winding-number sign, per-tile error = 0 up to fp rounding [S]. The strongest edge in the conversion graph: effectively EXACT.

## Scope
- Distance field: for each output tile, exact point-triangle distance against BVH-pruned candidate sets (fs-rep-mesh machinery), batched/SIMD across tile samples; f32 storage with f64 computation per SDF convention.
- Sign: generalized winding number (octree-dipole-accelerated) — robust on imperfect input; for CERTIFIED-exact claims input must pass watertightness/manifoldness checks first (validity-certificates), otherwise the edge downgrades its certificate to "winding-sign heuristic" honestly (Evidence reflects input quality).
- Narrow-band + full-field modes; FrankenVDB output for sparse domains; incremental re-generation for locally-edited meshes (dirty-tile tracking) — the optimization-loop path.
- Error model: per-tile bound = fp-rounding envelope (documented ulp analysis); certificate: interval-verified on sampled audit tiles + exact-arithmetic spot checks.

## Acceptance criteria
Against analytic fixtures (sphere/box meshes at high resolution): field matches analytic SDF within declared envelope; sign correct everywhere including near-surface (the hard zone); incremental mode bit-identical to full regeneration (G5-style); throughput targets (tiles/sec) ledgered per machine.

## Testing
G0: symmetry/translation equivariance (G3 frame invariance); distance-vs-brute-force on random meshes; adversarial: slivers, near-degenerate triangles, open boundaries (certificate downgrade path verified). Logs: per-tile error stats, winding fallback counts, BVH traversal stats.
close_reason: Implemented the certified mesh->SDF converter (plan 7.3 edge 1) bridging fs-rep-mesh -> fs-rep-sdf. mesh_to_sdf: exact point-triangle distance (BVH) + generalized-winding sign sampled onto TiledSdf dense grids (f32 store/f64 eval per convention). CERTIFICATE HONESTY is the centerpiece: assess_quality counts boundary + non-manifold edges; closed 2-manifold input -> enclosure-grade receipt that passes the Certified discipline; open/imperfect soup -> Estimate receipt whose ModelEvidence NAMES the winding-sign-heuristic card and the defect counts — nothing silently promoted (rmesh-007 verifies both paths). IncrementalMeshSdf: dirty-box re-sampling via the new TiledSdf::resample_box (samples recomputed at EXACTLY the original positions) — incremental update proven BIT-IDENTICAL to full regeneration over 400 seeded probes (G5); refreshed-sample counts recorded for audits. G3 translation equivariance verified (mesh+queries shifted -> matching fields). Analytic accuracy: icosphere-3 field matches the analytic sphere within declared envelope + chord band over 800 seeded points. Narrow-band/VDB output modes ride the existing NarrowBand::from_chart path (works with any chart incl. MeshChart). Lanes green: clippy -D warnings (both crates), fmt, fs-rep-mesh 3 lib + 7 conformance + fs-rep-sdf suites, ubs prior scans. Workspace state: all green EXCEPT two other agents' in-flight reds (fs-ivl taylor_golden_hash WIP 6ys.13; fs-plan missing CONTRACT.md mid-work gp3.8) — theirs, untouched. No-claims: sparse VDB output certificates + per-tile interval audits join the fs-ivl integration pass; dirty-box inference is the caller's until edit-tracking attributes land; throughput targets are the perf harness's.

title: Performance program: rooflines, machine-adaptive tuning, tropical analytics
description:
## Mission
The performance program (plan §14): performance claims as falsifiable rooflines with names attached, re-baselined continuously by the autotuner on actual machines — never vibes.

## Key targets (illustrative, re-measured on first run)
LBM D3Q19 stream+collide ≥1.0 GLUP/s (M-class) / ≥0.6 (TR 96c); GEMM f64 ≥75% of measured peak both ISAs; SpMV SELL-C-σ ≥85% of STREAM; matrix-free FEEC apply p=4 ≥30% peak FLOPs; batched small dense ≥60% peak; 3D FFT ≥40% of memory-bound limit; sphere-traced primary rays ≥80/120 Mray/s. Every target stored in the ledger with machine fingerprint — a target never re-measured is a lie waiting to happen.

## Structural positions
- Machine-adaptive execution: CCD-aware tile ownership (working sets inside 32 MiB L3 islands), first-touch placement, reduction trees shaped to CCD hierarchy; on Apple, P/E-asymmetric scheduling with E-core-sized tile variants.
- Tropical critical-path analytics (Bet 12 [F/M]): task-DAG timing in the max-plus semiring; steady-state throughput = tropical eigenvalue; the critical circuit tells the autotuner WHICH KERNEL TO TUNE NEXT and the planner where relaxed tolerance actually buys wall-clock.
- Performance regressions are test failures (nightly roofline runs → metrics table; below tolerance band = CI failure with auto-reconstructed flame-graph diagnosis).

## Members
roofline-harness, machine-adaptive-tuning, tropical-analytics, perf-regression-ci.

## Success Criteria
Nightly roofline CI on both reference machines writing to the ledger; regression = red build; dashboards make "what got slower this month and why" a one-liner.

title: Roofline harness: measured bandwidth/peak axes, kernel registry w/ 14.1 targets, ledgered attainment + staleness
description:
## What
The roofline harness (plan §14.1): performance claims as FALSIFIABLE targets — every kernel benchmarked against its arithmetic-intensity-derived limit on the actual machine, results ledgered with fingerprints.

## Scope
- Machine characterization: measured STREAM-class bandwidth (per core class, per CCD), measured peak FLOPs (per ISA tier, per precision) — the roofline's axes, from fs-substrate probes + dedicated microbenches; NEVER spec-sheet numbers.
- Kernel benchmark registry: each hot kernel registers (workload generator, intensity metadata — from fs-tilelang where generated — target expression as % of roofline). The §14.1 table encoded as data: LBM ≥1.0/0.6 GLUP/s; GEMM ≥75% peak; SpMV ≥85% STREAM; FEEC apply p=4 ≥30% peak; batched dense ≥60%; FFT ≥40% memory-bound limit; sphere-traced rays ≥80/120 Mray/s.
- Attainment computation: measured vs machine-specific limit; results → ledger `metrics` with machine fingerprint + kernel version; "a target that was never re-measured is a lie waiting to happen" — staleness tracking built in.
- Two-machine honesty: the M-class is a bandwidth monster PER CORE (LBM/SpMV love it), the TR-class wins on aggregate compute/cache — kernel-level winners FLIP by workload; the harness reports per-machine attainment separately (feeding the Rep Router's machine-specific cost models).
- Statistical discipline: warmup/thermal handling, repetition with dispersion reporting, frequency-state capture (a benchmark without variance bars is folklore).

## Acceptance criteria
Harness runs the full registry on both reference machines producing ledgered attainment tables; seeded-slow kernel correctly reported below band; measurement reproducibility within stated dispersion across re-runs; staleness alerts fire on fingerprint drift.

## Testing
Meta-tests: attainment arithmetic vs hand calculations; dispersion-bound self-checks; fingerprint-keying correctness. Logs: full roofline reports (kernel × machine × attainment × dispersion), trend series.
close_reason: Roofline harness v0 landed as crates/fs-roofline (L6). Measured axes only (never spec-sheet): STREAM-triad bandwidth single/all-core via fs-substrate probe + in-house FMA-chain peak-FLOPs microbench single/all-core; axes carry the fs-substrate topology fingerprint. KernelSpec intensity model (bytes/flops per elem, threading axis, optional target fraction) -> attainment = rate / min(bandwidth-limit, compute-limit) with binding RoofSide, relative-IQR dispersion, and WithinBand/BelowBand/NoTarget verdicts. Section 14.1 target table encoded as data with landed:false rows visible from day one (no silent coverage gaps). Registry v0: fs-simd axpy/dot/sum (report-only bands on shared runners, per 14 discipline) + SeededSlowKernel meta-kernel. Ledger integration: record_run writes one op (frozen Five Explicits) + per-kernel metrics + benchmark_result events + tune rows keyed kernel x roofline-v1 x fingerprint (added Ledger::tune_rows scan to fs-ledger); staleness() returns Fresh/FingerprintDrift/NeverMeasured. roofline CLI bin for the nightly lane (axes + kernel JSONL + coverage table + --ledger recording + staleness report), conformance-tested via CARGO_BIN_EXE. Acceptance proven: attainment arithmetic vs hand calcs (unit + rf-001), seeded-slow correctly BelowBand on real axes (rf-002), ledgered attainment with fingerprint keying + lint-clean (rf-003), staleness alerts fire on drift (rf-004), re-run reproducibility within stated dispersion allowance (rf-005), CLI smoke (rf-006). 9 unit + 6 conformance green; clippy -D warnings clean; xtask check-all 17 crates 0 violations. No-claims in CONTRACT.md: no machine perf numbers claimed (citable only as fingerprinted ledger rows); TR-class reference machine not yet measured (needs the machine); per-CCD/P-E-class axes, frequency-state capture, thermal controls = future scope; verdict gating deliberately absent on shared CI runners.

title: Machine-adaptive hardening: CCD pinning, first-touch audits, P/E scheduling, prefetch/hugepage sweeps — targets met on both ISAs
description:
## What
Machine-adaptive execution hardening (plan §14.2): the cross-cutting tuning pass that makes the SAME kernels roofline-honest on BOTH microarchitectures — verified, not vibes.

## Scope
- Threadripper/EPYC lane: CCD-aware tile ownership (working sets pinned inside their 32 MiB L3 islands — measured via cache-miss counters where available, timing proxies otherwise); first-touch page placement VERIFIED (page-to-node audits); reduction trees shaped to the CCD hierarchy; NPS-mode sensitivity documented.
- Apple lane: latency-lane work pinned to P-cores; throughput tiles streamed across P+E with E-core-sized tile variants (autotuner weights verified against measured per-class throughput); 16 KB page + 128 B line effects quantified.
- Hugepage-backed arenas everywhere they help (measured, with fallback paths exercised); software-prefetch distances autotuned per machine (stencil suite sweep); SIMD tier selection audit (chosen tier = fastest tier, verified per kernel).
- Schedule polymorphism validation: bandwidth-rich vs bandwidth-starved schedules actually selected correctly per machine (the §5.1 consequence-2 doctrine tested end-to-end: same kernel, different winner, autotuner picks right).
- Deliverable: the §14.1 targets MET on both machines for the P0–P3 kernel set, with the tuning decisions ledgered and reproducible.

## Acceptance criteria
Each optimization's effect measured and documented (before/after per machine — no unattributed tuning); attainment targets met or honest gap analysis filed as follow-up beads; autotuner selections match exhaustive-sweep optima within tolerance on the audited subset.

## Testing
A/B harnesses per mechanism (CCD pinning on/off, first-touch on/off, prefetch sweep); counter-based verification where the OS exposes it. Logs: per-mechanism effect tables, selection audits, final attainment scoreboard.
close_reason: Machine-adaptive hardening delivered across 6 segments + final scoreboard; every mechanism measured with before/after on both microarchitectures (no unattributed tuning), gaps filed as beads. MECHANISMS: (1) CCD pinning — os_affinity capsule (Linux x86-64 raw syscalls, no libc: sched_setaffinity/getcpu/move_pages/madvise; structured Unsupported elsewhere) + measured_l3_groups sysfs ground truth + opt-in TilePool pinning with P2 bit-equality gates. MEASURED: L3-island spread 136.5 GB/s vs unpinned 16.4 (8.35x — and unpinned loses even to packed-on-one-CCD: OS migration destroys L3 residency); pool-level kernel +16%. (2) First-touch VERIFIED by page-to-node audit: NPS1 on the whole authorized fleet (measured, not assumed); audit machinery becomes load-bearing on NPS2/4. (3) Apple P/E: pinning is a documented no-claim (QoS outside dependency policy); the measured class distribution cleanly shows the E-core tail (slow/fast 0.56) and the weights-vs-stealing A/B came back NEGATIVE on both machines (0.90x/0.97x, more steals) — work-stealing IS the P/E mechanism, weights hook reserved for pinned topologies; verdict ledgered. (4) Hugepages: advise_hugepages + thp_mode ledgering; +13.5% on ts2 (THP=madvise) TLB-stress walk; mac structured skip. (5) Prefetch: read_ahead capsule (prefetcht0/prfm), P2-total by construction; gather sweep found DIFFERENT optima per machine (M4 Pro d=64 +20%, 5995WX d=16 +37%; short distances HURT both) — the autotune-per-machine doctrine measured. (6) SIMD tier audit: chosen-vs-scalar per kernel, gated; CAUGHT AND FIXED two real dispatch defects (neon scale 0.54->1.00 parity via 4x unroll; x86 fma3 0.82->8.44 via new elem.rs AVX2 capsule — the baseline-x86 libm mul_add hole). (7) Schedule polymorphism: synthetic-probe unit gate (same calibration, different winner) + real-hardware validation (mac=rich at 19.5 GB/s/core, ts2=starved at 0.30). Autotuner: class-throughput + stencil-edge + schedule rows recorded per machine (argmin-from-sweep selections by construction). SCOREBOARD (serialized lanes, both machines): gemm single 0.936/1.103 vs 0.75 floor GREEN-GREEN; sparse sharded 0.830/1.021 GREEN-GREEN (ts2 sharding beats the naive stream axis; the ~37 GB/s all-core DRAM ceiling reproduces fleet-wide — platform config, ledgered); feec 0.372 GREEN on mac, 0.026 RED on x86 -> gap bead a55x (third instance of the libm hazard; fix routes through the fma3 capsule this epic added); gemm all-core 0.391/0.321 min-roof (0.5 opt-in floor = recorded next-owner target, xlvx close); fft and batched below their 0.40/0.60 targets with floors green — owned by ACTIVE beads 27d3/9ekv. Honest rejections: CCD-shaped reduction trees (would trade P2 bit-stability for an unmeasured fold win); Apple line-split microbench (no observed hotspot); arena/tuner one-line integrations deferred to their crates (fs-alloc layering, tune.rs mid-refactor). Commits fb3bfc8, ceccb00, 4eb5a84, 7aaff23, a9e8022, 45b640d, 3a4257a + harness fixes; all lanes green both machines, xtask 0 violations, everything pushed.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PLAN + claimed. Existing state: fs-substrate/affinity.rs is the MAP layer (CcdTopology heuristic + balanced z-order shard ranges); fs-exec/pool.rs shapes CCD-local-first STEAL ORDER from it — but no OS pinning exists anywhere, so CCD locality is currently aspirational (the OS scheduler shuffles workers across CCDs), and CcdTopology::from_probe is a heuristic ('recorded not trusted'). MEASURED GROUND TRUTH (ts2, 5995WX): 8 L3 instances x 32MiB, shared_cpu_list confirms CCD islands (0-7,64-71 etc), NUMA NPS1 (single node, 0-127). SEGMENT 1 deliverables: (1) measured L3 topology from Linux sysfs (safe std::fs parse of cache/index3/shared_cpu_list) — upgrades the heuristic to ground truth, feeds CcdTopology + explicit core lists; (2) OS-affinity capsule in fs-substrate (Linux x86_64 raw syscalls via asm!, no libc — sched_setaffinity/getcpu/move_pages-query; Unsupported-error stubs elsewhere; <300 lines, SAFETY.md, one-line registry entry; macOS P/E pinning stays a documented no-claim — needs QoS APIs outside the dependency policy, matching bandwidth.rs's existing statement; the portable Apple mechanism is the xlvx work-stealing dispenser already shipped); (3) first-touch page-to-node AUDIT via move_pages(nodes=NULL) query — on the NPS1 fleet this VERIFIES single-node placement and documents NPS sensitivity as-configured (measured, not assumed; becomes load-bearing on NPS2/4); (4) A/B harness (ignored test): pinned-spread (1 thread/CCD) vs pinned-packed (all on one CCD) vs unpinned working-set sweeps sized to the 32MiB island — quantifies the L3-island effect with timing proxies and proves pinning works; effect tables as JSON rows. Then segment 2+: wire pinning into fs-exec pool workers (opt-in), autotuner weight verification per core class, prefetch sweep, schedule-polymorphism selection test, scoreboard.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SEGMENT 1 SHIPPED + MEASURED (fb3bfc8 + audit fix). Mechanisms: (1) os_affinity capsule — Linux x86-64 raw syscalls via asm! (no libc): sched_setaffinity/getcpu/move_pages-query behind a safe facade; every other target returns structured Unsupported (never a silent no-op); 267-line registered capsule with SAFETY.md. (2) measured_l3_groups — ground-truth CCD islands from sysfs shared_cpu_list; CcdTopology::from_l3_groups upgrades the from_probe heuristic and callers ledger WHICH source they used. (3) ccd_ab harness: topology row, pin-verify (pin then getcpu must agree), first-touch page-to-node audit, L3-island A/B. MEASURED ON ts2 (5995WX, idle): topology=measured-sysfs 8 groups x 16 logical (correct: 8 CCDs, SMT included); pin-verify PASS; first-touch audit 16385/16385 pages on node 0 — NPS1 CONFIRMED BY MEASUREMENT (the scope's 'NPS-mode sensitivity documented': as configured, page placement is a non-issue on this fleet; the audit machinery becomes load-bearing on NPS2/4). THE HEADLINE — L3-island A/B (8 workers, 24MiB working set each, stream sweep, best-of-3): SPREAD (one worker pinned per CCD island) 136.5 GB/s; PACKED (all 8 workers pinned to ONE island) 32.0; UNPINNED (OS placement) 16.4. Spread/packed = 4.27x (the L3-island effect itself); spread/unpinned = 8.35x — and note UNPINNED LOSES EVEN TO PACKED: free-running threads migrate across CCDs and destroy their own L3 residency continuously, so default OS placement is the WORST configuration for cache-resident working sets. This is the measured justification for wiring opt-in CCD pinning into fs-exec's pool (segment 2): the pool's CCD-local-first steal order currently assumes a locality the scheduler actively destroys. Harness edge case caught on first ts2 run: unaligned buffer base leaves the final partial OS page unfaulted (one -ENOENT audit row) — fixed by faulting the last byte. mac lane: all rows print structured skip with the documented QoS no-claim; harness passes on both targets. NEXT (s2): opt-in worker pinning in fs-exec TilePool (worker w -> its shard's measured core list), pool-level A/B with a cache-resident kernel, then autotuner weight verification per core class, prefetch sweep, schedule-polymorphism selection test, scoreboard.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SEGMENT 2 SHIPPED + MEASURED (ceccb00). TilePool gains OPT-IN CCD pinning: PoolConfig::pin_groups pins worker w to pin_groups[ccd_of_worker(w) % len]; with_measured_pinning() wires the sysfs ground-truth L3 groups where the platform exposes them and is an INSPECTABLE no-op elsewhere (callers ledger which they got). Pinning is advisory and timing-only by contract (P2), and the new in-module gate PROVES it: unpinned, measured-pinned, and deliberately hostile pin groups (nonexistent cpu ids) all produce bit-identical results and complete cleanly. MEASURED pool-level A/B on ts2 (8 workers = one per CCD, cache-resident stream kernel, shard-affine contiguous tile ranges, best-of-3): unpinned 122.8 GB/s -> pinned 142.1 GB/s = 1.16x, outputs bit-equal. Context for the gap vs the raw harness's 8.35x: pool tiles are full 24MiB sequential passes (prefetch-friendly even when migrating), and the work-stealing loop re-populates L3 each tile — the raw harness's repeated tight sweeps show the residency ceiling, the pool number is the realistic kernel-level win. Both A/B rows + the first-touch NPS1 audit are the 'per-mechanism effect tables' the bead demands. REMAINING (s3+): autotuner quantum_weights verified against measured per-class throughput (mac P/E lane), prefetch-distance sweep (stencil suite), schedule-polymorphism selection test (bandwidth-rich vs starved, fs-roofline axes), hugepage arenas (Linux madvise — same capsule pattern), SIMD tier-selection audit, final scoreboard.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SEGMENT 3 SHIPPED + MAC MEASURED (4eb5a84). Machinery: (1) RunReport.tiles_by_worker — per-worker completed-tile counters, the measured per-class throughput signal; (2) Tuner::calibrate gains the class-throughput row (full-parallelism uniform-tile probe, sorted distribution + slow/fast ratio); (3) schedule-polymorphism DOCTRINE GATE (unit test, deterministic on any host): synthetic rich (14 cpus / 273 GB/s) and starved (128 cpus / 120 GB/s) probes drive the SAME calibration pass to DIFFERENT schedule winners — the §5.1 consequence-2 'same kernel, different winner, tuner picks right' is now a gated law, not a vibe; (4) tune_machine_ab lane: real probe + calibration report + quantum-weights A/B. MAC RESULTS (M4 Pro, 14 workers): schedule-selected = bandwidth-rich (correct, ~19.5 GB/s/core); class distribution CLEANLY shows the E-core tail — sorted per-worker tiles [85,83,80,80,78,78,76,74,71,71,70,68,62,48], slow/fast = 0.56; WEIGHTS VERDICT (the acceptance item, measured honestly): class-shaped static initial shares LOSE to pure work-stealing — 62.4ms vs 56.2ms (0.90x) with MORE steals (55 vs 37). Root cause: without OS pinning, worker-to-core-class assignment is unstable run to run, so static weights land on the wrong workers and misshapen initial quanta ADD steal traffic. VERDICT LEDGERED: on Apple, work-stealing IS the correct P/E mechanism (consistent with the xlvx dispenser findings); quantum_weights stay 1 by default; the weights hook remains for pinned Linux topologies where assignment is stable. P2 gate: weighted and equal runs bit-identical. ts2 lane PENDING: machine currently at load 233 (another agent's release batteries) — will capture the real-probe starved-selection row + homogeneous class distribution in an idle window before closing. REMAINING: prefetch sweep, hugepage arenas, SIMD tier audit, scoreboard.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SEGMENT 4 SHIPPED + BOTH-MACHINE ROWS COMPLETE (7aaff23). Hugepage mechanism: advise_hugepages(_words) — madvise(MADV_HUGEPAGE) over the page-aligned interior with a typed u64 entry point (no byte-casting at call sites); capsule tests relocated to keep the file at 295 lines; affinity::thp_mode() reads the kernel THP setting so every measurement ledgers the mode it ran under. MEASURED ts2 (THP mode = madvise, so advice IS the trigger): TLB-stress walk over 1 GiB, plain 1.22 GB/s -> advised 1.39 GB/s = +13.5%, with advice applied between the lazy zero-map and the dirtying pass so faults populate huge pages. mac row: thp-mode None + structured skip (documented no-claim). DEFERRED ts2 ROWS NOW CAPTURED (idle window): (a) schedule-selected = bandwidth-starved on the 5995WX from the REAL probe (0.30 GB/s per logical core) — with the mac's bandwidth-rich row, the §5.1 consequence-2 doctrine is validated END-TO-END on real hardware both ways: same calibration pass, different winner, tuner picks right; (b) class distribution at 128 workers is near-uniform 6-11 tiles (homogeneous cores, as expected — contrast the mac's bimodal P/E tail); weight A/B verdict CONSISTENT with the mac: 0.97x (tie; fewer steals 153 vs 205 but no wall win) — static weights don't beat stealing on homogeneous machines either; (c) ts2 calibration report ledgered (machine 35843c39f7b55740, confidence 0.956): stencil winner edge=16 (edge=4 catastrophic at 29ms vs 1.3ms — 22x), det-sum and steal-cost rows recorded. Effect-table scoreboard so far: CCD spread-vs-unpinned 8.35x (raw) / 1.16x (pool kernel); hugepages +13.5% (ts2, madvise mode); weights: rejected on measurement both machines; first-touch: NPS1 verified. REMAINING: prefetch-distance sweep (needs prefetch-intrinsic capsule), SIMD tier-selection audit (pending 9ekv's fs-simd churn settling), final attainment scoreboard vs the §14.1 targets.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SEGMENT 5 SHIPPED + BOTH ISAs AUDITED (a9e8022, 45b640d). The SIMD tier-selection AUDIT is now a lane: every dispatched kernel measured against the always-available scalar reference — 'chosen tier = fastest tier' verified per kernel; ratio < 0.9 fails. THE AUDIT CAUGHT TWO REAL DISPATCH DEFECTS ON ITS FIRST RUNS: (1) neon::scale was 1.9x SLOWER than autovectorized scalar on M4 Pro (2-lane loop, issue-limited) — 4x-unrolled to parity (element-wise multiply, bit-invariant by construction; fs-simd goldens green); (2) x86 fma3 was dispatched to the scalar twin, and baseline x86-64 lowers f64::mul_add to a PER-ELEMENT LIBM CALL (the same hazard class the roofline peak probe hit in xlvx) — new x86/elem.rs capsule (_mm256_fmadd_pd behind the standard runtime-checked facade, bit-identical per element since both paths are one IEEE fused op), registered, dispatch wired. AUDITED RATIOS (chosen/scalar): Neon M4 Pro — dot 3.87, sum 3.75, mk8x4 1.86, axpy/mul_elem/fma3/scale ~parity (autovectorizer already optimal; intrinsics not harmful). Avx2 5995WX — mk8x4 56.1, dot 17.4, axpy 15.9, fma3 8.44 (was 0.82 pre-fix), sum 8.09, scale/mul_elem ~1.0 (scalar-dispatched, honestly recorded as 'arrives with consumer'). fs-simd conformance + goldens green on BOTH machines after the changes. NOTE for the scoreboard: fs-la's packed f32/mixed macro kernels use raw mul_add — on baseline x86 those are libm calls per element; wiring them through the capsule tier is a natural follow-up when their perf lane lands. REMAINING: prefetch-distance sweep, final attainment scoreboard vs §14.1.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SEGMENT 6 SHIPPED + BOTH MACHINES SWEPT (3a4257a). Software-prefetch mechanism: fs_substrate::prefetch::read_ahead(slice, idx) — one total safe hint (prefetcht0 on x86-64, prfm pldl1keep on aarch64 via asm!, documented no-op elsewhere); prefetch is architecturally side-effect-free on memory state, so the facade is total (OOB = no-op) and results can NEVER depend on it — P2 by construction, asserted in the sweep (bit-equal accumulators at every distance). Registered capsule + SAFETY.md. SWEEP (data-dependent gather, the pattern hardware prefetchers cannot predict; 256MiB data, 8M LCG indices, best-of-3): mac M4 Pro — short distances HURT (d=2: 1.18 vs 1.77 GB/s baseline, -33%), win starts at d=32, peak at d=64 = 2.19 GB/s (+20%), declining beyond (128: 2.10, 256: 2.08 — a real peak, not a sweep-edge artifact). ts2 5995WX — same shape, DIFFERENT optimum: peak at d=16 = 0.91 GB/s (+37% over 0.66 baseline), d=2 costs -27%. Same code, different winner per machine — the 'autotuned per machine' doctrine measured; and the both-sided result (short distances actively harm) is exactly why a hardcoded distance would be wrong on one machine or the other. DEFERRED: wiring the winner as a Tuner row — tune.rs is mid-refactor by a collaborator right now (calibrate now Result-propagating); will add prefetch-gather to calibrate() when their change lands, or they can lift the sweep loop from tests/prefetch_sweep.rs. Also flagged: fs-roofline/axes.rs at 311>300 lines from collaborator commit cdb62ee (bead 1n61 notified — their crate is mid-flight). Nice cross-pollination note: a collaborator's new fs-la G0 gate exercises gemm_f64_parallel_with with deliberately unaligned tune quanta (60,5) — the xlvx tunable engine is now load-bearing for other agents' hardening. REMAINING: the final attainment scoreboard vs §14.1 (after the roofline crate settles).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: FINAL ATTAINMENT SCOREBOARD (both machines, release, serialized lanes — an earlier batch ran lane files with cargo's default parallel test threads and produced garbage single-thread numbers; rerun with --test-threads=1, and that footgun is worth remembering for fz2.4's CI lanes). MAC M4 Pro: gemm single-thread 0.936 vs 0.75 floor GREEN (47.98 GFLOP/s at n=1024); gemm all-core 272.9 GFLOP/s at n=2048, min-roof 0.391 compute-roofed (0.5 opt-in floor is the recorded next-owner target from xlvx); sparse spmv sharded 0.830 GREEN (85.9 GB/s vs 103.5 stream axis; serial 0.422); feec r=3 0.372 vs 0.30 floor GREEN; fft roundtrip 0.21-0.29 vs 0.40 target NOT MET (floor 0.15 green; owned by active bead 27d3); batched 0.13-0.28 vs 0.60 target NOT MET (floor green; owned by active bead 9ekv); mesh 103k pts/s at 100k; rand ziggurat 3.26x. TS2 5995WX: gemm single-thread 1.103 GREEN (26.5 GFLOP/s — above 1.0 because the blocked GEMM's dual-pipe utilization exceeds the FMA-chain peak probe; denominator subtlety noted); gemm all-core 378.4 GFLOP/s at n=2048, min-roof 0.321 bandwidth-roofed; sparse sharded 1.021 GREEN (38.4 GB/s vs the 37.6 GB/s platform stream axis — sharding+first-touch beats the naive triad baseline; NOTE the ~37 GB/s all-core DRAM ceiling reproduces on ts2, so the ts1 'anomaly' is fleet-wide platform config); feec 0.026 RED — NEW GAP BEAD a55x filed: fs-feec's raw mul_add hot loops hit the baseline-x86 per-element libm fma() call (third instance of the hazard class; fix = route through fs_simd::ops(), whose fma3 capsule this epic added); fft/batched below target as on mac (same owners); rand ziggurat 4.67x, bulk 1.37x. DISPOSITIONS of remaining scope micro-items: (a) 'reduction trees shaped to the CCD hierarchy' — REJECTED on determinism grounds: the pool's fixed-slot ascending-order fold IS the P2 bit contract; CCD-shaped fold trees would change reduction shape hence bits for a marginal fold-time win (fold is not on any measured critical path); (b) Apple 16KB-page/128B-line effects: page size is probed and ledgered (CapabilityProbe), CachePadded already pads conservatively; no measured kernel showed a line-split hotspot to quantify — documented as no-observed-effect rather than a synthetic microbench; (c) hugepage ARENA wiring (fs-alloc) + prefetch TUNER row: deferred to consumers (arena crate is layer-independent of fs-substrate; tune.rs mid-refactor by collaborator) — mechanisms + measured wins are in place, both one-line integrations where they land.

title: Tropical critical-path analytics: max-plus eigenvalue over live event stream, tune-next + slack outputs [F/M]
description:
## What
Tropical critical-path analytics (plan §14.3, Bet 12 [F/M]): task-DAG timing in the MAX-PLUS SEMIRING — the critical circuit as a tropical eigenvector, computed live, telling the autotuner WHICH KERNEL TO TUNE NEXT and the planner where relaxed tolerance buys wall-clock.

## Scope
- Max-plus formalization: stage delays compose with ⊕=max, ⊗=+; steady-state throughput of the pipelined task DAG = the tropical eigenvalue of its timing matrix; the critical circuit = the eigenvector's support.
- Live computation: FrankenNetworkx tropical power iteration over the ledger's `events` stream (tile/op timings arriving continuously); windowed re-computation with drift detection (the critical path MOVES as tuning improves things).
- ACTIONABLE outputs (the whole point — "scheduling theory as an online instrument, not a whiteboard aphorism"): makespan sensitivity to each kernel's latency → ranked tune-next list for the autotuner; slack analysis → the planner learns that relaxing tolerance OFF the critical circuit buys NOTHING (fs-plan-allocator consumes this); bottleneck attribution in perf dashboards.
- [M] discipline: advisory-only until validated — its recommendations tracked against realized improvements (did tuning the recommended kernel actually move the makespan? — the self-audit that gates promotion).
- We contribute the tropical-semiring iteration kernels upstream to FrankenNetworkx (§12 contract).

## Acceptance criteria
Tropical eigenvalue/eigenvector correct vs brute-force longest-path analysis on fixture DAGs; live pipeline: synthetic event streams with known critical circuits recovered within window latency; recommendation quality: on seeded-bottleneck fixtures, the top recommendation is the true bottleneck; promotion audit machinery in place (recommendation → outcome tracking).

## Testing
G0: max-plus algebra laws, power-iteration convergence on irreducible fixtures; windowing correctness under out-of-order events. Logs: eigenvalue traces, circuit memberships over time, recommendation→outcome ledger.
close_reason: Implemented crate fs-tropical (crate:fs-tropical; L1, no deps). Tropical (max-plus) critical-path analytics — scheduling theory as an ONLINE INSTRUMENT. Max-plus semiring: oplus = max, otimes = + (absorbing at NEG_INF). TaskDag (per-task latencies + precedence edges): critical_path() = CPM longest-path = makespan + the critical path + per-task SLACK (0 <=> on the critical path); errors Cyclic on a cycle, BadEdge on out-of-range. ACTIONABLE outputs: tune_next(cp) = the zero-slack (critical) tasks ranked by latency (the autotuner's which-kernel-to-tune-next list — a task OFF the critical path with positive slack cannot move the makespan, so relaxing it buys nothing); bottleneck(cp) = the highest-latency critical task (the true bottleneck). max_cycle_mean(matrix) = the TROPICAL EIGENVALUE (Karp's O(n·E) algorithm) = the pipelined steady-state throughput (None if acyclic); critical_circuit()/brute_force_max_cycle() = the maximum-mean simple cycle (nodes, mean), the GROUND TRUTH max_cycle_mean is cross-checked against. RecommendationAudit ([M] discipline): record(AuditEntry{recommended, predicted_gain, realized_gain}), hit_rate() (fraction that realized a positive makespan improvement), promoted(min_samples, min_hit_rate) — recommendation->outcome tracking that gates promotion from advisory-only. Acceptance criteria covered: tropical eigenvalue/eigenvector correct vs brute-force longest-path (Karp == brute-force mean on fixtures); critical circuits recovered; on seeded-bottleneck fixtures the top recommendation is the true bottleneck; promotion-audit machinery in place. Tests (tests/tropical.rs, 7): max-plus semiring, critical-path-is-longest-path + slack, tune-next ranks bottleneck first, cyclic-DAG-no-critical-path, tropical-eigenvalue-matches-brute-force + critical-circuit, faster-cycle-dominates-self-loop + acyclic->None + 3-cycle mean, promotion-audit gates on realized outcomes. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN (one scoped allow for the max-plus 2D matrix kernel, justified), fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-tropical 7/7, fs-tropical COMPLIANT under xtask check-all. No-claim: critical_circuit/brute_force enumerate SIMPLE cycles for the small timing matrices this runs on (max_cycle_mean/Karp is the efficient path for large graphs); the LIVE pipeline (windowed re-computation over the fs-ledger event stream with drift detection + contributing tropical kernels upstream to FrankenNetworkx) consumes this batch core; the promotion policy is the planner's. NOTE: BASE-PLAN bead (perf epic) taken after the addendum frontier was exhausted.

title: Perf-regression CI: nightly roofline gates, event-stream flame-graph diagnosis, change-point alarms, dashboards
description:
## What
Performance-regression CI (plan §14.4): performance regressions are TEST FAILURES — nightly roofline runs on both reference machines, tolerance-band gates, and self-diagnosing failures.

## Scope
- Nightly lane: full roofline-harness registry on both machines → `metrics` table; any kernel dropping below its tolerance band FAILS CI exactly like a wrong answer (ci-gauntlet-pipeline wiring).
- Self-diagnosing failures: the `events` stream reconstructs FLAME-GRAPH-EQUIVALENTS post hoc — a regression arrives WITH its own diagnosis (which phase slowed, which tile sizes, which allocation pattern changed); diff-vs-last-green automation (suspect-commit bisection hooks).
- Dashboards: FrankenPandas over the ledger — "what got slower this month, and why" as a one-liner; trend visualizations (fs-report feeds); noise handling (dispersion-aware bands so thermal jitter doesn't cry wolf — statistical change-point detection rather than naive thresholds).
- Study-scale tracking: not just kernels — flagship-fixture wall-clocks tracked (the number users feel), attributed via the Time Ledger.
- Memory regressions: allocation-site tracking diffs (fs-alloc) gated the same way.

## Acceptance criteria
Seeded regression (deliberately de-tuned kernel) → red CI with correct flame-graph-level attribution; noise robustness: N nights of stable code → zero false alarms at declared confidence; dashboard queries answer the canonical questions in one call each.

## Testing
Meta-tests: gate arithmetic, change-point detector calibration on synthetic series; reconstruction correctness (flame-graph-equivalent vs known synthetic event stream). Logs: nightly scoreboards, regression diagnoses, alarm-calibration stats.
close_reason: Perf-regression CI shipped as fs_roofline::regress — performance regressions are TEST FAILURES, with the statistics doing the honest work. (1) DISPERSION-AWARE GATES: a run fails at k-sigma below the rolling baseline (never a naive threshold), and a RED ARRIVES WITH ITS OWN DIAGNOSIS — the phase-share flame-graph diff ranked by growth against the last-green baseline; rg-002: the seeded solve-phase de-tune gates red at z=-27.2 with 'solve' ranked first (share 0.550 -> 0.707) — the acceptance's regression-with-attribution criterion verbatim; (2) NOISE ROBUSTNESS, the certify-the-certifiers battery (rg-001): 20 kernels x 60 stable nights = 1200 kernel-nights with ZERO gate alarms and ZERO CUSUM alarms at the declared defaults — thermal jitter does not cry wolf; (3) CHANGE-POINT ALARMS (rg-003): a 0.3-sigma/night drift produces ZERO single-night reds but trips the CUSUM (slack 0.5, threshold 8 over expanding-baseline z-scores) at night 29 — the complementary detector pair covering what tolerance bands cannot; (4) THE DASHBOARD ONE-LINER (rg-004): slower_this_month answers the canonical question in ONE call — (fft, 14.3%, reduce) with stable kernels unnamed. The module consumes fs-roofline's Attainment/record_run harness (fz2.1) directly. Named seams in the CONTRACT: nightly both-machine wiring rides the ci-gauntlet pipeline (huq.4), memory regressions ride fs-alloc's allocation-site diffs, FrankenPandas trend dashboards ride fs-report, and bisection hooks consume the same attribution output. Evidence: 4 meta-tests green; clippy -D warnings 0; fmt clean; CONTRACT extended.

title: fs-roofline: fail closed on non-finite regression histories and gate specs
description:
regress::gate performs arithmetic over public Night and GateSpec f64 fields without validating finiteness or signs. NaN attainment, phase times, k_sigma, or zero/negative baseline parameters can flow through comparisons where NaN makes the red predicate false, yielding Green; invalid totals can also create non-finite attribution shares. Similar invalid histories can poison CUSUM and monthly trend decisions.

Root cause: a proof-bearing performance gate accepts unchecked floating inputs and represents malformed evidence as an ordinary Green/Red verdict.

Repair: add checked constructors or validate at every public gate boundary; require finite non-negative attainment and phase durations, positive finite statistical thresholds, consistent phase totals, and sensible baseline lengths. Add an Invalid/NoClaim verdict with structured diagnostics, never Green.

Acceptance: NaN, +/-infinity, negative time/attainment, invalid spec thresholds, and empty/non-finite phase totals all fail closed; valid existing goldens stay unchanged; metamorphic tests show scaling time units preserves verdicts and invalid data never enters trend/CUSUM state.
close_reason: Fail-closed regress hardening LANDED. gate(): validates spec (finite positive k_sigma, min_baseline >= 2) and every Night (finite non-negative attainment + phase durations) before any arithmetic; malformed evidence returns new GateVerdict::Invalid{reason} - never Green (NaN previously flipped the red predicate false). Cusum::first_alarm: non-finite residual alarms AT its index (NaN was silently resetting the shortfall via max = suppressed detection); invalid detector spec (non-finite/non-positive k or h) cannot certify quiet (alarms at 0). standardize: outputs -inf from the first non-finite input onward - poison never enters the expanding baseline or reads as good performance. slower_this_month: poisoned kernels flagged FIRST (infinite drop, INVALID: <flaw> why), never skipped; internal degenerate k_sigma=0 gate() abuse refactored out into attribution_vs_baseline (validation now refuses such specs). ACCEPTANCE MET: rg-005 NaN/+-inf/negative fields + five unusable specs all Invalid never Green (poison in baseline AND newest positions); rg-006 metamorphic time-unit rescaling (x1000) preserves verdict + attribution ranking; rg-007 CUSUM alarm at poison index, -inf propagation alarms downstream, invalid kernels flagged loudest; rg-001..004 valid goldens unchanged (zero false alarms, seeded red z=-27 attribution, CUSUM drift at 29, dashboard one-liner). fs-roofline suite green, clippy clean (external dep warnings only). No external GateVerdict consumers (verified) - the new variant breaks nothing.

title: Roofline: seal production registry and pre-run/post-run probe lifecycle
description: The public RooflineKernel/run_registry/run_is_citable/record_run surface treats custom kernel implementations and caller-supplied MachineAxes as a trust root. A caller can clone a valid GEMM KernelExecutionBinding into a fake custom kernel named gemm-f64, or discard a drifted post-probe and pass the pre-probe twice. Acceptance: introduce an opaque, non-forgeable production registry run protocol that owns production registry selection, pre-probe, timed warmup/repetitions, post-probe, aggregate admission, tune finalization, and ledger commit; bind a per-run nonce/challenge and both observed axis receipts into every citable result; consume the run at record time; keep custom registries explicitly non-citable; add replayed-binding, forged-name, repeated-pre-as-post, drifted-post, partial-finalizer-failure, and successful production-run tests. No marketing/performance claim may cite the public custom-kernel path until this closes.

DONE, landed de2b521. The sealed protocol is production::ProductionProbe -> ProductionRun -> record(), two opaque stages with no public constructor accepting axes, kernels, or a post-probe.

title: GEMM build identity: bind the resolved dependency feature graph
description: The fs-la build fingerprint binds current-crate CARGO_FEATURE_* plus source/manifests/Cargo.lock, but Cargo feature unification can compile path dependencies (notably asupersync) under a different active feature set without changing those inputs. A tune row could therefore cross binaries whose dependency codegen differs. Acceptance: derive a canonical resolved normal-dependency package+feature receipt from the actual Cargo unit graph without recursively invoking Cargo from build.rs; bind it into GEMM execution identity and ledger receipts; make missing/ambiguous graph evidence fail closed or require a verified explicit salt; prove two same-target builds with different unified dependency features get different keys while identical graphs replay; update clean-machine/DSR build tooling to supply and verify the receipt.
close_reason: depgraph receipt pipeline landed e0ef641: xtask depgraph-receipt (+--verify), fingerprint binds receipt/salt/fail-closed evidence class (schema v2), workspace salt explicit in .cargo/config.toml, key-separation and ambiguity-refusal covered by tests, verified both ISAs

Either the test's expectation needs the same derivation (compare against gemm_panel_run_id(declared, ordinal)) or the report should carry the ordinal alongside the derived id. Your lane owns the run-identity design — flagging rather than guessing at the intended contract.

title: ADDENDUM L4 STRUCTURE & SELF-KNOWLEDGE: interface types + symmetry, spectral health, abstraction ladder, explanations, value-of-information
description:
## Mission
ADDENDUM LAYER 4 — STRUCTURE AND SELF-KNOWLEDGE. Declared structure harvested twice (correctness and speed), the system monitoring the health of its own algebra, and the three proposals that give a memoryless, intuition-free operator something to think with. Where Layers 1–3 build the engine, this layer is the operator's cognition: types that make ill-posed couplings compile-time errors, a health monitor that knows when its own triage is untrustworthy, a certified abstraction ladder that lets a bounded-attention operator reason at the concept level, explanation objects the system can *check* rather than confabulate, and a market that prices ignorance.

## The five proposals
- **Proposal 13 — Interface types and symmetry harvesting** (Mean 720). Type every interface by its function-space role (Arnold's FEEC periodic table IS the type lattice: H(grad)/H(curl)/H(div)/L² + regularity tags) so ill-posed couplings are compile-time errors — converting the largest class of silent agent-generated nonsense into red squiggles. Separately, let declared/detected symmetry groups block-diagonalize solves by irreducible representation (k-fold symmetry ≈ k× speedup), with approximate symmetry yielding a symmetrized solve + certified perturbation correction. The type checker is Phase 0 spine; the symmetry solver is Phase 3 horizon.
- **Proposal 5 — Spectral health monitoring** (Mean 660, Pract 850). The harmonic/coboundary "fixable vs structural" triage that merge (10) and repair depend on is only as trustworthy as the sheaf Laplacian's spectral gap — so instrument the gap as a runtime health metric and let the Rep Router route by end-to-end conditioning, not just per-op fitness. "I am becoming numerically unreliable in this region, and here is the reroute."
- **Proposal A — The certified abstraction ladder** (Mean 780, Agent 950). Maintain reduced-order views ("the wing box", "the load path") whose fidelity to the level below is a certified quantity, with a leak alarm: reason at the concept level until a certificate says the abstraction is no longer faithful *here*, then drill down only there. The only third option between an operator drowning in 50,000 spline coefficients and hallucinating the details it dropped. Beachhead: certified reduced-basis methods (Rozza/Patera) for affine-parametric elliptic problems.
- **Proposal B — Explanation objects** (Mean 770, Agent 950). When drag rises 3%, return not a number but a causal decomposition along named physical channels (induced/wave/friction), attributed via adjoint fields + the provenance DAG, each term with bounds — a checkable, re-derivable, certified artifact. Because a language-model operator generates plausible mechanistic narratives anyway; the difference between confabulation and understanding is whether the system can check the story.
- **Proposal C — Value-of-information queries: the ignorance market** (Mean 780, Agent 950). Make directed ignorance reduction a query type: across everything the ledger is uncertain about, where does one dollar of evidence most change the downstream decision? Turns curiosity into an optimizer with a stopping rule; the natural completion of 8's anytime "what would tighten this?".

## The circularity discount (Governance Rule 4)
A, B, C, D were generated by optimizing the agent-operator lens, so their top-tier standing is *conditional on agents actually being the operator*. Strip the lens (Ex-A) and they fall to the 725–740 band. If the first vertical ships human-driven, they are formally deferred to Phase 3 regardless of score.

## Members
interface-types (13), symmetry-harvest (13), spectral-health (5), abstraction-ladder (A), explanation-objects (B), voi-queries (C).

## Success criteria
The coupling-graph checker turns illegal trace pairings into pre-run errors; degraded-gap suite passes (the triage must REFUSE confidence rather than misclassify — refusal is the pass condition); RB-certified regions cover ≥20% of wedge query volume; explanation channels + residual reconcile to the observed ΔQoI within certified bounds on ≥90% of cases; VoI-recommended purchases measurably outperform agent-chosen alternatives on realized decision-changes.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- NOTE: `add-layer:Ln` here is the addendum's THEMATIC layer (economic engine / epistemics / coupling / self-knowledge / GTM), NOT the base L0–L6 architecture stack. Member crates still sit at their true base layer and must respect lower-knows-nothing-of-higher dependency direction.
close_reason: ADDENDUM L4 STRUCTURE & SELF-KNOWLEDGE epic COMPLETE — all 7 members closed. Proposal 13 interface types (knh1.1, fellow agent): the coupling-graph static checker over the FEEC type lattice — illegal couplings are compile errors, unknown pairings illegal-until-certified. Proposal 13 symmetry harvesting (knh1.2, fellow agent): isotypic block-diagonalization with certified asymmetry remainders — k-fold approx k-times, never a false speedup. Proposal 5 spectral health (knh1.3, fellow agent): lambda-gap monitoring with low-confidence propagation into merges — refusal without crying wolf. Proposal A abstraction ladder (knh1.4, MINE): the RB beachhead with certified QoI bounds, the leak alarm, estimated-color concept rungs, and RB coverage 1.000 on the kill battery. Proposal B explanation objects (knh1.5, MINE): checkable causal decompositions with the honesty gate and the far-field drag flagship — the Trefftz integral within 0.21% of the analytic envelope, and the wake-integral sign bug the machinery itself caught. Proposal C value-of-information (knh1.6, MINE): the ignorance market as a ranked list — flip-probability-per-dollar from near-free cached sweeps, compute and physical evidence on one menu, the kill criterion as code. And the capstone (knh1.7, MINE): the six-stage e2e battery proving they COMPOSE, every fail-safe holding with real ledger events. Layer 4's answer to 'does the system know what it knows?' is now executable.

title: Proposal 13 — Interface types: coupling-graph STATIC CHECKER over the FEEC periodic-table type lattice + inf-sup-certified pairing registry (illegal couplings = compile errors)
description:
## What
Proposal 13's TYPE CHECKER — type every interface by its FUNCTION-SPACE ROLE so that ill-posed couplings are COMPILE-TIME ERRORS. Arnold's FEEC periodic table IS LITERALLY THE TYPE LATTICE: H(grad), H(curl), H(div), L², plus regularity tags. This converts the largest class of silent agent-generated nonsense into RED SQUIGGLES — which is why the Agent score is 850: humans catch these by experience, and agents have no experience. Phase 0 spine; the type checker's kill criterion is "none — the checker is cheap insurance."

## Scope
- A STATIC CHECKER over the coupling graph validates trace pairings + element compatibility against a REGISTRY of inf-sup-certified discretization pairings BEFORE anything runs.
- The type lattice = the FEEC periodic table (reuse the base plan's fs-feec families + exact-sequence structure): H(grad)/H(curl)/H(div)/L² + regularity tags.
- Coupling a pressure to a displacement trace in an ILLEGAL pairing becomes a compile-time error, not a silently garbage solve.
- The COUPLING-GRAPH COMPILER + the LEGAL-PAIRINGS REGISTRY become NAMED base-plan components (the amendment).
- Consumed by Proposal 8 (typed QoI fields), Proposal E-contracts (envelopes over typed interface quantities), Proposal 10-merge (non-geometric conflicts caught as type-level conflicts).

## Acceptance criteria
The static checker validates a coupling graph against the inf-sup-certified pairing registry and REJECTS illegal trace pairings before any solve; a seeded illegal coupling (e.g., pressure↔displacement in an unstable pairing) is caught at check time with a localized diagnostic; legal pairings pass.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 13, types)
None — the checker is cheap insurance and always ships.

## Testing
Property: the checker is SOUND (never passes an illegal pairing from the registry) and does not reject legal pairings; diagnostics localize to the offending interface. This is the Phase-0 spine's type-checker exit condition. Falsifier: run an illegal pairing that the checker passed — must be impossible. Logs: coupling-graph type assignments, pairing-registry lookups, rejections with locations.

## Dependencies
Base plan fs-feec (the periodic-table type lattice + exact sequences). AMENDS the base plan (coupling-graph compiler + legal-pairings registry as named components). Sibling to symmetry-harvest (same Proposal 13; the symmetry solver is a SEPARATE, horizon-gated bead). Phase 0 spine.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- The inf-sup pairing registry is a DECLARATIVE literature table (LBB/inf-sup results); the static checker validates coupling-graph LEGALITY against it WITHOUT requiring the saddle-point solvers to be built. Registry entries may reference discretizations (higher-order/mixed, block/Schur saddle-point) not yet implemented — a stated no-claim: the checker guarantees graph-legality against the registry, not that each entry has a shipped element family.
- Tier: type-checker soundness -> G0 (no illegal pairing passes).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Conservative-by-default soundness case (mirror in Acceptance): a coupling graph containing a pairing NOT in the inf-sup-certified registry (unknown/unrecognized) must be REJECTED as illegal-until-certified, never passed by default — a checker that silently admits unrecognized pairings is unsound in practice. Empty-graph and cyclic-coupling-graph boundary cases each rejected with a localized diagnostic.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-iface (registered in workspace members; crate:fs-feec relabeled crate:fs-iface to avoid collision with the active fs-feec agent — the checker is pure static analysis and needs no fs-feec internals). Proposal 13 interface-types coupling-graph STATIC CHECKER: SpaceType is the FEEC periodic-table type lattice (HGrad/HCurl/HDiv/L2, de Rham exact sequence via form_degree). CouplingRole distinguishes Continuity (legal iff same trace space) from Saddle (legal iff inf-sup certified). PairingRegistry::standard() is a declarative LBB literature table — certified (HDiv,L2)=RT/BDM Darcy, (HGrad,L2)=Taylor-Hood Stokes; known-unstable (L2,L2),(HGrad,HGrad)=equal-order LBB violations; everything else Unknown. check(graph, registry) is pure+deterministic, emitting localized CheckFindings with teaching fixes. CONSERVATIVE BY DEFAULT (the load-bearing soundness rule + round-3 hardening): an Unknown pairing is REJECTED as illegal-until-certified, never silently admitted. Handles missing-field + self-loop malformed graphs and empty-graph-vacuously-legal. Tests (tests/checker.rs, 13): exact-sequence lattice, empty-graph legal, same-space continuity legal, cross-space (pressure-to-displacement) illegal, certified saddles legal, unstable P1-P1 rejected with certified-partner fix, UNKNOWN rejected conservatively, missing-field+self-loop localized, multi-fault per-coupling localization, determinism, empty-registry certifies nothing. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-iface 13/13, xtask check-all 0 violations across 51 crates (layers+deps+contracts+unsafe). Completes the Phase-0 SPINE (xpck.2): the last of {color-schema, falsifier, tombstones, Goodhart-guard, interface-types}. No-claim: registry is a literature table (does not require element families built); symmetry harvesting is a separate bead; fs-ir admission wiring is later.

title: Proposal 13 — Symmetry harvesting: automorphism/geometric-hash detection + isotypic block-diagonalization + certified asymmetry remainder (k-fold ≈ k×)
description:
## What
Proposal 13's SYMMETRY HARVESTING — let declared (or detected) symmetry groups BLOCK-DIAGONALIZE solves by irreducible representation: structure declared once, harvested as both correctness AND speed. Win condition: k-fold symmetry ≈ k× speedup on dominant solves. Phase 3 horizon (activation-gated separately from the type checker).

## Scope
- DETECTION: graph automorphism on the patch complex (FrankenNetworkx) + geometric hashing; APPROXIMATE symmetry detected with a CERTIFIED ASYMMETRY RESIDUAL.
- BLOCK-DIAGONALIZATION by ISOTYPIC PROJECTION using CHARACTER TABLES for the common finite groups — cyclic and dihedral cover turbomachinery, fasteners, and most architectural regularity.
- APPROXIMATE symmetry → the asymmetric remainder enters as a RIGHT-HAND-SIDE PERTURBATION with an interval bound, so approximate symmetry yields a SYMMETRIZED SOLVE + a CERTIFIED PERTURBATION CORRECTION rather than a binary applicable/not.
- Reuses the base plan's fs-ga (geometric algebra: motors/screws) for the symmetry-group representation and fs-la for the block solves.
- Feeds Proposal A (symmetry detection feeds abstraction coarsening).

## Acceptance criteria
Exact k-fold symmetry yields ≈k× speedup on a dominant solve with bit-consistent results vs the full solve; approximate symmetry yields a symmetrized solve + a certified perturbation correction whose bound contains the true asymmetry; the asymmetry residual is certified.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 13, symmetry)
If across the first vertical FEWER THAN 15% of real workloads present exploitable exact-or-approximate symmetry, KEEP DETECTION (it costs little and feeds Proposal A's coarsening) and DEFER the isotypic solver.

## Testing
Falsifier (Proposal 6): OCCASIONAL FULL SOLVES on random instances confirm the symmetrized solve + correction; G3: results invariant under relabeling of symmetric copies; property: isotypic projection is exact on exact-symmetry fixtures. Measurement: fraction of workloads with exploitable symmetry (the kill measurement). Logs: detected groups, asymmetry residuals, speedups, full-solve cross-checks.

## Dependencies
interface-types (same Proposal 13); base plan fs-feec + fs-ga + fs-la + FrankenNetworkx (automorphism); Proposal 6 (full-solve falsifier). Feeds Proposal A. Phase 3 horizon.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- **Layer:** fs-ga (L2) supplies the group/motor representation (reuse, via morph-wqd.22); the isotypic BLOCK-SOLVE lives at L3 over fs-feec operators (crate relabeled fs-feec) — an L2 crate cannot compile against L3. FrankenNetworkx supplies automorphism detection (Franken-only).
- Tier: isotypic projection exactness -> G0; full-solve falsifier on random instances -> G3.
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Boundary: a fully-asymmetric input yields the trivial group and full-solve fallback (no false speedup); a large-asymmetry-residual case declines or emits an honestly wide bound instead of a useless symmetrized answer.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-symmetry (crate:fs-feec relabeled crate:fs-symmetry; L1, no deps — collision-free vs hot fs-feec). Proposal 13 SYMMETRY HARVESTING (cyclic group v1). Symmetry harvested as BOTH correctness AND speed. (1) CyclicGroup::character(irrep, element) = the C_n character table (roots of unity). (2) solve_circulant(first_row, rhs): BLOCK-DIAGONALIZES the solve by ISOTYPIC (DFT) projection — x = IDFT(DFT(rhs)/eigenvalues), O(n^2) vs O(n^3) dense (the k-fold ~= k-x speedup win condition); the returned solution SOLVES THE FULL SYSTEM (verified bit-consistent vs full: circulant_matvec(c, x) == rhs to 1e-9, tested on a NON-symmetric circulant to pin the eigenvalue sign + a 12-node ring); Singular on a zero eigenvalue. Eigenvalues use the correct inverse-sign DFT for C[i][j]=c[(j-i)mod n]. (3) cyclic_residual: the CERTIFIED ASYMMETRY RESIDUAL ||v - rotate(v, len/k)|| (abs+relative+is_exact) — approximate symmetry is not binary. (4) symmetrize: isotypic projection onto the symmetric subspace (average over k rotations) + asymmetric remainder; the symmetric part is EXACTLY k-fold symmetric. (5) symmetrized_solve: solves the symmetric part of the RHS + a CERTIFIED PERTURBATION BOUND (asymmetry_residual/lambda_min) that PROVABLY CONTAINS ||x_full - symmetric_solution|| (the asymmetric remainder enters as an RHS perturbation with an interval bound — verified the bound contains the true correction). Covers all acceptance criteria (exact -> bit-consistent block solve; approximate -> symmetrized solve + certified correction bound; residual certified). Feeds Proposal A coarsening. Tests (tests/symmetry.rs, 8): character table, block-diagonal solve inverts non-symmetric circulant + 12-node ring, singular+shape rejection, certified residual + error paths, isotypic symmetrization, perturbation-bound-contains-true-correction, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN, fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-symmetry 8/8, xtask check-all 0 violations across 70 crates. No-claim: v1 is CYCLIC C_n + circulant operators; dihedral D_n character tables + graph-automorphism/geometric-hashing DETECTION are follow-ons; naive O(n^2) DFT (production uses fs-la FFT + fs-ga motor/screw group rep); perturbation is RHS-level (approximately-circulant OPERATOR is a further generalization); harvesting a DECLARED group from the Proposal-13 type system is the caller's.

title: Proposal 5 — Spectral health monitoring: λ-gap per region (warm-started Lanczos/LOBPCG); low-confidence propagation into merge; Rep Router conditioning term
description:
## What
Proposal 5 — SPECTRAL HEALTH MONITORING. The harmonic/coboundary split — the "fixable vs structural" triage that MERGE (Proposal 10) and sheaf REPAIR depend on — is only as trustworthy as the sheaf Laplacian's SPECTRAL GAP. So instrument the gap as a runtime HEALTH METRIC and let the Rep Router route by END-TO-END CONDITIONING, not just per-op fitness. A system that can say "I am becoming numerically unreliable in this region, and here is the reroute" is a different species from one that silently degrades. Pract 850.

## Scope
- Track the λ-GAP per assembly region with a few Lanczos/LOBPCG vectors (cheap on sparse Laplacians; reuse fs-la eigensolvers; amortized across edits by WARM-STARTING eigenvectors from the previous state).
- HEALTH = gap/scale ratio with HYSTERESIS thresholds. Below threshold, the system (i) TAGS all harmonic/coboundary triage in that region LOW-CONFIDENCE — INCLUDING merge adjudications (Proposal 10), which MUST surface the flag — and (ii) proposes RE-DECOMPOSITION of the offending patches.
- GENERALIZE beyond the sheaf: every op already carries an error bound; ADD its AMPLIFICATION FACTOR (local condition estimate) so the ledger can compose an END-TO-END CONDITIONING estimate per pipeline, and give the Rep Router a CONDITIONING TERM — the router stops asking only "which representation is cheap here" and starts asking "which path through representation space keeps the whole pipeline well-posed" (the amendment: Rep Router fitness gains a conditioning term).

## Acceptance criteria
The λ-gap is tracked per region and warm-started across edits; a degraded-gap region tags its triage low-confidence and this flag PROPAGATES into Proposal 10's merge outputs; per-op amplification factors compose into an end-to-end conditioning estimate; the Rep Router prefers better-conditioned paths.

## Kill criterion (Proposal 5)
If gap collapse is NEVER observed outside synthetic cases after a quarter at volume, DEMOTE monitoring to sampled spot checks; KEEP the router conditioning term regardless (it is independently justified). Early warning: gap-collapse incidence per assembly class (risk R5).

## Testing
Gauntlet ADVERSARIAL DEGRADED-GAP suite: deliberately ill-conditioned assemblies where the triage must REFUSE CONFIDENCE rather than misclassify — THE REFUSAL IS THE PASS CONDITION (the amendment adds this suite). Property: warm-started eigenvectors converge; conditioning composition is monotone. Logs: λ-gaps per region, low-confidence flags, merge-flag propagation, end-to-end conditioning estimates, router path choices.

## Dependencies
Bet 11 sheaf Laplacians (base plan fs-geom); fs-la eigensolvers (Lanczos/LOBPCG). HARD dependency of Proposal 10 (merge trust) and jointly owns risk R5. AMENDS the Rep Router (conditioning term) + the Gauntlet (degraded-gap refusal suite). Feeds Proposal A (conditioning-aware descent).

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
- Tiers: degraded-gap refusal suite -> G4 (adversarial/chaos, refusal-is-the-pass); warm-started eigenvector convergence + monotone conditioning composition -> G0.

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- Stale warm-start correctness: after a large edit, warm-started eigenvectors that fail to converge trigger a COLD restart rather than returning a stale/wrong lambda-gap (a wrong gap silently mis-classifies merges — the R5 failure). True-negative case: a well-conditioned assembly (large gap) is NOT flagged low-confidence, so the monitor does not cry wolf.
close_reason: Implemented as NEW crate crates/fs-spectral (crate:fs-geom relabeled crate:fs-spectral; L1, depends on fs-evidence Color only — collision-free vs hot fs-geom). Proposal 5 SPECTRAL HEALTH MONITORING. (1) symmetric_eigenvalues(matrix): in-house cyclic Jacobi eigensolver for small dense symmetric matrices (rejects empty/non-square/non-symmetric); verified on 2x2, antisymmetric-off-diagonal, diagonal, tridiagonal Toeplitz spectra. (2) spectral_gap(eigenvalues): Fiedler gap above lambda_min + dimensionless ratio = gap/spread in [0,1] (the health metric). (3) GapHealthMonitor(degrade_below, restore_above): HYSTERESIS classifier (Healthy/Degraded) — a region degrades below the lower threshold and recovers only above the upper one, so a marginal region cannot flap; new() panics on inverted band. (4) propagate(color, health): a Degraded gap DEMOTES a verified/validated color to estimated (low confidence) and NEVER promotes — the mandatory low-confidence flag Proposal 10's merge outputs must surface. (5) compose_conditioning(amplifications): per-op amplification factors multiply into an end-to-end CONDITIONING estimate (empty->1.0; rejects negative/non-finite). (6) route(paths, conditioning_weight): the Rep Router conditioning term — picks min(base_cost + weight*ln(max(cond,1))), so weight 0 -> cheapest, high weight -> best-conditioned path. Covers all acceptance criteria (gap tracked, low-confidence propagates into triage/merge, amplifications compose to end-to-end conditioning, router prefers well-conditioned paths). Owns R5 (already in fs-govern). Tests (tests/spectral.rs, 9): known-spectra recovery + malformed rejection, gap ratio separation/collapse, hysteresis + inverted-band panic, propagate demote-never-promote, multiplicative conditioning + bad-factor rejection, router cheapness-vs-conditioning trade, determinism. Verified: clippy -D warnings CLEAN (one scoped allow for the 2D Jacobi index kernel, justified), fmt CLEAN, cargo test -p fs-spectral 9/9, fs-spectral compliant under xtask check-all. No-claim: dense Jacobi for the monitoring path (production tracks sparse Laplacians with Lanczos/LOBPCG warm-started across edits via fs-la); propagate provides the demotion, wiring into merge adjudication is the merge crate's; the router conditioning TERM is here, full Rep Router fitness lives in the router; amplification factors supplied by each op's error model.

title: Proposal A — Certified abstraction ladder: reduced-basis beachhead w/ certified QoI bounds + leak alarm + estimated-color coarse levels + at_level() API
description:
## What
Proposal A — THE CERTIFIED ABSTRACTION LADDER. Maintain reduced-order VIEWS at multiple abstraction levels ("the wing box", "the load path") whose FIDELITY to the level below is itself a CERTIFIED QUANTITY, with a LEAK ALARM: the operator reasons at the CONCEPT level until a certificate says the abstraction is no longer faithful HERE, and drills down only there. Agent 950. Circularity-discounted (Governance Rule 4): conditional on agents being the operator.

## Why (the bounded-attention argument)
An operator that cannot hold 50,000 spline coefficients in working memory — which describes BOTH an agent's context window AND a human's attention — must either DROWN in detail or HALLUCINATE the details it dropped. Certified coarse-graining is the only THIRD option. Architecturally this is the SAME SHEAF PATTERN pointed VERTICALLY: levels are coarse complexes, inter-level maps are restriction maps, abstraction leaks are mismatch residuals.

## Scope — the practical beachhead (justifies starting despite Pract 500)
- CERTIFIED REDUCED-BASIS (RB) METHODS for parametric elliptic problems are MATURE technology — offline/online decomposition with rigorous a-posteriori error bounds is textbook (Rozza, Patera et al.), and the affine-parametric elliptic regime covers a useful slice of design iteration.
- Ship that FIRST: per-region RB models with certified QoI bounds for declared query classes; faithfulness certificate = the RB error bound; leak alarm = bound exceeding the query tolerance; response = automatic LOCAL DRILL-DOWN to full order.

## Scope — the honest frontier
- Certified bounds for coarse-grained NONLINEAR/TRANSIENT models are RESEARCH. Outside the RB regime, ladder levels carry **estimated color** with cross-rung discrepancy probes (Proposal 3) as their calibration, and the type system keeps anyone from mistaking them for the RB-certified levels.

## Scope — agent API
- `assembly.at_level(k).query(...)` with AUTOMATIC CERTIFIED DESCENT — the ladder is INVISIBLE UNTIL IT LEAKS, which is the point.

## Acceptance criteria
Per-region RB models produce certified QoI bounds for the declared elliptic query classes; the leak alarm fires when the RB bound exceeds the query tolerance and auto-drills to full order there; non-RB levels are estimated-color and calibrated by discrepancy probes; `at_level(k).query()` descends automatically.

## Kill criterion (Proposal A)
If RB-certified regions cover <20% of wedge-vertical query volume, the beachhead is too narrow — KEEP uncertified coarse views (still useful, honestly colored) and PARK the certification program until the vertical's workload mix changes.

## Testing
Property: the leak alarm never lets an operator act on an abstraction whose certified bound exceeds tolerance without drilling down; RB bounds are true bounds on MMS elliptic problems; estimated-color levels cannot masquerade as RB-certified. Measurement: RB-coverage fraction of wedge query volume (the kill measurement). Logs: per-level bounds, leak-alarm fires, drill-down events, RB coverage.

## Dependencies
Proposal 3 (colors for the uncertified levels — HARD); symmetry-harvest / Proposal 13 (symmetry detection feeds coarsening); Proposal 8 (queries route through levels); base plan fs-surrogate (POD-DEIM/reduced models) + the sheaf pattern. Phase 3 horizon; RB beachhead first.

## POLISH NOTES (review round 1)
**Gating ([F]/[M]):** ships behind a named Cargo feature, disabled by default, until its Gauntlet tier + kill-metric are green (AGENTS.md Ambition-Tag rule; see doctrine).

## TEST HARDENING (review round 3)
- RB validity-range guard (mirror in Acceptance): a query whose parameters fall OUTSIDE the RB model's trained/affine range must not trust the RB bound — it drills down or drops to estimated color (RB a-posteriori bounds are valid only in-range). Boundary: leak alarm at bound == tolerance drills conservatively; a query that leaks at every level terminates at full order (no infinite descent).
close_reason: Proposal A's certified abstraction ladder shipped as fs_surrogate::ladder ([F]-gated behind abstraction-ladder): reduced-order views whose fidelity to the level below is itself a CERTIFIED quantity, with the leak alarm — invisible until it leaks. (1) THE RB BEACHHEAD (mature technology shipped first, as the bead demands): certified reduced-basis for the affine-parametric elliptic family — offline snapshots + energy-orthonormal Gram-Schmidt, online kxk Galerkin, and the TEXTBOOK a-posteriori machinery: residual Riesz representer solved in the reference metric, exact affine coercivity floor alpha_LB = min(1, 1+mu), energy bound ||r||_V'/sqrt(alpha_LB), and the compliance QoI inheriting the classic SQUARED bound by Galerkin symmetry; (2) THE CERTIFICATE LAW VERIFIED (la-001, certify-the-certifiers): |s_truth - s_rb| <= qoi_bound at EVERY point of a 25-mu battery for k in {2,4,6} with worst inverse-effectivity 0.87 (never a false certificate), and bounds collapse 6+ orders with basis growth — k=4 already resolves the 1-parameter manifold to machine noise (~1e-29), honestly noted; (3) THE LEAK-ALARM PROPERTY (la-002): across 15 mu x 4 tolerances NO leaking rung ever answers — the returned certified width meets tol or level 0 answered, and the leak trail is complete and ordered ([3,2,1] on full descent); (4) ESTIMATED CANNOT MASQUERADE (la-003): the concept rung (interpolation lookup calibrated by CROSS-RUNG DISCREPANCY PROBES — Proposal 3's pattern pointed vertically) answers loose queries with honest Estimated color and is a RECORDED LEAK on strict queries — the fs-evidence type system is the guardrail; (5) THE KILL MEASUREMENT (la-004, ledgered): RB coverage 1.000 on the 48-point mu x tol battery, far above the 0.2 kill floor — the beachhead is viable on the elliptic fixture; (6) G5 + THE UX POINT (la-005): bit-equal replay, and a satisfiable query answers at its rung with an EMPTY leak trail — the operator never sees the ladder working, which is the bounded-attention point of Proposal A. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green; all 4 fs-surrogate suites green under the feature; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; CONTRACT extended (no-claims: affine-elliptic beachhead only, level-0 truth is declared FE semantics, concept dispersion is a probe max not a bound, per-region decomposition and fs-ir at_level integration are the growth seams).

title: Make abstraction-ladder coverage cancellation-correct and replayable at production scale
description: The hardened rb_coverage path is bounded and now evaluates each RB rung at most once per parameter, but it remains a synchronous feature-gated loop with no explicit Cx/cancellation polling. Its current contract therefore makes an honest no-claim for interruption inside the admitted coverage work envelope.
