title: Close TilePool operation leases over heap-owning outputs and direct kernel allocations
description: The reviewed wf9.16 implementation now enforces one shared OperationMemoryLease over checked executor root metadata and all lease-bound arena chunks, including pairwise-fold transient capacity. Rust's current TileKernel API still permits arbitrary direct heap allocation and heap-bearing K::Out values whose Vec/String/custom payload storage is invisible to size_of::<K::Out>(); charging only the header cannot enforce a full-run live-set ceiling, and post-allocation deep-size accounting would leave an over-limit live interval. Design a layer-correct lease-aware artifact/output allocator or an enforceable sealed output-storage contract so every production kernel allocation is admitted before ownership. Preserve deterministic reduction, cancellation drain, safe Rust, and pure-Rust sovereignty. Acceptance: direct heap bypass cannot compile or cannot enter the leased production path; nested outputs and panic diagnostics have explicit bounded storage; admission precedes allocation; cancellation/unwind releases every charge; hostile custom-output kernels prove bypass refusal; receipts distinguish tracked payload bytes from allocator overhead; no claim for thread stacks/platform allocator bookkeeping without measurement.
close_reason: sealed lease-admitted output contract landed c30572d: LeasedVec admission-before-allocation storage, sealed AdmittedStorage/LeaseAdmittedOut bound on both leased entries (hostile outputs fail to compile), Concat fold wrapper with documented merge-refusal containment, full battery green on ts1 both crates + dependents

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claiming (CloudyFinch lane - the wf9.16 author, building on the b1110a3 fail-closed review hardening). Design direction per the acceptance: (1) fs-alloc gains LeasedVec<T> - an owned growable container whose backing storage is ADMITTED through the OperationMemoryLease before allocation (reserve -> try_reserve_exact -> ownership; carries its LeaseCharge; Drop/unwind releases; grow re-admits before realloc). Receipts therefore distinguish tracked payload bytes (lease) from allocator overhead (no-claim, documented). (2) fs-exec gains a SEALED output-admission trait (private supertrait token, implemented for (), scalars, fixed arrays/tuples of admitted types, and LeasedVec<admitted>) and the leased production entry becomes bounded on it - a heap-bearing custom K::Out (Vec/String/Box payloads invisible to size_of) fails to COMPILE on the leased path while legacy unbounded entries stay unconstrained. (3) Cx exposes the operation lease so kernels construct LeasedVec through admission (typed TileFailure on refusal); nested outputs are admitted structures by induction; panic diagnostics get an explicit bounded-storage cap. (4) Battery: hostile custom-output kernel refused at compile time (compile_fail doctest), admission-precedes-allocation ordering test, unwind releases every payload charge, nested LeasedVec accounting, receipts split payload vs overhead. Preserves deterministic reduction (LeasedVec merges by explicit kernel-authored Reduce), cancellation drain, safe Rust, pure-Rust sovereignty.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: DONE, landed c30572d. Acceptance mapping: (1) direct heap bypass cannot enter the leased production path - both run_declared_leased_budgeted and the lx0e run_scoped lane bound K::Out: LeaseAdmittedOut, the SEALED inductive contract (private token; scalars, fixed arrays/tuples of admitted types, LeasedVec of admitted elements, Concat) under which every owned byte is inline or lease-admitted; hostile heap-bearing outputs (Vec/String/custom) FAIL TO COMPILE there - compile_fail battery in admit.rs proves both the bypass refusal and the sealing; Vec keeps Reduce for legacy unleased wrappers only. (2) admission precedes allocation - fs_alloc::LeasedVec reserves through the lease BEFORE try_reserve_exact, growth admits the full replacement first (old+new peak is the honest concurrent cost), RAII charge inside the value releases on drop/unwind. (3) nested outputs bounded by induction (LeasedVec<LeasedVec<..>> composes; Concat<[f64;4]> etc. covered in tests); panic diagnostics stay short static strings (no-claim documented). (4) cancellation/unwind releases every charge - unit + battery proofs incl. the merge-refusal unwind. (5) hostile-output refusal + admission-order + exact-append accounting + end-to-end leased list kernel with deterministic fold order all in the battery; the peak-starved run legitimately refuses at EITHER a tile (typed TileFailed) or the fold (documented ReductionPanicked containment) - both drain to used=0. (6) receipts distinguish tracked payload bytes from allocator overhead (LeasedVec charges exact payload; rounding is a CONTRACT no-claim, same for thread stacks/platform bookkeeping - no measurement, no claim). Coordination note: the lx0e lane's 35aef00 sweep folded my pool.rs hunks (entry bounds + ctx.lease arg) while my cx.rs 7-arg constructor was still uncommitted, so main briefly did not build for fresh clones; c30572d completes it (working-tree sweeps of half a cross-file change strike again - zevq/depgraph pattern). In-tile transient allocations remain out of scope per the bead's own wording; the stealer buffers are wf9.16.2. Evidence: fs-alloc+fs-exec 173/0 and dependents fs-la/fs-session 145/0 on ts1, clippy clean, 8/8 policy lanes.

title: Make TilePool work stealing allocation-stable under the operation lease
description: The reviewed root-memory formula admits initial deque headers/entries before launch, but the worker loop still uses VecDeque::split_off for half-steals. That allocates a new backing buffer while the victim retains capacity and the thief's prior deque remains live; repeated steal genealogy has no proven tight charge in the current OperationMemoryLease. Do not paper over this with an O(workers*tiles) bound that rejects reasonable runs. Replace the dynamic split protocol with preallocated fixed-capacity/range-based or otherwise allocation-stable work ownership whose peak storage is a checked function of the plan and admitted before launch. Preserve same-CCD victim order, half-steal semantics or document a deterministic replacement, fixed logical tile identity, cancellation drain, and worker-count determinism. Add adversarial multi-level steal storms, zero-post-launch-allocation instrumentation, exact root-formula sensitivity, limit-edge refusal, panic/cancel release, and G5 replay tests.
close_reason: TileRun contiguous-run ownership landed 1bfe64a: zero-allocation stealing by construction with verbatim take arithmetic, root formula drops the per-tile term, steal-storm + counting-allocator differential battery green on ts1 with dependents

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claiming (CloudyFinch lane, completing the wf9.16 family). Key finding from reading the post-lx0e worker loop: the deques already maintain a CONTIGUOUS-RUN invariant - they are seeded with contiguous weighted ranges, only ever pop_front, and steals wholesale-replace the (empty) thief's deque with the victim's split_off BACK half, which is itself contiguous. The VecDeque was never structurally needed: every deque is exactly one (next, end) range at all times. Replacement design: Mutex<TileRun{next,end}> per worker - pop is next+=1, half-steal is a midpoint split taking the back half with the IDENTICAL take=ceil(len/2) arithmetic, so tile->worker assignment is preserved exactly, not just semantically. Zero post-launch allocation BY CONSTRUCTION (Copy arithmetic on two u64s), peak storage is exactly workers x one cache-padded mutex-range admitted pre-launch, and the root formula DROPS the O(n) deque-entries component (strictly tighter than before, the opposite of the forbidden O(workers*tiles) padding). Same-CCD victim order, half-steal semantics, fixed logical tile identity, cancellation drain, and worker-count determinism all preserved verbatim. Battery plan: multi-level steal-storm (single-owner seeding forces cascade steals), differential zero-allocation instrumentation (counting global allocator in a dedicated integration binary: steal-heavy vs steal-free at identical worker/tile counts must show ZERO allocation-count delta), formula/CONTRACT updates retiring the wf9.16.2 no-claim.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: DONE, landed 1bfe64a. The load-bearing observation: the deques already maintained a CONTIGUOUS-RUN invariant (seed contiguous, pop only the front, thief wholesale-adopts the victim's contiguous back half), so the VecDeque was never structurally necessary - worker ownership is now a TileRun of two u64s in the same cache-padded mutex slot. Acceptance mapping: (1) allocation-stable ownership whose peak storage is a checked function of the plan, admitted before launch - one TileRun per worker inside the header slot; the root formula DROPS its O(n) deque-entries component entirely (strictly tighter, the opposite of the forbidden O(workers*tiles) bound); (2) half-steal semantics preserved VERBATIM, not just semantically - steal_back_half takes the back ceil(len/2) with the exact take arithmetic of the old split_off protocol, so the tile->worker transfer function is unchanged; same-CCD victim order, fixed logical tile identity, cancellation drain, and worker-count determinism untouched (fixed-slot reduction makes outputs bitwise schedule-independent regardless, re-proven vs a single-worker baseline); (3) adversarial multi-level steal storms - single-owner seeding (weight 10^6 vs 1) forces cascade steals at workers {2,4,8}, every tile executes exactly once, steals>0 asserted; (4) zero-post-launch-allocation instrumentation - counting global allocator in the dedicated test binary (outside the src unsafe scan, SAFETY-annotated pure delegation), DIFFERENTIAL design: two runs of identical shape with materially different steal traffic must show a ZERO allocation-count delta; serialized battery + min-of-3 windows reject libtest harness noise (first attempt failed from parallel-test counter pollution - worth knowing for future allocator-counting harnesses). Two honest instrumentation lessons recorded in the test: balanced seeding actually out-steals single-owner seeding (many small tail corrections vs few huge cascade halves), so the discriminator asserts DIFFERENT steal counts, not a direction. Evidence: fs-exec 116/0, dependents fs-la+fs-session 145/0 on ts1, clippy clean, 8/8 policy lanes, CONTRACT retires the split_off no-claim.

title: fs-simd: scalar baseline + NEON/AVX-512 capsules behind safe facades + experimental portable tier
description:
## What
`fs-simd`: the SIMD tier system — stable-Rust-first, per the patch Rev Q correction (std::simd/portable_simd is nightly-experimental, so it CANNOT be the committed baseline).

## Scope
- Tier 0 (committed baseline): scalar stable Rust, written so the autovectorizer has a fair chance (no aliasing hazards, explicit chunking) — correctness reference for every other tier.
- Tier 1: `std::arch` target-specific leaf kernels behind SAFE FAÇADES — NEON (aarch64) and AVX-512 (x86-64) intrinsics inside registered unsafe capsules (<300 lines each, SAFETY.md per unsafe-safety-cases bead).
- Tier 2 (experimental, feature-flagged): nightly portable-SIMD path for ergonomic exploration; never load-bearing.
- Uniform kernel-facing API: lane-width-agnostic primitives (fma, gather-ish patterns where supported, masked ops, horizontal reductions with FIXED tree shape for determinism), alignment helpers keyed to the 128-byte unconditional alignment policy (superset of both targets).
- Dispatch integration: fs-substrate resolves tables at startup; fs-simd provides the per-ISA implementations. Every intrinsic capsule has a scalar twin, and G0 property tests assert tier equivalence (exact for integer/logic ops; ULP-bounded for float, coordinated with fs-math contracts).

## Why
Plan §5.1 tier list adjusted by patch Rev Q: pretending portable-SIMD is stable would bake nightly into the foundation. The scalar twin + capsule discipline keeps P1 (memory safety) honest while still hitting roofline targets.

## Acceptance criteria
NEON + AVX-512 + scalar tiers for the core primitive set; tier-equivalence property suite green on both ISAs; all capsules registered with SAFETY.md; Miri-clean on capsule tests (via façade where Miri can't see intrinsics, plus interpreter-level checks on the scalar twin).

## Testing
G0 tier-equivalence laws (random inputs incl. subnormals, NaN, ±0); alignment-violation negative tests; per-primitive microbenches feeding the roofline harness. Logs: tier chosen, capability flags, equivalence-test ULP histograms.
close_reason: Implemented and verified ON BOTH ISAs. Scalar twin module (the semantic definition, fused mul_add per the FMA policy); NEON capsule = THE EXEMPLAR (SAFETY.md all 11 sections, registered, as_chunks-derived pointers, tails via scalar twin, <300 lines); x86 capsule (AVX2+FMA and AVX-512F variants of axpy/dot/sum, runtime-ladder safe facades that re-verify CPU features and fall back to scalar — dispatch tier is optimization, never soundness precondition); one-shot Ops function table keyed by fs-substrate tier; fixed per-tier reduction shapes documented (two-accumulator NEON/AVX shapes). EVIDENCE: (1) aarch64 NEON: tier battery green locally (M4 Pro), lens 0..67 x seeds, subnormal/NaN/pm-0/1e18, elementwise BITWISE vs twin, reductions enveloped, same-tier bit-stable; (2) x86-64 AVX2: EXECUTED on trj (Threadripper PRO 5995WX) — tier=avx2 battery pass, all suites EXIT:0 debug+release; (3) Miri lane green (scalar dispatch, compensating checks documented in SAFETY.md); (4) cross-compile checks green for x86_64-apple-darwin + x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu; (5) check-unsafe green with both capsules registered. Found+fixed en route: bandwidth plausibility bounds were load-naive (trj at load 27 measures <1GB/s in debug — bounds now catch accounting bugs only, lesson recorded). NO-CLAIMS: AVX-512 execution (Zen3 lacks it — needs Zen4/CI runner), x86 scale/mul_elem/fma3 scalar-fallback until fs-la demands them, f32 variants, performance numbers (roofline bead). Workspace: 135 tests green.

comments.text: UNBLOCKED: fs-substrate-detect closed (dispatch_tier()/SimdTier ready to consume; unsafe-capsule rails + SAFETY template ready for your NEON/AVX-512 leaves — first capsule must register in unsafe-capsules.json).

comments.text: Claimed by Claude (session e8aa053a). v1 primitive set (f64 slice ops serving fs-math/fs-rand/fs-la): axpy, scale, mul_elem, fma3 (elementwise, FUSED in scalar twin via mul_add so tiers match bitwise — FMA policy coordinated with fs-math), dot + sum (fixed-shape per-tier reduction trees; cross-tier ULP-bounded, same-tier bit-stable). NEON capsule = THE exemplar (SAFETY.md all 11 sections, registry entry, <300 lines, tail handling in safe wrapper). x86 AVX2+AVX-512 capsules written + cross-compile-checked from this machine if target available; execution verification -> CI bead. Portable-SIMD behind experimental flag. Miri lane attempted on safe facade.

title: fs-simd SME2 exploratory tier: runtime-gated streaming-mode GEMM prototype [F]
description:
## What
Exploratory SME2/streaming-mode matrix path for GEMM-shaped work on Apple Silicon — strictly [F], strictly feature-flagged, strictly runtime-capability-gated (plan §5.1 tier 5, §17 risk "SME2 intrinsics immaturity"; patch Rev Q demotion).

## Scope
- Runtime probe: detect streaming-mode/matrix-unit availability via capability probe; NEVER compile-time assumed. If absent or intrinsics unstable, the flag is inert and NEON remains the committed path.
- Streaming-mode GEMM microkernel prototype behind `frontier-sme2`: outer-product accumulation shapes, ZA-tile-style register management, integration with fs-la-gemm's packing/blocking so only the microkernel swaps.
- Honest benchmark protocol: measured vs NEON baseline on identical problems, ledgered with fingerprints; promotion to default requires beating NEON by a stated margin across the autotuner's shape sweep AND passing the full G0/G5 kernel suites.
- Explicit non-goals: no GPU/Metal work here (separate capability class per Rev Q); no determinism-mode support claims until FMA/accumulation-order semantics are characterized in the cross-ISA report.

## Why
The M-class matrix unit is potential upside for FEM batched-small-dense and GEMM, but intrinsics maturity is a named risk. The mitigation is exactly this bead's shape: committed NEON baseline, exploratory flag, autotuner as judge.

## Acceptance criteria
Flagged build compiles and runs where hardware supports it; falls back cleanly elsewhere; benchmark report exists in the ledger; G0 equivalence suite passes vs scalar twin.

## Testing
Equivalence property tests vs scalar/NEON; capability-absent fallback test; determinism characterization documented in the G5 cross-ISA report. Logs: probe outcome, kernel shapes tried, GFLOP/s tables.
close_reason: SME2 exploratory tier shipped in fs-simd behind feature frontier-sme2 ([F]), battery 4/4 green ON REAL SME2 HARDWARE (this session's box is an Apple M4 Pro: sysctl FEAT_SME2=1, SVL=512b — one of the few machines that can exercise the lane). (1) Runtime probe: sysctl subprocess on macOS / cpuinfo on Linux (the fs-substrate P1 no-FFI pattern), NEVER compile-time assumed; availability additionally requires SVL==64 bytes (the fixed prototype shape — other SVLs report unavailable rather than guessing); probe outcome + SVL ledgered. (2) Streaming-mode microkernel: 16x16 f32 fmopa outer-product accumulation on za0.s in ONE self-contained smstart..smstop asm region (streaming state never escapes; all v0-v31 declared clobbered; w12 slice indexing; bounds asserted before unsafe) — registered unsafe capsule #3 with full SAFETY.md, xtask check-unsafe green, <300 lines. Panel packing self-contained; fs-la-gemm packing/blocking integration recorded as the xdgf successor so only the microkernel swaps. (3) G0 equivalence MEASURED: fmopa vs the scalar mul_add twin (identical k-order) is BITWISE — worst 0 ULP over k in {1,3,17,64,257} — ledgered; NO cross-ISA determinism-mode claim per the bead's explicit non-goal. (4) Honest benchmark row: 263 GFLOP/s vs the RELEASE-build scalar twin 8.9 = 29.7x at 16x16x1024, ledgered with the explicit caveat that the twin is the equivalence reference, not an optimized NEON GEMM — the promotion gate (beat NEON across the autotuner sweep) stays with the perf lanes, unclaimed. Capability-absent fallback test runs everywhere (kernel refuses loudly; flag inert; NEON committed). Lanes: 4/4 battery (+3 existing suites), clippy -D warnings 0 both feature sets, fmt, ubs 0 critical, xtask 0 violations incl. check-unsafe with the new capsule.

title: fs-alloc: scope arenas (O(1) cancel reclaim), 128B alignment, hugepages, pools, site tracking
description:
## What
`fs-alloc`: scope arenas, pools, and hugepage-backed memory — the memory discipline of plan §5.3.

## Scope
- Scope arenas: bump allocators tied 1:1 to asupersync scopes; cancellation or completion reclaims in O(1); cross-scope escapes are COMPILE ERRORS (lifetimes do the enforcement — the arena's allocations borrow the scope). This is what makes speculative-race loser reclamation and e-racing kills instantaneous and leak-free.
- Long-lived artifacts "graduate" explicitly to the content-addressed store (ledger) — no silent promotion.
- 128-byte alignment UNCONDITIONALLY (superset of both targets' cache lines): padding + false-sharing avoidance helpers.
- Hugepage-backed arenas for field storage: 2 MB via madvise/THP on Linux; document Apple 16 KB base-page behavior; graceful fallback with the choice recorded.
- Pools for recurring same-shape allocations (tiles, small matrices) with per-CCD sharding hooks (first-touch by owning worker — the NUMA story from §5.1 consequence 3).
- Allocation-site tracking: lightweight tagging so memory usage feeds the Ledger and regressions are DIFFABLE between runs (§5.3).
- Unsafe capsules: the bump-pointer core and mmap wrappers, registered per unsafe-safety-cases with full SAFETY.md, Miri + fuzz coverage.

## Why
P7 (cancellation) and Bet 8 (e-racing) are only real if reclamation is O(1) and leak-proof; G4 storms assert exactly that via this crate's accounting. Arena scoping is also the anti-fragmentation strategy for 10⁸-cell field campaigns.

## Acceptance criteria
Arena lifecycle correct under G4 storms (zero leaks across 10⁶ random cancellations); escape attempts fail to compile (compile-fail battery); hugepage acquisition verified where supported; accounting reports match actual usage.

## Testing
Unit: alignment, growth, reset, OOM behavior (structured error, not abort). Property: interleaved alloc/cancel sequences preserve accounting invariants. Concurrency: arenas under parallel tile writers (Miri + storm). Logs: per-scope byte counts, hugepage status, allocation-site tables.
close_reason: Implemented fs-alloc: scope arenas over a single registered unsafe capsule (src/raw, 291 lines, SAFETY.md, Miri-green), 128B unconditional alignment + CachePadded, pool budgets with structured teaching AllocError (no aborts), hugepage decision probe with recorded fallback (no-claim: THP eligibility only, no madvise per P1), ShardedPool with first-touch + detach graduation, and diffable Site accounting feeding fs-obs events. Compile-fail battery proves escape/Drop/Sync rejection. Conformance alloc-001..009: G0 shadow-model + disjointness, G4 10^6-cancellation storm (quiescent, zero leaks), G5 deterministic reports. Lanes green: check, clippy -D warnings (crate), fmt, workspace tests (43 suites), xtask check-all (0 violations), cargo miri test --lib (24 tests). asupersync scope BINDING deliberately deferred to fs-exec (wf9.7) per contract; arena lifetime discipline is the primitive it consumes.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Note: fs-simd closed — SIMD primitives (ops() table: axpy/scale/mul_elem/fma3/dot/sum) + capsule exemplar pattern available. Verified NEON (M4 Pro) + AVX2 (trj 5995WX).

title: #[derive(Soa)]: in-house structure-of-arrays proc-macro with alignment + FrankenNumpy views
description:
## What
`#[derive(Soa)]`: the in-house structure-of-arrays proc-macro (plan §5.3) — ours because P1 forbids external runtime deps and because it must integrate with our alignment/arena/tile conventions.

## Scope
- Derive macro turning an AoS struct into an SoA container: per-field arrays, 128-byte aligned, arena-allocatable, with zip-style iteration, gather/scatter to AoS views, and length/capacity management.
- SIMD-friendly accessors: per-field slices exposed in chunk sizes matching fs-simd lane widths; masked tail handling.
- FrankenNumpy interop: each field array exposes a zero-copy strided view (the §12 membrane contract).
- Tile awareness: optional Morton-tile-chunked layout mode so hot field types (particles, lattice sites, element data) get tile identities for the executor for free.
- Derive-time diagnostics: clear compile errors for unsupported field types; no silent fallbacks.

## Why
"Structure-of-arrays everywhere hot" is a memory-discipline pillar; batched-small-dense (fs-la-batched) and LBM lattices depend on SIMD lanes running ACROSS elements, which is only natural in SoA.

## Acceptance criteria
Macro handles nested plain-old-data structs, generics with bounds, and Qty-typed fields; generated code passes clippy -D warnings; zero-copy FrankenNumpy view round-trips.

## Testing
Unit: layout/alignment assertions (addresses mod 128 == 0), AoS↔SoA round-trip equality, iterator equivalence vs scalar reference. Compile-fail battery for unsupported shapes. Property tests over random struct contents. Logs: generated layout descriptions in test output for auditability.
close_reason: #[derive(Soa)] LANDED as two crates. fs-soa-derive (UTIL, proc-macro): IN-HOUSE on std proc_macro ONLY (no syn/quote/proc-macro2 - Franken-only law applies to macro deps); hand token walker (attrs, vis, generics with bounds+const params re-emitted verbatim, where clauses, joint-spacing-aware rendering - the ::-splitting bug was caught first build), string codegen re-parsed to TokenStream; generates <Name>Soa with FieldBuf per leaf, nested containers via #[soa(nested)] + type-level SoaAble recursion driven in UFCS (no trait imports demanded), push/get/set gather-scatter, accessors, iter, field_views, layout_descr, Default+SoaAble+SoaContainer impls, all #[doc]'d and clippy-pedantic-clean GENERATED code. Structured compile_error diagnostics: tuple/unit/enum/union/lifetimes/generic-defaults/zero-fields/reserved-names/unknown-attrs. fs-soa (L0 runtime): FieldBuf<T:Copy> 128B-aligned with ZERO unsafe (over-allocate 128 slack elems + whole-element align_offset; degrades to best reachable power-of-2 for exotic sizes with achieved_align REPORTED in RawView); RawView strided-view descriptor = FrankenNumpy membrane shape with address-free descr() for deterministic logs; chunks_with_tail(_mut) masked-tail chunking; DEFAULT_CHUNK_QUANTUM=512 (E8 tile volume) tile-identity hook; SOA_ALIGN=128 per fs-alloc doctrine. EVIDENCE: 10-case battery green BOTH ISAs (alignment %128==0 asserted for [f64;3]/f64/u32/u8/f32/Qty leaves on M4 Pro AND Threadripper x86_64 /data/tmp/fssoa.Nz9Ahh - genuinely validates align_offset across allocators); AoS<->SoA bitwise roundtrip 500 elems; iter equivalence; capacity-hint address-stability; nested dotted views [inner.a, inner.b, flag]; GenericSoa<f32,4> with bounds; Qty columns stay dimensionally typed; 20k-op property battery vs Vec reference (fs-rand keyed streams); 8-case compile-fail battery via in-house offline harness (scratch cargo project, path deps, no trybuild) green both ISAs. Gates: clippy all+pedantic ZERO warnings (21 format_push_string->write!, 21 write->writeln, float_cmp, must_use fixes), fmt, xtask check-all clean for both crates (ten-section CONTRACTs; the one workspace violation is fs-session = other agent in-flight gp3.7). UNBLOCKS: 6ys.4 (p0 fs-la batched small dense) -> tfz.5 -> FLUX chain. NO-CLAIMS in CONTRACT: live franken_numpy wiring (descriptor only; no fnp dep exists in workspace yet), arena-backed variant, Morton content sorting, autotuned interleave (6ys.4), span-precise macro diagnostics, #[soa(skip)]/renames.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post tfz.12/fs-time). Rationale: graph-optimal — wf9.5 is the READY bottleneck under p0 6ys.4 (fs-la batched small dense), which gates the FLUX critical path (tfz.5→tfz.6→tfz.10→tfz.24→7tv.3, the top-4 PageRank beads). DESIGN: two crates. (1) fs-soa (L0 SUBSTRATE runtime): SoaVec<T> machinery the macro targets — 128-byte-aligned per-field buffers (own aligned raw alloc in a NO-unsafe wrapper if possible, else registered capsule; check fs-alloc arena API first), len/capacity mgmt, push/gather to AoS value, scatter from AoS, per-field slices + chunked accessors (chunk size = fs-simd lane width multiples) with masked tail via (full_chunks, tail) split, zip iteration, optional Morton-tile-chunked mode (tile quantum from fs-substrate::tile conventions, tile identity per chunk), zero-copy strided view descriptor for the FrankenNumpy membrane (ptr/len/stride/dtype tuple mirroring fs-substrate field views — verify what fs-substrate::field exposes and reuse). (2) fs-soa-derive (proc-macro=true): IN-HOUSE token parsing on std proc_macro ONLY (no syn/quote/proc-macro2 — Franken-only law; deterministic hand parser). Supports: named-field structs, nested POD structs (via a Soa-recursion marker trait SoaPod), generics with bounds (re-emit generics+where verbatim), fs-qty Qty<..> fields (transparent: array of inner repr with the Qty type carried on the accessor), derive-time diagnostics as compile_error! spans for tuple/unit structs, enums, unions, non-POD fields. Compile-fail battery via trybuild-style? NO external dev-deps — do compile-fail via a tiny in-house harness: invoke rustc on fixture files with --error-format=json in a test script? Simpler in-house: keep compile-fail fixtures + a battery test that runs 'cargo check' on a nested fixture workspace offline and greps expected messages (pattern used must stay offline-safe); decide after checking how other crates test diagnostics. Layout logs: generated-layout JSON description via a const fn describe() for auditability. Gates: G0 layout/alignment asserts (addr%128==0), AoS<->SoA roundtrip bitwise, iterator equivalence vs scalar ref, property tests over random contents via fs-rand, clippy -D warnings on GENERATED code (test crate turns lints up), xtask layer registration (fs-soa L0; derive crate layer-exempt or L0 — follow xtask policy for proc-macros, investigate), CONTRACT.md ten-section for both crates, trj cross-ISA run (layout is arch-independent but alignment/stride asserts must pass on x86_64 too). NO-CLAIM candidates (recorded if dropped): FrankenNumpy LIVE interop test (membrane crate may not be wired yet — then ship the strided-view descriptor + contract only), autotuned interleave width (belongs to 6ys.4's autotuner), arena integration if fs-alloc API is not ready for it.

title: Tile layout: Morton 8-cubed tiled fields, TileId identity, halos, CCD sharding, first-touch
description:
## What
Morton/tile-major field layout primitives (plan §5.3): fields live in Z-order tiles (8³ default; 4³/16³ autotuned), the layout that is simultaneously stencil-friendly, sparse-friendly (FrankenVDB leaves align to it), cache-oblivious-ish, and — critically — gives spatial work NATURAL TILE IDENTITIES for the executor (scheduling, cancellation, determinism, and NUMA placement are all keyed by tile).

## Scope
- Morton encode/decode (BMI2 PDEP/PEXT capsule on x86, magic-bits fallback; scalar twin), tile iteration orders (linear, Z-order, boundary-first), tile↔world coordinate maps.
- Tiled field container: dense 3D array of 8³ tiles with per-tile metadata (occupancy mask hooks for sparse use, first-touch ownership tag, dirty flags for incremental algorithms); f32-storage/f64-evaluate option (the SDF chart convention).
- Ghost/halo exchange helpers for stencil kernels: tile-local halo copies with deterministic ordering, prefetch-distance hooks (autotuned later).
- Shard-by-CCD placement: tile→worker affinity map produced with fs-substrate topology; first-touch initialization by the owning worker.
- TileId scheme: stable (grid-hash, tile-coord) identity used by Cx RNG keying, deterministic reductions, and ledger events — the SAME identity everywhere, which is what makes results independent of which worker ran which tile (P2 foundation).

## Acceptance criteria
Encode/decode bijective across domain sizes; halo exchange equivalence vs naive gather; affinity maps respect CCD boundaries on x86 fixture topologies; layout benchmarked vs linear layout on a reference stencil (expect wins documented, not assumed).

## Testing
Property: Morton bijection, neighborhood locality bounds. Unit: halo correctness at domain edges (all BC paddings), tile iteration completeness. Perf smoke into roofline harness. Logs: tile geometry choices, affinity tables, halo timings.
close_reason: Implemented tile layout in fs-substrate: 3D Morton codec (magic-bits reference + BMI2 PDEP/PEXT capsule, registered w/ SAFETY.md, function-table dispatch resolved once, G0 backend-equivalence law over 200k seeded cases + exhaustive cube); TileGrid/TileCoord/TileEdge(4/8/16) with structured TileError refusals, deterministic linear/z-order/boundary-first iteration, cell<->tile maps incl. partial edge tiles; TileId=(grid-hash, morton) with stream_key() u128 for Philox/reduction/event keying (P2 foundation); TiledField<T> tile-major z-order-slot storage w/ 128B-aligned tile bases (safe align_offset buffer), TileMeta (first-touch owner tag, dirty, occupancy hook), f32-store/f64-eval convention; halo gathers (reference + row-copy fast path, bit-identical on all tiles/BCs: Clamp/Constant/Periodic — conformance law); AffinityMap: balanced +-1 contiguous z-rank ranges, CCD fixtures (TR 12x8, EPYC 16x8, Apple 2-cluster) respected, deterministic JSON tables; shard_views_mut disjoint per-shard windows for first-touch (parallel==serial proven, owner tags recorded). Conformance sub-001..006 incl. tiled-vs-linear stencil smoke (bitwise agreement; timings emitted as benchmark_result events — documented, not claimed). Lanes: check+clippy -D warnings green (crate), fmt green, workspace tests 47 suites green, xtask check-all 0 violations. No-claims: from_probe CCD heuristic is a hint (fixtures are the contract); BMI2 runtime coverage lands with the x86 CI runner; perf verdicts belong to the roofline harness bead.

comments.text: Coordination (Claude, session e8aa053a): your fs-substrate additions (morton/tile/field/affinity) compile + pass in full-workspace runs on my side. fs-simd's ops() table + CACHE_LINE/dispatch_tier are closed/stable if you need them; fs-math det functions are cross-ISA-proven if tile code needs strict transcendentals.

title: fs-exec: two-lane executor — latency lane + CCD/P-E-aware work-stealing tile lane, Cx contract
description:
## What
`fs-exec`: the two-lane executor on asupersync — the beating heart of L0 (plan §5.2).

## Scope
- Latency lane: asupersync's native async scheduling for orchestration — ledger I/O, IR interpretation, progress streaming, watchdogs, inter-study coordination. Milliseconds matter, throughput doesn't. Serves HELM's ≤100 ms conversational guarantee even while campaigns saturate compute.
- Throughput lane: work-stealing fork-join pool — per-core Chase–Lev-style deques; CCD-AWARE STEAL ORDER (steal within CCD first, then socket) so working sets stay in their 32 MiB L3 islands; P/E asymmetry handled by per-core-class throughput weights (E-cores take proportionally smaller tile quanta rather than being ignored or stalling joins — weights measured by the autotuner).
- The TileKernel contract (Appendix B): `tiles() -> TilePlan` (geometry decided by autotuner), `run(TileId, &Cx) -> ControlFlow<Cancelled, Out>` with `Out: Reduce` on a FIXED-SHAPE reduction tree. Tiles are the unit of scheduling, cancellation, determinism, and NUMA placement.
- `Cx` carries: cancellation token (poll at tile boundaries; bounded latency-to-cancel ≤200 µs target), tile-scoped bump arena, counter-based RNG stream keyed by (seed, kernel_id, tile_id, iteration) — LOGICAL identity, not thread identity — budget accountant slice, and ledger handle.
- Failure containment: a panicking tile poisons its scope; siblings are cancelled; a structured error with full tile provenance propagates; the study continues or halts per policy — NEVER a process abort mid-campaign (policy per unsafe-safety-cases decision).
- Every unit of work spawns inside an asupersync cancellation scope (P7).

## Why
This is where asupersync's cancel-correctness stops being plumbing and becomes algorithmic leverage: statistical preemption (Bet 8) needs the ≤200 µs reclaim; determinism (P2) needs logical-identity RNG and fixed reductions; NUMA reality (§5.1) needs CCD-aware stealing.

## Acceptance criteria
G4 storm suite green (leaks, deadlocks, containment); cancel-latency p99 ≤200 µs on reference hardware, histogram ledgered; steal-order verified CCD-local-first on fixture topologies; RNG stream independence from worker assignment proven by shuffling worker counts (G5).

## Testing
Deterministic lab-runtime tests for steal/join/cancel protocols + asupersync model checker (via G4 harness). Unit: TilePlan execution completeness, reduction-tree shape stability. Property: arbitrary DAGs of scopes complete or cancel cleanly. Logs: per-scope lifecycle events, steal statistics, latency histograms — all as structured events consumable by the future ledger.
close_reason: Implemented fs-exec two-lane executor. Latency lane: thin configured handle on the asupersync runtime (block_on + structured spawn/join; request->drain->finalize inherited unmodified); measured 57us turnaround while the tile pool saturates cores (ledgered, no claim). Throughput lane: work-stealing fork-join TilePool — per-worker CachePadded mutex deques seeded with weight-proportional contiguous tile runs (P/E quantum hook), steal-half with CCD-local-first victim order from fs-substrate topology (pure victim_order/weighted_ranges functions verified on TR/EPYC/Apple fixtures), fixed-slot reductions folded in ascending tile order (bit-identical results across 1..8 workers proven with non-associative floats AND non-commutative concat). Cx contract: checkpoint/is_cancel_requested gate polls, tile-scoped fs-alloc arena (lifetime-bound), StreamKey (seed,kernel_id,tile,iteration)->key128 logical RNG identity (worker-shuffle invariance proven), asupersync Budget carried, ExecMode provenance. Failure containment: catch_unwind per tile -> structured TilePanicked with tile provenance, siblings drain via gate, pool survives; RunError::{Cancelled,TilePanicked,Incomplete} all teaching. Conformance exec-001..008: completeness+arena hygiene, G5 bit-identity, key independence, external-cancel drain w/ ledgered latency histogram, 300-run G4 storm w/ panic injection (110 ok/163 cancelled/27 panicked, all structured, quiescent throughout), fixture steal order, lane responsiveness, reduction-shape invariance. Lanes green: check, clippy -D warnings, fmt, workspace tests (52 suites), xtask check-all 0 violations. No-claims: 200us p99 gate deferred to release-build perf harness (histogram measured+ledgered per run); mutex deques v1 (Chase-Lev needs roofline evidence); speculative races + resumable solvers are wf9.8.

title: fs-exec/fs-rand: bind RNG streams to explicit logical run identity
description:
TilePool derives Cx.iteration from a pool-global AtomicU64 fetch_add. Reusing a pool means a kernel stream key depends on unrelated prior runs; concurrent invocation order changes keys. Tests use fresh pools and miss this. Separately fs-exec StreamKey uses four u64 fields and a lossy mixed key128, while fs-rand exposes seed u64 plus kernel/tile u32 and treats iteration as draw index; there is no typed conversion proving collision or replay semantics.

Root cause: execution order is being used as logical identity, and two crates independently define incompatible stream-key contracts.

Repair: require an explicit caller-ledgered RunId or generation in run APIs; derive per-tile keys solely from study, operation/run, kernel, tile, and declared iteration. Provide one canonical collision-analyzed key type or an explicit checked bridge to fs-rand, with versioned field widths and overflow/refusal rules. Pool scheduling and unrelated work must never perturb streams.

Acceptance: same logical run after arbitrary unrelated/concurrent pool activity is bit-identical; distinct declared runs diverge; worker count and arrival order do not affect keys; conversion collision tests cover truncation boundaries; replay reconstructs keys from ledger fields without hidden mutable state.
close_reason: RNG streams bound to explicit logical run identity - LANDED. (1) TilePool's pool-global AtomicU64 iteration REMOVED: stream keys no longer depend on unrelated prior runs or concurrent invocation order (execution order was being used as logical identity). run/run_with_gate now use the fixed implicit RunId(0); the new run_declared(kernel, gate, RunId) takes the caller-ledgered generation/trial/restart id - keys derive solely from (study seed, kernel id, tile, declared run). tune.rs trials now measure IDENTICAL work per trial (the old per-trial stream drift was unintended). (2) Checked fs-exec->fs-rand bridge (in fs-rand, since layering forbids the reverse dep): StreamKey::from_exec_parts(seed, kernel_id, tile, iteration), bridge v1 (EXEC_KEY_BRIDGE_VERSION), REFUSES rather than truncates - KernelOverflow/TileOverflow past u32 slots, IterationUnrepresentable for iteration!=0 (fs-rand's draw index is a within-stream counter, not identity; teaching Display points at ledgering the generation upstream). ACCEPTANCE MET: conformance test proves same logical run bit-identical after pool reuse + 3 unrelated runs + two CONCURRENT probes + arrival-order swap + worker-count change (4 vs 9); distinct declared runs diverge, same declared run replays; keys reconstruct from ledger fields alone (seed, fnv1a64(kernel), tile, run) with no hidden state; bridge collision tests cover every truncation boundary (2^32 refused, 2^32-1 accepted; the (1<<32)|5 vs 5 alias demonstrated refused). fs-exec 38+14 green, fs-rand suites green, clippy clean. CONTRACTs record the declared-identity invariant + versioned bridge. NOTE: accidentally created then removed an empty tests/philox.rs (zero-byte heredoc artifact, never committed) - disclosed per the no-delete rule.

title: fs-exec: speculative races, resumable/forkable SolverState protocol, statistical-preemption kill handles
description:
## What
The three executor behaviors most HPC runtimes lack and FrankenSim treats as core (plan §5.2): speculative races, resumable solvers, and statistical preemption.

## Scope
- Speculative races with loser-cancellation: spawn N strategies as sibling scopes ("try Delaunay refinement AND octree CutFEM concurrently; first to meet the error budget wins"); the loser's scope is cancelled and its arenas reclaimed in O(1). API: race combinator with budget-aware victory predicates and deterministic tie-breaking (P2 — ties broken by logical index, not arrival).
- Resumable solvers: the `SolverState: Serialize` protocol — Krylov, multigrid, and time-stepping loops as EXPLICIT state machines. A paused solve checkpoints to the Ledger, migrates, resumes, or FORKS (two optimizer branches continuing one warm-started solve — the forkable-worlds enabler). Deterministic-mode invariant: pause-serialize-resume reproduces the uninterrupted trajectory bit-exactly (G4 asserts this).
- Statistical preemption: kill-handle registry — the e-process layer (fs-eproc/e-racing) holds handles to candidate evaluation scope-trees; crossing an elimination threshold cancels the candidate's ENTIRE tree mid-flight; cancel-correctness guarantees no torn state, arena scoping guarantees no leaks, cores reclaim within the ≤200 µs bound. This is Bet 8's machinery.
- Graceful-degradation hooks for the resource governor (fs-session-caps): pause-serialize-resume as a first-class operation ("pause the LES, run the urgent trim study, resume" must be routine, not heroic).

## Acceptance criteria
Race combinator: losers always fully drained (G4-verified), winner's result deterministic across thread counts; a reference iterative solver (from PV skeleton) checkpoints/resumes/forks with bit-exact continuation; kill-handles reclaim a deep scope tree within latency budget under load.

## Testing
G4 storms targeting races mid-flight; property tests on serialize/deserialize round-trips of SolverState; fork-divergence test (two forks with different subsequent inputs stay independent); latency measurements ledgered. Structured logs: race outcomes with per-branch progress at kill time, checkpoint sizes, resume verdicts.

## Strategic note: distribution-readiness (plan §17)
The risk register rates "single-machine bias blocks future scale-out" LOW precisely because of this bead: serializable solver states + content-addressed artifacts + event-sourced sessions are EXACTLY the primitives distribution needs later. Design the SolverState protocol and kill-handle registry so that "migrate" can someday mean "to another machine" without API change (no shared-memory assumptions in the serialized representation; artifact references by content hash, never by pointer).
close_reason: Implemented the three §5.2 executor behaviors in fs-exec. (1) Speculative races: Racer/RaceBranch with per-branch gates+arenas+stream keys; deterministic victory = lowest-index ACCEPTED result recomputed from outcomes (timing moves when kills land, never who wins — proven identical winner index+bits across 10 jittered repeats, exec-010); early kills (j dies once i<j accepted); Fast mode = first-arrival, recorded; parent-gate watcher folds kill-handles into the whole tree mid-flight; losers fully drained (scope join) with arena quiescence oracle; panics contained per branch; NoWinner is a teaching error with per-branch JSON reports. Deterministic liveness caveat documented (below-leader branches must terminate on own budgets). (2) Resumable solvers: SolverState protocol on an in-house little-endian codec (floats as raw bits; self-contained bytes, no pointers — distribution-ready per §17), ResumableSolver::step bounded pause granularity, drive() where pause IS the cancellation path (governor hook), fork() round-trips through bytes proving serializability; pause-serialize-resume reproduces an uninterrupted CHAOTIC trajectory bit-exactly at depths 1/137/9999 (exec-011) plus Jacobi reference in unit tests; codec rejects truncation+trailing garbage with teaching CodecError. (3) Statistical preemption: KillRegistry (candidate id -> Arc<CancelGate>), idempotent kill, kill_where batch elimination (ascending, deterministic), release; deep candidate tree (1M-tile pool run) killed mid-flight drains quiescent with latency histogram ledgered (exec-012). Lanes green: clippy -D warnings, fmt, fs-exec 28 lib + 12 conformance tests, workspace 52 suites, xtask 0 violations. No-claims: 50us watcher stride + 200us gate are perf-harness territory; ledger spill of checkpoints is fs-ad/fs-ledger's.

title: fs-exec: make races panic-total and cancellation trees mandatory
description:
Racer::race_with_gate starts a parent watcher whose completion flag is set only by the last branch. With an empty branch vector the watcher never terminates. The branch body is caught, but the accept predicate runs outside catch_unwind; an accept panic unwinds the worker before filling its slot, so the watcher can again run forever. Direct fs-race consumers also construct empty KillRegistry values and ignore kill(false), allowing claimed cancellation storms to exercise no live evaluation tree.

Root cause: drain completion depends on normal branch epilogues and cancellation wiring is optional convention rather than an owned scope invariant.

Repair: reject empty races before spawning; wrap the full branch plus acceptance and finalization path in an unwind-safe guard that always records a terminal slot and releases the watcher; use structured errors, not hangs. Require candidate registration or return a structured missing-handle error from elimination; wire flagship consumers to gates owned by their actual evaluation scopes.

Acceptance: bounded tests for empty races, accept panic, branch panic, parent cancellation, missing candidate handles, and simultaneous completion; all return without leaks; losing evaluation trees observe cancellation and drain; G4 storm proves nonzero registered kills and quiescent arenas.
close_reason: Races panic-total, cancellation trees mandatory - LANDED. (1) Empty races refused with structured NoWinner (empty reports + teaching Display) BEFORE any spawn - the former path hung the parent watcher forever. (2) The branch body AND the acceptance predicate now run inside one unwind guard (an accept panic used to unwind the worker before its slot write, hanging the watcher); the terminal-slot epilogue (slot write + watcher release) is panic-free by construction. (3) Panic-totality: every Mutex use in race.rs and kill.rs is poison-tolerant (PoisonError::into_inner) - a predicate panic in kill_where can no longer cascade panics through the registry. (4) KillRegistry::kill_registered returns structured UnregisteredKill instead of an ignorable false; exported. (5) Flagship consumers wired: fs-race race_field and successive_halving REGISTER all candidate gates at scope start (the race owns its candidates' evaluation trees) and eliminate via kill_registered - a claimed cancellation can no longer exercise zero live trees (coordinated with in-flight 7tv.7.2, whose loss-span refactor is orthogonal; their battery green with this wiring). ACCEPTANCE MET, all bounded via a 20s recv_timeout harness (a hang fails, never wedges CI): empty race, accept panic (contained as Panicked, race terminates, clean branch wins), branch panic (pre-existing test), mid-flight parent cancellation (all branches Cancelled, drained), simultaneous instant completions x32 (deterministic lowest index), missing candidate handle (structured error), and the G4 storm: 12 candidates raced under registry-owned gates with external kill storms - every kill landed registered (12/12 nonzero), every race returned, arenas quiescent, registry drained to 0. fs-exec 38 tests green, fs-race 13+12 green, clippy clean. CONTRACT records the drain-totality invariant.

title: fs-exec: add a versioned self-authenticating solver snapshot envelope
description:
SolverState::to_bytes writes only implementation-defined payload bytes and from_bytes calls the selected type decoder. There is no magic, solver type id, schema version, payload length, checksum, or semantic provenance. A same-length snapshot from another solver or version can decode successfully into plausible but wrong state, and corruption can evade trailing-byte checks.

Root cause: payload serialization is treated as a distribution-ready artifact without an envelope that identifies and authenticates its schema.

Repair: wrap snapshots in canonical bytes containing magic, envelope version, stable solver/state type id, schema version, payload length, payload checksum/content hash, and required provenance. Validate the envelope before invoking the state decoder; provide explicit migrations or structured incompatible-version refusal.

Acceptance: cross-type same-layout bytes, bit flips, truncation, appended bytes, unknown version, wrong type id, and stale schema all refuse; valid pause-migrate-resume remains bit-exact; fork and ledger paths use the envelope rather than raw payloads.
close_reason: Versioned self-authenticating snapshot envelope LANDED (v1). Canonical layout: magic FSEXSNAP + envelope version u32 + stable state TYPE_ID u64 + payload SCHEMA_VERSION u32 + caller-ledgered provenance u64 + payload length u64 + FNV payload checksum u64, then payload — every header field validated BEFORE the payload decoder runs (envelope::open). SolverState now REQUIRES const TYPE_ID (never reused/changed) and const SCHEMA_VERSION (bumped on any layout change) — the compiler forces every state to declare identity; all three existing impls migrated (JacobiState, ChaoticState, fs-session ToySolver). New seal(provenance)/unseal -> (state, provenance) carry run/ledger identity (pairs with wf9.7.1 RunId); to_bytes/from_bytes are the unattributed convenience over the SAME envelope; fork round-trips enveloped bytes; content_hash covers the envelope. Incompatible schema = structured IncompatibleSchema refusal (the explicit no-migration-yet path). ACCEPTANCE MET (envelope_refuses_every_misbinding_class): cross-type same-layout bytes (TwinState with identical payload layout) refused WrongTypeId; payload bit flip -> ChecksumMismatch; magic flip -> BadMagic; header truncation -> Truncated; payload truncation AND appended bytes -> LengthMismatch; unknown envelope version 9 -> refused; stale schema 7 -> IncompatibleSchema{1,7}; valid seal round-trips bit-exactly WITH provenance (0xABCD preserved); existing pause-serialize-resume bit-exact conformance still green. fs-exec 39+14 green, fs-session 10 green, clippy clean. CONTRACT records the envelope contract.

title: fs-math: deterministic elementary functions, ULP budgets, FMA/subnormal/NaN policy, enclosures
description:
## What
`fs-math`: deterministic elementary functions and the floating-point policy contract (patch Rev O). Deterministic reductions and RNG are NOT enough — full determinism requires a math-function contract.

## Scope
- Deterministic scalar + vector implementations of sin/cos/tan/exp/log/log1p/expm1/sqrt/cbrt/atan2/pow/erf with DECLARED ULP contracts per function, identical results across ISAs in strict mode (no platform libm in deterministic paths).
- Strict vs fast modes mirroring ExecMode: strict = cross-ISA reproducible with documented ULP bounds; fast = platform-optimal, permitted only when the mode is ledgered provenance.
- FMA contraction policy: explicit contract for where FMA is allowed (per-kernel declaration), because contraction differences are a top cross-ISA divergence class (G5 report category).
- Subnormal policy (never flush-to-zero silently; document per-ISA behavior), NaN/infinity propagation policy (payloads not relied upon; canonical NaN on production paths), signed-zero handling.
- Interval-safe helpers: directed next_up/next_down nudging utilities and DECLARED ENCLOSURES for every elementary function so fs-ivl's Taylor models and interval evaluation get rigorous elementary-function bounds (this is what makes interval certificates clean — Rev O).
- ULP Ledger: every kernel declares a ULP budget for its sensitive operations; deterministic mode FORBIDS implementation-defined math paths unless wrapped by fs-math (lintable).

## Why
Cross-platform mismatches from libm/FMA/subnormals are a named risk; and fs-ivl's certificates are only as rigorous as their elementary-function enclosures.

## Acceptance criteria
Each function: ULP bound verified empirically against the double-double oracle across systematic + random + adversarial inputs (billions of samples nightly); bit-identical strict-mode results across NEON/AVX-512 for the committed function set; enclosure property (result ∈ declared enclosure) proven by G0 laws.

## Testing
Exhaustive f32 sweeps where feasible; oracle-sampled f64; special-value tables (±0, subnormals, ±inf, NaN); cross-ISA hash comparison in CI (G5). Logs: per-function max-ULP-observed tables, enclosure violation counts (must be 0).
close_reason: v1 CLOSED with cross-ISA proof; remainder split to fs-math-extensions (created, linked). Delivered: det::{exp,expm1,ln,sin,cos,tanh,sqrt} built from pure IEEE arithmetic; policy doc (FMA contraction forbidden-implicit/encouraged-explicit, subnormals never flushed, canonical NaN, strict-vs-fast); nudge_out/next_up/next_down for fs-ivl; ulp_distance. HEADLINE EVIDENCE: golden hash 0xeb79cab7a01643e5 IDENTICAL on aarch64-apple (M4 Pro) and x86-64 (TR PRO 5995WX) across 175k evaluations — cross-ISA bit-determinism proven empirically, not argued. Measured ULP maxima (budgets): exp 1 (3), expm1 2 (3), ln 1 (3), sin 2 (3), cos 2 (3), tanh 3 (5), both ISAs. THREE REAL BUGS found+killed by the test discipline en route, each now a regression test: decimal-rendered Cody-Waite constants losing exact trailing zeros (184 ULP); Taylor cores two terms short (166 ULP at |r|=pi/4); tanh oddness by-luck-not-construction (now sign-applied-to-magnitude). 4-part pi/2 reduction (fdlibm bit patterns) fixed the x=pi near-zero 7-10 ULP cases. NO-CLAIMS: |x|>2^20 trig (Payne-Hanek in extension bead), 0.5-ULP correct rounding, remaining function families. 13 tests green locally + on trj, clippy clean.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Note: fs-simd closed — SIMD primitives (ops() table: axpy/scale/mul_elem/fma3/dot/sum) + capsule exemplar pattern available. Verified NEON (M4 Pro) + AVX2 (trj 5995WX).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claimed by Claude (session e8aa053a). v1 function set (consumer-driven: FFT twiddles, EdgeLaw tanh, statistics): exp, expm1, ln, sin, cos, tanh + exact sqrt wrapper + next_up/next_down nudging helpers. Design: pure-IEEE-arithmetic implementations (Cody-Waite 3-part reduction for trig with documented |x|<=2^20 domain; atanh-series ln; k-reduction exp/expm1; expm1-based tanh) -> cross-ISA bit-determinism BY CONSTRUCTION; accuracy vs std-libm oracle w/ measured max-ULP asserted against declared budgets; GOLDEN CROSS-ISA HASH test (FNV over output bits on fixed grid) to be verified on trj this session. Remaining bead scope after v1: tan/atan2/pow/cbrt/log1p/erf, Payne-Hanek big-arg reduction, dd-oracle nightly billions-battery (needs fs-ivl), fast-mode variants, ULP-ledger lint.

title: WS5: flagship runner binaries — vessel + unified helm-run entry following the vskeleton pattern
description: WHY (reality check: the only user-invocable pipeline optimizes one scalar; every flagship is a test asset): the product spine. WHAT: (a) a vessel runner binary (pour study: tank SDF + lip geometry parameters -> WS1-E 3-D pour -> spout-quality objective from the existing vessel crate -> optimize -> ledger -> report), same verb set as fs-vskeleton/fs-marquee (run/replay/report), same Five-Explicits admission; (b) a thin unified dispatcher (helm-run study.fsir) that reads the study kind from the IR and routes to the registered runner — the operator catalog (WS5-B) is its source of truth, no hardcoded match that drifts; (c) both runners emit the SAME ledger schema so explain/report/render tooling is shared. ACCEPTANCE: quickstarts for both runners execute on a fresh clone (subprocess e2e tests, JSONL logged); bit-exact replay asserted; a deliberately malformed study refuses with teaching errors naming the missing Explicit; the dispatcher refuses unknown study kinds with the catalog's nearest-match suggestion. TESTS: CLI e2e batteries, refusal batteries, replay hash assertions. DEPENDS on 3-D free surface (vessel physics) and marquee runner (pattern + shared tooling).

title: [P0] Fail closed on non-finite evidence regimes and emit valid color JSON
description:
## Defect
The three-color evidence path violates fail-closed and canonical-JSON invariants in three related ways:

1. check_regime compares state values with less-than/greater-than only, so NaN satisfies neither comparison and a Validated claim remains Validated. Non-finite regime bounds have the same problem.
2. Composition of two Validated claims with disjoint regimes demotes to Estimated but records dispersion 0.0, falsely encoding perfect agreement when there is no jointly supported state. The existing regime-exit convention correctly uses infinite/no-claim dispersion.
3. Color::payload_json interpolates unescaped identifiers and bare non-finite floats. Regime demotion intentionally produces infinite dispersion, so ordinary demotion rows can contain invalid JSON (inf/NaN); quotes/control characters in dataset, estimator, or axis names can also corrupt structured ledger payloads.

## Scope
- Harden fs-evidence regime/color functions only.
- Non-finite state or non-finite/empty regime bounds must demote/refuse conservatively.
- Disjoint validated regimes must produce Estimated with infinite/no-claim dispersion.
- Color payload JSON must escape strings and encode non-finite values as valid JSON without pretending they are finite numbers.
- Update fs-evidence contract language and focused tests. Touch fs-ledger colors/tests only if required to validate emitted rows end to end.
- Do not redesign Certified<T>, package serialization, signatures, or budget pies in this bead.

## Acceptance
- NaN, +Inf, and -Inf state values cannot preserve Validated color.
- Non-finite regime bounds cannot preserve Validated color.
- Disjoint regime composition demotes with infinite/no-claim dispersion.
- Payloads for infinite dispersion and hostile quote/newline/control-character strings parse as syntactically valid JSON and preserve deterministic output.
- Existing color/ledger conformance remains green.

## Verification
Focused fs-evidence and, if touched, fs-ledger tests/clippy through RCH; rustfmt; ubs on changed files; git diff --check.
notes:
Implementation complete in fs-evidence/fs-ledger color paths. Core fs-evidence fix landed at 55787fb while this audit was active; focused contract and end-to-end regressions remain in the working tree.

- PASS: scoped git diff --check.
- UBS: scanner reports fmt/clippy/cargo-check/test-build clean in its shadow run; overall exit 1 is from pre-existing/foreign panic! calls in evd-008 test plus generic heuristic warnings.
- BLOCKED: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=... cargo test -p fs-evidence --test conformance -p fs-ledger --test color_battery. All healthy workers fail preflight because nightly-2026-07-06-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu is not installed; ovh-b is unreachable. Local fallback was cancelled. Matching RCH clippy proof is therefore not available.

Kept in_progress pending proof rather than claiming full acceptance.

title: WS7: declarative physics queries v1 — bounded questions with anytime + refusal semantics
description: WHY (Addendum Proposal 8, its highest agent-scored idea, ZERO bead coverage found in the reality check): the epistemic-engine product interface. WHAT (scoped to v1 over the runner study classes): (a) query IR: a question (functional-of-solution predicate, e.g. max compliance below X), a confidence, a budget — admitted through fs-ir with the Five Explicits; (b) the planner (fs-plan cost oracle + catalog) picks the cheapest discharge path among: cached ledger result (free), estimate-tier surrogate answer (Estimated color, wide certified interval), fixture-rung solve, full-rung solve — greedy over cost-per-certainty v1 (the Addendum's own fallback: if greedy cannot beat mid-rung-solve baseline by 2x, ship the interface anyway and freeze planner research — instrument that criterion); (c) ANYTIME semantics: returns immediately with the widest honest interval and tightens as rungs complete (streaming JSONL rows); (d) REFUSAL semantics: budget insufficient for the requested confidence returns the achieved interval plus the priced gap (the WS5-C grammar). ACCEPTANCE: 5-query battery over the vskeleton+marquee classes: each returns color-honest intervals that CONTAIN the known fixture truth; anytime rows tighten monotonically; a deliberately underfunded query refuses with the price; planner-vs-baseline instrumentation recorded. TESTS: containment battery, monotone-tightening property, refusal battery, kill-criterion telemetry. DEPENDS on catalog + estimate beads.

title: WS2: CutFEM vector elasticity — Nitsche + ghost penalty on SDF cuts
description: WHY (reality check NO_BEAD gap 1 — the single dependency gating the MARQUEE demo): fs-topopt::marquee is heat-conduction-only because its own docs say elasticity rides CutFEM elasticity when it lands, and NOTHING was landing it. This is the plan's Bet 3 (sec 8.1) completed for the vector case. WHAT: extend fs-cutfem from scalar diffusion to small-strain vector elasticity on SDF-cut cells: (a) vector Nitsche boundary terms (symmetric variant, penalty scaling with the cut fraction per the standard analysis), (b) vector ghost-penalty stabilization on cut-adjacent faces (jump of normal derivatives, order-matched), (c) cut-cell quadrature REUSED from the existing scalar machinery (do not fork it), (d) linear elasticity material via fs-material, assembly into the existing matrix-free apply structure where possible. ACCEPTANCE: (1) G1 MMS battery with a manufactured vector displacement field on a curved SDF domain — convergence-order slope gate build-fails on deviation (fs-cutfem battery precedent at line 404); (2) patch test: constant-strain state reproduced to roundoff on arbitrarily cut cells (the classic CutFEM correctness bar); (3) conditioning: ghost penalty keeps the stiffness condition number bounded as cuts degenerate (log kappa estimates across a cut-fraction sweep, assert bounded growth); (4) adjoint: the elasticity apply registers its VJP with fs-adjoint and passes the FD gradient gate. TESTS: unit quadrature/patch fixtures, MMS battery, conditioning sweep with JSONL logging, FD-adjoint gate. Layer L3.

title: Pin a dated nightly toolchain to stop CI/local clippy drift
description:
## What
rust-toolchain.toml pins the floating 'nightly' channel. CI runners resolve today's nightly while local rch build workers hold older cached ones, so clippy lint sets diverge: the first CI runs failed on lints (chunks_exact->as_chunks) that no local lane could reproduce, and lint drift will recur every time upstream adds a lint.

## Proposal
Pin a dated nightly (e.g. channel = "nightly-2026-07-01") and bump it deliberately on a cadence, treating a toolchain bump like a dependency bump (own commit, full both-ISA gate run). CONVENTIONS.md already calls each nightly feature a liability with a documented fallback; a floating channel is the same liability without the paper trail.

## Coordination
Changing rust-toolchain.toml invalidates every agent's and rch worker's cache simultaneously — land it at a quiet moment, announce in the tracker, and re-run bv/CI after. Verify fs-qty (generic_const_exprs) and fs-simd portable tier still compile on the chosen date.

## Context
Found while landing frankensim-epic-foundations-huq.4: run 28815907293 failed macOS clippy on lints absent from local toolchains; see docs/CI_GATES.md.
close_reason: Pinned rust-toolchain.toml to nightly-2026-07-06, which is BIT-IDENTICAL to the floating nightly every local agent had active at pin time (rustc 1.99.0-nightly 3659db0d3 2026-07-05, verified by installing the dated toolchain and comparing) - so the pin stops CI lint drift without changing anyone's compiler. ANNOUNCEMENT TO ALL AGENTS: the toolchain IDENTITY changed even though the binary did not, so cargo will re-fingerprint and rebuild the workspace once on your next command - expected, one-time. Bump policy documented in the file per the bead: deliberate bumps only, own commit, tracker announcement, full gate run after. Verified under the pin: fs-qty (const-generic dimension arithmetic, the flagged canary) builds + clippy -D warnings clean + 30 tests green.

title: Golden sentinel discipline: upstream coupling declarations + justified-bump verification protocol
description:
Two golden incidents in one day motivate this: the fs-la rand_nla golden broke and was re-pinned (bead 9ekv comment) with an incorrect justification — fs-rand d9ba0b6 only changes counter wrapping at the u64::MAX boundary and cannot have moved those bits; the actual observed values were build-mode-dependent (see the powi determinism bug bead). The re-pin froze release-mode bits while debug still produced the original value, so the sentinel failed or passed depending on profile.

Deliver: (a) a coupling registry — each golden/replay hash declares the upstream semantic surfaces it depends on (e.g. rand_nla golden depends on fs-rand Philox stream identity, fs-la qr/svd_jacobi/gemm bits), checkable by xtask or a test-harness helper, so an upstream semantic change points at every downstream golden that must be deliberately re-frozen instead of surprising it; (b) a written justified-bump protocol: a re-pin must reproduce the observed value at the committed tree (not a dirty working tree), in BOTH debug and release, and must name a root cause that can plausibly move the observed bits; (c) the protocol lands in the golden-evidence policy doc / CONTRACT template so agents follow it mechanically.

Acceptance: seeded upstream change without re-freeze fails with a pointer to the coupling row; a re-pin PR/commit that skips the two-mode committed-tree reproduction is detectable by review checklist; rand_nla and gemm goldens carry coupling declarations as the first two registry entries. Related: frankensim-epic-gauntlet-6nb.9 (failure compounding), frankensim-9ekv (original re-pin).
close_reason: Golden sentinel discipline LANDED. (a) COUPLING REGISTRY: golden-couplings.json at workspace root - surfaces declare a source version const (fs-rand STREAM_SEMANTICS_VERSION, fs-la FACTOR_BIT_SEMANTICS_VERSION, fs-la GEMM_BIT_SEMANTICS_VERSION, all v1, each documented with what counts as a semantic change); goldens declare file/const/depends_on pins/justification. First two entries as required: fs-la:rand-nla (pins stream-semantics + factor-bits + gemm-bits; justification records the CORRECTED incident history - the f3dcb63b re-pin wrongly blamed d9ba0b6, actual mover was optimization-dependent powi, current 0xeef1_0550 verified four quadrants by 4xnt) and fs-la:gemm (pins gemm-bits; KC retune = surface bump = deliberate re-freeze). (b) CHECKER: xtask check-goldens (wired into check-all + help): fails when a surface source const drifts from the registry (naming every dependent golden row to re-freeze) or when a golden pin lags a surface row (pointing at docs/GOLDEN_POLICY.md); also enforces non-thin justifications and live golden consts. ACCEPTANCE: xtask unit test seeds an upstream version bump without re-freeze -> exactly one violation naming BOTH the surface and the dependent golden + 're-freeze'; stale-pin path -> violation citing GOLDEN_POLICY; clean fixture passes; real registry passes (125 crates, 0 golden-coupling violations). (c) PROTOCOL: docs/GOLDEN_POLICY.md - committed-tree reproduction, BOTH debug and release (+ both ISAs where claimed), plausible-root-cause requirement, same-commit registry update, and the reviewer checklist that makes a skipped step detectable; CONTRACT_TEMPLATE.md gained the golden-couplings section. NOTE: check-all currently shows 2 pre-existing fs-ad powi violations owned by in-flight bead 4xnt (not introduced here; check-goldens itself is clean). xtask 16/16 tests green.

Two fix-forwards applied so the strict lanes stay green: hoisted the inner field() helper above the first statements (items_after_statements) and added an #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] with justification on check_goldens. check-goldens is green in check-all (0 violations, 125 crates) and your 3 self-tests pass in the 18-test xtask suite. Your files ride along in my 06yc commit so the committed tree stays green (main.rs wires both checks); closure of this bead stays yours.

title: Make gradient routing smooth-first without synthetic cost penalties
description: plan_gradient_route models differentiability with a fixed 1e6-second penalty. This can make an unavoidable affordable remesh route falsely infeasible under the caller budget and can still select remeshing when a valid smooth route costs more than the constant, contradicting the documented smooth-first/fallback semantics. Replace with deterministic two-pass planning: first the smooth-only subgraph under the unchanged request, then the original graph with EstimatedWithDiscontinuity downgrade. Add tight-budget unavoidable-remesh, smooth-above-old-penalty, smooth-over-budget fallback, no-route, and deterministic regressions; update the contract.
close_reason: Fixed: smooth-first filtered planning under original budgets, honest fallback grading, sealed-plan API migration, and regression coverage; 21 of 21 fs-adjoint tests and strict scoped clippy pass.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Implemented deterministic smooth-first gradient routing without synthetic cost penalties. plan_gradient_route now uses Router::plan_with_edge_filter under the original request budgets, falls back only on RoutePlanError::Infeasible, propagates malformed request, oracle, and arithmetic failures, and consumes sealed RoutePlan accessors. Added regressions for tight-budget unavoidable remesh, a smooth route above the former 1e6-second penalty, over-budget smooth fallback, no-path refusal, and deterministic ties; updated CONTRACT.md. Proof via RCH local fallback and target /Volumes/USB_NVME/cargo-target-frankensim-adjoint-smooth-first: cargo test --locked -p fs-adjoint --features diff-mitigations passed 21 of 21; cargo clippy --locked -p fs-adjoint --features diff-mitigations --test mitigate --no-deps -- -D warnings passed; rustfmt and scoped git diff --check passed. Scoped UBS found no production unsafe, security, concurrency, or allocation issue after removing one redundant clone; its nonzero status is test-only expect, panic, assert, and indexing heuristics. Dependency-inclusive clippy passed fs-geom and is blocked only by an unrelated shared fs-ledger borrow_as_ptr lint, with no fs-adjoint diagnostic.

title: fs-sparse constellation interop: fnx GraphSnapshot converters + fnp array views (split from wsbf)
description: SPLIT from frankensim-wsbf item 5 after the API scout (see wsbf segment-3 comment). fnx-classes' Graph is string-keyed and OWNED (no zero-copy graph view possible in that direction); the honest shape is: (a) Csr::row as the borrowed adjacency view (already shipped), (b) feature-gated converters to/from fnx GraphSnapshot {nodes, edges: Vec<EdgeSnapshot>} that COPY by necessity of fnx's ownership model — document the deviation from wsbf's 'wrap, do not copy' phrasing; (c) fnp array views — fnp-ndarray holds only NdLayout; FIND the owned array type in the fnp workspace first, then decide borrow-vs-convert. Follow the fs-ad torch-bridge precedent: optional constellation deps behind features (fnx-interop, fnp-interop), batteries gated on required-features, L0 stays lean by default. Also parked here: u32-index SELL variant — only worth building if SELL first beats sharded compact CSR on some fixture (it loses on both ISAs today; measurements in wsbf segments 2-3).
close_reason: Constellation interop COMPLETE - all three items. (a) Csr::row borrowed adjacency view: shipped pre-split. (b) fnx GraphSnapshot converters (feature fnx-interop): landed 0783411 - copies by necessity of fnx's string-keyed owned Graph (documented deviation from 'wrap, do not copy'), canonical edge order, multigraph summing, UnknownNode/NotSquare refusals. (c) fnp array views (feature fnp-interop, commit dc99094): scout verdict per the bead - fnp-ndarray holds only NdLayout metadata; the owned array type is fnp_ufunc::UFuncArray (owned Vec<f64> + shape) -> borrow-vs-convert DECIDED: convert by necessity, borrowed views stay Csr::row/to_dense. csr_to_dense_array (shape [nrows,ncols] row-major, +0.0 fill, usize-overflow refused, explicit-zero lossiness documented) + dense_array_to_csr (rank-2 only, f64 value plane, +-0.0 dropped, NON-FINITE refused with position); canonical CSR by construction. Tests: Csr->dense->Csr bitwise identity + dense->Csr->dense identity; rank-1/rank-3/NaN/negative-zero refusals. Default build dependency-lean (verified); fnx battery still green; clippy clean with the feature on. PARKED stays parked: u32-index SELL variant remains a recorded no-claim (loses to sharded compact CSR on both ISAs, wsbf measurements). CONTRACT records the scout verdict + conversion semantics.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
Item (b) — fnx GraphSnapshot converters — DONE and committed (0783411).

crates/fs-sparse/src/interop.rs, feature `fnx-interop` (OFF by default, so the
L1 numeric core pulls NO constellation crate unless opted in):
- csr_to_graph_snapshot(&Csr, CompatibilityMode) -> Result<GraphSnapshot,_>:
  square adjacency only (NotSquare error otherwise); node i -> key "i", stored
  (r,c,v) -> directed EdgeSnapshot r->c with weight v under WEIGHT_KEY,
  canonical row-major/ascending-column edge order.
- graph_snapshot_to_csr(&GraphSnapshot) -> Result<Csr,_>: nodes map to indices
  BY ORDER (keys may be arbitrary strings, not just decimals); weights from
  WEIGHT_KEY (Float verbatim, Int coerced, absent/non-numeric -> 1.0); parallel
  (r,c) edges SUM (multigraph -> simple); output via BTreeMap<(r,c),w> so the
  CSR arrays fall out already sorted+unique+canonical. UnknownNode error on a
  dangling edge key.
- COPIES by necessity (fnx Graph is string-keyed and OWNED; the honest
  deviation from wsbf's 'wrap, do not copy'). The borrowed neighbor view stays
  Csr::row (item a, already shipped).

8 gated unit tests: round-trip Csr->snapshot->Csr bitwise-identity (identity,
empty-row+self-loop fixture, 1x1), weight coercions, parallel-edge sum,
missing-weight default, unknown-node error, non-square refusal, arbitrary
string keys. Gates: default build pulls zero external deps; feature build
compiles against fnx-classes+fnx-runtime (precedent fs-truss/fs-rep-voxel);
clippy -D warnings clean default AND with feature; fmt clean; xtask check-all
0/125 (dependency policy accepts the optional constellation deps).

Item (c) fnp array views — SCOUTED, deferred as consumer-driven (documented in
CONTRACT no-claims). The owned f64 array is fnp-ufunc::UFuncArray (new(shape,
values, dtype); .values()/.shape()/.strides()); fnp-ndarray::NdLayout is
layout-only (no data). BUT fnp-ufunc transitively pulls rayon + fnp-linalg -- a
heavy dep for an L1 core, and the bead itself frames (c) as 'decide
borrow-vs-convert' + 'only if a consumer needs it'. So I did NOT speculatively
pull rayon/linalg into fs-sparse; when a consumer (LBM moments? a numpy export
path?) actually needs it, it lands behind a SEPARATE `fnp-interop` feature
(csr_to_dense_ufunc + optional component-array views). The parked u32-SELL
variant also stays parked (SELL loses to sharded compact CSR on both ISAs per
wsbf segments 2-3).

Releasing to open: the concrete interop deliverable (fnx converters) is
shipped and green; the remainder is honestly consumer-gated.

title: Self-expanding Gauntlet v1: minimize failures into permanent regression families
description: Operationalize failure compounding without speculative research scope. For every golden break, falsifier hit, certificate guard trip, or seeded invariant failure: capture replay data; deterministically minimize the counterexample; land a permanent regression family; probe a bounded neighboring parameter region; classify the invariant; and link the result to the closing Bead. Acceptance: first customer is a real prior failure (rand_nla golden or equivalent); minimizer/replay determinism; bounded work; no automatic certificate promotion; closure lint refuses a technical-failure Bead lacking regression evidence or explicit no-repro reason.
