title: fs-mesh v3: constrained INTERIOR facet recovery (CDT) + boundary-layer quality (residual full-Ruppert)
description: SPLIT from frankensim-uee3 (items 1-conforming/2-partial/3/4/5 all landed there; see its close reason for the full evidence trail). REMAINING SCOPE: (a) TRUE CONSTRAINED-Delaunay facet recovery for interior/non-convex PLC facets — cavity retetrahedralization (Shewchuk-style) or gift-wrapping within the facet plane, building on recovery.rs's segment machinery (twin adoption at shared midpoints, boundary-correspondence table) and refine.rs's diametral encroachment protection (facet_diametral_ball / encroached_hull_facet — already classical-Ruppert-rule-correct on hull facets); (b) BOUNDARY-LAYER QUALITY: the residual radius-edge blowup near boundaries (ledgered in tmesh-011: hull-split infrastructure measured 2.8e18 worst, diametral protection cut it ~8x to 3.5e17 — the remaining slivers come from near-boundary INTERIOR vertices, empirically confirming the classical coupling of Ruppert quality to full boundary protection); re-enable split_hull_facets by default once quality holds. Start from crates/fs-mesh/CONTRACT.md no-claims; Tetrahedralization::audit is the regression trip-wire; the perf lane (tests/perf_lane.rs, 115k pts/s at 1e7 on Mac16,11) is the throughput baseline any new machinery must not collapse.
close_reason: fs-mesh v3 resolved: item (a) COMPLETE, item (b) DECIDED BY MEASUREMENT with the honest continuation recorded. (a) CONSTRAINED INTERIOR FACET RECOVERY landed through swarm collaboration: recover_facets recovers every simple planar PLC facet as a union of mesh faces — convex loops by exact fans, NON-CONVEX loops by exact-predicate ear-clipping in the facet plane (fellow agents' commits 9a6d303/6510b028, tmesh-015 + the L-fixture battery: 36 sub-faces tiling the non-convex L at exact area 3.0, audit clean, bitwise replay, honest unrecovered counters at caps); (b) BOUNDARY-LAYER QUALITY, the question tmesh-011 ledgered, now answered with data (tmesh-017, MY segment): the refine(split_hull_facets) -> exude pipeline cuts the sliver census 27% (183 -> 134) and lifts the worst dihedral off exact zero, and the final mesh's longest/2*shortest EDGE-aspect is ~19 — proving the ledgered 3.5e17 'radius-edge' is CONFINED to near-coplanar hull slivers whose circumradius explodes while their edges stay tame; A HULL-EDGE PROTECTION TIER WAS BUILT AND MEASURED COUNTERPRODUCTIVE (worst 3.5e17 -> 4.3e18, reverted, documented in the CONTRACT): convex-hull edges of a point cloud are NOT PLC features — the classical segment rule protects INPUT segments only, and the measurement enforced the theory; split_hull_facets therefore stays default-OFF with the decision recorded: removing the residual near-coplanar class needs WEIGHTED exudation on an exact weighted insphere predicate — the recorded fs-ivl no-claim, demand-driven. Evidence: 19/19 conformance green (including the new tmesh-017 pipeline gates: audit clean end-to-end, census non-increasing, refinement fully accounted); clippy -D warnings 0; fmt clean; the CONTRACT carries the full measured decision.

title: Bound and authenticate fs-bisect failure-compounding families
description: Fresh-eyes audit of closed beads 6nb.9/hx4p found the self-expanding Gauntlet's claimed bounded and replay-safe surface is not structurally enforced. minimize bounds accepted steps but not candidates returned per step; probe_neighborhood/compound accept an unbounded neighbor Vec; tried can overflow; compound accepts empty tracking despite its landed-family invariant; public family construction can encode duplicate/empty labels; manifest interpolates unescaped caller-controlled strings into invalid/injectable JSON; InvariantClass::Other("golden-drift") hashes identically to the built-in GoldenDrift because only display names are encoded; COMPOUND_CANON_VERSION is omitted from the content preimage. Repair with explicit candidate/neighborhood/tracking/member/text bounds and typed refusals, checked counters, canonical variant tags, schema version in the hash, validated construction, JSON escaping, exact-boundary/limit+1 and collision regressions, and a deliberate golden-couplings version/hash bump. Preserve the no-automatic-promotion boundary.
close_reason: Completed fs-bisect compound v3 hardening: bounded canonical writer and aggregate work limits; sealed validated family construction; domain-separated versioned hashes; JSON escaping plus independent duplicate-key parser oracle; pure/complete Canon contract and replay identity checks; exact-cap fixpoint semantics; callback mutation detection across shrink generation, retained witnesses, neighbors, and replay; 22-case compound battery. Proof: cargo test -p fs-bisect --all-features --all-targets (10 base + 22 compound pass); cargo clippy -p fs-bisect --all-features --all-targets -- -D warnings; release exact frozen manifest test; xtask check-goldens (126 crates, 0 violations); scoped UBS no actionable production finding.

title: WS5: estimate() dry runs + budget refusal through the runners
description: WHY (plan sec 11.5/P4: estimate() dry runs and errors-as-guidance are core HELM promises; fs-plan's cost oracle exists but no user surface): budgets-first needs the front door. WHAT: (a) an estimate verb on every runner: parses + admits the study, runs the fs-plan cost/error-ledger oracle against the catalog schemas, returns predicted wall/memory/accuracy WITHOUT allocating solver state (the pl_008c refusal-before-allocation doctrine as a feature); (b) predictions carry evidence colors (a cost estimate is Estimated, never laundered); (c) run compares actuals against the estimate and records the calibration pair in the ledger (feeding the fs-session calibration machinery that already tracks estimator accuracy); (d) refusal semantics: budget below floor-estimate refuses with the gap priced (the declarative-query refusal grammar, previewed here). ACCEPTANCE: estimate on the fixture studies returns within-2x-calibrated predictions after 10 recorded runs (calibration battery, JSONL); zero solver allocation during estimate (lease accounting asserts); refusal messages name the missing budget delta. TESTS: calibration battery, allocation-zero assertion, refusal battery. DEPENDS on runner binaries + catalog.

title: Evidence schema v5: non-forgeable package claim origins and waiver transport
description: After schema v4 replaces the package/checker root with shared BLAKE3, public Color::Verified + Claim::new(Color) still lets any finite interval pass the standalone checker; content consistency is not evidence origin. Seal Claim at the package boundary (not the globally used Color algebra) with SourceCertificate/AnchoredSource/EstimatedSource/Derived/AuthenticatedWaiver origins, private fields/read-only accessors, bit-exact re-derivation, and schema-v5 transport. Waivers must travel with claims and require an injected verifier/date context. Migrate all package producers/tests; invalid/refuted/originless claims produce no positive coverage. Bump checker/crosswalk compatibility metadata and add raw-Verified, color-tamper, bad-parent, unrelated-anchor, expired/tampered/replayed waiver adversarial tests. Depends on the schema-v4 BLAKE3 root migration to avoid two incompatible format version 4 definitions.
close_reason: Schema v5/v6 origin sealing complete across two convergent lanes, verified and committed (final surface in the schema-v6 landing commit; groundwork in 10d1e2c-era slices + origin.rs foundation). Delivered: Claim sealed at the package boundary (private fields, origin-typed constructors - raw Color::Verified + Claim::new can no longer mint checker-passing claims; the Color algebra itself untouched); typed ClaimOrigin {SourceCertificate, AnchoredSource, EstimatedSource, Derived, AuthenticatedWaiver} bound into the BLAKE3 content address; VerificationCapabilities context with fail-closed deny_all default - SourceCertificate claims verify only through an injected typed SourceCertificateVerifier over the exact claim request, waivers only through an injected verifier + clock context (expiry/tamper/replay refused via the MAC over claim canonical bytes); origin-color consistency (unrelated anchors, estimator mismatches, Derived<->receipt iff) refused; v6 transport with strict parsing, earlier versions refused by version; checker protocol + SUPPORTED_PACKAGE_FORMAT aligned; producers/tests migrated (53 sites); originless/refuted/invalid claims produce no positive coverage. Battery verified green pre-commit: fs-package 38+51, fs-checker 20+15, fs-crosswalk 8+7, fs-ledger incl. color battery, fs-epi-e2e; check-all 126 crates 0 violations. Credit: the origin/capability lane drove the v6 capability redesign; CloudyFinch built the v4 root substrate, the sealed-Claim core + origin vocabulary, and the producer migration

title: fs-cheb remaining variants: colleague roots + certification, 2D low-rank, Fourier-periodic, Qty functions
description:
SPLIT of the remaining items from frankensim-urvw after its two load-bearing deliverables landed: (a) the complex nonsymmetric eigensolver (fs_math::c64 + fs_la::eigen_complex: Givens Hessenberg + shifted QR, Wilkinson/total_cmp deterministic shifts, typed EigFailure, lu_complex solve; battery incl companion roots-of-unity, Jacobi agreement, trace/det identities vs independent oracle, Hermitian reality, T6 colleague fixture; golden 0xf78cfbae5f12b4d4 cross-ISA) and (b) ORR-SOMMERFELD (fs_cheb::orr_sommerfeld: Trefethen clamped-D4 collocation, B^-1 A reduction, growth_rates sigma_1..k first-class query; ACCEPTANCE MET: Re_c = 5772.22 at alpha=1.02056, N=48, matching Orszag to displayed digits; golden 0x7b3be74ed5a6faad cross-ISA).

THIS BEAD (the deferred variants): (1) GENERAL colleague-matrix rootfinding in fs-cheb: build the Chebyshev companion (colleague) matrix for an arbitrary Cheb1's coefficients (balance the leading-coefficient division; the pure-T_n fixture is already validated in the eigc battery), roots = eigenvalues filtered to the domain (complex ones discarded with a tolerance policy - document), replacing/augmenting the v1 subdivision roots which MISS even-multiplicity roots (documented no-claim in fs-cheb CONTRACT); certification via fs-ivl interval Newton where requested (newton_roots landed). (2) 2D/3D low-rank function objects (Chebyshev outer products, adaptive cross approximation). (3) Fourier-periodic variant (closed curves/angular profiles; fs-fft has the complex FFT). (4) Qty-dimensioned function objects (units end-to-end per plan). (5) FrankenScipy special-function cross-checks (constellation wiring decision needed). Key files: crates/fs-cheb/src/{lib,orr_sommerfeld}.rs, crates/fs-la/src/eigen_complex.rs, crates/fs-ivl/src/newton.rs.
close_reason: fs-cheb variants shipped: colleague.rs + cheb2.rs + fourier.rs, battery tests/variants.rs 7/7. (1) COLLEAGUE ROOTS: Chebyshev companion matrix on the fs-la complex nonsymmetric stack urvw landed; documented ColleaguePolicy (trailing trim, im tolerance, domain slack, sqrt-eps cluster dedupe - a double root's eigenvalue pair measured splitting at 5e-9); RESOLVES the v1 even-multiplicity no-claim: (x-r)^2(x-s) fixtures the sign scanner provably misses are found (cheb-102 gates both the miss AND the find); simple roots to 3.3e-16, 1e-3 clusters separated. CONVENTION BUG CAUGHT BY THE BATTERY: Cheb1 stores Sigma-prime (c0 un-halved) - treating c0 as the true constant gave 2.2e-1 root errors; both the colleague and interval paths now halve on entry, documented in the contract. (2) CERTIFICATION: fs-ivl interval Newton on Clenshaw-in-interval-arithmetic: 3/3 simple roots CERTIFIED with 6.1e-15 enclosure widths (unique-root proofs); the double root honestly comes back Possible (its derivative encloses zero, as it must). (3) CHEB2 (Chebfun2-style ACA): deterministic max-residual pivots, FIXED-resolution slices (the adaptive builder's machine-plateau test provably cannot pass on ACA residual slices carrying absolute cancellation noise - measured panic, documented in-module); exact rank on separable fixtures (1 and 2), rank-8 at 3.3e-12 on the smooth non-separable, separable integration to 1.2e-17 on the odd fixture. (4) FOURIER: fs-fft real-transform interpolants: trig recovery 4e-15, spectral derivative identity 5.7e-15, tail decay 1e-16, Bessel-integral (2pi I0(1)) to 8.9e-16. cheb-107 bitwise replays across all three. A fellow in-crate agent hardened input validation + det-trig determinism mid-flight - kept and credited. Lanes: clippy 0, 19 tests green (7+4+8), fmt, ubs 0 critical, xtask clean. No-claims: 3D/tensor-train low-rank, complex-root reporting, circle rootfinding, Qty dimensions, FrankenScipy cross-checks.

comments.text: PLAN (CloudyFinch): three modules in fs-cheb. (1) colleague.rs: GENERAL colleague-matrix rootfinding - trim trailing coefficients by a documented relative policy, build the Chebyshev colleague matrix (three-term recurrence rows, coefficient-loaded last row scaled by -1/(2 a_n)), eigenvalues via the fs_la::eigen_complex stack urvw landed, filter to real roots in-domain with a documented imaginary/domain tolerance + cluster dedupe (fixes the v1 subdivision scanner's DOCUMENTED even-multiplicity no-claim: sign-scan cannot see (x-r)^2 roots, the colleague matrix can); certified_roots via fs-ivl interval Newton (Clenshaw evaluated in interval arithmetic for f and f' - rigorous enclosures, RootBox::Certified/Possible surfaced as-is). title: Report fsqlite IS NULL operator-precedence bug upstream (CHECK re-parse mis-association)
description:
## What
While implementing fs-ledger (frankensim-epic-helm-gp3.1), a STRICT-table CHECK constraint written as `CHECK((t_end IS NULL) = (outcome IS NULL))` failed on rows that satisfy it (e.g. t_end=200, outcome='ok').

## Evidence
fsqlite reports: "CHECK constraint failed: (t_end IS NULL = outcome) IS NULL" — i.e. the stored constraint text is re-parsed with `=` binding tighter than postfix `IS NULL`, evaluating `((t_end IS NULL) = outcome) IS NULL` instead of `(t_end IS NULL) = (outcome IS NULL)`. Stock SQLite gives postfix IS NULL higher precedence than comparison, so the parenthesized form must hold. Two different renderings of the same constraint appeared across runs ("t_end IS NULL = outcome IS NULL" vs "(t_end IS NULL = outcome) IS NULL"), suggesting parens are stripped when the schema text is normalized and precedence is lost on re-parse.

## Repro
fsqlite 0.1.14 (constellation lock f92683cc4572a198):
CREATE TABLE t(a INTEGER, b TEXT, CHECK((a IS NULL) = (b IS NULL))) STRICT;
INSERT INTO t VALUES (1, 'x'); -- should succeed; fails with CHECK violation via UPDATE path in fs-ledger's finish_op

## Workaround in fs-ledger
Rewrote the constraint and the matching lint query in AND/OR form: (a IS NULL AND b IS NULL) OR (a IS NOT NULL AND b IS NOT NULL). See crates/fs-ledger/src/schema.rs and lint() in src/lib.rs.

## Why a bead
Plan §12 constellation contract: semantic disagreements with the reference become bug reports for one side. This one is fsqlite's; file it in the frankensqlite repo with the minimal repro.
close_reason: Reported upstream: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite/issues/122. Bug RE-VERIFIED against current fsqlite 0.1.15 before filing (it persists past 0.1.14) with a fresh 20-line repro whose output is fully diagnostic: (a) satisfying row (200, 'ok') REJECTED with 'CHECK constraint failed: t_end IS NULL = outcome IS NULL'; (b) satisfying both-NULL row accepted; (c) VIOLATING row (300, NULL) ACCEPTED - constraint effectively inverted; (d) sqlite_master shows the stored schema text with the user's parentheses STRIPPED. All three truth-table rows match ((t_end IS NULL) = outcome) IS NULL exactly, confirming the diagnosis: normalization strips semantically-necessary parens AND the re-parser binds '=' tighter than postfix IS NULL (stock SQLite is the opposite, so even paren-stripped text would evaluate correctly there). Issue names the precedence fix as root cause, notes the paren-preservation hardening, documents the impact class ('these columns are set together' invariants silently invert) and the fs-ledger workaround (AND/OR expansion).

title: Persist exact canonical color bytes in ledger color-write rows
description: Fresh-eyes audit 2026-07-10: fs-ledger color-write schema v6 serializes Color::payload_json(), which is explicitly a display rendering and can alias exact f64/canonical identity values. The v9 node hash binds fs-evidence Color::canonical_bytes(), but ordinary non-waived Estimated sources and many Validated/derived rows do not persist those exact bytes, so an external row-only consumer can observe the hash without reconstructing its preimage. This does not create an in-memory hash collision and the current contract disclaims a persisted-row parser, so do not mutate schema v6 in place. Define a versioned next color-write row schema carrying exact canonical-color bytes (hex or typed bit fields), update hash/version/crosswalk contracts, implement independent row parse-and-rehash verification, migrate safely, and add signed-zero/non-finite/long-identity/derived/validated round-trip goldens. Exit: every persisted row accepted by the new schema independently reconstructs its exact node hash without consulting in-memory Color state; prior schemas remain explicitly version-refused or migrated.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: ROW-RECONSTRUCTION COMPLETENESS AUDIT (a CloudyFinch session) — the hard prerequisite is DONE in-tree; the exit criterion needs one bounded deliverable. VERIFIED FIELD-BY-FIELD against node_hash_from_canonical_payloads: every hash input is now recoverable from rows alone: name/operation/parents (ids -> hashes via earlier rows in the stream) + color_canonical_hex (v7 row) + origin_canonical_hex + origin_policy_fingerprint + demotion rows carrying value_bits hex (display-float aliasing already closed) + waiver_dependencies entries carrying authorizing_node/operation/policy_fingerprint/admission_day/full grant (signing_payload_hex + signature_hex) + waiver annotation + waiver_policy_fingerprint + waiver_admission_day + node_hash_version/color_algebra_version stamps. REMAINING for the exit criterion, one pass: (1) the independent row-stream verifier — pub fn over &[String] rows (or a stream) that parses WITHOUT any in-memory Color/ColorGraph state, rebuilds each node's canonical payload buffer per node_hash_from_canonical_payloads's exact encoding (the payload-buffer layout at colors.rs:1744-1843 is the spec; parent hashes come from the id->hash map accumulated over prior color-write rows; demotions matched by node id from demotion rows in emission order), recomputes, and compares to the row's hash — refusing rows whose schema_version != 7 or node_hash_version != current (prior schemas version-refused per the bead; no silent migration); (2) goldens: signed-zero (-0.0 vs 0.0 must produce different canonical hex and different hashes), non-finite dispersion/interval markers, long-identity strings, derived + validated round-trips — parse the emitted row, verify hex round-trips to the exact bits; (3) CONTRACT.md row-schema section. Releasing to the in-tree implementer (colors lane owns the file and its velocity; the gp3.20 precedent) — this map should make it one sitting.

title: TilePool workers must execute under asupersync scopes
description: Even after hot kernels traverse TilePool/Cx, TilePool's throughput workers are still implemented with std::thread::scope. Complete Decalogue P7 at L0: drive worker lifetimes under explicit asupersync scopes/Cx so cancellation, budgets, arenas, NUMA/topology, and drain/finalize semantics share one scope tree. Add G4 task-leak/deadlock/arena-quiescence storms and G5 deterministic reduction/scheduling audits. This is the L0 substrate follow-up, not a reason to bypass TilePool in L1.
close_reason: TilePool workers now run under explicit asupersync scopes: TilePool::run_scoped(task_cx, kernel, gate, run, budget, lease) drives the throughput workers as scoped CPU children of the CALLING task via Cx::scoped_cpu (asupersync fa371dcf, bead 691k). P7 complete at L0: the calling task blocks inside the scope until every worker joins, so the region cannot close under the pool — no faked scope tree, no leaked workers, one drain semantics for two signals (CancelGate requests and task cancellation/budget exhaustion both convert to the pool's gate-drain protocol at tile boundaries, charged one checkpoint per boundary). The worker protocol (seed deques, CCD-local steal-half, drain-on-cancel, per-tile panic containment) is extracted into ONE shared worker_loop driven by both harnesses — std::thread::scope for callers with no ambient task, Cx::scoped_cpu for tasked callers — so the lanes are bitwise-identical by construction. Fail-closed: entry refusal (pre-cancelled/exhausted task runs NOTHING), mid-run drain, and exit refusal (a cancelled task does not admit completed work) all surface as RunError::Cancelled. G4 storms: pre-cancel entry refusal, mid-run task-cancel drain (completed<total, arenas quiescent via stats().quiescent(), pool reusable after), tile-panic containment (panic localized to its tile, message preserved, siblings drained, scope joined, pool survives). G5 audits: scoped lane deterministic across reruns AND bitwise-identical to the std lane, tiles_by_worker sums to total. All under a REAL runtime root task (LatencyLane -> Cx::current), not a synthetic Cx. EVIDENCE: cargo test -p fs-exec = 84 lib + all integration suites green (existing 16 pool tests prove the extraction preserved the std lane exactly); cargo clippy -p fs-exec --all-targets -D warnings = clean; rustfmt --check clean. Commits feb6fa8 (bulk, swept mid-work by the tree-sweeper agent) + 35aef00 (completion). SCOPE NOTES: (1) callers with no ambient task keep the std lane deliberately — it is itself scope-sound (function-blocks-until-join) and forcing a runtime at every fft/roofline call site would invert the layering; HELM/session orchestration should call run_scoped. (2) OS-level spawn failure panics in the scoped lane (upstream scoped_cpu uses Scope::spawn) vs the std lane's structured RunError::WorkerSpawn — documented; upstream try_spawn would close the gap. (3) Task-budget EXHAUSTION mid-run is exercised upstream (asupersync-c8xqh6 tests); at fs-exec level cancellation covers the same checkpoint plumbing. (4) The persistent-parked-worker optimization (per-run spawn overhead, the 27d3 N-D attainment collapse) remains deferred per CONTRACT no-claims — this bead delivered scope honesty, not persistence; a follow-up can now build parked workers UNDER scoped_cpu without breaking P7.

comments.author: codex-5.6
comments.text: 2026-07-10 fresh audit blocker: current asupersync safe spawn surfaces cannot soundly host TilePool's borrowed kernel. Cx::spawn_in, spawn_blocking_in, spawn_local, and Scope registration require Send+'static or 'static, while TilePool deliberately borrows run-local K/deques/bands/A/B/poll and must join/drain before returning. Wrapping std::thread::scope in an unrelated root task would fake the scope tree and would not satisfy P7. Required upstream primitive: a lending ScopedCpuScope (or equivalent) accepting non-'static child closures with mandatory structured join/drain, cancellation/budget propagation, and panic containment. Keep open until that API exists; current fs-exec CONTRACT retains the exact std::thread::scope no-claim.

comments.author: jemanuel

The upstream implementation is cross-repo work requiring explicit operator authorization; until it lands and is pinned via constellation.lock, this bead should not surface as ready in bv triage. fs-exec CONTRACT retains the exact std::thread::scope no-claim meanwhile (CloudyFinch lane).

title: WS2: goal-oriented DWR for CutFEM elasticity (compliance functional)
description: WHY: the marquee pipeline is DWR-GUIDED octree refinement; the heat marquee already uses DWR splits concentrating at the boundary — elasticity needs its own dual solve and error indicators or the marquee cannot steer refinement. WHAT: dual-weighted-residual estimator for the compliance goal functional on CutFEM elasticity: dual problem assembly (self-adjoint for compliance — document and EXPLOIT this: the dual solve is the primal solve, which halves cost and is the honest reason compliance-first), element/face indicator split including the cut-cell and ghost-penalty contributions, effectivity tracking. ACCEPTANCE: effectivity index (estimated/true error, true from a fine reference solve) within [0.5, 2.0] across a 3-case fixture family, logged per refinement level; indicators concentrate at stress concentrations and cut boundaries on the plate-with-hole fixture (assert the top-decile indicator cells are within k cells of the hole boundary — a falsifiable localization claim); estimator drives the existing octree refinement loop (integration test with the marquee machinery in heat-parity mode first). TESTS: effectivity battery with JSONL rows, localization assertion, refinement-loop integration. DEPENDS on CutFEM vector elasticity.

title: fs-topopt eigenfrequency + stress-constrained SIMP: eigenvalue derivatives w/ clustered handling, adaptive p-norm stress aggregation
description: SPLIT from 7tv.11 (SIMP core + robust three-field landed in crates/fs-topopt slices 1-2; this bead adds the two remaining OBJECTIVE/CONSTRAINT classes on the same fixed-mesh pipeline). (a) EIGENFREQUENCY: maximize min eigenfrequency of K(rho)phi = lambda M(rho)phi via fs-la eigen (jacobi_eigh for fixture scale; LOBPCG when fs-solid stability's pencil machinery is reusable - check tfz.15, other agent); eigenvalue derivative dlambda/drho_c = phi^T(dK/drho_c - lambda dM/drho_c)phi / (phi^T M phi) with the SIMP chain through the fs-topopt pipeline pullback; CLUSTERED-EIGENVALUE handling (the classic trap: crossing modes make single-eigenvalue objectives nonsmooth - use smooth aggregation over the lowest cluster, e.g. p-mean-of-inverse or bound formulation, FD-verified NEAR a crossing on a fixture DESIGNED to have one); mass interpolation must avoid spurious low-density modes (the standard rho-linear mass vs rho^p stiffness mismatch trap - document the E_min/mass-floor treatment). (b) STRESS: per-cell von Mises from the P1 strain (B-matrices already per-cell in DensityElasticity); the SINGULARITY TRAP treatment = qp-relaxation (stress-interpolation exponent q < p so stress goes to zero slower than stiffness at rho->0, documented); ADAPTIVE p-norm aggregation sigma_pn = (sum w_c sigma_c^P)^(1/P) with the adaptive normalization factor c_k = sigma_max/sigma_pn updated per iteration (keeps the aggregate tracking the true max - the honesty knob); adjoint for the aggregated constraint (NOT self-adjoint - one extra solve, through fs-adjoint machinery); fixture: L-bracket-like reentrant fixture on the kuhn slab with the stress concentration resolved, verify optimized design reduces max stress vs compliance-only baseline at matched volume. Batteries: eigenvalue-derivative FD gates (simple + clustered), stress-adjoint FD gates at continuation stages, singularity-trap regression (rho->1e-3 cells do not dominate the aggregate), goldens + trj.
close_reason: EIGENFREQUENCY + STRESS-CONSTRAINED SIMP LANDED (fs-topopt::eigenfreq + fs-topopt::stress + per-cell mass/CB blocks in elasticity). SLICE A (eigenfrequency): generalized K(rho)phi = lambda M(rho)phi via per-cell consistent P1 mass blocks (kept separate like K_t for exact chain rules) + Cholesky reduction + fs-la Jacobi with M-normalized modes; MASS-INTERPOLATION TRAP handled AND GATED (linear mass above rho=0.1, continuously-matched rho^6 below - a mostly-void design keeps lambda_min = 9.3e-2 instead of hosting spurious modes); exact eigenvalue design gradients through the FULL filter/projection/SIMP/mass chain (FD rel 1.9e-10 - essentially exact); smooth-min aggregation with exact weighted-combination gradients for CLUSTERED eigenvalues, FD-verified NEAR the cantilever cube's symmetric y/z bending near-crossing (3.0e-8 - where single-eigenvalue objectives are nonsmooth); +67% lambda_agg ascent demo at fixed volume (0.234 -> 0.392, measured). SLICE B (stress): per-cell CB stress recovery + stress_pullback (the transposed map = the adjoint RHS builder); relaxed von Mises measures with qp-RELAXATION q=0.5 < penal; p-norm aggregation with ADAPTIVE normalization c = sigma_max/PN REPORTED per evaluation (the honesty knob, c ~ 1.4 measured); NON-self-adjoint design gradient - one extra adjoint solve, cross terms via the polarization identity on cell energies - FD-gated at TWO continuation stages (8.8e-9 / 1.3e-8); SINGULARITY-TRAP regression: void-block share 0.000% of sigma_max AND the gradient FD-verifiable AT the void floor (5.5e-8 - no numerical blowup, the trap's practical symptom); -22% max-stress descent demo at fixed volume. LESSON DOCUMENTED: the first trap-test direction was physically backwards (raising void densities LOWERS stress by stiffening - adding material reduces stress); the corrected gate tests what the relaxation actually claims (void cells cannot DRIVE the constraint). EVIDENCE: 14/14 both ISAs (trj /data/tmp/fsstress.9gK2Lt through sustained multi-agent contention), FOUR goldens frozen (pipeline, robust, eigen 0xbb7e_5ad3_851a_2bf1, stress 0xc539_ad97_34d8_1b66); fs-topopt clippy zero warnings, fmt, CONTRACT updated (public-types + conformance + no-claims: dense eigen at fixture scale w/ LOBPCG via fs-solid-stability when needed; the constrained AL driver composes at the consumer; L-bracket literature comparisons live in the CutFEM lane b7d0).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: STARTED (numerics-spine agent). Slice A this stretch = EIGENFREQUENCY: (1) per-cell consistent P1 tet mass blocks M_t added to fs-topopt::elasticity (12x12, |V|/20 pattern per component) kept separate like K_t for exact chain rules; (2) generalized eigenproblem K(rho)phi = lambda M(rho)phi on free dofs, dense at fixture scale via M = LL^T Cholesky reduction to standard form + fs-la jacobi_eigh; (3) the MASS-INTERPOLATION TRAP handled and documented: SIMP stiffness E_min + rho^p vs LINEAR mass gives lambda ~ rho^(p-1) -> 0 spurious modes in void regions - standard fix m(rho) = rho for rho >= 0.1, c*rho^6 below (continuous match at 0.1), documented; (4) eigenvalue derivative dlambda/drho_t = phi^T(dK_t/drho - lambda dM_t/drho)phi / (phi^T M phi) with the full filter/projection/SIMP chain through the landed pipeline pullback; (5) CLUSTERED handling: smooth-min aggregation -1/beta ln(sum exp(-beta lambda_k)) over the lowest cluster with its exact weighted-combination gradient - FD-verified NEAR a designed crossing. Slice B (stress p-norm + qp-relaxation + L-bracket) follows; re-split if context/scope demands per discipline.

title: Revert tune_put UPDATE-then-INSERT workaround once frankensqlite#123 is fixed
description:
fs-ledger tune_put (crates/fs-ledger/src/lib.rs) was rerouted from INSERT..ON CONFLICT..DO UPDATE to UPDATE-then-INSERT in 0e4534c because the upsert form corrupts the DB at fsqlite HEAD (upstream regression d1a543e, bd-kwei8 insert-side guard misses the upsert mutation path; https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite/issues/123, bead u8og).

When upstream fixes #123: (1) pull frankensqlite clones (Mac + ts1/ts2) past the fix; (2) verify the fix with the raw repro in the issue (3x ~2KB rows into a composite-PK STRICT table, then ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE - must NOT error); (3) restore the single-statement upsert in tune_put (doc comment there marks the spot); (4) rerun fs-ledger suite + fs-roofline conformance on both ISAs.

The two-statement form is semantically equivalent only under single-writer-per-connection - one more reason not to keep it forever.
close_reason: upstream #123 fixed in 3c388122 and pinned via 3a76824; raw repro re-verified clean both ISAs; atomic upsert restored; rf_004 8/8 on ts1 (the originally failing ISA) and tune suites green both ISAs

title: x86 mul_add libm sweep: route sparse/cheb/lattice hot loops through FMA-codegen capsules
description: The a55x close recorded the standing sweep: baseline x86-64 lowers f64::mul_add to a per-element libm CALL in every hot loop not routed through fs-simd dispatch or a target_feature body (measured 14-28x deficits: feec 0.026 attainment, batch-gemm 2-4% of roof, batch-lu 4.6%). Census (2026-07-11): fs-sparse 28 sites (CSR spmv/spmm, BSR, SELL-C-sigma, SpGEMM, perf.rs parallel), fs-cheb 9 (clenshaw eval loops), fs-lattice 4, fs-ivl 4; fs-la's remaining sites are inside already-capsuled or dirty-lane surfaces; fs-math's 52 are det:: internals (deliberately scalar, out of scope). Fix pattern (blessed by a55x): extract the loop body as inline(always), recompile under a registered target_feature(avx2,fma) capsule with runtime detection - one fused IEEE op either way, BIT-IDENTICAL (libm fma is correctly-rounded fused), goldens/batteries gate it. Reduction SHAPES stay fixed per the determinism contracts - this is pure codegen, never reordering. Acceptance per crate: capsule + SAFETY.md + registry, batteries green both ISAs, spot measurement on ts1/ts2 for at least one lane.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CSR SLICE LANDED + VERIFIED BOTH ISAs (be50c1c). spmv/spmm bodies extracted inline(always), recompiled under the registered target_feature(avx2,fma) capsule (fs-feec a55x pattern), runtime-detected, portable path = twin; SAFETY.md + registry. Verified: fs-sparse suites green on aarch64 AND natively on ts2 (capsule path ACTIVE on real AVX2, cross-format bitwise suites prove bit identity); x86 cross-compile clean; check-all 0 violations. REMAINING SWEEP (same mechanical pattern, all quiet surfaces): fs-sparse BSR (bsr.rs:153), SELL-C-sigma (sell.rs), SpGEMM (ops.rs:108/158/193), parallel perf.rs bodies; fs-cheb clenshaw loops (9 sites); fs-lattice (4); fs-ivl (4 - check the interval contract first: fused rounding changes interval WIDTHS is fine only if the contract permits, verify before touching). Spot perf measurement on a ts lane still owed for one of the swept kernels. Releasing for the next window.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SLICE-2 SITE MAP REFINED (CloudyFinch - attempted this window, backed off cleanly when generic anchors proved non-unique; no partial edits in tree). Exact remaining fs-sparse sites for the capsule treatment, each needing an INDIVIDUAL body extraction with its own unique anchor: (1) Bsr::spmv bsr.rs ~146-158 (block-row acc loop; NOTE the loop text appears TWICE in the file - the doc example duplicates it, anchor on the fn signature not the loop); (2) Sell::spmv sell.rs ~157-166 (perm/lane loop) AND Sell::spmv_chunked sell.rs ~185+ (lane-vectorized acc[l] loop - this one autovectorizes and mul_add is per-LANE independent, still a libm call each on baseline x86); (3) CsrCompact::spmv perf.rs ~97-110 + the PARALLEL shard closure ~193-202: closures are separate codegen units so target_feature does NOT propagate into them - extract the per-shard row loop as an inline(always) method and dispatch INSIDE the closure (one detection per shard, thousands of rows amortize it); (4) ops.rs SpGEMM (108/158) + blocked multi-RHS (193): whole-fn wrap like feec apply_mono (alloc-heavy, do LAST - lowest flop density of the set). Then fs-cheb clenshaw (9 sites), fs-lattice (4), fs-ivl (4, CONTRACT CHECK FIRST). The dispatcher module crates/fs-sparse/src/fma/mod.rs is already registered and has the pattern to copy. Releasing for a fresh window.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: SLICE 2 COMPLETE + SPOT MEASUREMENT DELIVERED (f2de143). All six remaining fs-sparse SpMV-class sites capsuled via a macro dispatcher pair in the registered fma module: Bsr::spmv, Sell::spmv, Sell::spmv_chunked, the SELL sharded shard body, CsrCompact::spmv, and the CsrCompact parallel shard - the two PARALLEL sites dispatch inside their spawned closures (closures are separate codegen units; one detection per shard). MEASUREMENT (ts2 5975WX, wsbf roofline lane, 4M rows / 32M nnz): serial attainment 0.848 of STREAM, sharded 0.990 - 39.4 of 39.8 GB/s all-core; SpMV is at its bandwidth roof on x86 with nothing left on the table. TRIAGE for the tail: fs-cheb (9 sites: clenshaw, dense matmuls) and fs-lattice (4) have NO perf lanes - per the measured-claims discipline the capsule treatment is mechanical but unmeasurable there; recommend either (a) documenting the class in their CONTRACT no-claims until a lane exists, or (b) lane-first then capsule. fs-ivl (4) stays contract-gated (fused rounding vs interval-width semantics - verify before touching). ops.rs SpGEMM whole-fn wraps remain available (alloc-heavy, lowest priority). Releasing - the sweep's measured core is done.

title: fs-simd NEON capsule exceeds the 300-line unsafe-capsule cap (410 lines) — split safe parts out
description: cargo run -p xtask -- check-all currently reports: capsule crates/fs-simd/src/neon/mod.rs is 410 lines; capsules must stay under 300 (AGENTS.md unsafe policy, enforced by check-unsafe). The GEMM perf slices (xdgf mk8x4, 9ekv btile4x4, xlvx additions) grew the capsule past the cap. Fix: move safe scaffolding (dispatch structs, doc text, scalar twins, test helpers) out from behind the unsafe facade, or split into two registered capsules each <300 lines with their own SAFETY.md sections. Until fixed, check-all is red for every agent. Found while wiring check-powi into check-all (bead 4xnt close).
close_reason: NEON capsule split under the cap - LANDED, check-all GREEN again (0 violations, 125 crates, all seven checks). The 410-line capsule split into three registered capsules, each with its own SAFETY.md: neon/mod.rs (elementwise axpy/scale/mul_elem/fma3/dot/sum, 145 lines), neon/gemm/mod.rs (mk8x4_f64 + btile4x4_f64, 197 lines), neon/fft/mod.rs (r4qrun_f64, 102 lines). Pure file move: bitwise contracts, dispatch table, and public neon:: paths unchanged (re-exports); tier-equivalence battery green; fs-la batched golden + fs-fft suites green; clippy clean. SAFETY.md content split accordingly (base file points at the new capsule SAFETY files; gemm/fft files carry the pointer-arithmetic proofs verbatim). unsafe-capsules.json gained the two entries - and I initially re-broke its line-based format with a pretty-print despite the note in the file WARNING about exactly that (7-violation incident, 2026-07-09); caught by check-unsafe before commit and rewritten one-capsule-per-line. Registry format note stands validated.

title: Five x86-red suites at HEAD ee4cf11 caught by the runtime sweep lane
description: The yuyy lane's first full-workspace x86 run (ts1, clean committed tree at ee4cf11, sealed constellation at the advanced pins, 463 suites green) caught five red suites. Retained log with full panics: the yuyy bead comments name the path. (1) fs-simd --lib gemm_geometry_overflow_is_refused_before_dispatch: panics inside x86/gemm.rs:61 with 'mk8x4 panel length mismatch' - the x86 facade's refusal message/order differs from what the (scalar-twin-shaped) test expects; ARM never routes through x86/gemm.rs, so this is a textbook ARM-invisible expectation gap - near-certainly genuine x86-only. (2) fs-checker epi_e2e:556: substituted source claim fails with content-address-mismatch instead of exact-subject-authentication refusal. (3) fs-ir planner pl_008b: ResourceLimit polynomial_cell_work requested 30000000 > limit 16000000. (4) fs-ledger color_battery col_012: Anchoring admitted_source assertion. (5) fs-surrogate ladder (see retained log). CAVEAT for (2)-(5): an ARM control at the SAME head is needed to separate x86-only divergence from red-at-HEAD fleet churn (the local ARM control attempt died on workbench dirt); whoever takes this should run the five suites on a CLEAN ARM checkout at ee4cf11 first, then fix per class.
close_reason: ATTRIBUTION COMPLETE via the clean-ARM control (all five surfaces clean locally, control run at HEAD 130efb4). Verdict: exactly ONE genuine x86-only defect - fs-simd btile4x4pf32 facade raw-multiply overflow vs checked bounds, FIXED + verified natively on ts2 (b556d54). The other four are RED-AT-HEAD ON BOTH ISAs - fleet in-flight regressions visible to any full-suite run, not ISA divergence: fs-checker epi_e2e_battery (substituted-source authentication; the epistemics lane's active surface), fs-ir planner pl_008b w/ ladder-planner feature (polynomial_cell_work 30M > 16M cap; the oaxj MMS-admission lane's surface - their cap-unification work moved the limit under the test), fs-ledger col_012 (Anchoring admitted_source; the 6xgf canonical-color lane's surface), fs-surrogate la_001 renamed rb_estimators_empirically_contain_fixture_errors (the knh1.4.x lane's surface). All four owning lanes are ACTIVELY in-progress on exactly those files - the regressions are their mid-iteration churn; each lane's own battery will gate them before landing, and the nightly sweep re-catches anything that survives. META-LESSON recorded: the sweep's first run produced 7 findings total (2 tree-hygiene, 1 x86-only, 4 red-at-HEAD) - the runtime lane's value split is broader than the ISA class it was built for; it is also the fleet's only whole-workspace regression net

title: fs-la batched GEMM 8x8 entry-tile geometry: raise FMA:load intensity toward the 0.60-of-peak aspiration (golden-bump path)
description: SPLIT from 9ekv at its target decision, PRESERVING the original ambition (nothing dropped — re-homed). The recorded batched-lane ceiling analysis (9ekv comments, both-ISA data): the 4x4 entry-tile kernels have FMA:load ratio ~2.0, which saturates neither the M4's nor the Threadrippers' FMA pipes at this arithmetic intensity; lanes are independent matrices so no operand broadcast exists to raise intensity within 4x4. Measured both-ISA state at re-scope time: x86 ts2 batch-gemm k=48 7.31 GFLOP/s / batch-lu k=32 6.99 (attainment 0.28-0.33), M4 similar band — an order of magnitude above the pre-capsule collapse (0.5 GFLOP/s libm hole), floor-gated and environment-valid, but structurally short of 0.60 of compute peak. THIS BEAD: 8x8 entry tiles (doubles FMA:load intensity) or an equivalent register-blocking redesign. CONSTRAINTS: changes bits -> this is a GOLDEN BUMP path (golden-bump protocol: committed tree, both modes, both ISAs, semantic justification, golden-couplings.json same commit — see the obq0 lesson: schedule changes MUST run both ISAs before blessing); measure against the sealed roofline admission (trusted baselines, quality-lane receipts); the 9ekv measurement infrastructure (batched perf lane, fmacc/btile capsules, ledgered peak models) is the harness to reuse. ACCEPTANCE: batched small-dense >=0.60 of compute peak per size class on both reference ISAs under admitted rows, OR a recorded ceiling analysis showing the honest limit of the 8x8 geometry with the measured band re-pinned — the target is falsifiable either way, per the fz2 epic's re-measured-never-vibes doctrine.
close_reason: ACCEPTANCE PATH (B) COMPLETE ON BOTH ISAs. The 8x8 entry-tile aspiration is answered by the register-budget theorem (recorded at lane spec + gate, commits 0df362e/17a54d4): lane-independent batched tiles need ti*tj accumulator vectors live across the whole reduction, so 8x8 = 64 accumulators is infeasible by 2x on NEON (R=32) and 4x on AVX2 (R=16); feasible intensity maxima 4x5=2.22 / 3x3=1.5 project measured attainment to <=~0.37; 0.60-of-compute-peak is structurally unreachable for this algorithm class (operand-sharing = different algorithm + bit contract, out of scope). Band re-pinned with MEASURED both-ISA data: x86 5975WX 0.28-0.33, M4 0.21-0.31 (first quiet window; the bands do NOT match - M4 also compute-roofs k=8/12 where x86 bandwidth-roofs them). Final pin 0.20 (BATCH_GEMM_COMPUTE_TARGET, one const): clears every row ever measured on either ISA, falsifiable UPWARD via the recorded 4x5/3x3 capsule experiments (+11-13% predicted). Gate verdicts: ts2 target_met=true floor_met=true environment_valid=true; M4 target_met=true floor_met=true environment_valid=true (17a54d4). No golden bump was needed - acceptance path (b) changes no bits. Falsifiable either way, per the fz2 doctrine.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (CloudyFinch — the 9ekv ceiling analysis author, now making it rigorous). REGISTER-BUDGET THEOREM for lane-independent batched tiles: lanes are independent matrices (no operand broadcast — the 9ekv recorded fact), so a ti x tj entry tile needs ti*tj accumulator VECTORS live across the whole l-loop plus ti+tj operand vectors per step; per-register FMA:load intensity = ti*tj/(ti+tj) subject to ti*tj + ti + tj <= R (the vector register file). NEON R=32: 4x4 = 24 regs, ratio 2.0 (current); best feasible = 4x5 = 29 regs, ratio 2.22 (+11%); 5x5 = 35 SPILLS; 8x8 = 64 accs alone — infeasible by 2x. AVX2 R=16: current ti-half 2x4 = 14 regs, ratio 1.33; best feasible 3x3 = 15 regs, ratio 1.5 (+13%); 8x8 infeasible by 4x. CONSEQUENCE: the 8x8 geometry named by this bead cannot exist in registers on either reference ISA — register-blocking it back down (proven by the x86 ti-half measurement: spills cost more than the intensity gains) reproduces todays ratios. The attainment ceiling scales with the ratio: measured 0.28-0.33 at ratio 1.33-2.0 projects to at most ~0.37 at the feasible maxima — 0.60 of compute peak is UNREACHABLE for lane-independent batched small-dense on NEON-128 and AVX2, full stop; only operand-sharing formulations (interleaving lanes WITHIN one matrix = a different algorithm and bit contract) could pass it. EXECUTING acceptance path (b): re-pin the lane target to the derived honest band (0.30 compute-peak, floor 0.08 unchanged) with this argument in the lane docs, verify target_met=true under admitted rows on ts2 + M4 floors, close. The optional 4x5/3x3 +11-13% experiments are recorded as available follow-ups with predicted gains — falsifiable, per the doctrine.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: ACCEPTANCE PATH (B) EXECUTED (0df362e). The register-budget theorem is recorded at the lane spec and gate: 8x8 needs 64 live accumulator vectors - infeasible by 2x on NEON (R=32) and 4x on AVX2 (R=16); feasible intensity maxima are 4x5=2.22 (NEON) and 3x3=1.5 (AVX2); measured 0.28-0.33 projects to <=~0.37 at those maxima; 0.60 compute-peak is structurally unreachable for lane-independent batched tiles (operand-sharing = different algorithm + bit contract, out of scope). Target re-pinned 0.60 -> 0.25, applied ONLY to compute-roofed size classes (bandwidth-roofed small-k answers to the binding-roof floor - they cannot meet any compute fraction by definition). VERIFIED x86 ts2 quiet/admitted: target_met=true floor_met=true environment_valid=true. RESIDUAL: M4 target confirmation needs a quiet window (ambient load 37-53 during both attempts - floor green on retry, target unjudgeable; the established 27d3/cwjn deferral pattern). FALSIFIABLE UPWARD: the 4x5 NEON / 3x3 AVX2 capsule experiments (+11-13% predicted) remain available - if built and measured above 0.25+margin, raise the pin. Releasing with only the M4 quiet-window run outstanding.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: M4 RESIDUAL RESOLVED (17a54d4). First workable quiet window (load 10-14, disk healed) produced the missing M4 compute-roofed band: k=8:0.214, k=12:0.241, k=16:0.297, k=24:0.312, k=32:0.214, k=48:0.268 - i.e. 0.21-0.31, LOWER than the x86 band (0.28-0.33) and straddling the 0.25 pin, which had assumed the bands matched. Also note the M4 classifies k=8/12 as compute-roofed where the 5975WX classified them bandwidth-roofed (different machine balance). Target re-pinned 0.25 -> 0.20 (single const BATCH_GEMM_COMPUTE_TARGET): clears every row ever measured on either ISA with margin, falsifiable UPWARD by the recorded 4x5/3x3 experiments (+11-13% predicted). Gate verdict on M4: target_met=true, floor_met=true, environment_valid=true. x86 cleared 0.25 a fortiori. Both ISAs now green on target AND floor - no residuals remain on this bead.

title: fs-ad adjoint integrations: FrankenTorch tape bridge, revolve ledger spill, binomial revolve, matrix-free IFT
description:
SPLIT from frankensim-epic-bedrock-6ys.17 (adjoint infra v1), which landed in fs-ad: ift module (dense column-wise Jacobians via Dual64<1>, adjoint solve via fs_la Lu::solve_transpose, IftReport with primal+adjoint residuals, typed singular-Jacobian error; tested vs re-solve FD and vs differentiate-through-converged-Newton), revolve module (binary treeverse; peak snapshots <= ceil(log2 L)+1 and fwd re-evals <= L*ceil(log2 L) ASSERTED via RevolveStats; HEADLINE: checkpointed adjoint BITWISE == full-storage adjoint - determinism makes checkpointing invisible to the numbers, tested), gradcheck module (dual-vs-central-FD gate primitive with JSON-line report; kills the value()/from_f64 round-trip bug class, tested on a seeded specimen - huq.4's CI should wire gradcheck into merge gates, primitive is ready).

THIS BEAD: (1) FrankenTorch tape bridge (reverse-mode for large N) - needs the ft-* constellation wiring decision (first ft- dep in the workspace; CONSTELLATION_PREFIXES already allows it); design: fs-ad Real values lifted to ft tensors on a tape, vjp callback surface matching revolve's reverse(i, state, bar) signature so checkpointing composes with taped segments. (2) Revolve snapshot LEDGER SPILL: budget exceeded -> spill oldest snapshots to fs-ledger (fsqlite) instead of refusing; coordinate API with fs-ledger owner (other agent); keep the structured-panic path as the no-spill default. (3) Binomially-optimal Griewank-Walther schedule (beta(s,r) binomial split) replacing bisection for FIXED small budgets - keep the bitwise==full-storage invariant test as the acceptance harness; recomputation counts must match the theoretical binomial bound. (4) Matrix-free IFT for large N: JVP-only dF/du access + Krylov adjoint solve (needs solver-stack bead flux-tfz.10; coordinate). Key files: crates/fs-ad/src/{ift,revolve,gradcheck}.rs, CONTRACT.md sections in force.
close_reason: fs-ad adjoint integrations shipped: (1) FrankenTorch scalar-tape reverse bridge behind feature torch-bridge (first ft-* constellation dep; TapeReal implements full Real; reverse_gradient + taped_vjp compose with revolve checkpointing — all tolerance-gated vs forward duals, determinism class declared honestly); (2) binomially-optimal Griewank-Walther revolve schedule with EQUALITY gate on the theorem count r*L-beta(s+1,r-1) and bitwise==full-storage across budgets 1..8; (3) SnapshotStore spill seam + spilling sweep, bitwise at RAM budget 2 via byte-exact store contract (fsqlite adapter handed to fs-ledger owner via bead comment); (4) matrix-free tangent IFT with caller-supplied Krylov solve and measured tangent residuals. 30/30 tests with bridge, 26/26 default; clippy 0 both feature sets; ubs 0 critical; xtask layers+dep-policy clean incl. the new ft- wiring.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: COMPLETE — all four items shipped in fs-ad (30/30 tests w/ torch-bridge, 26/26 default). (1) FrankenTorch tape bridge [feature torch-bridge, workspace's FIRST ft-* constellation dep: ft-autograd + ft-core path deps, xtask dependency-policy green]: TapeReal Copy-handle onto a thread-local ft scalar Tape implements the FULL Real surface in Strict mode — kernels generic over Real get reverse mode unchanged; reverse_gradient (one backward, any N; N=300 vs analytic 1e-12) + taped_vjp shaped exactly as revolve's reverse(i,state,bar) (composition gated: checkpointed sweep with taped vjp steps == forward duals, rel 1e-11); determinism class DECLARED: ft Strict != fs-math det, tolerance-gated never bitwise, mul_add composed/unfused on tape. (2) Binomial revolve: checkpointed_adjoint_binomial hits the Griewank-Walther optimum r*L - beta(s+1,r-1) EXACTLY (equality-gated, budgets 1..8), bitwise == full storage, beats treeverse at equal RAM (255 vs 356 @ L=100); optimal split via O(r) beta-breakpoint scan of the piecewise-linear DP cost — the closed-form 'lhat = beta(s,r-1)' split was MEASURED 10 re-evals suboptimal at s=2 (documented rejection). (3) Snapshot spill: SnapshotStore trait (byte-exact round-trip contract) + checkpointed_adjoint_spilling — RAM budget 2 on a 100-step chain stays BITWISE (92 spills/163 restores, store drained); no-spill default keeps the structured panic. TO THE FS-LEDGER OWNER: the fsqlite adapter is your L6 side — impl SnapshotStore<S> with byte-exact blobs keyed u64, write-once/read-many/evict-hint; the fs-ad battery's MapStore shows the contract. (4) Matrix-free IFT: ift_gradient_matrix_free — dF/du only APPLIED (one directional-dual pass), caller-supplied solve(apply,b) so fs-ad stays L1/solver-agnostic (fs-solver Krylov plugs in at L3); tangent residual MEASURED fresh, never trusted; validated vs dense IFT (2-sys 1e-9, N=50 tridiag 1e-7). Matrix-free ADJOINT (J^T v over vector residuals) = recorded no-claim pending the tensor-tape vector bridge. Lanes: clippy -D warnings 0 both feature sets, fmt, ubs 0 critical, xtask clean for fs-ad.

title: Certify fftnd floor gate in a quiet m4 window ([1024,1024] already clears 0.40 on ts2 parked-crew lane)
description: The tkr7 parked-crew lane recovered N-D FFT attainment on ts2 (5995WX, quiet, load 4/128): [256,256] 0.011->0.0696, [1024,1024] 0.12->0.4598 (CLEARS the 0.40 BindingRoof floor, within_band, dispersion 0.07), [128,128,64] 0.3098. The [1024,1024] row is one quiet-window m4 measurement away from a two-machine floored gate: the m4 run during swarm activity read attainment 0.028-0.039 with dispersion 0.24-0.34 (healthy dispersion on ts2: 0.02-0.07) — pure host-load noise, not evidence (perf-certification-needs-quiet-host). title: fs-uq seismic + anytime-valid stack: Kanai-Tajimi/CQC/IDA fragility, e-process stopping, CVaR risk functionals, adaptive MLMC
description: SPLIT from tfz.25 (fs-uq slice 1 landed: KL random fields w/ captured-variance evidence, PCE regression w/ closed-form gates, QMC-vs-MC measured >3x, MLMC w/ audited telescoping + >3x cost win). THIS bead = the remaining tfz.25 scope, gated on other lanes now in flight: (a) SEISMIC (the frame flagship's stack): stochastic ground motion via fs-scenario Kanai-Tajimi-class spectra + recorded suites; RESPONSE-SPECTRUM CQC fast path (modal combination for the quick screen - consumes fs-la eigen modal machinery); nonlinear time-history slow path through fs-solid-advanced fiber frames (tfz.14, in flight by the other agent); FRAGILITY via incremental dynamic analysis - P(exceedance) curves WITH confidence machinery. (b) ANYTIME-VALID STOPPING (Bet 5): every stochastic estimate under fs-eproc e-process confidence sequences - sample until the CS is tight enough FOR THE DECISION AT HAND then stop validly automatically (the 15.2 step-4 mechanic: the study stops itself when the fragility estimate is decision-grade). (c) CVaR/quantile risk functionals feeding ASCENT robust formulations (7tv.16 multi-objective/DRO is the consumer - coordinate). (d) ADAPTIVE MLMC: level addition driven by bias estimates (the slice-1 ladder is caller-fixed); weak/strong convergence-rate estimation from level statistics; tie level design to actual solver ladders (fs-feec/fs-solver refinement hierarchies). Slice-1 APIs to build on: KlExpansion (qmc_germs hybrid Sobol+Philox), fit_pce, mlmc_estimate (sampler contract: level correction driven by ONE germ).
close_reason: fs-uq slice 2 shipped (seismic + anytime-valid stack, additive modules matching slice-1 style — ungated committed scope): (1) SEISMIC (the frame flagship's UQ lane): KanaiTajimi PSD + spectral-representation ground-motion synthesis with COUNTER-BASED phases — bit-replayable per seed (uq-001, G5); sdof_peak Newmark response ordinates with the spectrum decaying above the ground band; CQC with Der Kiureghian coefficients — collapses to SRSS within 0.01% for well-separated modes and EXCEEDS it 34% for closely-spaced same-sign modes, the correlation the SRSS shortcut misses (uq-002); bilinear-hysteretic SDOF + ida_fragility: monotone exceedance curves sweeping the transition over a 24-motion suite (uq-003); (2) ANYTIME-VALID STOPPING (Bet 5, uq-004 — certify-the-certifiers): estimate_probability_anytime wraps fs-eproc's Gaussian-mixture CS (sub-Gaussian sigma=1/2 for bounded outcomes) and stops the moment the radius meets the decision's half-width — 60 replications with CS-DECIDED stopping: miss rate 0.000 at nominal 0.05 (optional stopping safe by construction), and a 2.5x looser decision stops 7x sooner (421 -> 59 samples) — the study stops itself when the estimate is decision-grade, the 15.2 step-4 mechanic; DEVELOPMENT LESSON in the CONTRACT: fs-eproc's interval() returns (CENTER, RADIUS) — misreading it as (lo, hi) produced 58% miss rates before the semantics were pinned; (3) CVaR at the UQ seam (matches the analytic tail mean; fs-robust hosts the ASCENT twin — the 7tv.16 consumer coordination noted); (4) ADAPTIVE MLMC (uq-005): levels added while the extrapolated bias |mean_L|/(2^alpha - 1) exceeds tol/2; weak/strong rates FITTED from level statistics — on the known-rate synthetic (alpha=2, beta=3) it recovers 1.98/2.98, adds levels to 6, lands within 0.7% of the telescoped limit, and stops with the bias criterion held at 3.7e-4. Evidence: 5 conformance tests green; fmt clean; clippy lane shows zero fs-uq findings (the 5 reported errors are another agent's in-flight fs-ascent code, bead ijil); check-all: fs-uq compliant; CONTRACT extended (no-claims: bilinear SDOF until tfz.14's fiber frames, synthetic motions until fs-scenario ingestion, bounded-outcome CS, geometric-decay rate fits).

title: Unify immutable canonical MMS class admission across fs-verify and fs-ir
description: The hardened verifier admits at most six coefficients and checks u(1)=0 by outward interval evaluation, while fs-ir admits 4096 coefficients and requires bit-exact point evaluation, so the planner can pay for solves the verifier must refuse and rejects valid cancelling polynomials such as [0,-0.3,1.3,-1]. MmsProblem/Poly fields are also publicly mutable; f/big_f can drift from u, empty/nonfinite/trailing signed-zero representations are noncanonical, and big_f is called exact despite rounded coefficient division. Define one lower-layer checked immutable problem-class value with canonical signed-zero/trailing-zero rules, shared caps and endpoint admission, derived f/big_f accessors, stable identity/canonical bytes, and fallible construction. Migrate fs-verify, fs-ir, fs-adjoint, flywheel, planner/cache keys, and tests. Adding/mutating any semantic field must change identity; no upper-layer dependency from fs-verify.

comments.author: codex-audit
comments.text: Audit follow-up: working tree now removes one live mismatch by defining fs-ir::MAX_FAMILY_COEFFICIENTS from fs_verify's six-coefficient exactness envelope. This cap is load-bearing: the equilibrated verifier integrates (c-F-slope)^2 with five-point Gauss, requiring degree(u)<=5. Planner polynomial-cell admission now counts all five quadrature evaluations, and legacy degree-10 fixtures were replaced with the admissible x(1-x)(0.2+x^3) family. The bead remains P0/open for the deeper immutable/canonical shared MMS type, endpoint-rule unification, stable identity, and any future higher-degree quadrature path; its original description's live 4096-vs-6 mismatch is superseded by this working-tree alignment.

comments.author: codex-audit
comments.text: Additional partial fix in the reviewed tree: fs-ir and fs-verify now share the six-coefficient class cap, all planner family/scaling admission uses fs_verify::Poly::is_exactly_zero_at_one, and that predicate sums stored binary64 coefficients exactly with a fixed 34-limb positive/negative superaccumulator rather than Horner rounding or interval inclusion of zero. Hidden-residue, true-cancellation, and extreme-exponent regressions were added and focused compile/clippy plus two targeted runtimes passed. The bead remains P0/open because MmsProblem/Poly are still mutable public structures without one lower-layer canonical encoding/stable identity and f/big_f derivation owner.

Both findings FIXED with regressions:

title: fs-cheb golden hash diverges across ISAs at HEAD: G5 bit-determinism break (radix-8 FFT attribution blessed aarch64-only)
description: FOUND by the first full x86 workspace test run (ts1, quiet, clone at 3ff3765, debug profile): cheb_golden_hash FAILS on x86_64 with 0xeea04b0a01de46cd vs the committed golden 0x22e7ea2158c9e587 — while the SAME test at the same tree state PASSES on the M4 (aarch64 produces the committed value). This is a real cross-ISA bit divergence, not a stale golden: the two ISAs disagree with EACH OTHER. The golden was re-blessed in e7bad5e 'fix(determinism): attribute Chebyshev bits to radix-8 FFT semantics' — evidently blessed on aarch64 without an x86 quadrant run (golden-bump protocol requires both ISAs; see the rand_nla mis-pin lesson). SUSPECT SURFACE: whatever fs-cheb now consumes from the radix-8 FFT path — twiddle generation, stage ordering, or the new frontier work (NEON transpose capsule is aarch64-only; if any engaged path is not bit-neutral on x86 the divergence follows). The libm/powi hazard class from the determinism playbook also fits (mul_add lowering differs on baseline x86). QUADRANT STATE: aarch64-debug PASS, x86-debug FAIL (hash above, full log ts1:/tmp/ws_test_x86.log), release quadrants UNMEASURED. NEXT per playbook: run both release quadrants; stage-wise bit bisection of the cheb transform vs the radix-8 stages on both machines (dump first-divergent-stage); check det:: routing of the twiddles; then either fix the non-neutral path or revert the golden to a both-ISA value with golden-couplings.json in the same commit. The x86 workspace run also completed 36+ green suites before this — cheb is the only divergence surfaced so far (run still finishing).
close_reason: RESOLVED AT HEAD and now verified across every quadrant; the gap was the missing verification, not a live divergence. DIAGNOSIS: the divergence found at 3ff3765 (aarch64 producing the committed 0x22e7ea2158c9e587 while x86 produced 0xeea04b0a01de46cd) was PER-ISA SCHEDULE DISAGREEMENT — not arithmetic non-determinism: fs-fft's DCT substrate moved to the radix-8/4/2 Stockham schedule with engagement differing by ISA at that tree state, so the two platforms ran different butterfly/twiddle orders (both internally deterministic — the bad case where each ISA is self-consistent and they silently disagree). e7bad5e/535aa839 unified the schedule and re-blessed the golden to 0xeea0_4b0a_01de_46cd, which BOTH ISAs now produce — but the re-bless recorded no x86 quadrant run, which is exactly what this bead caught (the rand_nla mis-pin lesson repeating). VERIFICATION NOW COMPLETE (this bead's work): fs-cheb full suite green at HEAD on aarch64 M4 Pro (debug AND release), x86-64 ts1 5975WX (debug AND release), and x86-64 ts2 5995WX (release) — five machine-mode quadrant runs, all producing the committed value; the G5 cross-ISA bit-determinism law HOLDS at HEAD. The fs-cheb:aggregate row in golden-couplings.json now carries the completed quadrant evidence and the divergence history (fbfb899), so the next fresh-eyes sweep sees a verified pin rather than an aarch64-only bless. Incidental fix en route: ts2's untracked baseline-store copy blocked pulls after the store was committed — resolved via stash -u (checksums identical). PROCESS NOTE for golden discipline (y4pt's territory, no new bead needed): a re-bless that changes which ALGORITHM SCHEDULE engages must run both ISAs before landing — the registry's justification field is the right place to prove it, and it now does for this row. xtask check-all 0 violations.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: FOUR-QUADRANT TABLE COMPLETE (CloudyFinch — evidence only, your claim untouched): aarch64-debug PASS, aarch64-release PASS, x86-debug FAIL (0xeea04b0a01de46cd), x86-release PASS. The divergence is X86-DEBUG-ONLY, which reframes the incident: not an aarch64-only blessing — the golden encodes FUSED-multiply-add semantics that three quadrants produce and x86-debug does not. CONTRACTION HYPOTHESIS (fits all four cells + the e7bad5e radix-8 attribution): somewhere in the newly-attributed radix-8 path there is a bare a*b + c (NOT explicit mul_add) inside or downstream of an fma-capable compilation context. aarch64 has fmadd in its BASELINE target features, so LLVM contracts at every opt level -> fused bits both profiles. x86-release reaches fused bits via the avx2+fma target_feature capsule body (LLVM contracts inside such bodies — measured during the a55x/global-fma investigation, see .cargo/config.toml's documented rejection). x86-DEBUG at opt-level 0 does not contract (and baseline x86 lacks the instruction outside the capsule) -> two-rounding bits -> divergence. DOCTRINE FIX (if the hypothesis holds): replace the bare a*b+c with explicit f64::mul_add in the shared kernel (bit-neutral in ALL quadrants: libm fma == vfmadd == fmadd), rather than re-blessing any golden — this is the same class the check-powi lint covers for powi; a check-contraction sweep of fma-feature bodies may be the permanent lint. FALSIFIER: objdump the debug-vs-release x86 cheb/fft hot path for vfmadd presence, or stage-dump first-divergent-value on ts1 (clone ready, failing binary at target/debug; full log /tmp/ws_test_x86.log). Happy to run any both-machine bisection legs you want — I hold M4+ts1 sessions.

title: fs-robust violates fragility ordering and accepts invalid dominance costs
description: Fresh-eyes trace of fs-robust found two public contract defects. fragility_curve documents and relies on points being in increasing intensity order, but preserves arbitrary caller order, so its advertised monotonic curve invariant is false. dominated_by_nominal silently treats NaN/Inf as an ordinary not-dominated result, which can suppress the Proposal-F kill criterion. Sort finite intensities deterministically before evaluating the empirical CDF; make dominance validation fallible and reject non-finite costs; update callers/contracts and add unsorted/NaN/Inf regressions.

title: WS3: render perf lane — Mray/s release rows under sealed admission, both ISAs
description: WHY (plan sec 14.1: 80/120 Mray/s primary-ray targets; reality check: Mray/s only ever measured in DEBUG builds — the numbers are meaningless): perf claims need the same honesty machinery every other kernel now has. WHAT: an admitted perf lane (FRANKENSIM_BASELINE_STORE pattern, both ISAs via committed stores) measuring primary-ray throughput for the un-gated sphere tracer and BVH on canonical fixtures (F-rep turntable frame per plan sec 16.1 P1 exit), release profile only, with the anti-collapse floor set from first honest measurement and the plan target REPORTED (the fz2 re-measure doctrine, cwjn/9ekv precedent). Include per-ray work model in the lane header so attainment is against the honest axis (compute-bound, not bandwidth). ACCEPTANCE: citable rows both ISAs ledgered on the bead; floor wired; debug-timing paths deleted from any claim surface (docs updated). DEPENDS on the un-gating bead.

title: Stabilize session ledger sink identity and bound scoped flush state
description: Post-e6888a5/f9b9f2c adversarial review found pointer/path-based sink identity can change on handle moves or alias/reuse a different ledger, repeated level-3 pressure overwrites an outstanding pause request, submission receipt hashes omit immutable ledger_scope, and global flush scans/allocations/I/O remain unbounded under the governor lock. Introduce stable ledger instance/database identity, coalesced or refused pending pause semantics, scope-bound receipt vNext, hard session/idempotency/event/flush batch limits with transactional cursor behavior, and focused move/reopen/alias/retry/storm tests.

comments.author: GoldenBrook
comments.text: Implementation started: reserved fs-ledger identity and fs-session authority files. Scope includes move-stable/persistent LedgerInstanceId, opaque governor-bound ScopeFlushPermit, bounded per-scope state and chunked flush with unlocked DB I/O, receipt v2 scope binding, nonblocking duplicate submissions, repeated-pause refusal, evidence/ordinal/collection limits, contracts, and exact-boundary/retry tests. No staging or commit; closure remains for independent review.

title: Variable-exponent f64::powi is build-mode-dependent: purge from deterministic paths, add fs-math powi_det + lint
description:
CONFIRMED with a minimized counterexample: 0.7f64.powi(k) differs by 1 ulp between debug and release builds from k=4 upward (0.7^4 = 0x3fcebb98c7e2823e debug vs ...3f release, cargo-built, pinned nightly 2026-07-05, aarch64). Root cause: std powi precision is documented as unspecified and its lowering (runtime exponentiation-by-squaring vs LLVM const-folding) depends on optimization level. Exponents <=3 are safe (squaring and sequential rounding orders coincide); divergence starts at 4.

This broke the fs-la rand_nla golden (debug observed 0x3e928bac8cf9fd48, release 0xf3dcb63be63f8ab9) and provoked an unjustified re-pin. The fixture is now fixed with a sequential product chain, but the same hazard is LATENT IN PRODUCTION PATHS:
- fs-feec/src/highorder/vecfam.rs (~10 sites): high-order basis monomials xi.powi(order) — G5 bit-stability hazard for r>=4 FEEC spaces across build modes/const-folding contexts.
- fs-ad/src/dual.rs:263-264: powi(n), powi(n-1) in dual-number pow rule.
- fs-dfo/src/cma.rs:245: powi(2*generations) — exponents in the thousands; maximal exposure.
- fs-la/tests/factor_battery.rs:50 fixture (test-only).

Fix: (1) fs-math gains a deterministic integer power (fixed evaluation order, conformance-tested, with a cross-build-mode golden); (2) migrate the sites above, bumping any affected goldens with justification; (3) a lint/xtask check forbids std powi with non-literal exponent or literal >3 outside fast-mode/no-claim paths (fits the existing ULP-ledger lint direction in wf9.14/r6r5).

Acceptance: powi_det golden identical across debug/release and both reference ISAs; migrated sites' batteries green; seeded violation caught by the lint. Found while executing the failure-compounding protocol (frankensim-epic-gauntlet-6nb.9) on the rand_nla golden break.
close_reason: DONE with evidence. (1) fs_math::det::powi landed (swept as 8c6db27): LSB-first pinned-order binary exponentiation; a fellow agent refined it in-tree with underflow recovery (re-evaluates from the reciprocal base so extreme negative exponents recover subnormal results instead of collapsing through 1/inf). POWI_GOLDEN 0xe971352e4c0a5f29 verified in ALL FOUR QUADRANTS: aarch64(M4)+x86_64(trj) x debug+release (trj:/data/tmp/powi_verify, 2026-07-10). (2) All inventoried sites migrated: fs-feec vecfam (10 sites via mpow helper — high-order goldens survived unchanged in debug, confirming det::powi==__powidf2==debug-bits design), fs-ad Real-for-f64 chokepoint (+ fellow agent's i32::MIN dual rule n*x^n/x), fs-dfo cma powi(2*gens), fs-opt eval (fellow agent extended to full det::* libm migration), fs-qty powi_pinned private copy (UTIL may not dep L0 — documented), fs-regime pi+scaling (+ fellow agent's ExponentOutOfRange refusal for >i32 exact exponents), fs-render planck (+ dep fix 300a179), fs-verify interval test (const-base powi(40) was the exact release const-fold case), fs-rand qmc, fs-la factor_battery. det-ok annotations: fs-img film, fs-ivl predicates x2 (base-2 exact) + taylor x3 (Interval::powi — already a pinned outward-rounded sequential product; my initial migration attempt there was wrong and the type system caught it), fs-marquee study, fs-math payne x2 (base-2 exact). Also fixed pre-existing release-only failure in fs-math pow_budget_and_specials (const-foldable literal base made the std-powi oracle build-mode-dependent; black_box pins it to runtime __powidf2). (3) xtask check-powi wired into check-all: multi-arg powi calls skipped (builder methods), literals -3..=3 allowed (lowerings coincide through cube), det-ok escape same/preceding line, fs-wasm excluded (demo tier), self-tests seed violations; 0 violations across 125 crates at close. REMAINING (tracked, not blocking): fs-mesh/src/hexdom.rs powi(4) is in xtask POWI_PENDING — file owned by active hexdom work; owner should migrate to det::powi and delete the pending entry. Verification matrix: 13 crates x {debug,release} green; clippy -D warnings green on touched crates; fmt clean; ubs no findings on changed lines.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (CloudyFinch session). Plan and file reservations, in order: (1) fs-math/src — add powi_det mirroring compiler-rt __powidf2 LSB-first square-and-multiply order; this is the exact order debug-mode std powi already lowers to, so debug-recorded goldens should survive migration untouched. (2) Migrate variable-exponent sites: fs-feec/src/highorder/vecfam.rs, fs-ad/src/dual.rs (or the Real trait f64 impl if that is the chokepoint), fs-dfo/src/cma.rs, fs-la/tests/factor_battery.rs, plus anything a fresh sweep finds. (3) xtask check-powi lint (literals -3..=3 allowed, det-ok escape comment). Please avoid editing these files until I close or release; will verify affected batteries in BOTH build modes.

comments.text: Follow-through complete: the last deferred site (fs-mesh hexdom.rs frame_samples powi(4)) is migrated to det::powi (bit-neutral — literal-4 lowerings were already (x²)²; fs-mesh hexdom battery green in debug and release with frontier-hexmesh). The POWI_PENDING exception mechanism was removed by the tests-scan extension agent since nothing is pending anymore. check-powi now covers src AND tests dirs across the workspace with ZERO violations and ZERO exceptions; check-all is fully green (7 lanes, 0 violations) — the first all-green check-all since the lint family landed. The checker now scans sources, tests, examples/benches and fs-wasm, catches whitespace/UFCS forms, and reports 0 violations across 125 crates. title: Prove the real dependency receipt through production tune dispatch and staleness
description:
The depgraph receipt parser/build binding and roofline retained-receipt verifier now have extensive unit and synthetic protocol tests. The remaining critical proof is the actual production chain: xtask emits a locked single-root receipt, the selected Cargo invocation supplies it to fs-la build.rs, fs-session uses the resulting build identity in the tune key/dispatch binding, roofline retains the exact receipt artifact, and staleness revalidates it after a fresh process restart. Current production tests inject synthetic receipt authority and therefore cannot catch environment/export/invocation drift between these layers.

Build a DSR-owned end-to-end lane using a real bounded GEMM production invocation and isolated ledger. Capture exact Cargo executable/version/selection, receipt bytes/domain digest, compiled fs-la graph class, session decision, production operation, retained artifact edge, and Fresh verdict. Then mutate unified dependency features, target/features, receipt bytes, export omission, local path package content, and executable identity.

Acceptance: one clean real run reaches Fresh and warm-process dispatch reuses the exact admitted row without resweep; every mutation either produces a different build/tune key or a named fail-closed refusal; no development salt can become citable; logs and artifacts bind one before/after tree snapshot; both reference ISA families execute the lane.

comments.author: codex-audit
comments.text: Fresh-eyes hardening landed in the working tree ahead of this e2e: dependency-receipt path hashing now preflights at most 512 distinct local packages, meters both coherence snapshots under a 4 GiB/2,000,000-entry aggregate budget, and makes per-row package_identity lookup-only; package-count refusal proves zero hash callbacks. Constellation acceptance now requires two identical complete clean+pinned observations, each bracketing status with matching HEAD reads. Three new regressions passed individually; xtask locked no-run and strict clippy passed. This bead remains open for the real production tune-dispatch/Fresh chain described in its acceptance criteria.
