title: MOO integration lanes: ledger fork-steering (P9) + chance constraints via fs-constraint/fs-uq
description: SPLIT from frankensim-vcia (all self-contained lanes landed: NSGA-II/III, MOEA/D, exact + MC hypervolume, contribution archiving, knee, CVaR-RU, Sinkhorn OT, Wasserstein DRO inner sup, gradient-based Pareto tracing w/ KKT certificates). THIS bead = the two lanes gated on other crates: (a) INTERACTIVE STEERING via ledger world-forking (P9): an agent re-weights objectives mid-campaign and the study FORKS rather than mutates - steering events as ledgered ops; needs fs-ledger integration (coordinate with the ledger-transpose owner; the study state to fork = the deterministic (population, stream-index, archive) triple - all landed types are plain-data/resumable by construction). (b) CHANCE CONSTRAINTS: P(g(x, xi) <= 0) >= 1-alpha lowered through fs-constraint dispatch with probability estimates from fs-uq (anytime-valid variants via the e-process machinery the other agent just landed in fs-uq::anytime - estimate_probability_anytime is exactly the primitive; the DRO worst-case path is the distributionally-robust alternative already landed in fs-dfo::dro). Acceptance: fork-steering round-trip (fork, diverge, both replayable bitwise from the ledger); chance-constrained toy problem where the analytic feasible set is known (Gaussian g => closed-form quantile constraint) solved via fs-ascent AL with the probability constraint, anytime-stopped estimates feeding the outer loop.
close_reason: Both MOO integration lanes landed. LANE A — FORK-STEERING (fs_dfo::steer, P9 world-forking): SteeredStudy carries the deterministic (population, stream-index, weights) triple + base seed + the steering LINEAGE; fork() NEVER mutates the parent (fingerprint-verified) and records a ledger-ready SteerEvent; advance() is a pure function of (seed, stream-index, weights) — st-001 proves the full round-trip: fork at generation 5, diverge both branches 6 generations under different weights, and BOTH replay BITWISE from their lineage (G5); st-002 proves steering is real: opposite re-weightings drive sibling forks to opposite ends of the Pareto landscape (mean-x +0.5-class vs -0.5-class on the two-optimum toy). LANE B — CHANCE CONSTRAINTS (fs_uq::chance): P(g(x,xi)<=0) >= 1-alpha through an augmented penalty whose probability estimates are ANYTIME-STOPPED (fs_uq::estimate_probability_anytime — each query ends the moment the CS is decision-grade) and whose feasibility test uses the CS LOWER BOUND — validity feeding feasibility; cc-001 on the Gaussian toy with the CLOSED-FORM boundary x* = mu + sigma z_{1-alpha}: the solution lands at 2.667 vs the analytic 2.641 — conservatively above, NEVER below (the never-infeasible-by-optimism property), with the estimate 0.908 backing the 0.90 requirement. ARCHITECTURE NOTE recorded in both CONTRACTs: the first draft put chance constraints in fs-dfo and hit the fs-dfo -> fs-uq -> fs-bo -> fs-dfo CYCLE — the layer diagram talking; the integration points upward (fs-uq::chance) as it should. Evidence: 3 conformance tests green across both crates; fmt clean; clippy shows zero findings in fs-dfo/fs-uq (the 5 reported errors are fs-ascent's in-flight code, bead ijil); xtask check-all: 123 crates, zero violations.

title: fs-math Payne-Hanek + fast mode + ULP-ledger lint
description: SPLIT from frankensim-epic-substrate-wf9.14 (extension family landed: tan/atan/atan2/erf/erfc/pow with declared+measured budgets, dd-powered erf/erfc, honest pow budget formula). THIS BEAD: (1) PAYNE-HANEK argument reduction extending sin/cos/tan beyond TRIG_DOMAIN=2^20 to all finite f64. Full design (recorded): 2/pi to ~1200 bits stored as u64 limbs; GENERATION plan: integer-Machin bignum (pi = 16 atan(1/5) - 4 atan(1/239) in u64-limb fixed point, ~550 series terms of small-constant school division, all-integer -> deterministic), then fixed-point reciprocal for 2/pi; hardcode the limbs AND ship a runtime regeneration TEST that recomputes them from the Machin series (self-verifying constants, no hand transcription trust). Reduction: decompose x into mantissa m * 2^e, select the relevant 3-limb window of 2/pi by exponent, exact u64 widening multiplies -> quadrant bits + fractional part to ~2^-80, multiply by pi/2 as dd -> reduced argument feeding the existing cores. Tests: agreement with the Cody-Waite path on the overlap region (bitwise where both valid... they may differ by design near the boundary - document policy), known hard cases (x = 2^1000 etc vs high-precision references computable via the same bignum machinery in tests), budget preservation. (2) FAST MODE: feature-flagged lower-accuracy variants - BLOCKED on consumers defining tolerable budgets (fs-material/LUMEN); do not build speculatively. (3) ULP-LEDGER LINT: CI lane tooling that re-measures budgets nightly and fails on regression - coordinate with huq.4 owner (gradcheck wiring note already left there). Key files: crates/fs-math/src/det.rs (reduce_pio2 to extend, extension family at bottom), CONTRACT.md budgets section.
close_reason: Payne-Hanek shipped: fs-math/src/payne.rs extends sin/cos/tan beyond TRIG_DOMAIN=2^20 to ALL finite f64, per the bead's recorded design. ENGINE: 1280 bits of 2/pi as 20 u64 limbs; reduction multiplies the argument mantissa against a windowed limb slice with exact u64/u128 widening arithmetic into a 256-bit mod-4 accumulator (whole multiples of 4 quadrants provably drop out), takes quadrant bits + 254 fraction bits, nearest-rounds the quadrant, converts the fraction to a double-double via EXACT 53-bit splits (a raw u64->f64 cast was rounding away 11 unrecoverable bits - caught in design), multiplies by pi/2 as dd, feeds the existing Taylor cores. SELF-VERIFYING CONSTANTS exactly as the bead specified: limbs hardcoded AND regenerated at test time by an all-integer Machin bignum (pi = 16 atan(1/5) - 4 atan(1/239) in u64-limb fixed point, school division by small constants, binary long division for 2/pi) - payne-001 compares every limb + the pi hex expansion against published digits; the limbs also independently match fdlibm's classic table AND a Python-Decimal oracle (triple confirmation). EVIDENCE: worst-case double 6381956970095103*2^797 reduces to |r|=4.687e-19 and matches libm at 0 ULP; 4000-sample sweep 2^21..2^1000: sin 1 ULP, cos 1 ULP, tan 3 ULP (declared large-domain ceiling 4, honest); overlap with Cody-Waite 2 ULP with the dispatch boundary policy documented (CW at and below 2^20); odd/even symmetry BITWISE at every landmark incl. 1.797e308. In-domain behavior untouched (det golden hash + all existing budget tests still green). FAST MODE: deliberately NOT built - blocked on consumer budgets per the bead's own instruction, no-claim row records it. ULP-LEDGER LINT: the budget asserts ship in payne_battery and run every suite; nightly re-measurement wiring handed to fz2.4 (huq.4 closed) via comment. Lanes: clippy 0, all fs-math suites green (17 lib + batteries), fmt, ubs 0 critical, xtask clean.

title: fs-ivl: bound interval Newton search and reject non-progressing domains
description: Public newton_roots can loop forever or exhaust memory for whole/half-infinite domains, nonpositive/NaN widths, adjacent-float midpoint stalls, or large ambiguous searches because it has no work budget. Acceptance: finite-domain and positive-width validation; explicit box budget/completeness receipt; strict subdivision progress; exhausted regions remain Possible; compatibility path cannot hang; termination tests.
close_reason: Added finite validated RootSearchConfig, explicit work/completeness receipt, strict split progress, and conservative Possible boxes; taylor_battery 10/10 pass.

title: Roofline Freshness must bind live authority and return a typed recorded receipt
description:
The sealed production roofline protocol now binds registry, pre/post axes, dependency receipt, build identity, manifest, and ledger rows. Two authority gaps remain: staleness_at accepts only a baseline hash and cannot observe that the signing/promotion authority has since been revoked; ProductionRun::record returns a bare i64 op id, so callers can conflate committed recording with a row that has subsequently passed Fresh revalidation.

Extend freshness with an explicit pinned authority/policy context and revocation state. A revoked or policy-rotated baseline must never remain Fresh merely because its bytes/hash are intact. Replace or supplement the bare op id with an opaque RecordedProductionRun receipt carrying the op, run receipt, baseline/dependency authority fingerprints, record time, and a method that revalidates to a typed FreshProductionEvidence under current policy. Report-only/custom/refused runs cannot construct the positive type.

Acceptance: authority revocation, key rotation, policy change, clock rollback, baseline replacement, dependency receipt revocation, and record-then-tamper all fail closed; re-promotion under the new authority recovers; compile/API tests prevent bare i64 from satisfying citation APIs; existing honest sealed runs stay Fresh; CLI language distinguishes measured, recorded, revalidated Fresh, and citable.

comments.author: codex-audit
comments.text: Independent audit fixed an adjacent authority leak in the working tree: RooflineKernel::run_once is now fallible; GEMM session/key/receipt failures return structured diagnostics; run_registry aborts every kernel after any peer execution failure; finalize_registry_tuning compensates every local lifecycle after any hook/identity diagnostic; GemmKernel abort is idempotent and clears pending/active/binding/tuner state. Regressions cover warmup/timed propagation, later-peer abort, middle-finalizer compensation, and GEMM abort. Also unified the batched GEMM row/gate target at one 0.25 constant. Static review, fmt/diff checks, and UBS's embedded check/clippy/no-run lanes passed; direct runtime was blocked by the shared build lock/host executable issue. This does not close the bead's separate live-authority/typed-recorded-receipt acceptance work.

title: Roofline CLI: baseline bootstrap (promote) subcommand + fleet reference stores
description: dfh3 landed governed baseline promotion (promote_baseline: >=3 mutually-agreeing quiet probes + named operator/justification) and the citable gates now REQUIRE a baseline store — but promote_baseline is API-only: no operator can bootstrap a store for a machine (the roofline bin only consumes --baseline). Every citable perf lane on every reference machine is queued behind this. Deliver: (a) a promote mode in the roofline bin — probe axes N>=3 times, build candidates, call promote_baseline with operator+justification, create-or-append the JSONL store, print refusal diagnostics verbatim (a loaded host must refuse, per the dfh3 field trial); (b) bootstrap the reference machines that are quiet (ts1/ts2; M4 expected to refuse under fleet load — an honest recorded outcome); (c) commit fleet reference stores under perf-baselines/ so gates are reproducible (operator-trusted/tamper-evident until fz2.7 adds signatures — say so in the README). Related: dfh3 (closed, the machinery), fz2.7 (authentication layer, unblocked by this), cwjn (first waiting consumer).
close_reason: DONE. roofline promote subcommand landed (4c8c9f9): probes axes N>=3 times, content-derived source receipts (fs-roofline.cli-baseline-source.v1 domain), governed promote_baseline (named operator + justification + age policy; drift bands refuse loaded hosts), create-or-append bounded JSONL store, verbatim refusal diagnostics; suite green (5/5 targets), verified END-TO-END by three real promotions: M4 Pro (80cb534fbaf60b50, 90.4 GB/s single) and ts1 5975WX (614b09f101a1e33b, quiet load 1.99) both promoted; fleet stores committed under perf-baselines/ with the README stating the fz2.7 trust boundary (operator-trusted/tamper-evident, NOT independently verified). First consumer already served: the cwjn both-ISA citable gate passes ran against these stores within the hour, including a live environment_invalid refusal during a compile burst (the governance catching real contamination). NOT DONE (honest): ts2 has no store yet — its only tree is a gitless rsync snapshot that the fs-la build identity correctly refuses; bootstrap it after a proper clone set exists there (the ts1 recipe: 8 shallow sibling clones). fz2.7 (signatures) remains the authentication layer.

title: Bound roofline staleness history with authenticated snapshots
description:
Roofline staleness currently reconstructs manifested runs by scanning tune history and repeatedly querying sibling rows/artifacts. The integrity checks are strong, but cost grows with retained history and can become quadratic across a large long-lived ledger. An attacker or merely sustained CI can turn verification into an unbounded resource sink.

Define bounded, authenticated history snapshots/checkpoints for production roofline evidence. Index by run receipt/build/baseline/machine, retain the complete ordered manifest root and dependency authority, and verify one snapshot plus bounded deltas rather than rescanning all history. Compaction must preserve tombstones, corrupt-evidence findings, and historical failure knowledge; it must never make a previously corrupt row Fresh.

Acceptance: explicit row/byte/query bounds; adversarial 10^5-row fixture stays within declared time/memory/query budgets; snapshot/delta tamper, missing rows, duplicate manifests, rollback, and compaction interruption fail closed; result matches the exhaustive verifier on bounded randomized histories; cancellation is checked at chunk boundaries; performance evidence and bounds are ledgered.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claiming (CloudyFinch lane - the gp3.15 manifest-recompute author, so I own the cost structure this bead bounds). Plan: (1) map the current verification cost precisely - per-row validate now walks the op-bound manifest and tune_gets every sibling, so staleness over a kernel with H historical runs is O(H x K) queries and the whole-ledger audit is the quadratic the reviewer named; (2) design the bounded authenticated snapshot: a checkpoint row indexed by (run receipt, build, baseline, machine) retaining the ordered manifest root (the existing domain-separated receipt hash IS the root - snapshots authenticate by rehashing stored roots into a chained checkpoint digest, no new crypto claims, operator-observed per workspace doctrine), so verification = one snapshot check + bounded deltas since it; (3) compaction keeps tombstones and corrupt-evidence findings as first-class retained records - a compacted history can refuse but never un-corrupt (previously-corrupt can never become Fresh); interrupted compaction fails closed to the uncompacted path; (4) battery: explicit row/byte/query budget asserts, 10^5-row adversarial fixture within declared budgets, snapshot/delta tamper + missing rows + duplicate manifests + rollback + interruption all fail closed, and DIFFERENTIAL EQUIVALENCE - bounded randomized histories where the snapshot verifier must agree with the exhaustive gp3.15 verifier verdict-for-verdict. Files: crates/fs-roofline/src/lib.rs (staleness section) + likely a new snapshot module; fs-ledger read APIs as needed.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
PROGRESS CHECKPOINT (design finalized against today's code, counter primitive built). Cost map at current HEAD: per-kernel staleness fetch is now CAPPED upstream (fs-ledger MAX_TUNE_ROWS_PER_KERNEL=1024, 16MiB scan guard - the bounds lane's work), but the per-row exhaustive validation is DEEPER than at gp3.15: op fetch + canonical-production-op parse + protocol axes validation + dependency-receipt structural fetch + manifest-membership checks + the K-sibling tune_get walk + artifact fetch per sibling. So one kernel probe over H retained runs of a K-kernel registry costs O(H*(K+4)) ledger queries with H up to 1024 - the quadratic the bead names, now precisely measurable: I added Ledger::read_queries(), a Cell counter on the five typed read APIs (tune_rows/tune_get/get_artifact/op/edge_exists) with an exact-count unit test - this is the basis for the acceptance's explicit query budgets. (Counter currently rides the shared working tree entangled with the in-flight LedgerInstanceId lane's WIP in the same file; single-file self-contained, safe under any sweep.)

Refined checkpoint design incorporating the current validation stack: checkpoint rows live in the tune table under a reserved kernel ('roofline-staleness-checkpoint'), machine-keyed like production rows, shape_class 'roofline-ckpt-v1:<ordinal>', measured = canonical JSON {schema, ordinal, prev_digest, upto_op, runs:[{receipt, op, manifest_root, build, dependency_receipt_hash, recorded_at_ns, verdict: valid|corrupt}]}, params = {schema, chained_digest} where digest_i = blake3_domain(prev || canonical_bytes) - authentication is the chained digest over roots the exhaustive verifier produced (operator-observed, no-crypto no-claim per workspace doctrine). Fast path: 1 bounded checkpoint fetch + chain recompute + per-row cheap identity check against the checkpointed entry + CURRENT-context classification (build/dependency/age compare using the checkpointed per-run build+dependency+recorded_at - no sibling walks); rows newer than upto_op get the exhaustive path (bounded delta). corrupt verdicts are TOMBSTONES: compaction can refuse but never un-corrupt; a broken/partial chain fails closed to the exhaustive path. Battery: budget asserts via read_queries(), 1e5-row fixture (spread across kernels under the 1024/kernel cap), snapshot/delta tamper + missing rows + duplicate manifests + rollback + interrupted-compaction fail-closed, and randomized differential equivalence vs the exhaustive verifier. Implementation next stretch on fresh context - this checkpoint verifier is security-relevant and deserves a full budget.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Audit marker (CloudyFinch): full beads-compliance-and-completion-verification pass starting on this workspace (comprehensive mode, threshold 700, completion-debt policy). This comment also regenerates the missing beads.base.jsonl merge anchor via the flush it triggers.

title: fs-la: core GEMM safe APIs permit release-mode extent overflow
description: Fresh-eyes audit found unchecked m*k, k*n, m*n, strided-view extent, and tunable pack-size arithmetic across public gemm_f64/gemm_f32/gemm_mixed/gemm_f64_parallel(_with)/gemm_f64_op. Debug panics mask release wrapping; wrapped slice checks can admit impossible shapes and later panic or enter unsafe SIMD through a misleading safe facade. Acceptance: checked shape/pack/view arithmetic before mutation or dispatch; exact controlled-error regressions in debug+release for every public entry family; alpha/beta no-read behavior preserved; no golden change unless arithmetic semantics truly move.
close_reason: Checked all public GEMM extents/strided views and padded tunable packs before mutation; debug and release gemm_suite 4/4 pass.

title: fs-rep-voxel: reject frame mixing and bound dense distance transforms
description: Occupancy boolean ops silently combine identical integer coordinates across different origin/voxel_size frames. DT/complement arithmetic overflows at i32 extrema and unchecked dense bbox volume can panic/OOM. Acceptance: nonforgeable validated frame; mismatch returns error before mutation; checked i64 spans/volume with explicit voxel budget; structured DT/try_new errors; checked complement halo; adversarial tests and honest contract.
close_reason: Completed: immutable validated occupancy frames; fail-before-mutation boolean errors; fallible world conversion/no-claim invalid queries; checked i64/u128 DT and complement admission with explicit budget and strict f64 exactness bound; fallible nonempty chart construction; exact voxel support. Proof: cargo test -p fs-rep-voxel (8 conformance passed, 0 failed; unit/doc 0); cargo clippy -p fs-rep-voxel --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings passed; UBS reviewed with no production true positive.

title: Seal scoped cost-model authority across HELM consumers
description: The exact roofline loader returns the same freely constructible CostModel as CostModel::fit(raw observations), and CostPrediction drops kernel/shape/machine/op/run/build/time/trust provenance. fs-ir AdmissionContext and fs-session estimate accept caller-keyed raw CostModel maps, so callers can bypass every receipt check and acquire identical routing/budget authority. Split raw provisional CostFit math from a sealed Scoped/AdmittedCostModel minted only from validated evidence. Carry exact scope, receipt/build/timestamp, and evidence/freshness class through predictions; require the sealed type in authoritative admission/session/router consumers while retaining explicitly provisional raw fits for tests and non-authoritative hints. Coordinate with admitted scientific color and the shared roofline evidence verifier. Discovered while independently reviewing bead frankensim-0aeh / commit e63d3ad.

title: Semantic identity coverage registry and replay-hash mutation battery
description:
Several audits found replay identities that omitted semantic fields or hashed display-oriented encodings: RNG stream counters invalidated a cross-crate golden; color algebra needed a schema/domain rotation; explanation fingerprints used rounded JSON; roofline rows needed dependency receipts, manifests, and exact build identity. Fixing each incident locally does not prove future receipt schemas remain complete.

Create a generated registry of every persisted/replayed identity schema (ledger nodes, packages, checker decisions, tune rows, planner/cache keys, explanation receipts, RNG goldens, CI snapshots). Each owner declares schema version, canonical encoder, semantic fields, explicitly non-semantic fields, domain string, and consumers. A mutation battery must change each semantic field independently and prove identity/replay changes; adding a struct field or transport field without classification fails the policy gate. Display JSON is forbidden as hash input unless it is the canonical transport and exact-bit round-trip is proven.

Acceptance: current schemas are inventoried; seeded omitted-field and same-rendered/different-bit mutations fail; schema/domain changes require golden-coupling annotations; replay rejects stale versions rather than guessing; generated coverage is deterministic across ISAs; xtask/DSR runs the registry and points directly at the unclassified field or missing mutation.

title: WS1: sparse active-tile infrastructure — Morton tiles, TilePool parallel sweep, deterministic
description: WHY (plan sec 5 + sec 14.1): the GLUP/s targets are for SPARSE-TILE stream+collide on many cores; the scale workstream (WS4) and the free-surface flagship need active-tile sparsity (most of a pour domain is empty). WHAT: (a) Morton-ordered 3-D tile grid (tile size autotuned start at 4x4x4 through 8x8x8, recorded via the fs-roofline tune path with its sealed admission), active-tile list with deterministic ordering (Morton key sort, not insertion order); (b) TilePool-parallel stream+collide sweep using the KernelRunner trait the fleet just landed (15b2c03), with the same bitwise-across-worker-counts guarantee the FftNd pooled path proved (Mutex-chunk disjoint tiles, per-tile order identical to serial); (c) tile activation/deactivation rules for free surface (mass-tracking hooks, WS1-E consumes); (d) halo exchange between neighbor tiles with a deterministic two-phase scheme (no read-write races, no ordering dependence). ACCEPTANCE: bitwise-identical results across worker counts 1/2/7 on a seeded cavity fixture (G5 gate, the FftNd precedent shows how); sparse run over a 10 percent-active domain allocates memory proportional to active tiles (arena accounting assertion, G4-style); golden frozen. TESTS: Morton round-trip property tests, halo-exchange unit fixtures, worker-count sweep battery, arena-accounting battery. DEPENDS on D3Q19 core + 3-D BCs.

title: Unify empirical CVaR and prevent finite-sample overflow across UQ/DFO/flagships
description: Cross-crate trace after frankensim-zsvk/a4rm found four remaining empirical-CVaR implementations (fs-uq, fs-dfo, fs-frame, fs-vessel) with duplicated order-statistic logic and unsafe intermediate sums. fs-uq's corrected fractional-boundary estimator can still overflow boundary*weight + tail_sum for finite f64::MAX samples; fs-dfo builds suffix sums that can overflow/cancel; frame/vessel duplicate RU formulas. Establish one layer-correct risk-algebra implementation (prefer fs-robust as the existing ASCENT risk algebra, or a narrowly justified shared risk module), expose CVaR plus VaR/minimizer metadata as needed, migrate all callers without cycles, and remove duplicate formulas. Add fractional-tail, ties, mixed-sign, f64::MAX constant, permutation, integer-boundary, and cross-owner parity tests; preserve or deliberately re-freeze affected goldens with semantic justification.

title: Roofline GEMM receipts must bind compute-axis target and tune decision
description: The registered gemm-f64 row currently applies its 75% target to the generic binding roof, although plan section 14.1 says 75% of measured peak FLOPs, and its citable receipt omits the exact scoped tune key/MC-NC plan/source/row identity. Add explicit target-axis semantics, compute-axis synthetic regressions, stable-decision checks across timed reps, sealed tune provenance in Attainment/ledger receipts, and tamper refusal.

title: x86 runtime sweep findings at e6888a57: 5 red suites / 477 green — first full-lane catch batch
description: FOUND by the first end-to-end run of scripts/ci/x86_runtime_sweep.sh (ts2 5995WX, admission fully sealed: heads equal, worktree clean, constellation snapshots content-hash-bound; retained log target/x86-runtime-logs/e6888a5750ba-ts2.log in the clean caller at /Volumes/USB_NVME/frankensim-clean). FIVE FAILURES, classified for their owners: (1) fs-simd tests::gemm_geometry_overflow_is_refused_before_dispatch — X86-ONLY CLASS: the overflow-refusal path panics inside the x86 capsules ('mk8x4 panel length mismatch (programmer error)' at x86/gemm.rs:61 plus btile.rs:36/scalar.rs:90/lib.rs:324) instead of refusing gracefully; this test literally cannot run on aarch64, so no ARM lane can see it — likely zevq-adjacent (safe GEMM facade overflow). (2) fs-surrogate ladder la_001_rb_estimators_empirically_contain_fixture_errors — ISA-SENSITIVE FIXTURE: 'canonical off-grid fixture stopped showing its observed adjacent tightening' with values [2.8e-4, 3.47e-17, 5.55e-17] — an aarch64-tuned empirical expectation that x86 bits do not reproduce (test-expectation sibling of the obq0 class; fix = ISA-robust expectation or det:: routing in the estimator path). (4) fs-ir planner pl_008b_large_admitted_rung_is_budget_checked_before_mesh_allocation — TOPOLOGY-SENSITIVE LIMIT: ResourceLimit{polynomial_cell_work: requested 30000000, limit 16000000} — the limit appears machine-derived and the 128-thread ts2 derives a smaller budget than the fixture assumes; the test encodes one machine's budget. (5) fs-ledger col_012_sources_are_minted_from_origin_evidence — empty-regime Anchoring admission assertion failed; most likely the in-flight colors wave at this HEAD rather than ISA (re-run after their next land; if it persists on x86 only, escalate). Each failure has the full panic + suite output in the retained log. The sweep lane itself also caught + fixed its own self-dirt bug en route (hermetic dir now under gitignored target/, commit pushed).

(1) fs-simd gemm_geometry_overflow_is_refused_before_dispatch: PIN RACE, ALREADY FIXED - the swept HEAD e6888a57 predates b556d54, which replaced the x86 btile4x4pf32 facade raw-arithmetic assert ((i0+3)*k*mb overflows in debug: the exact 'attempt to add with overflow' at btile.rs:36:16 in the retained log) with checked_btile4x4p_lengths. VERIFIED at current HEAD 7583344 on ts2 (5995WX): cargo test -p fs-simd --locked = 10/10 green in debug, sweep conditions, including the full refusal chain past the btile step (r4qrun/trn1c64 x86 expectations exercised for the first time - also green). NOT zevq-adjacent after all: zevq's checked-helper program was the FIX vehicle (gemm.rs/scalar.rs sites already refused gracefully in the swept log; only the btile facade lagged, fixed by b556d54 in the nkaq arc). (2) fs-surrogate la_001 + (4) fs-ir pl_008b: fixed earlier this batch (see triage comment), suites 10/10 and 16/16 green. (5) fs-ledger col_012: still red at 7583344 on ts2 (empty-regime Anchoring admission assert, color_battery.rs:1633) - colors wave actively dirty on fs-ledger at this moment + 6xgf lane open; their wave. The nightly sweep re-catches any survivor. Sweep lane value confirmed twice: 5 findings, 3 fixed, 2 routed to owners.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: TRIAGE UPDATE + TWO FIXES (a CloudyFinch session): findings (2) and (4) RECLASSIFIED and FIXED — both reproduce identically on the M4, so they were red-on-main from in-flight lanes, NOT ISA-specific (the sweep still gets credit: nothing else was running full test suites at HEAD on any machine). (4) fs-ir pl_008b: the new MAX_POLYNOMIAL_CELL_WORK=16M validation (the sealing lane's fail-closed resource work) refuses the test's own MAX_PLANNER_CELLS input (6 coeffs x 5 Gauss x 1M = 30M) before the budget-refusal path the test exists to prove; the test file already imported the new constant but pl_008b was left unadapted. Suite 16/16 green (+1 test). (5... wait, (2)) fs-surrogate la_001: an estimator improvement collapsed the k=4 and k=6 off-grid bounds to the roundoff floor (3.5e-17, 5.6e-17) where the fixture's strict adjacent-tightening observation is numerically meaningless noise. FIXED floor-aware: every adjacent pair tightens OR both sit at the 1e-15 floor (matching the test's own effectivity guard), and the first refinement must still tighten visibly from above the floor. title: WS1: free-surface 3-D — VOF mass tracking, interface reconstruction, the pour primitive
description: WHY: the vessel flagship (the spout that never dribbles, plan sec 15.3) is a free-surface pour; the 2-D crate has dam-break and Plateau-Rayleigh validated free surface; 3-D is required for a real pour. WHAT: generalize the 2-D free-surface machinery to D3Q19 sparse tiles: cell fill-fraction tracking, interface-cell classification (gas/interface/fluid) with deterministic tie-breaking, mass-conservative flux redistribution at interface cells, disjoining/surface-tension model port (whatever the 2-D crate has, no more - do not invent new physics here), tile activation as fluid advances (consumes WS1-D hooks). ACCEPTANCE: 3-D dam-break column collapse matches the classic Martin-Moyce front-position data within the same tolerance band the 2-D test uses; global mass conserved to 1e-9 over the full collapse (interface redistribution is the hard part - log the worst per-step violation); a minimal pour (cylinder tank, lip, gravity) runs end to end at 64^3-fixture scale and the poured mass equals tank loss to 1e-9. TESTS: Martin-Moyce battery with JSONL verdicts + reference data in-repo, mass ledger battery, seeded pour golden (deterministic mode). DEPENDS on sparse tiles bead.

title: fs-blake3: separate domain hashes from raw artifact namespace
description: hash_domain(d,p) currently equals raw hash_bytes(len(d)||d||p), so a raw artifact containing that encoding has the same shared ContentHash without a BLAKE3 collision. Before package schema v4 is finalized, implement a mode-separated domain hash (prefer official BLAKE3 derive-key context/material flags with independent oracle vectors), update contracts/tests, prove tagged-vs-raw non-equivalence, and keep package leaf/node/root domains distinct. Bump any affected protocol/crosswalk metadata; no ad hoc cryptographic claim.
close_reason: Derive-key mode separation landed in 10d1e2c by the t7x3 lane (verified in main: context/material flags, multichunk oracle vectors, tagged-vs-raw non-equivalence regression, distinct fs-package v4 domains, protocol v2 + crosswalk v2/format-4 bumps, contracts updated); closed by CloudyFinch after verification to unblock the 7uq9->krym P0 chain

This close unblocks 7uq9 -> krym. All implementation credit to the t7x3 lane - I only verified and filed.

title: fs-ad Real trait: add acos/asin/atan(2) for eigenvalue-derivative consumers (Ogden hyperelasticity)
description: fs-material's Ogden law needs principal-stretch derivatives, i.e. eigenvalues of C=F^T F differentiated through fs-ad duals. The closed-form symmetric-3x3 eigenvalue route (Cardano/trigonometric) needs acos on the generic scalar, and robust variants want atan2. The fs-ad Real trait currently exposes sin/cos/tanh/exp/ln/sqrt/powi but no inverse trig, and orphan rules prevent downstream crates from extending it. Ask: add acos (and ideally asin/atan/atan2) to Real + the Dual chain rules (d/dx acos = -1/sqrt(1-x^2), guard |x|->1), plus gradcheck coverage. Unblocks the staged Ogden law documented as a no-claim in crates/fs-material/CONTRACT.md.
close_reason: Inverse trig shipped end-to-end. fs-math det: asin/acos via atan2 on the FACTORED complement sqrt((1-x)(1+x)) — endpoint-conditioned (1-x^2 cancels at |x|->1); ASIN_ULP_BUDGET=6, measured worst 3 over 200k samples vs platform libm; asin odd BITWISE; acos(+-1)={0,pi}, asin(+-1)=+-pi/2 exact via atan2's IEEE table; |x|>1 -> NaN; reflection + pi/2 identities gated at the identity's own scale (the pi-acos form inflates conditioning ~16x — measured 7 ULP, documented in battery + CONTRACT). fs-ad: Real trait gained asin/acos/atan/atan2 (f64 -> det::*, verified no external implementors); Dual chain rules — asin' 1/sqrt((1-x)(1+x)), acos' negated, atan' 1/(1+x^2), atan2 binary partials (x dy - y dx)/(x^2+y^2) with NaN-honest origin. Gradcheck: inverse gauntlet vs central FD (500 pts rel<5e-9), primal BITWISE vs scalar path, analytic first derivatives at 5 points + atan2 partials in all quadrants incl. x<0 pi-branch, second derivative asin'' via nested duals to 1e-13, honest endpoints (asin'(1)=+inf; acos'(-1)=-inf since acos DECREASES — the first test draft wrongly expected +inf, caught by the battery). Ogden no-claim in fs-material CONTRACT updated: upstream blocker RESOLVED, staged Ogden law remains that crate's follow-up. Lanes: fs-math full suite green (incl. untouched det golden hash), fs-ad 22/22, clippy -D warnings 0 both, fmt, ubs 0 critical both, xtask clean for these crates.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PLAN (claimed): (1) fs-math det: add asin(x)=atan2(x,sqrt((1-x)(1+x))) and acos(x)=atan2(sqrt((1-x)(1+x)),x) — factored product keeps endpoint conditioning; specials (NaN, |x|>1 -> NaN, +-1 exact via atan2 IEEE table) fall out; declared ASIN/ACOS_ULP_BUDGET measured against platform-libm oracle in extensions_battery + bitwise-odd asin + acos(-x)~pi-acos(x) identity; CONTRACT rows. (2) fs-ad Real trait: add asin/acos/atan/atan2 (f64 impls -> det::*; no external implementors exist, verified). Dual chain rules: asin' = 1/sqrt(1-x^2), acos' = -that, atan' = 1/(1+x^2), atan2 binary partials (x dy - y dx)/(x^2+y^2). Endpoint honesty: derivative at |x|=1 is +-inf (same documented convention as sqrt at 0 — never clamped). (3) gradcheck: new inverse-trig gauntlet (grad vs central FD, primal BITWISE vs scalar path, analytic first+second derivatives via Dual<Dual>, endpoint/NaN table). Unblocks staged Ogden (fs-material CONTRACT no-claim).

title: fs-exec parked persistent workers under Cx::scoped_cpu — recover N-D FFT attainment (27d3 follow-up)
description: MOTIVATION (measured, 27d3): per-run worker spawn overhead collapses N-D FFT attainment — every FftNd axis pass calls TilePool run (2D roundtrip = 4 pool runs, 3D = 6), respawning scoped workers each time; ts2 measured 0.011 attainment at 256x256 vs 0.11-0.13 at large sizes, and the m4 numbers show the same small-size collapse. The 27d3 N-D roofline rows ([256,256], [1024,1024], [128,128,64]) are REPORT-ONLY in crates/fs-fft/tests/perf_lane.rs for exactly this reason (floor gates assert settled claims, not aspirations). WHAT: park a persistent worker pool once per TilePool (or per session epoch) UNDER the asupersync scoped lane (TilePool::run_scoped / Cx::scoped_cpu, landed by lx0e at feb6fa8+35aef00, upstream asupersync fa371dcf) so per-run cost drops to wake/sleep instead of spawn/join, WITHOUT breaking P7 scope honesty: the parked workers must live inside a task's scope (e.g. a long-lived session task parks them; runs hand work in and out via the existing deque protocol), never as free 'static threads. The pool CONTRACT defers this alongside the lock-free deques (no-claims) — landing it must update the CONTRACT and keep the G4 storms green (drain, arena quiescence, panic containment, reuse) plus G5 bitwise determinism across lanes and worker counts. ACCEPTANCE: N-D FFT report-only rows flip to floored gates (target 0.40 BindingRoof per 27d3 spec) on both m4-pro-macos and ts2-5995wx-linux baseline stores with pre/post reprobe; small-size attainment recovers by roughly the spawn-overhead factor measured above. Prereqs all landed: 691k (upstream primitive), lx0e (scoped lane).
close_reason: DELIVERED: parked persistent workers under scoped ownership, end to end. (1) fs-exec crew capsule (crates/fs-exec/src/crew.rs, commit 950fda3): N workers park on a condvar INSIDE an owning scope and execute per-run jobs handed over as a lifetime-erased borrow — the ONE unsafe op, justified by the rayon-latch argument (dispatch blocks until the last worker clears the slot, so the borrow provably outlives every dereference); registered in unsafe-capsules.json with full SAFETY.md (invariants, aliasing, lifetimes, panic/cancellation/concurrency behavior); job panics are captured so the latch can never hang; CrewShutdown releases parked workers on unwind so the owning scope's join can never hang. xtask check-unsafe green. (2) TilePool integration (commit 9a6573e): Launch becomes a three-way strategy (own std scope / Cx::scoped_cpu task scope / parked crew) all driving the SAME worker_loop — bitwise-identical across lanes by construction; with_parked_crew parks the crew as scoped-CPU children of the CALLING task (P7: entry/exit checkpoint refusals -> CrewScopeError::Cancelled fail-closed; task cancel/budget bridges at tile boundaries via each worker's park-time CpuCx); with_parked_crew_local is the std twin for untasked callers; excess crew workers no-op on runs with fewer tiles. G4/G5: parked lane bitwise-matches std across reruns on one crew, tile-panic containment with crew survival, gate-drain with crew reuse, task-scoped crew bridges mid-run cancellation and refuses pre-cancelled tasks at entry. 95+ fs-exec tests, clippy -D warnings, fmt all green. CONTRACT no-claims updated (parked deferral delivered opt-in; Mutex deques still deferred). (3) KernelRunner trait (commit 15b2c03): TilePool and ParkedTilePool interchangeable at every call site; FftNd pooled entries generalized source-compatibly. (4) Measurement lane (commit 46a6856): fftnd sweep parks ONE crew for all rows. MEASURED RECOVERY on ts2 (5995WX, quiet): [256,256] 0.011 -> 0.0696 (6.3x — the bead's 'recovers by roughly the spawn-overhead factor' acceptance), [1024,1024] ~0.12 -> 0.4598 (CLEARS the 0.40 floor, within_band, dispersion 0.07), [128,128,64] 0.3098. GATE FLIP NOT INCLUDED, deliberately: floors assert settled claims on BOTH baseline machines, and the m4 was uncertifiable during swarm activity (attainment 0.028-0.039 at dispersion 0.24-0.34 = host-load noise; quiet-host constraint). The one-quiet-run flip is frankensim-o582; the residual small-size gap is granularity (256 pencils / 128 woken workers), tracked with a concrete tuning design as frankensim-3f6c.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PLAN (CloudyFinch): (1) New fs-exec parked-crew capsule: Crew<Caps> — N workers parked on a condvar INSIDE an owning scope, executing per-run jobs handed over as lifetime-erased &dyn Fn(worker, Option<&CpuCx>) with the rayon-latch safety argument (dispatch blocks until every worker reports done and the job slot is cleared, so the erased borrow provably outlives every use; the transmute is the capsule, <300 lines, SAFETY.md beside, registered in unsafe-capsules.json). (2) TilePool::with_parked_crew(task_cx, f) parks the crew as Cx::scoped_cpu children (P7: crew lives inside the calling task's scope; a Drop shutdown-guard wakes parked workers on BOTH normal return and unwind of f, so the scope join can never hang) + with_parked_crew_local(f) as the std-scope twin for untasked callers (perf lanes). (3) run_inner gains a Launch::Crew arm: dispatch instead of spawn; worker_loop is UNCHANGED (same deque seeding, steal order, drain, per-tile containment) so parked runs are bitwise-identical to spawned runs by construction; runs with fewer tiles than crew workers no-op the excess workers (deque/victim tables keep run-local sizing). Task-cancel bridging comes from each worker's own park-time CpuCx at tile boundaries, exactly like lx0e. (4) KernelRunner trait (fs-exec) implemented by TilePool and ParkedTilePool; fs-fft run_pooled generalizes its pool param to &impl KernelRunner (source-compatible for &TilePool callers) so FftNd axis passes reuse one parked crew instead of respawning per pass. (5) G4/G5: parked-vs-spawned bitwise equality both lanes, sequential-run determinism, mid-run task-cancel drain + arena quiescence + reuse, tile-panic containment, unwind-through-f shutdown (no join hang), fewer-tiles-than-workers. (6) CONTRACT update: parked crew lands, Mutex deques remain deferred (no-claims unchanged). (7) Measure fftnd rows m4 + ts2 with the parked path; flip report-only rows to floored 0.40 ONLY where both machines clear it with band margin — floors assert settled claims.

title: WS4: cancellation-latency certification at scale — the 200us tile-boundary claim measured
description: WHY (plan sec 5.2/9.6 promises latency-to-cancel <= 200us at tile boundaries; reality check: contract exists, number never measured): e-racing economics (Bet 8) depend on this number being real. WHAT: instrument the TilePool cancel path with the timestamping the KernelRunner trait allows: issue cancels at randomized moments during the WS4-A scale sweeps, measure request-to-drain latency distributions (p50/p95/p99), on both reference machines, under the admitted-lane environment protocol (a loaded machine invalidates latency claims exactly like it invalidates throughput). Gate on an honest floor (p95 under a generous first bar, tightened by re-measurement); REPORT the 200us target. Feed results into the fs-exec CONTRACT as measured envelopes replacing the aspirational number. ACCEPTANCE: latency rows on both ISAs ledgered; storm-with-latency battery green; CONTRACT updated with measured values and machine fingerprints. DEPENDS on the scale battery.

title: fs-bo scale + fidelity: TuRBO trust-region BO, multi-fidelity cost-aware acquisition, inducing-point sparse GPs, tape acquisition gradients
description: SPLIT from 7tv.5 (fs-bo core landed: Matern GPs w/ QMC-multistart LML fitting, EI/q-EI over fixed Sobol normal banks, BO-beats-random on Branin ledgered, bitwise replay, cross-ISA golden). THIS bead = the scale/fidelity lanes: (a) TuRBO-class trust-region BO for 30-300d (local GPs in adaptive hyperrectangles, success/failure counters for expansion/shrink, restart on collapse; acceptance = published-class performance on 100d benchmarks e.g. Ackley-100 vs the landed CMA-ES baseline, ledgered ERT); (b) MULTI-FIDELITY: cost-aware acquisition (cost-weighted EI / knowledge-gradient class) over a fidelity-indexed kernel (e.g. downsampling kernel k((x,m),(x',m')) or linear discrepancy model per evidence-model-ledger); acceptance = correct cheap-vs-expensive allocation on synthetic two-fidelity fixtures with KNOWN correlation, cost-to-target documented win vs single-fidelity; (c) INDUCING-POINT sparse GPs beyond ~1e4 points (Nystrom/variational, fs-la-randomized machinery) with accuracy-vs-exact gates on subsampled fixtures; (d) FrankenTorch TAPE gradients through the q-EI reparameterization (the fixed-bank acquisition surface is differentiable by construction: mu(X) + L(X)z needs dL/dX through Cholesky - ft has the tape; replaces CMA-ES inner argmax where dimension makes it worthwhile); (e) heteroscedastic noise from UQ variance estimates + e-process-compatible stopping (coordinate with fs-uq bead tfz.25 when it lands). Each lane independently landable - split further on scope pressure.
close_reason: ALL IMPLEMENTATION LANES LANDED CROSS-ISA (fs-bo::turbo/mf/sparse; the two cross-crate integration lanes - tape acquisition gradients + heteroscedastic/e-process - split to frankensim-a2g2 with a full design). THE LEDGER: (a) TuRBO - local GPs in ARD-weighted trust regions, Thompson sampling over Sobol candidates w/ fixed Philox noise (bitwise-replayable), success/failure region adaptation, collapse restarts; Ackley-30 at matched 300-eval budget: 11.09 BEATS QMC-random 12.22 with CMA-ES REPORTED alongside (10.22 - numbers not cherry-picks); forced 3 robustness fixes incl. the adaptive-jitter predict_joint upgrade (one documented golden bump, semantic justification recorded). (b) MULTI-FIDELITY - two-fidelity ICM kernel w/ LEARNED between-fidelity correlation (rho ~ 1.000 recovered, variance reduced 10/10 probes), MFEI cost-aware fidelity rule: 78% cheap-evaluation share at 10:1 costs and THE HEADLINE - MF 0.3995 vs single-fidelity 1.7179 at MATCHED total cost on Branin (essentially solved vs barely started). (c) SPARSE GPs - DTC/Titsias with the ELBO bound INSTANCE-CHECKED (tight at Z=X to relative 1e-6 with the two-factorization-path roundoff argument documented; monotone under nested farthest-point selection; RMSE ladder 0.114 -> 0.003 at m=10 -> 80 on n=300). GOLDENS: turbo 0xe671..., mf 0x6411..., sparse 0x0138..., bo bumped once to 0x5db8... (documented). All verified on both ISAs - the sparse verification survived an OOM-killed first attempt and a saturation queue (load 140, 120-way asupersync 0.3.6 rebuild storm) with the relaunch completing clean. Gates: clippy zero warnings throughout, fmt, CONTRACT current (turbo/mf/sparse semantics + determinism + conformance + no-claims).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: STARTED - lane (a) TuRBO first. fs-bo::turbo: TuRBO-1-class trust-region BO - local GP fit on in-region data (standardized y, QMC-multistart hyperparameters via the landed fit_hyperparams), candidate generation = center + TR-scaled scrambled-Sobol perturbations with ARD-lengthscale-weighted box sides, selection by THOMPSON SAMPLING over the candidate set through predict_joint's covariance Cholesky with a FIXED normal bank (common random numbers - deterministic per seed, the house discipline); success/failure counters (succ_tol doublings, fail_tol halvings), restart on TR collapse (L < L_min) keeping the global best. GATES: TR-mechanics G0 (success streaks grow L, plateaus shrink it, collapse restarts - synthetic objectives); Ackley-30 at matched budget: TuRBO beats QMC-random AND matches-or-beats GLOBAL EI-BO (the dimensionality-ceiling claim, median over fixed seeds, ledgered) with the CMA-ES comparison REPORTED alongside; bitwise replay; golden + trj. Lanes (b) multi-fidelity, (c) inducing points, (d) tape gradients, (e) heteroscedastic + e-process stay on the bead.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: LANE (a) TuRBO COMPLETE (fs-bo::turbo). LANDED: TuRBO-class trust-region BO - local GP in an adaptive hyperrectangle (ARD-lengthscale-weighted sides normalized to the geometric mean), THOMPSON SAMPLING over Sobol candidates through predict_joint's covariance Cholesky with fixed Philox noise (common random numbers - bitwise-replayable), success/failure counters (double at succ_tol=3, halve at fail_tol=8), restart on collapse keeping the global best; LOCAL DATA CAPPED at max_local nearest points + hyperparameters refit every refit_every iterations (the standard TuRBO economy - unbounded local sets with per-iteration 32-param FD refits measurably stalled the 30d battery, documented in CONTRACT). THREE ROBUSTNESS FIXES FORCED BY THE HIGH-D REGIME (each found by the battery): (1) hybrid Sobol+Philox hyperparameter starts beyond the 10-dim table cap (the fs-uq KL-germ pattern); (2) predict_joint's flat 1e-10 jitter FAILED on near-duplicate candidate sets in collapsed regions -> adaptive escalation (rel*max_diag from 1e-10 up) + diagonal fallback for numerically void joints (marginals kept, cross-correlations dropped only where the joint is void) - this bumped the bo_battery golden ONCE with semantic justification recorded (0x4f5a... -> 0x5db8_f433_fd71_f738 per the golden-evidence policy); (3) Thompson noise from Philox not Sobol (candidate counts exceed the table; Thompson noise gains nothing from QMC). EVIDENCE (turbo 3/3 + bo 7/7 BOTH ISAs - trj /data/tmp/fsturbo.5uklri, both frozen goldens passing on x86_64; TuRBO golden 0xe671_9eef_01a1_b960): TR mechanics G0 - smooth bowl tracked to 2.6e-6, needle objective collapses into 5 restarts; ACKLEY-30 at matched 300-eval budget: TuRBO 11.09 median BEATS QMC-random 12.22, CMA-ES REPORTED alongside at 10.22 (both legitimate high-d optimizers - the ledger records numbers, not cherry-picks); bitwise replay. Gates: clippy zero warnings, fmt, CONTRACT updated (turbo semantics + determinism bump + conformance + no-claims). REMAINING LANES: (b) multi-fidelity cost-aware acquisition + discrepancy models, (c) inducing-point sparse GPs, (d) FrankenTorch tape acquisition gradients, (e) heteroscedastic + e-process stopping.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: LANE (b) STARTED - multi-fidelity. fs-bo::mf: two-fidelity joint GP via an ICM/intrinsic-coregionalization kernel K((x,m),(x',m')) = B[m][m'] * k_x(x,x') with B = LL^T (Cholesky-parameterized 2x2 fidelity covariance - PSD by construction, the between-fidelity correlation LEARNED as a hyperparameter), Matern-5/2 spatial part with ARD; LML fit by fs-ascent L-BFGS over log-lengthscales + L-params + noise with QMC multistart (the house pattern). ACQUISITION: EI on the HIGH-fidelity posterior, argmax by CMA-ES; FIDELITY SELECTION by the MFEI-class rule alpha(x,m) = EI_high(x) * corr_m(x) * (cost_high/cost_m) - evaluate cheap when the posterior between-fidelity correlation at x times the cost ratio beats 1 (corr from the joint posterior covariance at [(x,lo),(x,hi)]). GATES: (1) correlation recovery - learned b12/sqrt(b11 b22) within tolerance of the fixture's empirical inter-fidelity correlation + variance-reduction-at-high measured when low data is added; (2) ALLOCATION at 10:1 cost ratio - low-fidelity evaluations must dominate the count (the correct-allocation acceptance); (3) COST-TO-TARGET on a two-fidelity Branin (known linear-bias low fidelity): MF-BO reaches the target at lower or equal total cost than single-fidelity EI-BO, median over fixed seeds, LEDGERED; (4) bitwise replay + golden + trj.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: LANE (b) MULTI-FIDELITY COMPLETE (fs-bo::mf). LANDED: two-fidelity joint GP via the ICM kernel K((x,m),(x',m')) = B[m][m'] k_x(x,x') with B = LL^T Cholesky-parameterized (PSD by construction, l21 signed via tanh squashing - the between-fidelity correlation is a LEARNED hyperparameter, reported per run); Matern-5/2 ARD spatial part; LML fit by fs-ascent L-BFGS with hybrid QMC multistart (house pattern); mf_minimize = EI on the HIGH-fidelity posterior (CMA-ES argmax) with the MFEI-class fidelity rule - evaluate cheap when corr^2 x cost-ratio > 1, corr computed from the JOINT posterior at [(x,lo),(x,hi)]; cost-indexed best-so-far traces ledgered. EVIDENCE (4/4 BOTH ISAs - trj /data/tmp/fsmf.eyKWgw, GOLDEN 0x6411_f077_1d5e_9f88 frozen-assert-verified on x86_64): CORRELATION RECOVERY - learned rho ~ 1.000 on the linear-bias Branin pair AND high-fidelity posterior variance reduced at 10/10 held-out probes when cheap data is added (the joint model demonstrably transfers information); ALLOCATION at 10:1 costs - low-fidelity evaluations dominate at 78% share (the correct-allocation acceptance); COST-TO-TARGET at MATCHED total cost: MF-BO median 0.3995 vs single-fidelity EI-BO 1.7179 (optimum 0.3979 - MF essentially SOLVES Branin where SF is still far away; the acceptance's documented win, exceeded); bitwise replay. Gates: clippy zero warnings, fmt, CONTRACT updated (mf semantics + conformance + no-claims: TWO-fidelity; deeper ladders + discrepancy models join with consumers). REMAINING LANES: (c) inducing-point sparse GPs, (d) tape acquisition gradients, (e) heteroscedastic + e-process stopping.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: LANE (c) STARTED - inducing-point sparse GPs (fs-bo::sparse). DTC/SoR predictive with the TITSIAS VARIATIONAL ELBO as the honesty instrument: ELBO = collapsed DTC log-likelihood - (1/2 sigma^2) tr(K_XX - Q_XX), computed via the matrix-inversion-lemma identities (log|Q+s2I| = log|A| - log|Kzz| + n log s2, A = Kzz + s^-2 Kzx Kxz) - EVERYTHING through fs-la Cholesky. Inducing selection: deterministic farthest-point sampling. THE LAW GATES: (1) EXACTNESS RECOVERY at Z=X - sparse mean/variance == exact GP predictions and ELBO == exact LML (Q_XX = K_XX makes the bound tight - a sharp algebraic identity); (2) the ELBO LOWER-BOUNDS the exact LML for every m < n (the variational guarantee, checked instance-wise, not cited); (3) ACCURACY LADDER - RMSE-vs-exact decreasing in m on an n=300 fixture with the m=inducing-count trend ledgered; (4) golden + trj.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: LANE (c) SPARSE GPs COMPLETE ON aarch64; x86 VERIFICATION QUEUED (fs-bo::sparse). LANDED: DTC/SoR inducing-point GPs with the TITSIAS ELBO as the honesty instrument - ELBO = collapsed DTC likelihood - (1/2 sigma^2) tr(K_XX - Q_XX), all via matrix-inversion-lemma identities through fs-la Cholesky (log|Q+s2I| = log|A| - log|Kzz| + n log s2), O(n m^2); deterministic farthest-point inducing selection; predict = DTC mean/variance. EVIDENCE (4/4 on M4 Pro, GOLDEN 0x0138_e24a_db84_4bec frozen): EXACTNESS RECOVERY at Z=X - mean/variance match the exact GP to 2.8e-10/1.5e-9 and ELBO tight to the exact LML at RELATIVE 1e-6 (the two sides come from DIFFERENT factorization paths - absolute 1e-6 was measurably dishonest: the K_ZZ jitter amplified by sigma^-2 through the trace term produced a 2e-5 gap at noise 1e-4, documented in the test); the ELBO LOWER-BOUNDS the exact LML at every m, monotone under nested farthest-point selection (-38011 -> -4411 -> -175 -> 137.77 <= 137.77 at m=5/15/40/120 - the variational guarantee checked instance-wise); ACCURACY LADDER: RMSE-vs-exact 0.114 -> 0.019 -> 0.003 at m=10/30/80 on n=300. Gates: clippy zero warnings, fmt, CONTRACT updated (sparse semantics + conformance + no-claims: fixed inducing locations, variational location optimization joins at true 1e4 scale). TRJ NOTE: verification bundle at /data/tmp/fssparse.QZca26 (test2.log) - the box is saturated (load ~100-140, 120 parallel rustc jobs rebuilding the asupersync 0.3.6 bump across the other agent's staging dirs; my first attempt was OOM/contention-killed mid-compile). The frozen golden assert will verify on x86_64 when the queue drains - follow up next stretch. REMAINING LANES: (d) tape acquisition gradients (FrankenTorch), (e) heteroscedastic + e-process (fs-uq::anytime just landed by the other agent - the integration primitive exists now).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: LANE (c) x86 VERIFICATION COMPLETE: 4/4 on Threadripper (/data/tmp/fssparse.QZca26/test2.log) with the frozen GOLDEN 0x0138_e24a_db84_4bec assert passing - full cross-ISA evidence now on record (the queue drained after the asupersync 0.3.6 rebuild storm; first attempt was contention-killed, relaunch in the same dir completed). tzeh state: lanes (a) TuRBO, (b) multi-fidelity ICM, (c) DTC/Titsias sparse GPs all LANDED cross-ISA; remaining (d) tape gradients + (e) heteroscedastic/e-process are integration lanes (FrankenTorch; fs-uq::anytime).

title: fs-roofline tune-ledger write corrupts on x86-64 (fsqlite 'page freed in concurrent transaction')
close_reason: fsqlite upstream regression (frankensqlite#123, bisected to d1a543e); fs-ledger tune_put rerouted UPDATE-then-INSERT in 0e4534c; rf_004 fail->pass verified on ts1, both-ISA suites green

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
DETERMINISTIC x86-64-ONLY CORRUPTION (found via cross-ISA verification on ts1,
Threadripper 5975WX, Zen 3; the ARM-only CI fleet cannot see this). Reproduce:
  cargo test -p fs-roofline --test conformance rf_004 --release
  -> rf_004_staleness_alerts_on_fingerprint_drift PANICS at conformance.rs:373:
     `inject valid-JSON payload corruption: Sql { context: "tune upsert", detail:
      "database disk image is malformed: page 52 was freed earlier in concurrent
       transaction 5" }`
3/3 runs fail with the IDENTICAL page (52) and transaction (5) -> deterministic,
not a flaky race. The failing op is a normal `tune_put` WRITE to the tune ledger
(fs-roofline -> fs-ledger -> fsqlite), during the test's setup that overwrites a
row with "{}" to exercise CorruptEvidence detection; fsqlite's own page manager
reports the DB image malformed BEFORE the write completes.

PASSES on aarch64 (M4) — so it is x86-specific: an fsqlite MVCC/page-management
edge case (a page freed by an internal "concurrent transaction" then referenced),
OR an fs-ledger/fs-roofline transaction pattern (atomic evidence transaction +
rollback + row reuse, crates/fs-roofline/src/kernels.rs:640-838) that fsqlite
tolerates on aarch64 but corrupts on x86.

IMPACT: (1) fs-roofline conformance is RED on x86 (rf_004). (2) The tune ledger is
the SAME fsqlite store the fz2.7 perf-baseline infra (FRANKENSIM_BASELINE_STORE)
writes to — so promoting/writing perf baselines on the Threadrippers likely hits
this too, blocking honest x86 perf rows. Next step: minimal fsqlite repro of the
tune_put write sequence on x86 to localize (fsqlite page-free-in-txn vs fs-ledger
transaction reuse); relates to the existing fsqlite bug bead lar1.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (CloudyFinch — the cross-ISA forensics lane; also the author of the c40j baseline tooling this blocks). Plan: (1) reproduce rf_004 on ts1 at HEAD; (2) extract the exact tune_put SQL sequence from fs-ledger and replay it in a minimal fsqlite-only program on x86 to classify fsqlite-upstream vs fs-ledger-misuse (lar1 precedent: re-verify against current fsqlite before filing); (3) land the fix or a documented workaround, rf_004 green on BOTH ISAs, close with the classification. Reservations: crates/fs-ledger tune paths if the fault is ours; a scratch repro program otherwise; fs-roofline conformance only if the test itself needs a fix.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
LOCALIZED (frankensim-side triage; the residual bug is fsqlite-core). Findings:

1. tune_put is a PLAIN UPSERT (fs-ledger/src/lib.rs:1666): `INSERT INTO tune(...)
   ON CONFLICT(kernel,shape_class,machine) DO UPDATE SET params=..., measured=...`.
   No explicit DELETE/VACUUM; but the DO UPDATE reorganizes the tune B-tree page.

2. tune_put is CLEAN IN ISOLATION on x86 — fs-ledger's own suite (incl.
   tune_upserts, many sequential tune_put) is GREEN on x86 (my batch-3 run). So
   tune_put alone does NOT corrupt. The trigger is ACCUMULATED STATE.

3. The distinguishing element in rf_004 vs the passing fs-ledger tests:
   `record_run(...)` (conformance.rs:277) runs fs-roofline's ATOMIC EVIDENCE
   TRANSACTION (crates/fs-roofline/src/kernels.rs:640-838 — explicit begin/commit,
   op + tune rows written together, rollback-with-retry-marker path). That
   transaction frees B-tree pages onto the freelist. The LATER standalone tune_put
   UPSERT then reuses one of those freed pages, and fsqlite reports "page 52 was
   freed earlier in concurrent transaction 5" — transaction 5 == the evidence txn.

HYPOTHESIS: fsqlite freelist/MVCC page-reuse across a transaction boundary is
mis-tracked on x86-64 (a page freed inside the committed evidence transaction is
still seen as owned by that snapshot when a later write reuses it). aarch64 handles
the same sequence correctly (rf_004 GREEN on M4), so it is an x86-specific fsqlite
core bug, NOT fs-ledger/fs-roofline misuse (both ops are normal).

MINIMAL-REPRO DIRECTION for the fsqlite owner: {open ledger; run one explicit
multi-write transaction that frees a tune/B-tree page; commit; then a standalone
UPSERT that reuses the freed page} on x86 — should reproduce without the rest of
the roofline machinery. FRANKENSIM-SIDE MITIGATION if an fsqlite fix is far off:
force a WAL checkpoint (or a fresh Connection) between record_run and the next
tune_put so the freelist is reconciled before reuse — a workaround, not a fix.
Relates to lar1 (other fsqlite reparse bug). Blocks fz2.7 baseline promotion on x86.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
MITIGATION TEST — reconnection does NOT work (refines the diagnosis). Inserted a
fresh `Ledger::open(&db)` (new fsqlite Connection to the same file) immediately
before the corrupting tune_put in rf_004, on ts1/x86. STILL FAILS identically
(conformance.rs:374, "page 52 was freed earlier in concurrent transaction 5").

CONSEQUENCE: the malformation is PERSISTED IN THE DB FILE by record_run's evidence
transaction (transaction 5) — it is NOT a live per-connection snapshot/cache
artifact (a fresh connection reads the same corrupt on-disk freelist state). So the
bug is WRITE-SIDE: record_run's committed transaction writes the freelist/MVCC
page-free bookkeeping for page 52 INCORRECTLY on x86, and fsqlite's integrity check
fires when the later tune_put upsert tries to reuse that page. This is squarely an
fsqlite-core freelist/page-free bug on x86-64; there is NO frankensim-side
reconnection workaround. (A full VACUUM between record_run and tune_put would rebuild
the freelist and likely mask it, but that is heavy and still a workaround, not a
fix.) The proper fix is in fsqlite's freelist page-free bookkeeping on x86 —
recommend an fsqlite-direct repro: one committed transaction that frees a b-tree
page, then a fresh write that reuses it, on x86, checking the on-disk freelist +
per-page "freed-by-txn" bookkeeping for an x86 mis-encode (endianness/width/layout).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
LOCALIZED + FIXED (workaround landed 0e4534c). Root cause is an fsqlite UPSTREAM regression, not fs-ledger misuse.

Mechanism: INSERT..ON CONFLICT..DO UPDATE on the tune table corrupts the DB ('page N was freed earlier in concurrent transaction M') when the conflict seek lands after a leaf split. Geometry-gated: needs 3 tune rows with measured ~1265B (600B and 2000B both dodge it); 2 rows fine; plain UPDATE fine; REPLACE INTO fine; DO NOTHING fine; same-size DO UPDATE also corrupts (not about shrinking). Pragmas/journal mode irrelevant.

Bisected frankensqlite 75ba1b7ed..46caaa9 (58 commits) with a raw fsqlite-only repro: first bad commit d1a543e 'fix(btree): rebuild rootless cursor path before INSERT OR REPLACE (bd-kwei8)' - its seek-cache fast path now always leaves a rootless single-entry cursor stack, and the guards it added to delete/table_insert do NOT cover the upsert's DO UPDATE mutation path, so balance treats the leaf as root and double-frees.

Filed upstream with minimal repro + bisect: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite/issues/123 (lar1 precedent).

NOT ISA-specific: reproduces on M4/macOS too at fsqlite HEAD. rf_004 only LOOKED x86-only because macOS receipt JSON lengths dodge the 1265B window. Heads-up: I fast-forwarded the local Mac frankensqlite clone to 46caaa9 (upstream HEAD, matches ts1) - if any fsqlite-backed test suddenly fails with 'page N freed earlier', it is frankensqlite#123, not your change.

Workaround: tune_put now UPDATE-then-INSERT (equivalent under single-writer-per-connection), doc-marked for revert once #123 fixed. Evidence: ts1 rf_004 fail 3/3 before -> conformance 8/8 + fs-ledger 77/0 after; local fs-ledger 87/0, rf_004 green. (Local rf_001 EnvironmentInvalid on the loaded M4 is the known busy-host artifact, pre-exists this change, passes on ts1.)

title: fs-mesh v2: constrained recovery, full Ruppert, sliver exudation, parallel coloring
description: Successor to frankensim-epic-morph-wqd.16 (the kernel bead). The v1 kernel in crates/fs-mesh delivers BRIO incremental Bowyer-Watson on exact predicates with exact self-audits, weak-Delaunay canonical conflict rules (insphere Zero = no conflict; ghost conflicts via orient3d + in-plane incircle), radius-edge refinement with hull-escapers counted, and full determinism (bitwise P2/G5, relabeling invariance, dyadic G3). Remaining scope split out here: (1) constrained boundary recovery - PLC conformity with Steiner edge/face insertion + boundary correspondence back to source charts (the DWR mapping); (2) full Ruppert refinement with local feature size + small-input-angle policy + encroachment splitting (replaces v1's counted hull-escaper skips, see RefineStats.unrefinable_remaining); (3) sliver exudation via weighted-Delaunay perturbation; (4) deterministic parallel domain coloring (same mesh at any thread count, merging against the sequential kernel's bitwise outputs); (5) the 1e7-point perf lane. Start from crates/fs-mesh/CONTRACT.md no-claims; the exact audit (Tetrahedralization::audit) is the regression trip-wire for every one of these.

title: fs-cheb Orr-Sommerfeld stack: complex nonsymmetric eigensolver, colleague-matrix roots, 2D low-rank, Fourier variant
description:
SPLIT from frankensim-epic-bedrock-6ys.15 (fs-cheb v1), which landed: Cheb1 adaptive Chebyshev objects on FIRST-KIND points (values<->coeffs = fs-fft DCT-II/III exactly), plateau truncation at 2.2e-15 rel (deliberately ABOVE the DCT noise floor - bring-up measured sin(20x) inflating to degree 1090 when chasing the floor at 8.9e-16; constant documented in-code), Clenshaw eval, differentiate/integral/add/mul, subdivision+Newton roots, Chebyshev-Lobatto diff_matrix with negative-sum-trick diagonal (bitwise-testable: diagonal exactly negates off-diagonal sum in construction order), dirichlet_laplace_eigs demo via FD-surrogate-shifted inverse iteration (validates collocation path vs analytic (k pi/2)^2 to 1e-6 rel). Golden hash 0xaee480021eea9097 cross-ISA.

THIS BEAD is the missing eigensolver stack + the deferred variants:
1. COMPLEX NONSYMMETRIC EIGENSOLVER - the first deliverable everything else here needs: complex f64 arithmetic type (or (re,im) pairs on fs-math strict kernels - keep cross-ISA determinism), Hessenberg reduction + shifted QR (Francis double-shift for real matrices, single-shift complex QR for complex), balancing. Home decision: fs-la (natural) - coordinate if another agent holds an fs-la bead then.
2. ORR-SOMMERFELD acceptance (the bead's original criterion): plane Poiseuille U=1-y^2, OS operator via D2/D4 collocation with clamped BCs (u=u'=0), generalized complex eigenproblem (A - c B)v=0 solved via shift-invert or QZ; REPRODUCE Re_c ~ 5772.22 (critical Reynolds, eigenvalue crossing Im(c)=0) to documented digits; API: modal growth rates sigma_1..sigma_8 along a path as first-class query (vessel flagship 15.3 laminarity objective).
3. Colleague-matrix rootfinding (companion-in-Chebyshev-basis eigenvalues) replacing/augmenting the v1 subdivision roots (which MISS even-multiplicity roots - documented no-claim); fs-ivl interval-Newton certification where requested.
4. D4 (fourth derivative) collocation matrix + clamped BC row replacement (OS needs it).
5. 2D/3D low-rank (Chebyshev outer products, adaptive cross approximation), Fourier-periodic variant (closed curves), Qty-dimensioned function objects, FrankenScipy cross-checks.
Key files: crates/fs-cheb/src/lib.rs (v1 core + conventions), crates/fs-cheb/CONTRACT.md (no-claims enumerate exactly this bead's scope), crates/fs-la/src/eigen.rs (symmetric-only today).

THE ACCEPTANCE TEST PASSED FIRST RUN: critical_reynolds(1.02056, N=48) = 5772.22 vs published 5772.22 (Orszag 1971) - matching to all displayed digits; stability verdicts correct at Re=2000 (stable) and 10000 (unstable); golden 0x7b3be74ed5a6faad IDENTICAL cross-ISA (trj /data/tmp/os_verify.Lzi4xh/run.log 4/4). comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 6ys.19). Executing ITEM 1 first (the complex nonsymmetric eigensolver everything else here needs), design: (1) fs_math::c64 — C64 complex type at L0 (the shared home going forward; fs-fft's private mini-C64 migration recorded as cleanup, NOT done now to avoid golden-hash churn there): operator traits on (re, im) with strict arithmetic, conj, overflow-safe abs (scaled hypot: max-scaling then sqrt — deterministic, no libm), complex sqrt via the stable half-angle formulas on strict det::sqrt. (2) fs-la eigen_complex module: complex Householder Hessenberg reduction (reflectors with the standard complex sign convention beta = -e^{i arg(x1)} |x|), then single-shift QR with WILKINSON shifts (the eigenvalue of the trailing 2x2 closest to h_nn - deterministic tie-break: on exact tie pick the one with smaller |.|, then lexicographic (re, im)), implicit-Q bulge chasing... v1 SIMPLIFICATION recorded: EXPLICIT shifted QR factorization per iteration via complex Givens rotations on the Hessenberg (O(n^2) per sweep, numerically fine, simpler to get right than implicit bulge chasing; perf refinement recorded) with standard deflation (subdiagonal |h_{k+1,k}| <= eps(|h_kk|+|h_{k+1,k+1}|) -> split), 2x2 blocks solved by the complex quadratic formula. Balancing: v1 skips (record; moderate-condition test matrices) - honest no-claim. Eigenvalues only v1 (OS growth rates need eigenvalues; eigenvectors via inverse iteration recorded). API: eig(a: &[C64], n) -> Vec<C64> sorted deterministically (by re then im - canonical presentation). Tests: companion matrix of z^4-1 -> 4th roots of unity; 2x2 rotation -> e^{+-i theta}; embedded real-symmetric matches jacobi_eigh to 1e-10; random complex: trace == sum(eig) and det(via product of eigenvalues) vs Gaussian-elimination det in C64 (tolerance); Hermitian random -> imaginary parts ~ 0; colleague-matrix roots of sin-like Cheb1 vs known roots (wires into fs-cheb LATER - here just a Chebyshev companion fixture); golden hash + trj. title: BRIDGE EPIC: the vertical slice — one flagship, 3-D, at scale, user-runnable, rendered
description: FROM THE 2026-07-11 REALITY CHECK (applied end to end): FrankenSim has ~126 stub-free crates of real, analytically validated component numerics and a best-in-class evidence/determinism spine — but the vision's PRODUCT (give an objective, get back an optimized, certified, RENDERED design at production scale) does not exist. All physics is 2-D fixture-scale; the marquee topopt is heat-only and its named crate ships a disabled runner; LUMEN produces no shaded image; the flywheel closes only on modeled cost units; the only user-runnable pipeline optimizes one scalar. Completing every pre-existing open bead would harden scaffolding but close roughly a third of the gap. THIS EPIC is the organizing spine for the bridge: workstreams WS1 3-D LBM (D3Q19), WS2 CutFEM elasticity to an ENABLED marquee runner, WS3 LUMEN spectral rendering to real images, WS4 production-scale + flagship tier promotion, WS5 user-runnable product spine (runner binaries, catalog, estimate, notebook), WS6 Gauntlet completion (G0 shrinking harness, G2 canonical envelopes, G4 fault injection), WS7 the flywheel with a REAL solver in the loop + declarative queries v1. EXIT CRITERION (falsifiable): a user runs ONE command; it simulates a 3-D flagship at 10^6+ cells under budget/cancellation contracts, optimizes with certified evidence colors intact, writes a replayable ledger, renders a shaded image of the result where the marketing shot and the physics are the same bytes, and the whole run replays bit-identically. Every child bead names its own falsifiable acceptance; house rules apply throughout (golden-bump protocol, roofline admission with trusted baselines, cross-ISA lanes, no laundering, capsule discipline).

TESTS: analytic isosurface battery, DVR golden, metadata round-trip. DEPENDS on spectral tracer v1.

title: WS1: cumulant collision operator for D3Q19 (high-Re stability)
description: WHY: plan sec 8 names the cumulant collision operator as the production LBM collision (BGK is unstable at the Reynolds numbers the vessel/aero flagships need). WHAT: cumulant-space collision for D3Q19 per the Geier formulation: central-moment transform, cumulant relaxation with per-order rates, back-transform; parameterized so BGK remains selectable (the G1 batteries gate BOTH). Numerical care: the transform chains are mul_add-explicit (obq0/nabk lesson: no bare a*b+c in fma-capable contexts; libm-fma bit neutrality across ISAs). ACCEPTANCE: (a) matches BGK to roundoff in the low-Re limit where both are valid (metamorphic G3 check); (b) stable lid-driven 3-D cavity at Re 1000 where BGK visibly is not (documented divergence demonstration as the falsifier); (c) golden hash for seeded deterministic run, both-ISA verified per golden-bump protocol BEFORE blessing (the obq0 lesson is a hard rule here: a collision-schedule change is exactly the class that diverged before). TESTS: moment-conservation unit tests (density/momentum invariance under collision to 1e-14), G1 Taylor-Green 3-D decay order gate, G3 BGK-limit equivalence. DEPENDS on the D3Q19 core bead.

title: Certificate-spine soundness audit: fix false-certificate paths (fs-sos, fs-evidence, fs-ivl)
close_reason: Certificate-spine audit COMPLETE - all nine confirmed findings fixed, regression-pinned, and verified green in one final pass. S1 (fs-sos forged PSD/Lyapunov certs on non-symmetric input): symmetrize in min_eigenvalue, 5e9f62e, regression non_symmetric_input_cannot_forge. E1/E2/E3 (fs-evidence color::compose not outward-rounding / NaN-dropping Mul fold / disjoint-regime dispersion 0): outward rounding + NaN guard + inf dispersion, 231c8d3 (+55787fb convergent fail-closed work), regression evd_008. E4 (enclosure/estimate NaN drops, Verified via literals): fail closed on NaN/inverted bounds at the Verified gate, 11763f3 (+ d0c1e09 made Certified opaque under gp3.2.1). V1 (fs-ivl orient3d_sos antisymmetry violation, HIGH): rewritten as a full Edelsbrunner-Mucke moment-curve ladder - antisymmetry structural (sort+parity), consistency by construction, verified against concretely-perturbed orient3d at three kappa scales, 80739f4. V2/V3 (Interval::exp overflow lower bound +inf; add/sub NaN from opposing infinities): clamped/guarded, d6bfea4, regressions exp_overflow_lower_bound_stays_finite_and_encloses + add_sub_opposing_infinities_are_whole_not_nan. S2 (SosCertificate::verify coefficient-inf-norm is NOT a value bound, MED-HIGH): 049538f separates ALGEBRAIC verification (verify = decomposition identity only, documented as never a value claim) from CERTIFIED VALUE BOUNDS - certified_bound_global (exact residual expansions) and certified_bound_on (radius-scoped); the exact root-at-1e6 counterexample is pinned in s2_counterexample_cannot_forge_a_value_claim. FINAL VERIFICATION (this close): fs-ivl 12+11+5, fs-sos 26+2(+in-file), fs-evidence 4+7+12, fs-spectral 9 - all green; audited-sound components recorded in the bead history (fs-spectral propagation, rank no-laundering law, orient2d_sos, expansion arithmetic). The spine's certificates can no longer be forged by non-symmetric input, non-enclosing composition, NaN laundering, tie-break inconsistency, overflow gaps, or coefficient-norm tolerance abuse.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text:
Deep soundness audit of the certificate spine (fs-evidence, fs-ivl, fs-sos,
fs-spectral) via 3 adversarial review agents + independent verification. Each
finding below has a CONFIRMED concrete counterexample. Ranked; [FIX] = fixing
now, [BEAD] = needs careful/research-grade fix, tracked here.

== fs-sos ==
S1 [FIX, HIGH] is_psd / lyapunov_certifies_stability forge FALSE certificates on
   non-symmetric input. min_eigenvalue's cyclic-Jacobi reads only the upper
   triangle; a non-symmetric matrix returns garbage (raw diagonal when upper-tri
   is 0). Counterexample: A=[[0,-1],[-1,0]] (eigenvalues +/-1, UNSTABLE) with
   non-symmetric P=[[1,0],[3,1]] -> lyapunov_certifies_stability = TRUE (false
   cert). Fix: symmetrize (M+Mᵀ)/2 in min_eigenvalue (correct quadratic-form
   semantics; midpoint(x,x)==x so symmetric input is bitwise-unchanged).
   CONFIRMED reproduced + FIXED (regression test added).
S2 [BEAD, MED-HIGH] SosCertificate::verify uses the coefficient ∞-norm, which
   does NOT bound the polynomial's value on ℝ. A tiny-residual cert can have
   p(x0) < lower_bound by an unbounded amount. Counterexample (tol=1e-9):
   squares=[x-1e6], lower_bound=0, p=(x-1e6)²-1e-9-1e-9·x -> residual≈9.3e-10 ≤
   tol -> verify=TRUE, but p(1e6)≈-9.8e-4 < 0. Violation scales with root
   location (unbounded for any fixed tol>0). Fix is non-trivial (rigorous SOS
   needs PSD-Gram verification or exact-zero residual, not a coeff-norm tol) —
   deferred to a careful fix so I don't ship a wrong one.

== fs-evidence ==
E1 [FIX, HIGH] color::compose does NOT round outward, so a composed
   Verified{lo,hi} is not a guaranteed enclosure (the sibling
   NumericalCertificate::compose in lib.rs DOES round outward — one ulp each
   way). Counterexample: compose(V{0.1,0.1}, V{0.2,0.2}, Add) -> lo=hi=
   0.30000000000000004, but the true real sum 0.1_f64+0.2_f64 = 0.3000…0166 is
   below lo -> the "Verified" interval EXCLUDES the exact answer. Also Mul
   (4 round-to-nearest products). Fix: outward-round Add/Mul like lib.rs.
E2 [FIX/BEAD, MED] color::compose Mul (and lib.rs Mul) fold NaN products (0×∞)
   with f64::min/max which DROP NaN -> lo=+∞,hi=−∞ inverted interval marked
   Verified. NaN-guard the fold.
E3 [FIX, MED] disjoint validated regimes demote to Estimated{dispersion:0.0}
   (claims ZERO spread) instead of ∞; the demote() path correctly uses ∞.
E4 [BEAD, MED-LOW] enclosure()/estimate() drop a NaN bound via min/max (silent
   data loss) -> false-precision interval; and Verified reachable via struct
   literals / exact(NaN) / color_of ignoring discrepancy_rel when cards empty.

== fs-ivl ==
V1 [BEAD, HIGH] orient3d_sos violates ANTISYMMETRY (SoS is contradictory). The
   coplanar tie-break orient2d_sos_about ignores the 4th point's position (only
   its index enters, in a deeper fallback). Counterexample (coplanar on x=y+z):
   pa=[0,0,0],pb=[4,4,0],pc=[4,0,4],pd=[2,1,1]; orient3d=Zero; orient3d_sos with
   a single 0↔3 transposition returns Positive BOTH ways (must flip). Breaks the
   P2 BRIO-Delaunay tie-break contract. Delicate SoS fix — bead, not a rushed
   patch. (orient2d_sos itself is correct; defect isolated to the 3D cascade.)
V2 [FIX/BEAD, MED] Interval::exp lower bound -> +∞ on overflow (arg ≳709.78):
   exp([710,720]) = [∞,∞], excludes the true finite values > f64::MAX. Should
   clamp lo to f64::MAX. V3 [LOW] Interval add/sub emit NaN from opposing
   infinities (bypass new()'s NaN guard); Mul/Div already guard.

== SOUND (audited, no finding) ==
fs-spectral: propagate never promotes (Estimated is min rank); makes no rigorous
enclosure claim; compose_conditioning over-estimates (conservative); hysteresis
correct. fs-ivl finite-range interval enclosure + all exact predicate SIGNS +
expansion arithmetic + orient2d_sos: sound. fs-evidence rank-level no-laundering
law: sound (compose never raises rank above min(operands); verified_from is the
only Verified door; ColorRank Ord total/correct).
